boardgame: Civì: La Terra delle Grandi Opportunità
description:
CiVi is a game for 2-6 players for frantic and exhilarating job search. Will you find the job of your dreams? There will be those looking for a permanent job, those on their own business, those trying to become an expert in their field and those who are always looking for clever and “very Italian” shortcuts to fame and success. Each player will use a card representing a candidate, with his knowledge, his strengths and weaknesses while trying to achieve one possible winning goals. 
Players take turns, representing two weeks of real life, during which, while the candidate acts with his "magheggio" (cheat) or "lucky" cards, opponents will try to hinder his efforts using a myriad of Event Cards really .... "unlucky"!

There are many sudden events we never consider possible that could happen to us. There are as well many realities that we ignore or perceive far away from us. But this is not how life works, and in this game you are going to discover it. 
Moving to another city it's always a big change – to someone it can be more stressful than to others. You, as part of the Fonte family, are going to face events that will change your life.  
Living a quite normal, if not lucky, life, Eric is a model student. Member of the football and the astronomy club, Eric is going to start college soon after family relocation in Arlington.  
Looking at him and his sister Emily, it seems it's her who is going to have the most problems, moving during high school and leaving all her friends. Everyone has great expectations out of him, but then again nobody knows what can happen. 
Many unexpected things will happen along the way, and only standing together and focusing on Eric's wellbeing you will all be able to reach the final goal: start a new life again in where Eric’s disturb will be accepted and lived with, giving you a new vision of life. 
Together with the other family's member you'll help Eric to go through the discovery of his own schizophrenia, watching him and all of you react and adapt to the situation.  
It's not a simple task, but life never is.  
Put to test your family relationship and chemistry and discover what you are capable of.

AIM OF THE GAME
Your aim is to prevent Eric's stress exceed the value 10. You'll also have to avoid all characters to be in Recovery at the same time(see Recovery Phase).
If one of these conditions occurs, the game ends immediately with players defeat; if otherwise you are able to reach the eleventh turn of the game, you win (see End of the game). Crazy Brother is a cooperative game, meaning that if you win, everybody wins. If you lose, you'll share the defeat.

The game unfolds in turns, up to eleven. Each turn is made up of four phases:

1. Events and Accidents Phase

2. Actions Phase - made up of  three under-phases
2.1 selection of the Action Cards
2.2 resolution of the Action Cards
2.3 discard of the Action Cards

3. Eric Phase

4. Recovery Phase

description:
Upon the Wind is a programmed adventure using Dark City Games Legends of the Ancient World rule set. These rules provide gameplay very similar to that of Melee and Wizard from Metagaming. This adventure is compatible with those games as well.

Battles are fought with counters on a hex map that is included with the game.

From the publisher:
"...You surmise that you have been transported to none other than the fabled Ships Graveyard, the place where the wrecks of ships lost in the air are said to go..."

description:

From the box:

A Twist on the classic game. A Fast playing game of fun and skill. Throw the colourful Yatzy Prisms up to 3 times to score on your turn. Can you match a pair, a full-house, a small or high straight or even 4 of a kind? Matching 8 of a kind scores the highest value Yatzy.

Dead Ends is a solitaire card game where the player shuffles the cards, then places the Entrance card on the top of the deck, and the Exit card on the bottom.

The cards consist of Path Cards - hallways, intersections, and dead ends - Item Cards - which can be used at certain spots in the game - and Choice Cards - where the player needs to make a decision regarding their path.

The goal is to reach the end of the deck without getting lost.  The player loses the game by getting "lost", meaning that they have no possible exits from the most recent intersection on their map.

boardgame: Cast Off: A Game of Inappropriate Casting
description: Cast Off is a party game where you’ll put actors, characters, and other voices in an audition for a well known film. You’ll score points as your team tries to guess information about the film, and who was trying out for what part, with as few as 4 people or as many friends as you’d like to laugh with.

Welcome to Chicago. Don’t smell the gunpowder in the air or the scent of perfume coming from the whorehouses or the aftershave of the mobsters hanging around the corner.

Stay alert, grab your bucks before your enemies beat you to it, build your gang fast and better than all the rest.  Because the next thing you smell could be the flowers on your grave.

The game is based by an innovative system of play called "confronts": each mobster, according to his skills to do an action, must confront the power of the Gang (coming from influence tokens) with all other powers in the same district.

Each of the players plays a Boss of a Gang on Chicago or the Police Chief at the beginning of the 1920. To rule the city, as Boss, you must command your mobsters as pawn to earn money coming from robbery, run clubs and assassinate enemies. If you represent the Law, instead, you must fight the crime with your policemen, closing the clubs, capturing mobsters and also try to send one of the Bosses to jail.

Kingdom is a game where players battle against each other trying to be the first to match “The Ideal Kingdom” which needs to be made of the right collection of cards. However, the right cards needed in this game may not be the right cards in another game. True, you may pull the same set of cards, but it is highly unlikely there are 531,441 different combinations. You could push ahead getting the right cards in the positions you need them to be in, or do you work to thwart other players efforts. Adding the change a winning combination of the ideal kingdom allows for changes in strategy from one game to the next. Each player starts with three cards and needs to work to create the “Ideal Kingdom” which is a combination of six cards. Each color of card has specific attacks, defense, and special action. Certain colors cards can only sit next to other certain color cards, and coming in contact with opposing cards leads to inactivity or removal. That has something to do with court politics. Cards are drawn and played in turn order, with actions being directed by the cards you have laid out in front of you. Some of those actions allow you to draw extra cards, look at another player’s cards, steal cards, or battle. But, your strategy has to be planned so you can create the ideal kingdom to win. There are 9 major game changer cards in the deck, like creating Immunity from the Black Plague, or Jesters that will play tricks and change the cards. This actually helps the player with the least amount of cards to actually come back and try to win.
This Add-on features 13 chips! 2 Titans and a giant 3 chip crab for solo fights in RoR, and 2 full battle reenactments for TLC (Hannibal Barca and his elephant army and Attila the Hun)!

boardgame: FOOD FIGHT: A Game of Scones
description:
A competitive skirmish nano card game by Charles Ward.

SUMMARY
Players play on a card size board with 9 spaces. The spaces are not connected equally and some spaces have an advantage when doing a ranged attack. Players move tokens on the board trying to eliminate the opponent's pieces and be as close as possible to their opponent's picnic spot at the end of the game. Players take actions by playing their hand of 8 cards, or both sides of the four cards. The game ends at the end of round 8, or after all cards have been played.

GAMEPLAY
Players choose 1 card from their hand (either the top or bottom half of that card) and simultaneously reveal them. The player with the highest initiative number (in the coin) goes first, and each player either takes 1 of the 2 actions on their card's chosen half or passes. Cards that have been played once are flipped from the silver coin side to the gold coin side and return to the players hand; cards played twice are discarded.

On their turn a player may move 1 or more of their tokens, attack in the same location or a target location, block, hide, taunt, or add 1 reinforcement. Although the actions are resolved in order (highest number card), a block or hide can cancel a certain type of attack as a reaction or preemptively.

The game ends after 8 rounds, when all cards have been discarded. Equidistant tokens are removed after which the player nearest to the opponent's star wins.

boardgame: Imperial Settlers: 3 Is a Magic Number
description:
Imperial Settlers: 3 Is a Magic Number, the second Empire Pack for Imperial Settlers, introduces a new rule: SET. This new ability allows players to score and trigger new effects each time they build three cards in a particular color. With this rule, the drafting phase and the choice of the "right" card to draft becomes even more interesting.

This expansion includes new cards for the common deck as well as new cards for each of the five factions and two cards for use in the solitaire game.

description: A promo tile originally available as a reward through the 2016 Dice Tower Kickstarter.

CREATE your wizard deck from a combination of spell, race & treasure cards.

ADVENTURE board game or RPG-style!

IMPROVE your wizard deck with epic loot and advanced spells.

---

Young Wizards is a card-based tabletop RPG/board game where each player assumes the role of a... Well... A Young Wizard.

Each player constructs a deck of cards representing their spells, equipment and other features (like race). That deck can grow more powerful over time as the player adventures into twisting caverns and dark forests, discovering treasures & gaining experience.

All of that sounds great, and can be done with other tabletop RPGs. How does Young Wizards differ?


     The game can be played without a Game Master(GM)/Dungeon Master for a more "board game" feel.
     If played with a GM, setting up adventures & encounters is a breeze, leaving you more time to focus on your story.
     More schools of magic than you can shake a wand at. Deck-builders rejoice! (of course, we have deck pre-constructions for the wee ones)
     The card-based approach provides the perfect "bridge" between younger players and the deeper world of tabletop RPGs / board games.

boardgame: Fluxx: The Board Game – Scramble Colors
description:
An action card for Fluxx: The Board Game. It reads:

Action - play + discard
Scramble Colors

Gather up all color cards currently in use, shuffle them together, and deal them back out.

You must do this either before or after you make all of your moves.

Who'd want to be a mouse? There's so little cheese around, and mousetraps are everywhere! 
 Not only that, enemy mice are afoot, armed to the teeth with crossbows, plasma grenades, shotguns, flamethrowers, bazookas, evil space lasers and a host of special powers! 
 In the fast-paced card game Cheese Or Die, players take on the role of mice competing to horde the most cheese, whilst arming up in a bid to steal cheese from their opponents. Will you take your chances and draw from the Cheese Deck - a deck which becomes increasingly 'dangerous' as the game goes on - or play the long game by collecting weapons and bonuses, and smashing your rivals in combat?

To win, you'll need to be ruthless and pick your fights carefully.
 Oh, and avoid proximity mines...

In Cheese Or Die, the first player to collect 6 cheese cards wins... but this can be difficult! To do this, players can attack opponents by choosing a weapon card from their hand, adding any bonuses, and playing any number of special cards on top. Expect brutal fights with lots of surprises! Special cards allow you to alter the normal victory conditions of the game, or mess with your opponents' combat total, for example. Only if attackers win can they draw a card from the defender's cheese cards. Just don't draw a mine!

There's also the Cheese Deck, which has its own dynamics. Players can take a chance and draw directly from this deck, or set traps for their opponents by looking ahead and loading the deck.

All in all this is a fast, fun and tense card game that provides some hilarious moments!

Timeline: Cannes & Cinema is a card game played using 55 cards. Each card depicts a Movie on both sides, with the year in which that movie was selected at the Cannes Film Festival occurred on only one side. Players take turns placing a card from their hand in a row on the table. After placing the card, the player reveals the date on it. If the card was placed correctly with the date in chronological order with all other cards on the table, the card stays in place; otherwise the card is removed from play and the player takes another card from the deck.

The first player to get rid of all his cards by placing them correctly wins. If multiple players go out in the same round, then everyone else is eliminated from play and each of those players are dealt one more card for another round of play. If only one player has no cards after a bonus round, he wins; otherwise play continues until a single player goes out.

Timeline: Cannes & Cinema can be combined with any other title in the Timeline series.

Contains 55 cards in French and 55 cards (the same) in English.

A deck of 9 Scenario cards used for changing the way Evolution is played.

To use these cards, you draw one card at random from the scenario deck, and the rule described on it is in force for the entire game.

boardgame: Tiny Epic Kingdoms: Heroes' Call – Deluxe Edition
description:
Deluxe edition of Tiny Epic Kingdoms: Heroes' Call, containing three more factions (Imps, Ooze and Insectoids) and a mini-expansion (Keys of Aughmoore Exploration Tiles).

The war continues and spreads further and further. New factions have arrived from the north igniting more epic battles than before. Heroes rise over the fallen bodies of their enemies. Kingdoms spread across the frozen wastelands in search of silver. War Towers grow taller than the trees. Who will be victorious in the end?

Tiny Epic Kingdoms: Heroes' Call introduces heroes to the war. Heroes will bring to play a variety of benefits unique to their class. Players will use a larger meeple to represent the hero of their faction.

Additionally new mechanisms are introduced to the building action of the game. This will have players tracking their tower progress by placing tower cubes onto regions they possess. Players are encouraged to keep these tower cubes safe because losing them in war results in a loss of progress on the tower card.

Furthermore Tiny Epic Kingdoms: Heroes' Call introduces a slew of new factions, new regions with new mechanisms and a new resource type, and even brings solo play to Tiny Epic Kingdoms!

The lore of Tiny Epic Kingdoms: Heroes' Call occurs after Tiny Epic Kingdoms and before Tiny Epic Defenders.

Advertised as a “Dark Fairy Tale for Grownups”, The Teind is a non-collectable card game for 2-5 players that plays in about 90 minutes. Players take the role of 1 of 5 Fairy factions who vie to collect victory points. Players deploy a portion of 80 unique characters to various locations to collect Babyteeth (the game’s currency), kidnap children, summon beast familiars, and discover new glamour upgrades. The combat system uses simultaneous player decisions and a 4-set non-transitive dice set that reflect the character’s attributes of strength, agility, intelligence, and social acumen.

"My code name is Chronos, and now you are my Agents. The threads of the time have been torn and mysterious artifacts suddenly appeared in the world. The most dangerous areas of the metropolis are in turmoil. The Chronomancer is destroying the Reality, it is your task to defeat her!"

A team of fighters and experts is looking for OOPART - Out Of Place ARTifacts, and is ready to clash with the worst criminals of the Planet. That group of agents has dared defy the Chronomancer to restore the Reality and Time's order".

Chronomancer is a cooperative board game, for 2-6 players with a 1-3 hrs/game duration, set in metropolitan areas in hot-spot countries all around the world. Each player controls an Agent of the Chrono Team and has to complete the Mission of the game by recovering weapons, valuables and ancient artifacts. Moving through a scenario that simulates a dangerous city, Agents will fight against mercenaries, evil bosses, powerful creatures that came from the past, and sometimes with the Chronomancer too.

Different kinds of action are available for each Agent, such as melee, fire-arm attacks, technological tools and bombing raids.

The main feature of Chronomancer is the modular design of the board that allows creating countless environments and collision situations, offering a new game experience at every time.

Depending on the missions and on the related objective to be achieved, Chronomancer also suggests a multiple level game structure and an evolving plot. All the Agents get involved in the main quest that must be completed for the final Victory. Each Agent can earn honor points during the fulfillment of the mission, but will also have his/her own agenda of personal goals to be reached. If the main quest has been resolved, the Agent with the greater amount of honor points wins over everyone. Moreover, by following the numerical order of the main missions (Mission 1, Mission 2, etc.), you will discover the Chronomacer's plans, living a long adventure game by game. But you can also pick missions at random and play them on their own, with different sets and enemies every time.

A modular board, 10 mission, 8 characters, 4 bosses an several time themed creature and characters from the past grant an always new gaming experience and great longevity.

A Card/Dice Game for us IT Nerds.

Bug Industries is a game with cards and dice. Each player has an IT project assigned, in which a bunch of little, nasty bugs are hidden. 64 of these ulcer causing monsters must be eliminated for the project to run smoothly. The first bug-free player wins the game. You're not a developer, but are a part of the IT world? Don’t worry, Bug Industries has enough bugs and fun for you as well.

The game rules: On the game board – the headquarters of Bug Industries – you move your game chip up or down – depending on whether you can deduct or receive more bugs. You roll two dice: a numbered dice called “bug dice” and an “action dice.” The result of the bug dice can be directly deducted from your total bug count. The action dice gives you different options: pause, roll the dice again, draw a bugs card or draw a code card.

There are two kinds of bug cards: The good ones reduce your total bug count and the bad ones increase it. With the second type of cards (code cards) you can influence the whole game. While you have to play the bug cards immediately, you can keep the code cards for yourself without showing them to the other players and then play them when the time is right. Knowing when to drop the code cards on the other players is a key part of the strategy.

From the publisher:

"Seguindo a descrição da testemunha, a polícia faz um retrato falado. Com base nele, o detetive detém os suspeitos para averiguações e tenta prender o culpado. Não é interessante? Em RETRATO FALADO isto é ainda mais, já que às vezes você é o detetive, outras a testemunha e até o desenhista da polícia. Não é um desafio?"

Or, in a free translation:

"Following the description of the witness, the police makes a police sketch. Based on it, the detective holds the suspects for questioning and attempts to arrest the culprit. Isn't it interesting? In RETRATO FALADO this is even more, since you are sometimes the detective, other times you are the witness and even the police artist. Isn't it a challenge?"

In Retrato Falado, a cooperative game for only three people, one person - the witness - will try to memorize some cards with faces drawings and then make a police sketch of one randomly selected card (without seeing). A second player - the police artist - will "draw" the face described using the tiles representing eyes, nose, mouth, etc. Finally, the third player - the detective - will try to find out what was the card that the witness described. If he chooses correctly, everyone scores.

The heavy ISU self-propelled guns weighed almost 50 metric tons and combined three battle roles: assault gun, tank destroyer, and artillery. The ISU- 152 could deliver devastating explosive shells at range while the ISU-122 boasted better armour piercing capabilities.

Named after the Soviet leader Joseph Stalin, the IS series of heavy tanks was designed to help the red army break through enemy positions. Its gun fired heavy high-explosive shells that could destroy enemy tanks, entrenchments, and bunkers alike.

The SU-85 and SU-100 were built on the excellent T-34 chassis, replacing the turret with a hull mounted gun. These assault guns were nicknamed ‘Cat-killers’ for their ability to knock out heavy German tanks like the Panther and Tiger.

From it’s first encounters in 1941 the T-34 would become one of the most influential tank designs of the Second World War. The T-34 was continously refined over the years to increase effectiveness and decrease costs, allowing vast numbers to be fielded at once. Eventually, the T-34/85 variant was built to combat the appearance of heavy German tanks.

This pack contains:
1x Unassembled T-34 tank
1x T-34 tank card
1x T-34/85 tank card
2x Hero card
1x Crew cards
2x Upgrade cards

Dead of Winter: Rich Sommer is a promotional item for Dead of Winter or Dead of Winter: The Long Night that was initially available with preorders of the latter title from the Plaid Hat Games online store.

This promo includes a new character, standee, and Crossroads card.

Part of the Dead of Winter series.

From the publisher:

Bad Campaign is the presidential party game. You are a candidate running a half-baked campaign for the Presidency against your friends.  Players take turns reading question cards containing campaign slogans, political promises, and awkward statements.  The candidates submit one of the hundreds of crazy answer cards to fill in the blank. The combinations are read out loud and the reader votes for their favorite. The candidate that gains the most votes by the end of the game wins and is moving into a plush crib on Pennsylvania Avenue. Just like in real presidential campaigns you will make plenty of horrible promises and terrible statements, but in Bad Campaign the more ridiculous you are, the better your chances for getting elected.  The game pulls content from real quotes and situations.  Combined with our offbeat answer cards you will be laughing with your friends at the fat cats in Washington in no time.


     90 Fill-in-the-blank Question Cards- Containing Real Quotes, Awkward Statements, and Questionable Campaign Promises



     450 Crazy Answer Cards- Creating Millions of Ridiculous Combinations



     Bi-partisan fun. No political knowledge necessary or needed!



     Fancy Gray Box Perfect for Holding Your New Favorite Game



     Sense of Satisfaction You Will Derive By Participating in Our Country's Great Electoral Process OR Your Opportunity To Make Fun Of It

In MiniSkull Caverns, 1-3 heroes will compete to explore the caverns beneath the ruins of MiniSkull Castle and return with riches beyond their wildest dreams.  You are one of those optimistic explorers.  Are you prepared to risk your life for diamonds the size of a wild boar?  Along the way you will encounter monsters, battle other heroes, and recover some priceless gems.  Be careful though, the more noise you make, the more you will attract the deadly denizens of MiniSkull Caverns.  Before you can escape with your spoils you must battle the affectionately, though unsuitably, named Tiny.  Dragons don’t like it when you try to burgle their baubles.

Object: The object of MiniSkull Caverns is to guide your hero through the maze of caverns, managing your strength, and battling monsters  and other heroes, all while searching for priceless Gems.  Once you have acquired two of these Gems you must return to the center space on the map and battle Tiny in order to escape.

2 Player Variant: When playing with two players you must defeat Tiny and escape with three gems instead of two.

Solo Variant: Collect all four gems and escape before time runs out.

Mechanics: The mechanics use a combination of action selection using a rotating action card and player selection control card, similar to what is in Empire Engine, along with straight up dice combat against monsters and PvP dice combat with strategic modifiers the players 
choose.

Across eight turns, you will live out your life in a city somewhere in the world.

Each turn represents a phase in one’s life:

1. Starting Out
    2. Meeting that special someone
    3. Settling down with that special someone
    4. Kids arrive (by birth, adoption or otherwise)
    5. Kids grow up to be teenagers
    6. Kids leave home, the nest is empty
    7. Getting old
    8. Approaching this life’s end

The game is played on a five by five tile 'city' - with 13 fixed tiles (representing CBD, four inner suburbs, four outer suburbs and four corner 'holiday home' locations) and 12 Modifiable (Mod) tiles which represent different landmarks (for example restaurant, art gallery, rubbish dump etc).

Each Mod tile has a scoring value each turn, which can change depending on the turn (phase of life) you are in. For example living near a retirement home scores well in later rounds.

You start the game with a house in an inner suburb. Players draft a hand of 'mod' tiles which allow them to modify the landmarks on the board (for example turn a park into a 'fancy' park, which scores more highly) - they also receive one temporary closure tile (means a tile is not scored for the current round) and one demolition tile (which permanently removes a Mod tile and replaces it with one from the side of the board).

Your objective is to optimise where you live in relation to your particular stage in life, and this is scored at the end of each turn.

Each turn you either:
1. Move house
2. Buy a holiday home (allowing you to place a second home on a corner of the board and then score the adjacent tiles each turn) - you may only perform this action once per game, or
3. Modify a Mod tile

You cannot pass in Living.

Each turn you then score the tiles orthogonally adjacent to your home and your holiday home (if you have one).

At the end of the eighth turn, the highest score wins the game!

Whether you’re a mad scientist, cult leader, mechanical genius, or just someone with a God complex, pull up a chair and prepare to build a better beast.  By harnessing the powers of the occult, genetics, technology, and even the forces of nature, you’ll be creating you’re own monstrosity in no time.  Of course, having your own personal “Igors” to gather bio-scrap, build some spare heads, and place a few wagers couldn’t hurt either.

So jump in, grab the best parts for yourself, and get ready to pound your pals into a pulp!  Victory might only cost you an arm and a leg...

Build and battle unique creatures through a combination of card drafting, worker placement, and dice fueled combat in this game of monster melee mayhem for 3-5 players!

Rock On! The Geology Game of Brains & Sheer Determination

Spark or expand kids' interest in Rocks, Geology and our magnificent Earth via the EXPANDED VERSION of this teacher-recommended, fun, learning game.

Now includes 5 levels of play offering fun, challenge and education for the pre-reader through elementary school age.

This American-Made product includes 60+ polished Rocks & Minerals and a 15-Specimen Rock & Mineral Collection any kid would be proud to own.

Player Cards for up to 6 players along with a Master Rock Guide and 18 Rock Call Cards chocked full of educational & fun facts.

How to play:
Rock Hound Level - 
Players take turns drawing a call card from the pile.  If the rock on the call card matches a rock on the player cards, place any random one of the 60+ rock marker chips on top of the picture on the player cards.

Winning: The first player to cover all the rocks on their player card wins by saying "Rock On!"

Pink, Green, Purple and Orange Levels -
Choose to play 1 of the 4 pick axe levels on the call cards.  Each level is played the same way: The 1st player draws a card from the call card pile. Without revealing the card, they read the name of the rock.  Anyone who has that rock on their player card, places a random marker chip on top of the picture on their player card.  Also match and place the rock specimen on its picture on the master rock guide.  Next, the player who drew the call card asks the player to their left a question from the correct level of play.  If they answer correctly, they place a marker chip on one of the "Rocky" the Geologist squares on their player card.  If answered incorrectly, continuing clockwise around the table, players take turns trying to correctly answer the question.  Of a "Rock my world" card is drawn and the question is answered correctly, 2 "Rocky" squares may be covered on the player card.

Winning: The first player to cover all the squares on their player card wins.  Or, After all call cards have been drawn, the winner is the player with the most squares covered on their player card.

From the card pack:

"CAUTION: This pack will add 3 hours of fun and laughter to your next trip or party.

Why Sixes? Play six rounds, write down six things that fit the category, or write just one thing that fits into six different categories. Most points after six rounds (15 minutes of play time) is the winner.

In Match rounds, you are trying to match your Opponents' answers, and everyone is your opponent. In Unique rounds you search for answers uniquely yours. In Lightning rounds, you want six matches all in different  categories.

Great for groups of folks who do not know each other! Terrific social get-togethers; family events; or game night fillers. This game takes only 15 minutes per session but you will want to play it over and over again for hours!"

From publisher:

SiXeS! Travel & Promo Pack is a promo pack for SiXeS! that provides rules and enough cards (6) to play the game. Great fun for gamers age 12 and up.

SiXeS is about things that are similar and different. Play six rounds, thinking of six things each round and trying to predict what you will write that will match — or not match — what the other players write, depending on whether you are in a "match" round or a "unique" round. The player with the most points after six rounds wins.

SiXeS is #6 in the E•G•G Series from Eagle-Gryphon Games.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Little Storm on Holiday is a children's game. Each player has a holiday board. One player after the other draws a tile, and the tile goes to the player who has the picture on there board. First player to get four tiles on their board is the winner. The theme in the game is different kinds of bicycles, cars, aircraft, ships and buses.

War of the Races is a fast-paced card game that is fun, easy to learn and extremely strategic.

5 armies - Orcs, Humans, Elves, Dwarves and Demons - fight for supremacy in the ancient kingdom of Erathmor. Command your armies, send out troops, utilize heroes, sorcerers and more to eliminate opposing armies and leave your mark in history with the War of the Races.

Each race is sold as a separate deck of cards, each with unique abilities and advantages. Players send out infantry to attack opposing armies or the King, who start with 50 and 5 health, respectively. The infantry consist of soldiers, archers, rogues, guards and fighters. These cards can be enhanced with special spells. In addition, a guard can be used to prevent that card from being easily defended against. Next, players send out their sorcerers, who utilize water, earth and fire spells to attack the opposing armies. Other players must counter with other sorcerers, creating a simple but effective 'rock, paper, scissors' effect! Lastly, heroes are sent the field, each with unique abilities, such as healing the army, reflecting any opposing attack back to the dealer, calling for reinforcements and more. Strategy cards are also used to take opposing soldiers as enemies, revive your King, and more.

The last army to remain wins the War of the Races!

Little Storm playes hide'n'seek is a children's game. Each player has a board. One player after the other draws a tile, and the tile goes to the player who has the picture on there board. First player to get four tiles on their board is the winner. The theme in the game is Little Storm pictures in houses or behind trees etc, with focus on the child's understanding of prepositions: over, under, next to, in front of and behind.

Little Storm Thinks is a children's game. The players are each given one wooden stick of each colour. Then one by one they roll a die with different colours on the sides, and got to take a wooden stick of that colour they get on the die. Each player also have a card (thought card) with a figure on, and have to make their figure on the card with there wooden sticks. After the dice roll a player can place as many sticks on the card that is possible. There are three versions to play the game. The easy game is played with the white thought cards. A figure is complete when the wooden sticks are in the correct places (as shown on the thought card). The difficult game is played with the coloured thought cards. A figure is not complete unless the wooden sticks are in the same colours and in the correct places as shown on the thought card. There is also a cooperative game version.

Gæt & Form med Storm is a children's cooperation game. The players must jump, sing, stand on one leg, and more, while the players gamepartner shapes figures in plasticine. The longer the player can keep on the longer the mate can shape. Then the player must guess what this figure is, and if the player do not guess correctly the other team may try. 2 cunning jokers and a memory game like concept gives the game a strategic dimension and also tests memory.

Tragedy has struck the prestigious girls' prep school. Psychic radiation emanating from unspeakable abominations has awakened a dark phenomenon: the girls' emotions have become manifest, taking on terrifying forms. Staff and students have vanished, leaving behind only whispers of horror.

Trinity, the head girl, now a patient at the maximum-security wing of Darkwood Asylum, revealed a chilling truth: the girls' imaginations have become reality. Her words echo through the asylum's corridors: "The things we imagined came to life..."

In Trinity's Challenge, you'll experience the chaos of midterm exams, where emotions run high and psychic energies clash. You'll fight for survival, unconsciously unleashing your frustration and anger upon your opponents. The madness will only subside when your targets are eliminated.

Roll the dice to accumulate psychic energy, then unleash devastating waves to shatter your opponents' minds and bodies. But beware: a single misstep will trigger a crippling backlash, inflicting a debilitating headache that will leave you reeling.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Storm på Ordet is children's word game. By moving on the board and draw cards, the played can get the mission to say sausage while you blow, make the sound of a helicopter, put together words in a fun way, or even make foot gymnastics. One point for each mission completed. The player with most points at the end of the game is the winner.

Calm before the Little Storm is a children's card game of motion, concentration and memory. Each turn first begin with a part of concentration, and when the storm comes and it's time for the activity. In the calm before the storm, participants must remember the cards they draw, until they pull a Storm card, then it would be time for the activities drawn. For each motion remembered from the cards, the player can keep the card. The player in the end of the game with the most cards is the winner.

A trivia game for the globetrotters ! Like the other games in the Fin Finaud family, you don't have to know the answer but rather guess who's going to be right or wrong. The game consists of 400 question cards related to geography, nearly the half on the countries, their capitals, flags, continent, and the other half on the world in general.

The door creaks open, its hinges wailing in protest. Jane's voice trembles as she asks, "What are you doing?!" You reply, your voice laced with a mix of excitement and trepidation, "This decrepit mansion could hold secrets...and treasures. We'd be fools to ignore it." Your companions nod in agreement, their eyes gleaming with a mix of curiosity and fear.

As you ascend to the attic, the air grows thick with the stench of decay. You begin to rummage through the dusty relics, but your search is abruptly interrupted by the door slamming shut. Jane's scream pierces the air, and she stumbles toward the door, her finger quivering as she points to the opposite corner.

You follow her gaze, and your heart freezes. A figure stumbles toward you, its movements jerky and unnatural. A z-zombie, its eyes vacant, its skin gray and decaying, it shambles closer. Your companions cluster around Jane, their faces etched with terror, as they realize the door won't budge...it's locked, trapping you with the unnatural creature.

You and your companions are trapped in a desperate bid for survival. To escape the zombie-infested mansion, you'll need to crack the combination locks on each door, room by room. Your fate will be decided by the roll of the dice, as you strive to achieve the crucial goals that will secure your escape.

The stakes are dire: failure will condemn you to a gruesome demise, with the zombie feasting on your flesh. The horrific consequence of defeat is that you'll rise again, joining the undead hordes as a mindless, flesh-eating monster.

Will you and your companions successfully navigate the treacherous mansion, overcoming the zombie threat and emerging alive? The dice will decide.

WH1CH GAM3, a.k.a. W3LCH35 5P13L, is a small card game that can be used to determine which game your group should play next.

Each person chooses one game they wish to play, with the player who finished last in the previous game (or the host) choosing a second game. Place all of these games on the table. Each person draws a card at random, face down. The person with the lowest numbered card removes a game, then the person with the next lowest card removes a game, etc., until only one game remains on the table. Now it's time to play!

Multiple versions of WH1CH GAM3 have been published, all of which play the same and all of which have different sets of numbers in them. Shuffle one or more sets together to determine what to play when you have more than eight players on hand...

Originally available as a promo in the 2016 Easter Basket (Brettspiel Osternest).

The promo consists of a council board.

Originally available in the 2016 Easter Basket (Brettspiel Osternest).

A small expansion consisting of 8 penguin islands and rules to play.

boardgame: Haus der Sonne: Lava Tiles Mini Expansion
description:
Originally available through the 2016 Easter Basket (Brettspiel Osternest).

A mini expansion consisting of 6 lava tiles and rules to play.

A small expansion originally available from the 2016 Easter Basket (Brettspiel Osternest).

The expansion consists of 4 vagrant tiles, 1 itinerant preacher tile and the rules to play.

Originally available in the 2016 Easter Basket (Brettspiel Osternest).

The promo consists of 3 Beacon tiles and the instructions on how to use them.

description:
A small promo originally available in the 2016 Easter Basket (Brettspiel Osternest).

The expansion consists of 12 tiles and the rules on how to use them.
Bad Medicine: Second Opinion includes 90 new cards for Bad Medicine and a new "Complication" mechanism that makes your pitches even more hilarious and memorable.

description:
Originally available in the 2016 Easter Basket - egg #01 (Brettspiel Osternest).

The expansion consists of 9 wagon expansion tiles and the instructions on how to use them.

The Spirits are Restless …

Some deep conniving has led to a change in leadership for the Morgan Cattle Company.

Meanwhile, stars are aligning which are sure to shed light into some of the darker corners of Gomorra.

Doomtown: Reloaded is a fast‑paced expandable card game set in the Deadlands™ universe of gun slingin’, spell slingin’, and mud-slingin’!

With 40 new cards, Doomtown Pine Boxes are the premium expansion to give you new ways to customize your Doomtown decks. Whether your style is shootin’, spell castin’, or just trying to show that you have more money and influence than the next person, Ghost Town expands your options for takin’ over Gomorra!

This product is an expansion, the base game is required to play.

This one-tile expansion comes with two tiles: One for the traditional version of Carcassonne, one for the current version; designed by the respective artists Doris Matthäus and Anne Pätzke. It's found in the "Spiel doch!" magazine from the W. Nostheide Verlag.

The Labyrinth tile connects up to four separate streets and combines them to one street. Part of the Carcassonne series.
Promo cards with new characters to be use with the game City of Spies: Estoril 1942.

Attention golddiggers! As you move your piece from start to finish on the board, you accumulate and discard husbands, always keeping track of your growing liquid cash assets (a tiny calculator and note paper is included in the box). Sometimes, you get to roll to steal other players' husbands. When possible, sink your liquid assets into real estate and businesses, in case a divorce goes wrong and you are the one who loses your shirt. Along the way, you land on "Up" or "Down" spaces which provide windfalls or disasters that affect your net worth. Once everyone has reached the finish space, everyone tallies up their total net worth and the one with the most money wins.

From game description: Up to 6 players. Each player begins with a zero balance in their bank accounts. Using the custom dice included in this game, each player takes turns moving their number of spaces around the board. The object is to become the richest by collecting Men, Homes & Businesses. Battle it out with your friends to see who will become the wealthiest. Travel around the game board trying your luck at earning great riches while dealing with the rewards and pitfalls of real life. Marry men, buy homes and collect businesses to end up the wealthiest player. The Game of Divorce is an absolute must to add to your board game collection. For entertainment purposes only.

Silantra: the game, is a 1-4 player cooperative trick taking card game inspired by the restaurant Silantra. In Silantra, you play as chefs in a kitchen serving customers their favorite entrees. As customers are fed, they reveal their special powers scoring you more points and abilities! 
To feed customers, you and the other chefs take turns playing ingredient cards from your hand to match what your customers want to eat, which is displayed on the front of the card. After feeding the customers, you flip the card over and activate their special power and continue drawing more cards and feeding more customers! Good luck on your first wave of customers.

Totenstadt is a hack & slay card game that takes the zombie apocalypse into a German city setting!

Totenstadt allows you take control of many different mercenaries like a firefighter, biker, priest, soldier, hitman, doctor, scientists and more — each with their own set of abilities — to defeat the gruesome hordes and fight for the survival of mankind!
But beware because zombies are not the only problems you have to face in the dark streets as crazy people and even mutants will stand in your way!

On your way through the city, you will make some friends, too. Soldiers, adventurers, merchants and more will help you to fulfill your mission, grant you helping abilities, or give you equipment. It's all fun and games until you run into one of the events and all hell breaks loose!
Are you ready to clean up the town and find out what really goes on behind the scenes?

In Death Pit Duels, you and your rival assemble a team of fantasy warriors who then duel in a series of one-on-one matches. Win the duel and take the spoils; lose and...well, it's not called a death pit for nothing! When the dust settles, who will be declared the victor?

The game is divided into two phases: Draft and Duel. In the Draft phase, players take turns splitting up randomly drawn fantasy fighter cards of various races (Cyclops, Goblin, Human, Orc) into two pairs. Their opponent then gets to select which pair to take. The player who originally split the cards into pairs must take the remaining pair of fighters that was unselected by their opponent. This continues until both players have built a team of twelve fighters.

The game then moves on to the Duel phase in which the players fight for a randomly drawn coin by revealing one of the fighter cards that they drafted. Each fighter has a base combat value and may also have a special ability to help them in the duel. The players simultaneously select a Duel card from their hand and reveal at the same time, adding the value of the Duel card to their fighter's combat value. The fighter with the highest adjusted combat value wins the coin. The game continues in this way until all twelve of a player's fighters have fought one duel each. The player with the highest total value in coins wins.

description:
2016 marked the 140th anniversary of the Little Bighorn Campaign. This struggle has been depicted in numerous conflict simulation games; it is certainly one of the most fascinating subjects in the wargame hobby. One-Minute Little Bighorn is a microgame attempting to portray the battle with a small map and a limited number of counters.

One-Minute Little Bighorn is a solitaire challenge. The entire battle can be fought in a minute or two. However, the game offers a player most of the tactical choices available to Custer in 1876.

The player represents the commander of the 7th Cavalry and army units represent battalions containing several companies. Tribal warriors like Crazy Horse are included in the groups of Native American fighting men swarming out of the village to block the advance of the cavalry. Historical factors like the escape of tribal noncombatants and the arrival of Benteen are contained in the rules. Victory is determined by the level of success achieved by Custer... if he survives the battle.

This companion game to the previously released One-Minute Bulge is a free Print & Play game with a single 8.5" x 11" map, charts, rules, and two dozen playing pieces or markers.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Holzbox edition was limited to 500 copies.  It contained E1/E2 Company Track which was a mini-expansion that was exclusive to this limited edition and was never released by itself.

Company E1 allows investing players to interact with discarded card.
Company E2 allows investing players to permanently have diamond merchants and bonus mining icons for the company.


In Mombasa, players acquire shares of chartered companies based in Mombasa, Cape Town, Saint-Louis, and Cairo and propagate trading posts of these companies throughout the African continent in order to earn the most money.

Mombasa features a unique, rotating-display hand-mechanism that drives game play. Each round players choose action cards from their hand, then reveal them simultaneously and carry out the actions. These cards are then placed in a discard pile, and the previously played cards recovered for the subsequent round.

Each company has a double-sided company track, so games will vary quite a lot based on which tracks are revealed and at which companies they are placed.

Each player has 16 playing pieces: 7 hunters, 4 catchers, 2 scouts, 2 squires and 1 bux-master. The goal of the game is to capture the opponent bux-master. The hunters move up to 2 spaces, the catchers up to 3 spaces, the scout up to 4 spaces, the bux-master and his squires 1 space (no jumping over other pieces allowed). If pieces of both payers are on three connected spaces in a straight line, the minority piece is eliminated from play unless it is able to escape in the next turn.

There are rules for 2 variants included:

Buxelkuh: One cow has to break through a line of 9 peasants and 1 mayor. The latter try to trap the cow.

Buxtehu: The players try to get all 16 playing pieces to the opposite end of the game board. Each time a player gets one of his pieces to a space at the other end of the board, he may take one or more enemy pieces off the board. The game ends if all 11 spaces at one end of the board are occupied or if there ar no more pieces on the board. The player with the most captured pieces wins the game.

Block 2 Block was designed with players and customization in mind. There are many variant rule sets which make the game a new yet familiar experience. We invite you to come join the community of players and enjoy.

Players roll dice to determine how many blocks they can place on tiles at various tiers. As the tiles are revealed and claimed, new tiers worth more points open up and players can start revealing and claiming tiles there. Different tiles have different powers, some let you remove blocks from other tiles, some remove tiles completely, and some move or switch tiles in different tiers.

The ultimate goal is to accrue the most points, through skillful use of tile powers, wise investments, and some luck with the die.

Second expansion for the game Caçadores da Galáxia (Galaxy Hunters). Includes 3 new pilots, 3 new robots, components to add a 5th player (or 6th if using Froteiras Expansion) and add 2 new modules to the game: New Planets Colonizing and Robots Upgrades and Initial exclusive powers

First volume of Infantry Assault

Infantry Assault is a skirmish game between 2-4 players on various fields of battle. The objectives are varied and include a campaign which can be separated into several scenarios if you wish.

The major asset of Infantry Assault is to provide a fluid game system with ease of access, to share the fun of miniature games with family and friends, and for people outside of the hobby to discover the pleasure of the game. The simplicity of the game is thanks to a unique and ingenious system, this will keep the tactical advantage of this type of game, implementing a storyline. In campaign mode, the winner will be the player who gains the more points at the end but there is no obligation to play each scenario in an orderly manner.

After numerous wars and conflicts in which history has forgotten their names, the planet Earth has become a vast territory where only irradiated animals and humans fighting for their survival live in this devastated world, cities comprise of the last humans unaltered by toxic winds that now sweep the surface. Directed with an iron hand by draconian dictatorships, they are the theater of war between the various institutions in place. These wars give grounds for many skirmishes despite an omnipresent police force overwhelmed by the violence of this closed universe.

2184 is a skirmish game between 2-4 players on various fields of battle. The objectives are varied and include a campaign which can be separated into several scenarios if you wish. You will choose a clan among the 6 available and will evolve its leaders over 3 generations. When your leader has gained 10 points of experience, his son will succeed him then the son of his son, the third generation will be able to access a new field of competence :  PSY powers.

The major asset of 2184 is to provide a fluid game system with ease of access, to share the fun of miniature games with family and friends, and for people outside of the hobby to discover the pleasure of the game. The simplicity of the game is thanks to a unique and ingenious system, this will keep the tactical advantage of this type of game,  implementing a storyline and tracking the progress of your clan leader who will become stronger over sessions. In campaign mode, the first player to reach level 10 with his 3rd generation wins the game. There is no obligation to play each scenario in an orderly manner. The life of this game is like an open book with many possibilities.

2184 Generation War is a rulebook. No miniatures brand imposed.

In Platoon Command you take the role of a platoon leader, guiding your platoon into combat in order to capture critical battlefield objectives. Platoon Command is a quick-playing game that uses cards for combat, command and control, fog of war, and attrition. The mission objective is to gain Objective Points by controlling areas of the battlefield. You accomplish this objective by issuing orders to your command group, three rifle squads, and specialized personnel.

Dungeon crawler with large amounts of Massive Multiplayer Online influences.  Characters will have multiple specializations.  There will be quests to complete, dungeons to explore, as well as large raids you can complete.  All while playing solo or with your friends and family.

You use actions, to move your character around a map, while using dice, and playing cards to kill the enemy's.  Receive loot cards to increase your characters abilities and gear to progress through the campaign.

boardgame: Castle Assault: Halting Force Promo Card
description:
Promo Card for Castle Assault given out at GAMA 2016

Epic Neutral Command
Cost: 1
Target Unit gains -3 Movement while Charging for 1 turn (does not move backwards)

boardgame: Gulf States (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop. You can use stations if you wish.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city: they also give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules. Crossing borders require extra attention.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board has only one grey track and no parallel tracks, though there are limited selective options for a colour change.

There are many new options in the rule:exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn double points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

In this survival co-op, you and your crewmates find yourselves in the middle of an ocean on an alien world after a mishap during interstellar travel.

In 30 days a planet-wide storm will knock out all communications. You need to find three islands and repair the rescue beacons that were launched when your spacecraft was destroyed.

During your exploration you are as likely to find unwelcoming creatures as helpful items.

Decide how to use your stats, abilities, and dice allocation to face these challenges, and the challenge of propelling your watercraft through this ocean, and activate the distress signal. Otherwise, you will likely suffer the fate of this planet's previous inhabitants.

Our fair city is under attack by militant anarchists and their nefarious time bombs. In response the Police Commissioner has selected you, our most expendable officers, to form Precinct 9's first Bomb Squad.

But it's not just the bombs you'll have to contend with. There's also the new sniffer dog that keeps setting them off, not to mention your back-stabbing colleagues who are all out for personal glory at your expense. Meanwhile the bombs keep on ticking down, one slip up means disaster, and someone keeps hiding the wire cutters!

Bomb Squad #9 is a game of bluffing and deduction, set against the slow ticking of a time bomb. Players search for the bomb and the wire cutters amongst nine face down cards. Each card has a special ability: allowing a second card to be seen (wire cutters, sniffer dog, metal detector), mixing the cards around (anarchist, panic), or just reacting to other cards (bomb, rat, can). On their turn, a player secretly looks at a card and declares it to the others. If they are not accused of lying, or if accused and acquitted, they get to perform the declared action. Certain combinations make the bomb explode, and if not defused the bomb explodes after six turns regardless. Bomb Squad #9 was derived as a combination and miniaturisation of Lost Legacy and Memory, but with a metal detector that actually beeps!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Nécropole is a boardgame designed for 2 to 6 players, and can be played in several mode (teams, competitive or versus a game master).

Each player take control of up to 3 undead heroes. They can be vampires, necromancers or ghosts.
Each hero is chosen amongst three classes: sorcerer ★, warrior ⌽ or assassin ☾.

Classes will have great impact within attack phases during fights. They will give your heroes dedicated abilities (all unique).

⌽ Warriors master "talent" to dominate the battlefield.
★ Sorcerers are spell casters, specialized in control and area effect damages.
☾ Assassins use their skills to make surgical strikes against their preys.

Abilities and legendary artifacts are won by exploring the various Necropole's districts and accomplishing epic quests.
You can also attack your opponents and go into the slummy areas to trade souls for gear.

Possibilities are endless to fulfill your objectives and win the game.

As each card is unique, each game is unique too.

Now is your turn to master the Necropole, city of the dead.

Necropole is an epic game of exploration and skirmish fights.
Players will battle against other undeads (controlled by theor opponents or the game master), using cards in their hands and rolling dice.

In this new game you travel the world with your own fleet of cointainerships and manage large seaports. You want to be the biggest but there are many dangers in the way: storm, clashes, pirats,... You are in direct conflict with other shipowners, these are your competitors.

A smart strategy, making decissions fast, choosing the best routes, buying new ships and seaports, buying-out another shipowner, reacting on a crisis ... A nose for business and a dose of luck will determine if your fleet will win this game.

Sea Giants is a game for 2 to 4 players, you roll dice and move your ships forward on the course they are traveling, you get money if you end on a harbour spot and have the opportunity to buy the harbour, if no one else owns it yet. You can also spend your turn buying new ships for different courses. The game can end either by setting a time limit or by buying all harbours from other players so you are the monopolist.

Have a nice course !

Yggdrasill A game for 2 players set up in Nordic setting and based on Nordic mythology, where one side (good gods of Asgard and vikings) try to eliminate the bad guys (evil gods and monsters) by defeating a giant snake Jormungard living in the crown of the Yggdrasill tree.

In the beginning, the good side can play only with 12 vikings, who are not too powerful. Their task is to send the message to the gods of Asgard to ask them for help in the war with evil forces. They can do it by either building the ships and sailing to Asgard, or by killing one of evil monsters (Trwyth), after which, they will be allowed to go through the stone forest and reach Asgard. Evil side, of course, tries to prevent it from happening, but with slick tactics, vikings are able to achieve it. And this is when the real fun begins. With the help of almost 20 gods, the good side begins an even battle.

The evil gods and monsters try to chase and eliminate the good gods and vikings, to prevent them from reaching Yggdrasill. the tree on which Jormungard the snake lives. If the good manage to kill it,they win. If the evil, eliminate all good ones, they win.
 Every character in the game has 2 traits: strength and magic power + some of them, are able to use spells. Duels are, unfortunately simple, you choose either strength or magic, roll a die and add the result to the chosen trait. The higher result denotes the winner. After the 1st wounded the character's token is flipped and remains wounded until healed (successful die roll)or attacked and wounded again, which is the end for a given character.

The game is played on a huge board, divided into lands. There are seas, rivers, mountains, forests and places to explore, where magic items can be found. Characters can also learn spells in magic schools.

There are also event cards, drawn at the beginning of a new round.
The game, though simple, and rather visually not too attractive, is a lot of fun to play. Because of the size of the board and numerous charters, it is never played the same way. The good may have many possible routes to get to Yggdrasill, and the abilities (spells) of some of their mightiest gods, can change a hopeless situation.
If one changed the rules of battling, made a new colorful board and substitute cardboard tokens with plastic figures, the game would be a hit.

In the dark tunnels and caves far below the surface of our world, evil creatures lurk in the shadows.
Some of them, such as the cunning dark elves, have developed a civilization – and in every successful civilization games are played, so the masterminds can sharpen their wits, while the lower townsmen have their share of distraction.

Arachne is such a game: While you can find some elements of the chess game played by the surface dwellers, there are quite a lot of differences, too:
First of all, as a devotion to the Spider Goddess, Arachne is played on the strands of a spider web. Also, the cruel and traitorous nature of the dark elves is reflected in Arachne, too: Aside from being able to capture his own playing pieces, each turn, a player gets the option to move and to capture with an opposing playing piece.
So, whatever you plan; always be aware, that the true enemy strikes from within!

The game has been created during the 2016 One Page PNP Contest.

Heretik is a chess variant in which cards are used to improve abilities and undermine opponents. Unlike other chess card games, Heretik makes chess more elaborate without sacrificing the strategic elements of regular chess.

Heretik begins like regular chess, but players draw a card after each move. These cards may be played, declared, or discarded to move in new ways, move multiple pieces at once, or even push your enemies off the edge of the board.

There are sixty unique cards available to download from the website. Playing Heretik requires two copies of each card and a regular chess set. You may also need extra pieces and pawns (or tokens to represent them), and a couple of small rocks that can be placed on the squares.

In Sinking Island, players are survivors of a shipwreck who have to a deserted island. Players must try to gather resources and find food on the small island to keep themselves alive. The player who manages to build the most useful tools and stay strong will stand the best chance to survive as a castaway!

The Story
You’ve finally found a place to call home! There’s a safe space for the nest and plenty of cheese lying around. There are just two problems, you aren’t the only mouse here and, worse yet, a bunch of cats are roaming about. But it’s dinnertime and nothing’s going to stop you from getting that cheese!

Objective
Be crafty, strategic, and fast! You’ll need to split your time between progress and sabotage as you and the other mice race to be the first to return TWO cheeses to the nest.

boardgame: Mare Nostrum: Empires – Theseus Promo Card
description:
This is the Theseus Promo card given out at the Festival international des jeux in Cannes (France - 2016), and in the Plato Magazine #84 (French boardgames magazine).

Theseus gives one additional legendary good per capital city controlled.

He completes the Daedalus Labyrinth triptych, with Ariane and the Minotaur - both KS exclusives.

The thief starts at the hotel and tries to reach the train station to win the game. The 6 kid detectives start at various spaces on the board and have to try to trap the thief so that he can't move. The kid detective who makes the last move to trap the thief wins the game. Movement is one space per turn.

Another version is called "Achtung! Feind hört mit", where the hunted game piece is a spy.

The players try to form certain patterns (house, tower, pagoda, double pagoda)with their tiles on the board. They get points for completing their own patterns (Waschi) or for completing patterns of their opponent (Wurti). Tile placement and movement is controlled by dice. If the player rolls a black 1, 2 or 3 he may place or move the corresponding number of tiles. On the roll of a red 1 or 2 he may move 1 or 2 tiles of an opponent player. If he rolls the black dot, he has to skip a turn. The game ends when a player gets two Waschi or two Wurti.

Blade and Brush is a simple yet intellectual game of shared storytelling through the medium of haiku.

Players take on the roles of unique, self-defined characters such as wandering monks, plucky adventurers, and questing warriors. Throughout the game, as the characters journey, they encounter a series of dilemmas. Using poems to solve problems, help people, and get into trouble, the players must collaborate and compete to weave the most entertaining story. The game can be played at a family gathering with children and grandparents over cups of warm tea, or at a late night gathering of close friends with a bottle of warm sake.

La Bataille de Schöngrabern 1805 , which was made famous in Leo Tolstoy’s War and Peace,  recreates the fight between French Imperial Prince Joachim Murat’s advance guard, having just taken Vienna from the Austrians, and the Russian rear guard led by General Peter Bagration, Bagration was desperately trying to buy enough time for Prince Mikhail Kutuzov to unite two separated parts of his large Russian-Austrian army in the maneuvering before the Battle of Austerlitz.


Murat has dallied for a few days after his capture of Vienna, trying to accomplish victory through diplomacy and trickery rather than force of arms, and the Russians used Murat’s vanity to keep the formerly hard-pressing French from catching the spread-out Russian forces in detail.  The result is a small but sharp battle on November 16, 1805 which allowed the Russians enough time to unite their armies.

La Bataille de Schöngrabern 1805 includes more than 100 counters, representing both the French Imperial forces and such leaders as Oudinot and Suchet; and the Russian rearguard represented by counters for Bagration; assorted Russian Cossacks, Austrian Hussars and Russian Jaegers.  There are two 11 X17 maps, with much of the territory taken up by the extensive vineyards.  
The Schöngrabern Special Rules feature a "La Bataille" first combat in the vineyards, which makes this game very different from any other La Bataille game.  There is also a commemorative essay about Marshal Murat’s efforts and the battle itself.
