Description from the publisher:

Designed by Richard Launius, the creator of Arkham Horror, the Grave Consequences expansion for Elder Sign introduces fifty new cards in three modular decks: Phobia, Epic Battle, and Epitaph.


    Phobia cards give investigators lasting negative effects.
    Epitaph cards build a tiny graveyard for the fallen.
    Epic Battle cards ramp up the difficulty and danger when battling the Ancient One.


All three of these decks and their cards can be used together or separately, and they’re all fully compatible with the core game, as well as all of its expansions. Whether you use one deck, or all of them, you’ll find they increase the challenge and drama of your Elder Sign adventure. Even more, they’ll immerse you more deeply into its world and help you connect more fully to the investigator that you're playing.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
In Cat's Pow! you and your friends are cats and as cats you have paws! The objective of the game is to collect as many toys as possible using your paws, only the fastest cat can win!

The game turn takes place at the same time for all players. At the beginning of every turn the players count to 5 and then try to touch the cards they'd like to get. You have to use all the fingers of both your hands to touch the cards, but you can't use the thumbs. You can, for example, touch a card with 4 fingers and use the others to touch other cards, or you can touch a single card with 8 fingers. Once the players placed their hands on the cards they check what happened. The player with the most fingers (cat's claws) placed on a single card, gains the card and the points written on it.

The basic strategy of Pirate Attack! is to build up your ships while sinking your opponent's ships. Meanwhile other players are trying to do the same thing to you.

After sending your ship to sea, you can play Blacksmith cards to increase your cannons or decrease another players cannons, Carpenter cards to increase your strength or decrease another players strength. Draw an Attack Card and then destroy an opponent's ship (if you can). When a ship is sunk, receive gold which can be spent at The Market on Witch Doctor Cards so you can curse your opponent's ships. Buy some Fortune Teller Cards to protect yourself from others placing Witch Doctor Cards on your ships.

Summon a Sea Creature to attack other player's ships. Force two other players to attack each other. Anything you can do to destroy your enemies and become the ruler of the seas.

Use a Spy to remove useful cards from other players or a Thieving Monkey to take a card from an opponent and keep it. Better keep a good watch on your gold with a Guard or someone may try to steal it with a Thief.

Lower the prices at the Market so you can buy more items or close the Market so your opponents cannot buy items to hurt you with.

Once you have several ships at your command, use the Armada Card to join them all together and fight as one to destroy your enemies.

Force your opponents to Walk The Plank or strand them on an island so they lose a turn.

The play is simple. Draw 1 card, play 1 card (some cards can alter this). Play a ship, increase your ship, decrease another players ship, open/ close a market, steal cards or if you draw an attack card then attack a player with your ship. Receive gold and buy items to make you stronger or hurt your opponents.

Each expansion pack adds more things you can do and more markets to buy from to help you on your quest to rule the seas.

The main game includes 350 cards. There are 6 expansion packs ranging from 50 to 100 cards each. Each expansion pack adds a new level of play to the game.

The first expansion pack of 50 cards "Pirate Attack- The Magic Shop" adds a Magic Shop full of things to help your ships and hurt your enemies.  Double your ships cannons or strength, reincarnate ships, confuse where a ship attacks, and some main deck cards like Royal Navy to attack all ships and Hurricanes.

The second expansion pack of 50 cards "Pirate Attack- Raise The Dead" adds bigger ships, cards to steal Magic Shop cards, more Sea Creatures, larger Royal Navy, Raise the Dead cards, Duplicate a ship, Spirit of Good Fortune, Eliminate Cards and Sails of Invisibility.

The third expansion pack of 50 cards "Pirate Attack- Miss-Direction" adds more Ships, more Sea Creatures, Whirlpools, Windstorms, Stranded On An Island, Exploding Cannons, and Miss Direction.

The forth expansion pack of 100 cards and 2 Dice "Pirate Attack- Walk The Plank" adds another Ship, Walk The Plank, The Trap, Smoke Screen, Power Play, The Magician, Damn Pirates, Insurance, Ship Bonus, Card Bonus, Into The Sea, Davy Jones Locker, End Of The World, Force Fields, Visions, Return To Port and The Gambler.  So many more ways to mess with other players.

The fifth expansion pack of 100 cards and 1 Chip "Pirate Attack- The Tavern" adds a whole new market. The Tavern is a place where you can bribe a drunk sailor into telling you secrets and info, some good, some bad.  Get Double Play, Sneak Thief, Pick Pockets, add cannons and strength to all of your ships, give away your hand, make someone lose their gold, mix all cards and re-deal, give all ships a Witch Doctor, hurt another player with minus cannons and strength to all their ships.

The sixth expansion pack of 50 cards and 2 dice "Pirate Attack- Special Abilities" gives players (who chose to) a Special Ability, but only if they are willing to Sell Their Soul to get it.  Get a bad item like no ships bigger than 7/7, overcharged at markets, no gold when killing a ship.  But then get a good item like Black Market, The Vault, or Money Master.  This deck also includes The Blockade so you can stop cards from being played and 20 new ships with abilities.
The main game can be played as a stand alone and expansion packs can be added to give the players more things to do during the game. Best when played with 3 to 10 players. Ages 8 and up.
This is a high quality game. It comes in a nice, sturdy 9" x 12" x 3" game box with plastic insert to hold all the cards from the main game and each expansion pack, so all the cards are together. Expansion pack boxes can be folded and placed under the plastic insert for keeping.

The game has great replay ability. Not the same old game every time. Certain cards make things different every game. You can even remove cards to give the game a different play. Play with or without expansion packs.  You can play to a certain number of Fame Points, play for a specified amount of time and most Fame Points at that time wins or play all the cards and go for last ship standing wins.

This game is a solo or co-op game depending on if you have any friends who would like to play.

Movement is on a grid based system which is turn based. Each character has a number of Life Points which equal to abilities within the game as well.  When they are wounded their life goes down which also reduces their abilities within the game.

Players complete their mission by surviving the game map (which is random apart from a few key elements that will always appear) and completing their mission objective by returning to their starting vehicle.

It maybe to retrieve something, destroy something or any manner of events.  These events are decided by the roll before gameplay beings of the Dice of Destiny which give a primary, secondary and bonus mission.

Equipment is varied and unique in some cases.  Players start with an amount of points that they can purchase equipment which they can carry and keep in the starting vehicle as this has capacity to store extra equipment.

The characters available are classic type heroes who range from spies to thieves. The single player can control two of these while co-op each player can only control one each.

The scene and era is pulp fiction / cult era and general classic caper / 70's film type scenario!!!

The missions are random generated and can include such types as:


     Stop a doomsday device.
     Save the husband of the bride.
     Kill the undead allied zombies.
     Replace the Six Million Pound's man bionic parts with lesser quality items.
     Save Schrödinger's cat.
     Rob the safe.
     Collect evidence.


Everything you need to play the game apart from the dice will be included within the Print and Play rules that are free.

This game can be supplemented with additional furnishings and miniatures. The system allows for very easy expansion and 3rd party supplemental material.

The game also offers basic characters’ progression and surviving characters can be moved from one adventure to another.  This in essence means that you may benefit from equipment from a prior mission if you play wisely.

A space ship full of hungry space monsters are invading your house to steal your food!  Work together as a team to squish and smush the little creatures before they reach the fridge!  The goofy monsters are molded from the colorful Play-Doh and players use a silly sneaker or boot smasher to mash them.  Itès giggly, squishy fun!

In Monster Smash, players mold as many monsters out of Play-Doh as they wish.  They will place a monster at each of the flying saucer spaces on the board.  Once a piece is moved, they replace it with another monster.  To move pieces, players use a spinner and depending on what color it lands on, they move that colored monster to the next space on the board.  If the player spins a smush or a double smush, they get to smush monsters on the board and remove them from the game.  If players eliminate all of the monsters from the game, they win.  If any three monsters reach the fridge space on the board, you lose!

Ante Portas is a 2 player card game about the campaign of Hannibal against Rome. You have to use your cards to defeat your enemy. The game is assimetrical as the two player have different option for the use of the cards, so playing one or other side is a different playing experience. The game is card driven with a strong bluffing element.

About the gameplay:
Every card has a Roman and a Carthagian part and the players can use only one part, but the card goes to the other player after use.
Each card can be used as an Event, army or other special use depending on your side. Marching time for the Carthagians to move your army or morale/money for the Romans to keep your Empire together. Your choices will decide the faith of your Empire.
It has only 20 Event cards so its very quick, but there is the possibility to deeper startegy and tactics.

Boardgame based on a hugely popular game-show on Finnish TV from 1985 to 2005 ("Tenthousand") while the boardgame version is likely from the last years of Paletti at the end of the 1980's.

The idea is to give a one sentence clue for a single word for other players, higher points are given, if only one other person will get the answer. Think Dixit with words instead of pictures.

The game box features Riitta Väisänen, who hosted the show for its entire 20 year run.

A version aimed at younger audiences was published a little bit later as Junnutonni ("Youngsterthousand")

Outer Gods and Interlopers is a social deduction game where Cultists are performing rituals to the great and powerful Outer Gods.  A few investigators have attempted to infiltrate and subvert the rituals from within but the Cult Leader has becoming increasingly weary of this and has taken to sacrificing those within the cult.

Your objective as a cultists is to successfully get Rituals to pass and as the investigator team to sow enough discord that the cult will fail in their task of bringing upon certain doom to the world.

During the ritual phase the cult leader will reveal the top ritual from the deck, it will have one or more components required for successful completion.  It will also have one disruptive component that if added to the ritual will bring discord to the cult.  All other players will commit a component, from their hand of three cards, face down to the ritual.

All players will have a hidden background that may force them to play a certain way, ie. if you are the Madman you must play any offering components you have.

The cult leader will then shuffle and reveal the committed components and if the necessary requirements have been met the ritual will succeed and favor from the outer gods will be gained.  If the ritual does not succeed the investigators will add more discord and get one step closer to winning the game.

The game features a unique moderator role that makes decisions with the game, as well as unique player abilities that provide many opportunities to either sniff out the Investigators or sow discord.

An audio-only game (no board or other pieces) in the MindTrap series. Players listen to one of the eighty short mysteries on the two tapes, then rewind it and listen again for more clues. After discussing the solution among themselves, one person checks the answer book. If they are correct, congratulations! If not, the rest of the group asks questions of the reader, who answers "Yes", "No", or "Irrelevant" until they guess the answer or everyone gives up.

Fou Raide is a party game that should awaken the craziest side of your players, only by reading the challenge cards.

Hum a great song with crazy sounds, reproduce a fresco with fellow players, make faces, mime, your guests will be Fou Raide.

A member of a team pick a card, reads the category aloud (Fresco, Mime, Humming, etc.), and attempt to have his team guess the answers before the timer runs out. If they succeed the team earn 3 points. If the timer run out, they player can reveal a hint from the card. If the team guess correctly with the hint, they earn one point. 
First team to 25 point wins.

Turn the tiles and find the goods on your shopping list. Pay with cash and make sure that you get the right amount of money back. If you can't pay, you have to roll the die to get more money out of the bank. The first player who has bought all the goods on their shopping list, wins the game. The game contains: 12 shopping lists, 36 goods, 33 coins, a die and rules.

Giant war machines duel in the 41st millennium!

Dominating the battlefield with their size and firepower, Imperial Knights are towering bipedal weapons platforms that hold no quarter in eliminating their targets – but like anything else, they are vulnerable to the dark clutches of Chaos. The Freeblade Knight once known as the Living Litany is an unfortunate example; nobody knows what truly happened to this once-valiant hero, only that it has renamed itself Litany of Destruction, and has been leaving a brutal trail of devastation in its wake. The Imperial Knight Ever-Stalwart has made planetfall on Tellerax Prime with one goal in mind – hunt down his fallen brother and restore the honour of the knightly houses, or die trying!

In Imperial Knights: Renegade, you take control of an Imperial Knight- a giant, walking, mechanised weapons platform, and try to win scenarios using movement, firepower and close combat. Every game turn is split into two phases- the plan phase and the execute phase. In the plan phase, each player secretly writes down what actions they intend to perform in the execute phase, consulting the action table. Each action has an action point cost with a budget of 3 per turn. When each player has worked out what they intend to do, they move on the execute phase and reveal their actions. Each player carries out their elected actions in the order they appear on the action table. if both players choose the same action, you roll off to see who goes first. The more powerful or useful an action is, the later that actions comes in the order of play, so choose your knight's actions wisely. The goal of each game varies with the scenario chosen and can range from total elimination to reaching an important objective on the board.

Imperial Knights: Renegade is a game of bluff and feint, outwitting your opponent, making clever use of terrain, movement and ion shields to secure victory. The right action at the right time can snatch a glorious victory from the jaws of bitter defeat, and an incautious move can leave your knight's lifeless hulk rusting in the pitiless sun.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Divide up into two teams and throw a bug token to the hungry frog. Each team takes a turn until all tokens are played. The team that feeds the frog with the most bugs is the winner.
The player that can stack and balance the tree and the birds quickest, without making it fall is the winner.

User summary
The third game from Kunpu is bound to stir up some controversy amongst our current health-concerned society.

Smoking has been labeled as a dirty habit.
Increasingly more places are banning smoking and the price of cigarettes is continuously raised. Tobacco has been villainized to the point that there are specialized clinics and monetary rewards from employers for quitting.

But to heavy smokers, cigarettes are a simple pleasure.

The players of this game become heavy smokers trying to smoke as many cigarettes as possible before their lungs turn completely black. Even if you fail to smoke during a round, your lungs may still grow progressively blacker all the same from the secondhand smoke of your peers.

This game is both for those who are proud to call themselves smokers and those who disdain them. Outwit your fellow smokers to puff your way to victory (and an early grave).

Game Play
Each player gets an ashtray in which they place smoked cigarettes.

-Draw 3 cards
-Players choose 1-2 cards to place face-down on the table (one card must be a cigarette card)
-Players open the chosen cards at the same time
-The player with the highest number of cigarettes wins (unless special cards are involved)
-The winning player can smoke that round (therefore he collects the cigarettes into his ashtray). However, he also must gather tokens that mark the deterioration of his lungs, based on the number of tokens written on the card(s) he played.
-The losing players cannot smoke (they discard their opened cards) but they may receive the effect of secondhand smoke from the winner's played cards. They gather tokens according to this lung cost.
-Refill hand and continue until someone reaches 10 lung tokens (and therefore dies of blackened lungs).

The ultimate winner (even in death) is the person who smoked the most.

A fun and fast paced game of negotiation on the high seas. Beautiful and silly illustrations come to life as players struggle between helping the Captain voyage to new and promising lands or causing a raucous and leading to mutiny.

Game play is a simultaneous action, keeping everyone engaged and guessing, as they each try to become the Captain for the day. The captain will find themselves challenged in thought provoking actions that can appease their crew or sail on towards their own agenda.

boardgame: Der Schuh des Manitu: Das Brettspiel
description:
Whether wadding blow on the Puder Rosa or mime with Abahachi. Or would you rather make a rhyme with Ranger about Santa Maria? Skill, creativity and wit will bring you nearer to coveted treasure map. And in between you are allowed laugh until the Shoshone come ...
With this communication and party game each round will get in mood!

boardgame: Plot Twist: A Storytelling Card Game
description:
Plot Twist is a storytelling card game for four to six players. All players are Storytellers and stitch together Plot Cards, combined with a little imagination, to tell short stories prompted by Trope Cards.

Trope Cards provide a familiar story prompt inspired from all over fiction, media, and history. Storytellers use this information to tell a story using their hand of Plot Cards.

Plot Cards contain tired clichés, action beats, random events, and hilarious happenings. Storytellers stitch together as many Plot Cards as they need to tell their version of the selected Trope Card prompt, making up the events between cards as they go. Stories can be simple tales, outrageous romps, or outright-insane cartoons – as long as you can justify the Plot Cards you use in your story, you can use them however you'd like!

After all Storytellers have told their stories for the round, they cast votes for their favorite stories of the round. Votes in Plot Twist are weighted – you get three Story Point Cards to use each round and you can give them out however you want. Feel free to spread your votes around to multiple Storytellers, or give all three to your favorite story!

The story with the most votes at the end of each round wins that round's Trope Card as a point. The Storyteller with the most points at the end of the game is crowned the Master Storyteller – and the winner of the game!

The Tcho-Tcho Expansion adds a new faction to Cthulhu Wars. The Tcho-Tchos are a human-based faction representing the physically advanced, but ethically repulsive Tcho-Tcho tribe with the ability to interfere with other players by worshipping their Great Old Ones! Your own Great Old One, Ubbo-Sathla, is a tool and slave, not an object of worship!

Unique among Cthulhu Wars factions, the Tcho-Tchos have access to three high priests. In addition to the standard power generation and pacing benefits granted by recruiting additional high priests, the Tcho-Tchos exploit this advantage through two spellbooks (Hierophants and Martydom) based around high priests. The other distinction of the Tcho-Tchos is the way they interact with other factions; the spellbooks Tablet of the Gods and Idolotry enable the Tcho-Tcho faction to leech off other players, and Soulless makes your cultists less attractive to capture.

The Tcho-Tchos are generally weak defensively at first, but enemies are typically reluctant to attack an Area containing your High Priests (due to Martyrdom). If they don’t send in Great Old Ones, the riff-raff can often be handled with Proto-Shoggoths--particularly with the Terror spellbook. The Tcho-Tchos have a major power advantage in the early game, because you normally spend 0 Power on your Great Old One. This power can be used not only to capture or build Gates, but to place units in enemy Start Areas to harass them with Tablets of the Gods.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In SuperP.L.E.X. Wrestling, players each take the role of an intergalactic pro wrestling superstar and battle it out with over-the-top moves in hopes of being the last wrestler standing!

Players start off by assembling their unique hand of 6 maneuver cards, which they will use as their arsenal in the battle ahead. Players also position their wrestlers on a "Ring", which will become an important part of their assault, as where they and their opponent are in the ring at any given time could equal some BIG damage opportunities...if you play your moves right!

Played over the course of rounds, each round starts with a "Lock-Up" in order to determine who will start with the offensive edge. After the Lock-Up, each player then position their maneuver cards as "Offense" or "Defense" depending on who won the Lock-Up. One by one, players reveal their face down maneuver cards to see if the player on Defense was able to correctly guess what type of maneuver their opponent played. If they guess correctly, the opponent's maneuver is countered and the Defensive player now goes on the Offense! If they guessed wrong, the player on Offense gets to execute their maneuver!

Maneuvers become more and more deadly the further along in a combo they are, so do your best to juke and jive your Defensive opponent to get the most out of your combo! The wrestlers themselves get more deadly as well! Once a wrestler's stamina goes below 1/2, they get to add their super effective Finishing Maneuver card to their arsenal to help turn the tide of battle!
family: Game: Super Pocket League Extreme Wrestling

description:
Tin Soldiers In Action, Fair and Squares Rules cover  

     the age of Enlightenment
     the Napoleonic age 
     the Victorian era
     all scales
     all types of basing 
     solo games
     skirmishes and large battles with a single rule system. 


Included are 

     army lists from 1680 up to 1900 
     point systems and modular systems for army constructions 
     a tutorial on the American Civil War in a military academy setting 
     a detailed scenario of the Battle of Bergen in the Seven Years War
     a fantastic scenario Black Cup in the Victorian era in a Jules Verne setting


Unique rules which will test your courage:

You are commander-in-chief of your tin soldiers, in charge of strategy, seizing the opportunities – every move must be a conscious act.

You are directly confronted with the stark consequences your decisions.

Will you demonstrate true nerves of steel? Will you lead your tin soldiers to victory? Share in the excitement and thrilling experience of Tin Soldiers In Action!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A journey into the world of feelings
With exciting and erotic game instructions

It turns throwing and advanced on a path the corresponding number of spaces . Then, the instructions on the destination field will be followed. The goal is to get to know each other and may be the first to reach the 'Love Nest'.

boardgame: Cthulhu Wars: Library at Celaeno Map
description:
The Library at Celaeno Map Expansion contains a new map with two figures, the Librarian and the Custodian. This map is inside the cyclopean alien facility in the Hyades, renowned in Lovecraft lore. This map includes Special Collections, where the first player to build a Gate can seize amazing new powers in the form of extra spellbooks. But beware! If you lose control of your Special Collection, your new spellbook becomes overdue, and the terrifying Librarian menaces you.

The Custodian cleans up chambers by moving units in the Area to another Area termed the Oubliette. Over the course of the game, this Oubliette tends to get filled with units. Sometimes fights break out.

The Librarian can only be activated if a player has an overdue Book, in which case it can wreak some real damage. Fortunately, you can mitigate the damage by returning the Book, though not necessarily eliminate all threat.

The Library at Celaeno introduces a new mechanism in Cthulhu Wars: Silence tokens. These are used to trigger certain special actions including moving the Custodian, Librarian, and triggering some of the special Library books on this map.

The well-known and most popular dice game enters a new dimension. Now the points just acquired may be pilfered from other players. And now is the "road" away ... but wait, I'll get it back again!

Das bekannte und weltweit beliebte Würfelspiel erfährt eine neue Dimension. Nun können die gerade erworbenen Punkte von den Mitspielern stibitzt werden. Und schon ist die "Straße" weg... doch warte nur, die hol' ich mir wieder!

Shindra is a tactical 2-player boardgame opposing the gods of two civilizations.

The aim of the game is to have more cards on the board showing your color than your opponent's at the end of the game.

The game progresses with each player placing a card on a 4 by 4 grid and flipping the neighboring opponent's cards with a cascading effect. Once the grid is full, the player with the most cards in their color wins.

You can play a partnership game with 2 boxes.

Play the role of a thief Searching Dungeons, lock picking treasure chests, pickpocket other players, battle monsters and dungeon bosses for gold and loot!

Pocket Dungeon Thief is a 1 to 3 player card and dice game. Each player draws cards from the search, treasure and loot decks to collect items, gold and loot. Treasure chests are lock picked with the thief dice and monsters or dungeon bosses are confronted with the battle dice.

Combinations of sneaky tactics can be performed to outwit your opponents to gain an edge, for shiny coins!

But gathering gold does come at a price... A treasure chest can be cursed and spawn one of four dungeon bosses that every thief player must unite and battle

boardgame: Cthulhu Wars: Great Old One Pack Four
description:
Cthulhu Wars: Great Old One Pack Four adds three new Great Old Ones to Cthulhu Wars. As in the other Great Old One Packs, each Old One may be added to any game of Cthulhu Wars and is available to all factions on a first come, first served basis. Each Great Old One has its own special ability, plus its own unique spellbook, which is earned separately from your own.

The Old Ones in this pack are Byatis, Nyogtha, and Tulzscha. Byatis lures enemy cultists to their doom, Tulszcha subverts the purpose of the Ritual track, and Nyogtha takes prisoners away to its lair.

The Cosmic Terrors are neutral, particularly powerful Monsters that may be added to any game of Cthulhu Wars and are available to any player on a first come, first served basis. You seize control of one by spending your Doom points, and it remains loyal thereafter. This pack includes the gigantic Dhole, destroyer of worlds; the Great Race of Yith, with the power to seize men's minds; and the demonic Quachil Uttaus entity, Treader of the Dust.

boardgame: Cthulhu Wars: Ramsey Campbell Horrors Pack 1
description: The Horror packs bring creations of Ramsey Campbell to Cthulhu Wars. These packs introduce neutral Great Old Ones and Monsters that may be added to any game of Cthulhu Wars; they are available to any player on a first come, first served basis. You seize control of one by spending Doom points, and they remain loyal thereafter. This pack includes two Great Old Ones - Eihort and Gla'aki. Also included is a new Monster - the famous Insects from Shaggai. You get three hives for the Insects should you demand their loyalty, so the pack includes a total of 5 large figures.

boardgame: Cthulhu Wars: Ramsey Campbell Horrors Pack 2
description: The Horror packs bring creations of Ramsey Campbell to Cthulhu Wars. These packs introduce neutral Great Old Ones and Monsters that may be added to any game of Cthulhu Wars; they are available to any player on a first come, first served basis. You seize control of one by spending Doom points, and they remain loyal thereafter. This pack includes the Great Old Ones Daoloth and Y'Golonac, plus three Satyr monsters - humans degenerated through contact with the Outside - for a total of five figures.

Starting with $1.2 million in cash, players (or teams) buy and sell stocks, bonds, real estate, and gold. First player to $10 million wins.

Players start on any square on the board and move clockwise by rolling the dice. Some squares allow you to optionally buy or sell various assets, other squares require you to buy or sell assets. Asset value can change during play when a player lands on one of the "Change In Interest Rate" squares, causing the "Inflation Rate Indicator" (a plastic spinner) to move up or down, indicating a new value for everyone's assets. After the rate change, the player who landed on the square is required to sell from half to all of one of his assets.

The Inflation Rate Indicator starts at "5-10%" and during play will move up and down to "0-5%" or "10-15%". Players may borrow from the bank, but it must be paid back before someone reaches the $10 million goal.

This game is no longer in production.
If replacement pieces are needed, please contact SimplyFun. We'll do our best to replace game pieces as long as they are available in our warehouse.

Bid A Letter for a chance to go first each round. Bids are won based on alphabetical order. On your turn, use your Letter Tile to build, expand and/or change a word in one of the four Word Chains. Select your Letter carefully, have the Letter that comes first in the alphabet to win the bid and go first. The first player to play all of their Letter Tiles is the winner

boardgame: Dungeon Storming: La Torre di Kunrat
description:
From the back of the box:

"Alone, in the depths of the dungeon, surrounded by monsters. A priestess and a thief fight their way trhu hideous ghoul back from the dead. Rich treasures await you, with which you can get new weapons, armor, spells and scrolls.

A collaborative game for two players or solo. It can be played with more players by combining two or more sets of Dungeon Storming!"

The game is played by moving the miniatures of your heroes on the map, room by room, battling the monsters with a simple roll of a die, dodging the traps, collecting treasures and magic objects, until the heroes reach the last room and try to overcome the boss monster (the heroes can exit the dungeon and buy new objects with the money found).

The map and the layout of the rooms for this dungeon are fixed (all the tables for the encounters, treasures and magical objects are printed on it), but it can be played with four levels of difficulty.

____________________________________________

From the publisher (Italian):

Quarto capitolo di Dungeon Storming, il gioco da tavolo d'avventura che vi permetterà di vivere le emozioni di un dungeon crawler in modo tascabile! La Torre di Kunrat è luogo dove il potente stregone lich estende il suo dominio sulle lande ghiacciate, al comando di un'orda di temibili scheletri. Nei panni di una Barda e di un valoroso Paladino dovrete tentare di porre fine al lugubre regno del lich Kunrat, senza che questo vi utilizzi per infoltire le sue schiere di non morti.

Esplora corridoi e stanze, trova oro e tesori e scappa prima di essere sconfitto! Arrivando al villaggio potrai compare armi e incantesimi per tornare all'avventura ancora più forte, e avvicinarti al terribile mostro finale... e forse riuscirai persino a sconfiggerlo!

Giocate questa avventura stand alone o combinate il dungeon di questo capitolo con gli altri 3 per dar vita ad un'avventura ancora più epica! Gli eroi di ciascun capitolo sono utilizzabili in ogni altro, si tratta di un gioco duro, modulare, che in ogni set presenta miniature e schede di due Avventurieri con poteri e abilità peculiari, di mostri piccoli e di un grosso mostro finale, oltre a una “vera” mappa, un emporio con oggetti unici e una missione tematica!

ATTENZIONE: le miniature all'interno non sono dipinte.

boardgame: Die Jagd nach dem verschollenen Schatz
description:
A pirate & treasure game with stamps for 2 to 4 players aged 6 years and up.  
Each player picks out a pirate. 
The game sheet is torn from the game pad, crumpled up and smoothed out again, so that it is wild and weavy as the sea. 10 treasures are placed onto the game sheet using the treasure chest stamp and the gold ink pad. 
The game is played in turns in a clockwise direction. On your turn, put the die onto the game sheet. Cover it with the dice cup so that you can no longer see the die. Now raise the dice cup slightly and push it over the game sheet like a ship on water and stop at a free treasure of your choice. Carefully lift up the dice cup and see what is on the die:
Saber: You can keep the treasure! Stamp your pirate stamp onto the treasure. It now belongs to you and no one else can ever steel it.
Jolly Roger (pirate flag): The black pirate has struck and stolen the treasure! Take the Jolly Roger stamp and the black ink pad and stamp the treasure out. It is now lost for everyone.
Treasure chest: Take the treasure chest stamp and the gold ink pad and stamp another treasure chest on any free space of the game sheet.
The game is over as soon as all treasure chests have been stamped. The player with the most treasure chests wins. There can be more than one winner.

boardgame: Blankr: An Awkwardly Hilarious Card Game For ★★★★★
description:
From the publisher:

Are you sad? Do you have no friends? Perhaps your partner left you because you have no sense of humour. Maybe no one comes to your parties because, quite frankly, they are incredibly boring. Are you a loner? Are you finding it difficult to impress someone with your pathetic attempt at trying to be funny? Are you relying on the old "knock Knock" jokes. Lord have mercy! Well don't worry boring person... Help is here! BLANKR is an awkwardly hilarious card game that will get the whole party roaring with laughter. When you see people trying to escape through windows to flee boredom, just pull out BLANKR and see those lips smile again. Bringing a new twist to the 'complete the sentence' game, Blankr encourages players to think and write down the most awkward and ridiculously funny sentences. The rules are simple, one player reads out a Blankr sentence containing a blank and the others think of a word or phrase to fill in the blank. At the end of each round all the answers are read out aloud and the funniest answer wins a point (actually lets make that 3,873,093 points). Don't worry if you're low on wit and/or lack a sense of humour as the references cards contain over 330+ weird and wonderful ideas to get your brain juices flowing. See your friends and family curl their toes and chuckle as they read out the answers. Perfect-size travel box to put into your bag or back-pocket. Perfect for girls night, boys night, girls and boys night, university game, ice breakers, drinking game etc.. Get yours now! Get some friends! Everyone is enjoying it, about time you did too. BLANKR: An Awkwardly Hilarious Card Game for ★★★★★ ****************************** A great and hilarious game!!!!! **********************************
Each player is trying to reach the goal as quickly as possible. The route takes around two turning points. But beware , there are many places where it is temporarily completely windless.

boardgame: Morphogea: A Modular Hex Map (fan expansion for Power Grid)
description:
Morphogea is a modular map expansion for Power Grid, which provides some 275 quadrillion map configurations — a number which excludes bottlenecks and peninsulas and any configuration with excess cities — via 14 individually configurable map hexes. If the players agree to peninsulas and/or bottlenecks, there are septillions of configurations — nonillions if the players agree to cover unavailable cities as in the base game.

The map hexes are "sliced" into equilateral triangles, so that connections pointing off the edge of the map can be removed by flipping over the edge triangles. Additionally, the connection costs were determined according to the first-edition Funkenschlag rules, based on the terrain the connections traverse.

Because the base game's gameboard is no longer required for the map, this expansion includes an octagonal board to replace the resource market, heptagonal boards to provide distinct city connection tracks for the different player counts, and a hexagonal turn order / auction status track.

Finally, to provide extra variety, the expansion includes a deck of 32 resource cards, which can be used to randomize the starting resource market.

In Colours and Shapes players take on the role of one of the eight colours and shapes:

Red Triangle;  Orange Oval;  Yellow Line;  Green Square;  Blue Circle;  Indigo Diamond;  Violet Rectangle;  or Grey.

Each has their own special power creating a unique gameplay experience. Battle friends in this match making card game and learn your colours and shapes!

The base game of Colours and Shapes is an exciting 2-4 player game that can either be played in minutes, or for hours, requiring you to combine thoughtful strategy, luck, and in-your-face gameplay to create a fun, fast, and casual game that can be played anywhere, anytime.

Players decide whether to lay down sets of at least three of a kind, or push their luck to wait and lay down more than three.  However, if they wait too long and get caught with cards, each one is -2 points. After playing certain sets, players may activate extra powers that stop their opponents from winning or help them move a step closer to victory.

Pop the apple out of the tree. Win by collecting the correct three!

Kids dexterity game.

User summary:

Moral Choice is a philosophical game published in Canada in 1985 by Moral Choice Associates.

In the game 2-6 players move around a spiral track representing their growth from childhood to adulthood to old age by rolling a die. Most of the squares have a large K or M on them in five different colours. There are also special Horseshoe (Opportunity), Lightning Bolt (Crisis), Light Bulb (Bright Idea) and Superdilemma squares.

When they land on K or M square, they must choose a card of the colour they landed on from a box divided into 18 groups. These cards describe moral dilemmas (brief case studies) on one side, with four multiple-choice options and the real solutions for both the K and M philosophies on the other.

These cards are divided into three major categories based on age (Youth, Adult and Senior), each of which is divided into six colour-coded sub-categories based on the type of dilemma one must solve (Business/Professional, Biomedical, Social/Political, Environmental, Personal/Sexual, Superdilemmas).

The K squares ask players to answer a dilemma according to the duty-based ethics of Immanuel Kant. The M squares ask you to solve the same dilemma according to the utilitarian ethics of John Stuart Mill. The game comes with summary cards for Kant and Mill listing their seven key ideas in each case.

Players can use either the easy multiple-choice option, or answer the dilemma in their own words, with the other players voting on how close they came to matching the true solution. In the case of a Superdilemma, no solutions are given, so players propose their own, with others voting to determine whether it’s correct. They can also time the game, giving each player 3-5 minutes to answer a dilemma.

If you get the right answer, they can move forward next turn. If they get it wrong, they move backwards when they roll again. The winner is the first player who gets to the end of the spiral track.

description:

Make a perfect pizza but do beware, there could be a slug or bug under there!

A fun colour and shape matching game, but unlike a traditional pizza, the toppings in this game are sometimes very unusual and yukky, which children find hilarious.

Make a delicious pizza by matching colours and shapes with the help of the Head Chef. Beware of yucky toppings and other players who want to swipe your slices!

Subtitled "The New Adult Game that Peps up the Party - Smart - Saucy - Sophisticated", Scrambled Slogans consists of six pads (labeled A-F) of perforated and gummed labels. Each pad contains 48 phrases or sayings, such as "Red-headed woman suspected", "Down on the farm", "No metal can touch you", and "What a Young Girl Ought to Know".

The game also includes a pad of blank sheets. Each player or team then takes one or more of the gummed sheets and attempts to write a funny story, using as many as possible of the gummed phrases. The players are allowed to bridge gaps by hand writing any names of people or places, pronouns, prepositions such as in, at, of, on, for, to, etc. as well as a dozen common verbs: be, do, go, can, say, have, see, meet, give, think, know, hear.

After the stories have been completed, each is read aloud and a winner is chosen either by the hostess or by a vote of the players.

Rude, rambunctious, and raunchy. Diminutives hate civilization and they are on a one-way trip to end it all! Equipping themselves with unique and powerful gear (such as Yo Dong Wang’s Doom Frisbee and the Dreaded Unitard of the Old One) they fight while assembling their ultimate weapon of destruction. Will your diminutive be victorious and be the one to destroy it all?!

Diminutives Opposing Civilization is a card game for 3-5 players. Each player uses quality and gear cards to outfit their diminutive, defeat other diminutives, gather pieces for their ultimate weapon, and destroy civilization! There are literally millions of hilarious combinations in DOC.

If you think that Diminutives Opposing Civilization is just a straight-forward, predictable game, well… think again! DOC also has both “Twist of Fate” and “Quirk” cards. Twist of Fate cards create situations and effects that no can predict. Quirk cards shift the fate of battle in unpredictable ways.

Spontaneous and unpredictable, with just enough strategy, means that Diminutives Opposing Civilization will keep things hilariously interesting!

Reinos e Relíquias is a fantasy card game in which players take the role of a hero from one of three kingdoms (Light, Darkness and Neutral) trying to defeat heroes from other kingdoms and, by doing so, stealing relics to his kingdoms. Players can equip new itens, cast powerfull spells and recruit new allies playing cards from their hands. Each weapon/spell has a different range and power and, if the defending player does not evade or absorb the damage by using his armor, he subtracts his hitpoints. When a player reaches zero life, the attacking player takes one  relic from the pile and one from the defeated player.
When all the relics are gathered, the players score relics for their kingdoms (based on the value for that particular kingdom printed on each relic card). The players with the most points (in other words, the kingdom with the most points) at the end, shares the victory.

Tip the Scales tests your knowledge and luck against your opponent to tip the scales in your favour.

KopFit is a Game which includes 10 Games to sharpen your mind. It is a trainer for mental fitness.
When you play KopFit, you have to go through 10 Competitions to get points. The one with the most points at the end is the winner.
The ten games are:
WORTWECHSEL: You have to change a word into another, letter by letter in four steps.
MEMODEMO: It's a Memory-Game where you must find the cards in a given sequence.
AUF EIN WORT: You must complete letters to words.
SUMM SUMM: You must try to get the biggest or smallest amount by connecting numbers by making different calculations (+/-/x/:).
WIMMELBAR: You must try to keep objects in mind.
LETTERATUR: You must sort objects depending on their 1, 2, or 3 letters, after the cards are removed from the table.
MONOVIEL: You must find words with the same starting letter for as many categories you can manage in 30 seconds.
LÄNDERSPIEL: You have to sort countries depending on Population, size of area and inhabitants of their capitol city.
SAG SELBST: You must complete quotes in your own way.
STARKE SPRÜCHE: You must vote for quotes, and try to vote, how your opponents vote.
The points are marked with colored wooden pins into the brains of the players mats.
A counter clock, which is needed for some games is added.
Electronic Quiz-Game with Gamemaster-Console, which contents more than 1000 Questions.

Sten, Sax, Påse: Superdueller is a variation on the classic Rock, Paper, Scissors. In this game you have cards with Rocks, Scissors and Pouches (in Sweden it's not paper but pouch), that you play to win matches. But before every match you also draw and read a card with special rule for that match. It can be that more then two player gonna play, or that a player must win two times in a row. The game is played until one player has a certain amount of wins.

A world map with 22 countries, each with different ressoruces.

Players strive for world domination starting with 2 small villages and 2 horses. With strategic retail one player will outperform the opponents and become the new Tsar.

AIM OF THE GAME
Players have to occupy countries with their horses, houses or castles to receive winning points. To win the game, 15 points have to be collected.

GAME PLAY:
Every player starts his journey with 2 horses and 2 huts which are placed in a certain order.

Then it is time to collect the resources of every country. From now on, every turn a player rolls 3 dice and adds up the numbers. The total determines which country receive resources. Every player who has one or more buildings in this country, receive the appropriate resource. If a player rolls a 3, 4, 16, 17, or 18 he must return half of his cards to the bank.

Using these resources, players can update their buildings and thus their influence on the different areas.

B ATAKY! ("To the attack!") is a Soviet-era board game described as being for secondary school students clearly has its roots in chess, with a mapboard laid out in squares, 15 across by 20 deep. There are three terrain features: a six-square box at the centre of each side representing the headquarters that must me captured; a river (which slows movement) across the middle, two squares wide in the centre and one square on the sides; and four yellow squares on each side closest to the players, which are reserve areas in which pieces may be placed but not attacked.

Each side starts with 21 pieces: 8 infantry, 4 tanks, 4 artillery, 3 missiles and 2 airplanes. As in chess, each piece moves differently and destroys an enemy piece by moving onto its square.

All moves are orthogonal, and pieces may attack in any of the four directions. Infantry may move one or two squares. Tanks may move 1 - 3 spaces. Missiles may move up to 5 spaces but when attacking are destroyed along with their target. Artillery moves one space but may attack a target up to 3 spaces away (without moving to that square). Airplanes may attack up to 10 squares away. Finally, each player may record one space that is mined and will destroy an enemy piece that moves onto it.

There is a screen that allows players to make their initial placements in secret. The first player makes a single move; his opponent then makes two; the first player makes two; and then the opponent makes three. Thereafter each player's turn includes three moves, enabling a variety of assaults, probes and counter-attacks.

Set around the events of ‘the Secret War’ Achtung! Cthulhu Skirmish introduces players to the terrifying conflict fought to stop the sorcerous Nazi cult of the Black Sun from unleashing a tide of Mythos horrors against the Allies. Achtung! Cthulhu Skirmish: Rise of the Black Sun is the title of the first wave of releases during 2016 which will complete a whole story arc set around the first battles fought by the secretive Allied forces Section M, set up by the British to combat the Mythos threat and their US counterparts Majestic.

Set against them are arrayed the powerful forces of Exarch Weissler’s Black Sun, who as well as conventional troops and weapons can bring devastating magical power to the battlefield, summon ancient and terrible creatures like the Servitors of Nyarlathotep as well as powerful allies such as the Deep Ones and Mi-Go.

Take command of brave Allied forces like Badger’s Commandos and the Pathfinder Demon Hunters alongside historical army units, and lead them to victory against the Axis hordes. Deploy special hero units like Sgt Brandon Carter, Professor Richard Deadman or Ariane Dubois to take on the might of the Nazi war machine.

As the Axis, strike terror into your opponents by commanding the sorcerous Black Sun and their Mythos allies, bringing new levels of fear onto the battlefield. Call on the terrifying Deep One War Party or let the unholy Servitors of Nyarlathotep supplement your forces and crush the Allied threat.

Achtung! Cthulhu Skirmish is powered by Spartan Games’ Dystopian Legions 2.0 rules set (re-written by Matthew Hope (Relic Knights, AE-WWII, Wrath of Kings, Sedition Wars). The game allows for fast playing battles from small skirmishes up to full scale conflicts. Modiphius has added new rules for Mythos powers, creatures, artefacts and spells to allow players to experience the full flavour and terror of the Achtung! Cthulhu universe with their heroes and troops. The range is designed to work alongside other popular 28mm miniature lines.

Commander set contents: 144 Page Rulebook, 1 A5 Template Sheet, 1 A5 Token Sheet, 1 Tracker Sheet, 1 Double-sided Reference Sheet, 15 D6 (5 red, 5 blue, 5 black), 1 54 Card Deck (TAC cards, Mythos Events, Spell Cards)

Achtung! Cthulhu Skirmish Rules Feature:


     A fast paced wargame rule set combining World War Two and the Cthulhu Mythos.
     Uses the highly successful Dystopian Legions by Spartan Games for a fun but challenging tactical experience.
     Based on the award-winning Achtung! Cthulhu roleplaying game setting by Modiphius Entertainment with
     A major cast of heroes and villains, secret organisations, terrifying horrors and ancient artefacts.
     Track the growing influence of the Mythos over the battlefield. As the Mythos Influence Level rises, strange and terrible things begin to happen to those involved in the conflict.
     New Tactical Action Cards and ‘Model Assigned Rules’— MAR’s that capture the feel and flavour of the Achtung! Cthulhu settings.
     Introducing rules for Mythos powers, spells and artefacts.
     Stats for Achtung! Cthulhu forces as well as normal historical infantry, support weapons, vehicles and tanks.
     Players from both sides can bring powerful spellcasters to bear—weaving spells across the battlefield to ensure victory over their enemy.
     Written and converted by Matthew Hope (Relic Knights, AE-WWII, Wrath of Kings, Sedition Wars)
     Fully compatible with the existing Achtung! Cthulhu miniatures range.
     Expand your campaigns with hero and villain characters like Sgt Brandon Carter, Ariane D’bois or ‘Sweet’ Liesel Bohm, Adawolfa Gabler, or Franz ‘the Blackbird’ Amsel.
     Call for reinforcements like the Deep One war party, Servitors of Nyarlathotep, Badger’s Commandos or
     Demonhunter Pathfinders to rule the battlefield.
     All purchases come with free download of our massive Forest of Fear background storyline and full campaign briefing with full mission suite for both Section M and Black Sun operatives.
     Narrative base campaigns and expansion sets throughout the year will expand and enhance your adventures!

Make the fastest route from one place to another in Norway .

For example: You are in Svolvaer and may only travel further by car or boat . Do you find a destination that suits ?

Travel by plane, boat , train, bus or car to seven sites in Norway . Put your cards together for the fastest itinerary , complete the journey first and win the game.

Extension: 20 new cards + 1 cover card with rules

Rule changes: You start with 3 out of 6 cards instead of 3 cards randomly distributed.

Variant for experts: Before the game begins, each player gets half of the cards and discards 5 of them. These are out of the play.

From the designer:

Grandpa is visiting, and you and a sibling are going out with him. Your delicious destination: the ice cream stand in the park. Your walk will take you through eight friendly neighborhoods, and there are plenty of interesting objects to explore and collect along the way: stones, insects, bottlecaps and such—pretty much anything you can stuff into your pockets. Stay out of the mud puddles, though, or your mom won’t be happy with you when you get home.

For each round, one player rolls all the dice. Grandpa moves along the path automatically, then each player will have a choice of moves. It's good to maneuver yourself into going first, since that player will have first crack at snapping up objects, but he will also have to deduce what his opponent might do. Other considerations for both players include getting more different objects than their opponent, when to re-roll their dice, and the need to stay close to Grandpa. If you lag too far behind you'll have to entreat Grandpa to "wait up!"-- which will yield negative victory points at the end of the game.

Light fun for kids and semi-serious gamers, Wait Up, Grandpa! provides 30 minutes of game time well-spent.

Components:
  1 Game Board
  5 Dice in three colors (2 blue, 2 red, 1 gray)
 24 Object cubes in eight colors
 10 “Wait Up/Hurry Up, Grandpa!” markers, double-sided
  3 Mud puddle markers
  2 Player tokens (1 blue, 1 red)
  1 Grandpa token (gray)

The Olympians are more wrathful than ever, and Zeus has opened the gates to dread Tartarus itself. Unleash Legendary Monsters from Greek mythology on the faithless mortals in this colossal expansion for Monstrous - the game of mythic mayhem.

The Release the Kraken expansion for Monstrous includes one Location card (Tartarus), as well as 8 Legendary Monster cards and 10 Tentacle cards. Players who hit Tartarus with a monster get to draw a Legendary Monster.

The Kickstarter edition of Monstrous included the Dark Ages edition of this expansion, using public domain art. A Kickstarter for a full art version of the expansion is planned for 2016.

Welcome to the new version of Dust Tactics! Whether you’re a new player or a veteran who wants to get into the Allied faction, this starter set is made for you!
Inside this Starter Set you’ll discover the new Dust Tactics rules and a complete army that will allow for countless games. This army is also a strong base to build a larger force upon, or to add to another Starter Set to field an even larger force! Everything you need to play is in the box, you just need to find an opponent!

CONTENT OF THIS BOX:

     one rule booklet with the introductions rules to Dust Tactics

     one catalog
     one leaflet


These miniatures come with their unit cards, to use with Dust Tactics and Dust Tactics Battlefield rules.
Like all regular Dust miniatures, these are already assembled and primed. They are ready for the battlefield, or for painting, as wish! You can paint directly on them, since the priming serve as an undercoat and is compatible with any type of paint.

Kill the King is a tactical board-wargame where two players control one army each. One player attacks a castle while the other player try to defend it and prevent the attackers from killing the king. The attackers have to hurry because the defenders will soon be reinforced by its own cavalry. Returning to save the day.

You can also play two alternate game modes of Kill the King in addition to the normal mode. The first is a regular battle between the two players armies outside the castle walls. The second is a big version of the normal mode where you combine two game boards. One player defends the combined castle of the two game boards, while two players try to attack the castle on one side each.

The pictures are not from the finished game but from the prototypes.

description:
Create the video game of your dreams! In Gamecraft you are in charge of a digital games studio, contracting employees with differing expertise and competing to find out who is the best game developer from Games S/A.

Start small, upgrade your studio and crew, buy improvements, and train your professional squad with new abilities. Being dumb is now fun with the I'm With Stupid Trivia Board Game. One of the most internationally recognized logos and sayings for over six decades, the global t-shirt phenomenon is now a multi-player board game. For ages 12 and older, the adult board game features bizarre and “Stupid" trivia with attitude. This game is designed for three or more players and will have everyone entertained. The I'm With Stupid Trivia Board Game includes one game board, a card box, 300 “Stupid" question cards, six game pieces with stands, one “Stupid" hat and easy-to-follow rules.

Description from the publisher:

xChess is based on a Hexagonal Chess Game invented in Canberra Australia, in 1973 by Wolfgang Flatow. The Flatow Chess Board is a 65 hexagon 8 x 4 x 4 hexagonal matrix, one more hexagon than the traditional 64 square matrix. The chess piece moves are intuitively and logically mapped by closely correlating adjacent and diagonal moves used by the traditional square Chess game. xChess has 2 openings, the Flatow opening can be played using a traditional set of pieces, having 2 bishops (all on the same diagonal grid) and the Fortress opening having 3 bishops (one for each diagonal grid) per side. The Game was refined over 2 years of intensive exploratory play by Canberra Chess enthusiasts and was self-published in 1976 in Australia.

The Flatow Chess Board and openings present the neatest and most compact form of Hexagonal Chess and xChess is the combination of the best openings and pawn variations discovered over the years.

The X in xChess stands for HeXagon, eXtra dimension, eXtra power, eXtra possibilities, eXtra fun and X Factor.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Planechase: Chaos Reigns
description:
Chaos Reigns is a five-color deck built around cascade, from Alara Reborn.

Planechase was originally released in 2009. This deck was released in 2012.

Planechase is a card game and a variant of Magic: The Gathering with an emphasis on multi-player games. The set utilizes new oversized Plane cards, cards that are based on various locations (Planes) within the Magic multiverse, to modify the rules of gameplay.

To play a game of Planechase, each player needs his or her own planar deck, consisting of at least 10 different oversized cards. The players may also use a "shared planar deck," which requires ten cards per player. Players cannot use more than one plane with any given name. When the game begins, the player going first reveals the top of his or her planar deck (or the shared planar deck), which is the plane the game starts on. Each plane card has a name, a type, one ability that applies at all times (either to all players or to whoever is the turn player at the time), and one "Chaos" ability.

Players also need a six-sided die, called the "planar die." Each Planechase pre-constructed deck comes with a "planar die" that has four blank faces, one "Planeswalk" face with the "Planeswalker" symbol, and one "Chaos" face with its own symbol unique to Planechase. During a player's main phase, that player can roll the planar die, with three possible outcomes. A blank roll of the planar die does nothing. If "Chaos" is rolled, the "Chaos" ability of the current plane card triggers. If the "Planeswalk" symbol is rolled, the player "planeswalks away" from the current plane (put it on the bottom of its owner's planar deck) and he or she reveals the top card of his or her planar deck, which becomes the new plane that has been "planeswalked to." A player can roll the planar die as many times as he or she likes, but successive rolls cost an additional one mana for each time the planar die has been rolled this turn.

Other than the above, the normal rules of the game are followed, and Planechase can be adapted to any multiplayer variant.

Planechase 2012 introduced the "phenomenon" card type. A player may have up to two different phenomenon cards in his or her planar deck, which cannot have the same name. When a player planeswalks to a phenomenon, he or she follows the instructions on the card, then immediately planeswalks away from it.

description:
Players take turns laying triangle-shaped cards, with each of the three corners having one of 5 colors and one of 5 patterns.  Depending on the game variant desired, players match colors, patterns, either colors or patterns, or avoid matching anything to empty their hand and claim victory.

Includes 35 triangular cards.

Projective Set or ProSet is a real-time game like SET in which you're trying to find groups of cards that go together.

Each card in the deck has 1-6 different symbols on it. Seven cards are dealt onto the table, then players try to find a set of cards ranging from 3-7 cards such that each symbol is present on the selected cards an even number of times. (With seven cards on the table, at least one set is guaranteed to exist.) Whoever first spots a set claims those cards, then you refill the display to seven cards. When the deck is empty and no more sets can be found, whoever has the most cards wins.

description:
Three Little Ducks is a card game in which the players cooperate and compete to build a path out of "danger" and move their three baby duck cards along it to the safety of home. Players take turns to pick up a card from the deck which will either help them build a path or move one of their ducklings along it. Watch out for the big bad wolf card! He will send your duck back to the start. The first player to get all three of their ducklings home is the winner!

The Three Little Ducks game consists of a deck of 52 cards. 
It is for 2 to 4 players and takes 30 minutes to play.
It is suitable for young children even if they can't read yet.

Corporate Shadows by Mike Haverty is a Two or Four Player action based card game where players manipulate corporate assets and initiate covert operations against opposing criminal organizations. Support from one of the two major solar system wide governments, research and military actions bolster the players' stance and eventual outcome of the shadowy maneuvers between the players.

Gameplay is based on the Action Rummy Concept with the use of Oversight Cards allowing the player to score points during a Hand at the risk of bringing down the High Court on the player (Player scores no points) if too many are played.

Players may also play Alliance cards to form temporary teams during the course of a hand in order to score points jointly with that Alliance member.

Corporate Asset Cards are collected by the players during each hand in order to score points.  The game is played to 150 Points.

A bluffing card game for 3-6 players.

Players secretly choose role cards and play as disease causing agents.  Players bluff and try to play cards to change the moisture and temperature conditions in the body.  When a player believes conditions are right, he plays an infection card to take over the body and win the game.  Penalties and rewards come with the bluffing system so that it is in players' best interest to bluff and call out other players' bluffs.

Coming to Kickstarter summer 2016!

A card and dicing game for two players.

Big Money Baseball is divided into two phases.

Phase One - The draft.  Each player gets 100 dollars to spend on drafting a team of players.  The player card deck is shuffled and the first five cards are laid out.  Each player card is assigned a position, a set of statistics and a minimum bid amount.  The players draft a team of baseball players (at least nine, to fill every position, but bench players are encouraged) by bidding against each other.

Phase Two - The game.  Players field their teams and roll dice to determine the outcome of each at bat.  Play begins with a pitcher's roll, which is checked against a results chart.  Play then continues with a batter's roll and a fielder's roll (if necessary).  Play follows normal professional baseball rules.  The game can continue for either 3 or 9 innings.

A joining of 4 classic drinking card games

Kings – Lay all eighteen cards on the table face down.  Each player takes a turn drawing a card and performing the Kings action described on the card.  Any players not required to drink earn a point for that round.  After each action is complete, lay the card on the table in rows to form a pyramid.  The bottom row will have five cards and the top row will have one card.  Once the pyramid is complete, move the remaining three cards in front of all players.  Each player will choose one of the cards (multiple players can choose the same card.  Now flip over the three player cards.

Pyramid – each player takes a turn flipping over a card, starting at the bottom row of the pyramid.  If the flipped card is higher than the player’s card, the player drinks for one second and keeps the card.  If the flipped card is lower than the player’s card, the player gets a point and keeps the card.  If the flipped card is equal to the player’s card, the player drinks double and keeps the card.  Play continues into the second and third and fourth rows, with losing cards resulting in drinking for two, three and four seconds, respectively (still for twice as long for an equal number).  After the last card at the top of the pyramid is flipped and drinking or points are resolved, it stays on the table face up.  The players claim the player cards, so that all players have roughly the same number of cards.

Circuit – each player puts his cards into a face down pile.  Players take turns flipping their top card and laying it next to the last card on the table.  If the card is the same color or same number (the white bordered number nine cards are wild), the circuit continues, the player gets a point and does not have to drink.  If the circuit is broken, the player takes a drink and play continues.  At the end of the game, keep the longest circuit on the table face up.  Put all of the other cards in a face down pile.

The Long Road Home – each player gets one chance to walk the road (which was the longest circuit).  Starting with the first card on the road, the player says whether the top card on the pile is higher or lower than the face up card.  If he is correct, he moves to the next card on the road.  If he is wrong, he takes a drink and has to start over.  If he completes the road before the deck runs out, he gets five points.  Every player gets the same chance to walk the road.  The highest total score after this game wins.

"Spiele-Messen-Metaller" is a promo for the German board game Rock'n Rodeo.

"Spiele-Messen-Metaller" does not want to camp near Hippies but wants to camp near other Metal fans.

You are a crew member in an alien ship. Alien for humans of course. The ship is malfunctioning and you are stranded in the human solar system.
Using primitive detectors, you are trying to identify as many planets as possible.

The quest is finished when Earth has been claimed and no one disproved the claim after 2 rounds or when a crew member receives maximum influence from their captain.

The winner is the crew member with the most influence.

Each player holds a detector card and a bunch of colored tokens.
Each round players execute 2 actions each turn.

The possible actions are: Detect, Claim and Disprove.

Detecting
Players use the detector cards and place them under each hidden planet.
Once flipped the player secretly sees a part of the planet and notes down the information.

Claiming
Players move the claimed card from the game board onto it's claimed location.

Disproving
Players use their detector card to disprove an existing claim.

boardgame: Batman Miniature Game: The Flash and Arrow
description:
As the war against crime rages on in the streets of Gotham. New allies, villains and cities become embroiled in the fight. The Flash at Central City and The Arrow at Star City, come out from the shadows and take on the own evil arch-rivals and join Batman to rid their cities of crime. Bringing their own super powers and special skills to the streets you will be able to zoom at lightning speeds and loose a boxing glove arrow at a foe with this epic expansion book from Knight Models.

The Flash and Arrow expansion book is a special limited 160 page full colour hardcover edition that brings in update with the rules for use of these epic heroes, their allies, villains and much, much more.

With the rules for using the Speed force, new teams, vehicle rules as well as 80 new character traits, this expansion is a must for any collector of the Batman miniatures game.

In Picnic Panic command a tribe of warrior ants fight other tribes for control of a stash of candy that has fallen on the ground. The game is easy to learn and quick to play. Best of all, you get to eat the candy that you capture!

Each player gets the same number of newsstands, one lottery ticket, a pawn and 18 game token (money). The newsstands may be placed on any space in the color of the players pawn. The remaining newsstands can be bought by the players. The players move the pawns around the board according to the results of their dice rolls. Whenever a pawn lands on a space with the newsstand of another player, the owner of the pawn has to pay 3 token to the other player. If a pawn lands on a space with a circled number, the player has to take an (often humorous) event card and follow the printed instructions. The lottery tickets have value for one lap around the board. At the beginning of each round the Player may renew his ticket for 2 token. Lottery winnings are distributed by event cards. After an agreed on number of laps the player with the most money wins the game.

Description from the publisher:

Dwarves mining in the Scavewyrm Pit have brought back news that the mythical cavern of Soloth has been discovered. Although unexplored, it is rumored to contain exotic weapons and treasures from the reign of Soloth, the ancient Vampire Lord of the Underworld. Furthermore, sinister acolytes laboring in a forgotten labyrinth seek to reanimate Soloth's malevolent queen.

In the Catacombs: Cavern of Soloth expansion, four new heroes set out to confront this impending evil and prevent disaster.

A Trick Taking Game with a pirate theme! Multiple ways to play and with a dice option, this game is fun for everyone! The game is for 1-4 players, it consists of 54 cards and 2 dice. To start the game, you shuffle the deck, draw 5 cards and roll the dice.To win the hand, you must either equal the number rolled or get as close as you can without going over. If there is a tie, you continue to play out your hand until there is winner.

From the publisher:

In this second expansion kit for designer Chris Taylor’s tiny titan of a 4X game, Imperial Stars II, designer/developer Alan Emrich expands the boundaries of gameplay between the warring human factions. Building on the first expansion kit (which is required to fully enjoy this second expansion kit), there are new worlds to conquer with the addition of two new maps, each featuring coveted planetary systems (yielding double value). These balanced maps have proven very popular for their “narrative” they engender during gameplay.
 New Event and Technology cards broaden the story scope of the game, particularly with the introduction of Allies to the mix of outcomes. The Thaeon Order and the Free Settlers are off-map rivals who might send forces – but only sufficient to nudge the balance of power in your conflict. A new “Event +6 Ops” marker for each side increases the frequency that Events occur, so there are a few more surprises in every game!
 New player aid mats now uncouple the Alpha and Beta Zone costs from the map, so that you can experiment with different Ops costs to retrieve ships from them, and each mat includes a cost summary in the Scrap Yard for easy reference.
 As a final touch, we have included cubes to replace Planet markers on the board. In this way, you can draw Planet markers from a cup to ensure randomness. All this adds more epic to your space epic, as it can only be told in Imperial Stars II.

description:
Elvyn, an elderly, much-loved adventurer has disappeared, and an escaped goblin slave confirms that he's been captured by kobold slavers. Now the town council has offered you a nice sum of money to enter the caves and bring him back.

Dare you enter the kobold caves and return before nightfall?
