Per the text, it's recommended for characters that are level 1 or 2.

In Storm the Bridge, you play as both a leader in charge of a party of crew members and, sometimes, as writer able to change the narrative. You will recruit crew members (some better than others), sabotage other players (all in the name of peace, of course), and fight for control of ship sections (sorry, no holodeck). Action is cut-throat, a well-played card can change the direction of the game, and in the end, only one leader will arise as the new captain of the FSS Polemus.

Storm the Bridge is completely card-based, with 14 oversized cards (nine leaders and five ship sections) and 140 poker-size cards (35 crew members and 105 action cards). Players use their leaders to recruit crew members, and then use the leaders and crew members to recruit better crew and attack ship sections, while using action cards to add bonuses, sabotage the other player, modify crew members, and more. The goal is to control the Bridge ship section card and two other ship sections, then use the Bridge's unique ability - Victory!

Game description from the publisher:

Keep your wits about you and your eye on the cards to win at this fast-paced Star Wars version of the Hands Down card game! All you have to do is watch for matches on the spinner to the Star Wars character cards in your hand. If the spinner lands on C-3PO and you have that card, slam it down on the Target token as fast as you can! Get rid of all your cards first, and you're the ruler of the galaxy – and the winner!

Includes 20 cards, 2 Target tokens, spinner with arrow and base, and instructions.

From the publisher:

Dozen's Donuts a fast paced tile shifting game from the creative minds of Monocle Society. Play as one of six characters at Debbie Dozen's robot powered food truck, matching up your favorite donuts along the way!

The game is based on the classic match game, but redone in a 4x4 grid. As you make matches, you shift the cards in the grid one direction, and fill in the holes with new donuts. Along the way you'll see different donut types, some that are stale and cannot be matched, golden donuts that match with everything, and power-up donuts that when match give you a power to use on yourself or your opponents. The first to 9 matches wins!

Don't forget to download the companion app on iOS and Android for interactive score cards, print and play options, and more!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Kurgill the Wizard has gone crazy. Watch out, as you and your fellow monkey climbers must escape his hazardous spells. Bananas, faeries and anvils, oh my! You never know what Kurgill will throw at you next. Only the Treetop is safe. Climb as quickly as you can, battle to knock each other down, dodge the traps, and above all, beware of the Gnome. It’s a race to the top.

Monkey is a fast-paced, goofy card game where the odds are against you. It can be played with up to 4 players and even includes a solitaire mode. Each player is a monkey who starts at the bottom of the collective “Tree.” Four cards make up the “Trunk” of the Tree and three cards make up the “Treetop.” Each turn starts with you drawing a card into your hand from the draw pile, then flipping over the top card of the “Spell Deck” (usually this “spell” harms you, but you might get lucky!). Finally, you get two Action Points (APs) to spend during your turn. You can spend APs to draw cards, to attempt to climb up the Tree (ie. to the next position up), or to set traps to impede the progress of other players. Each Tree card (whether a Trunk or Treetop card) has a number (1-10, 15 or 20), which is colored (yellow, blue, green, red, black or multicolored). Each time a player attempts to climb a Tree card they combine cards from their hand until their sum exceeds the number on the Tree card. But there’s a twist: color matters! If the Tree card is red, yellow, green or blue, then the player may use anything except that color in their attempt to climb. (For example, if the Tree card is a yellow 10, a player could combine a blue 6 and a green 5 to beat it. However, a player could not have used a yellow 15 to beat it since the Tree card itself was yellow and such "color matching" is not allowed). There are two exceptions: Black can only be beaten by black, and multicolored cards can be beaten by any cards.

Just when you think you have it, Kurgill the Wizard might prevent you from climbing, or even worse, might send you falling back down the Tree. The standard gameplay will keep you entertained, but in case you want to mix it up, Monkey also has three variations: “Insane Mode,” in which you remove the main good cards from the Spell Deck; “Super-Tree,” in which each player has their own Trunk to climb but a shared Treetop from which they can oust other players; and finally “Krazy Kurgz,” in which Kurgill the Wizard joins as an opponent to all players, to wreak havoc along with his birdie minions. Monkey is a great family game, quick to pick up, and always entertaining!

boardgame: Marvel Dice Masters: Doctor Strange Team Pack
description:
The “Sorcerer Supreme” is joining the Marvel Dice Masters game with the Doctor Strange Team Pack.

This new expansion features Doctor Strange himself, along with a supporting cast including Clea, Wong, and Dormammu.

Team Packs are intended as a starting point for players who want to leap in with a new team, be they new players or veterans looking for something different.  By combining a Team Pack with any Starter Set, a player has everything they need to field the new team.

The Doctor Strange Team Pack includes 24 cards and 16 dice.

boardgame: DC Comics Dice Masters: Green Arrow and The Flash
description:
Two popular DC Comics characters come together in the next expansion for WizKids’ ever-growing DC Dice Masters game with the Green Arrow and The Flash Foil Packs, scheduled to release in August.

This expansion features classic and modern characters from the Green Arrow and The Flash comics, including Jay Garrick, Barry Allen, Green Arrow, and Felicity Smoak, and villains like Gorilla Grodd, Merlyn, Clayface, and Giganta.

Marvel Dice Masters: Deadpool features characters from the Deadpool comics, the "Weapon X" project, and assorted What If? comics.

Naturally, the focus of the new expansion is Deadpool and related characters, including Lady Deadpool, Dogpool, and Evil Deadpool. Additional characters include Wolverine, X-23, Mr. Sinister, and the Stepford Cuckoos, as well as the introduction of the Inhumans and new characters from the Spider-Verse to Marvel Dice Masters. More than thirty new characters, both heroes and villains, are included in the complete set.

From the publisher:

HoliMaths X - a family multiplication strategy card game - 10 ways to play it -1 to 6 players - 7 and up - innovative card game aimed to meaningful/significant learning - develops logical thinking - converts an individual learning process into a collective one generating social interaction - helps develop tolerance to frustration - promotes strategical thinking to solve problems - aimed for intergenerational integration involving parents and grandparents in a fun way for the whole family!

The main attributes of the games include helping/incentivizing early development of Higher and Lower Order Thinking Skills including creative and critical thinking in addition to comprehensive logical reasoning - Produces positive emotional engagement while providing the intended learning experience – Easy to learn, fast to start – Character building for the child involving honesty, responsibility, cooperation during play - High REPLAYABILITY values because of its 10 ways to play - HoliPlayers.com  10 lost cards Free-Replacement-Guarantee - Money back “No Questions Asked” Guarantee

HoliMaths X is an innovative family multiplication strategy card game directed to meaningful learning of multiplication tables that allows through interaction with other people, to learn the mathematical operation of multiplications in a fun and collective environment. The name of the game implies an holistic understanding- learning as part of the human system as well.

Game Features: HoliMaths X is an educational family card game that allows the interaction of several players and encourages social interaction, as opposed to learning multiplication only through memorization, which is individual and not a collective or group process that isolates more than unites. Since it has 10 different ways of playing, the game has multiple variations; some are hard even for adults, thus, a fun and simple but challenging, mindful and expansive game recommended for all ages.

The mechanics of the game, based on Problems-Solutions solving, helps develop logical thinking, decision making and problem solving in children in their learning stages, plus the development of strategies to earn points and solve problems. The game includes HelpCards as aids to find solutions which allows the child to eliminate the anxiety or stress that can be caused by not remembering the result of an operation through memorization alone.

It is a tool designed for association and pedagogically meaningful learning. It helps develop tolerance to frustration, healthy competition and the development of strategies throughout the game to solve problems.

Alternatives for playing the game: The game has 10 alternatives that can be played which allows the child to practice mathematical operation of multiplication in different manners ensuring the continued use without boring the game participants.

Meaningful learning is learning that students understand while they are learning. Meaningful learning is the leading trend for more useful and significant learning and how it is retained. This learning serves as to use what they have learned in new situations, in different contexts, so more than memorizing, this learning must be understood asimilated. Meaningful learning is thus opposed to mechanical learning.

This Foundation certifies: The game HoliMaths X is an innovative educational tool for comprehensive learning of the mathematical operation of multiplication. Our Foundation recommends it as a learning tool for organizational, familiar and educational environments.

We will summarize one of the ten ways to play, the "original" game play:
6 problem cards and 6 solution cards are given out to 2-6 players. You must try to match the cards through its correct multiplicating result, after being able to have two matches in hand, you can lower them for everybody to see. You can use the wild cards as whatever you need. Now we are playing!
On every turn you get a new problem and solution card or you can use the top card of the discarded ones of each.  
Everybody will do the same and now the game shifts into a drafting card game with multiple possibilties to "give problems", be very alert to commutation and holimaths special plays in which strategy of what cards to play or keep, while combining with the wild cards that are on the table, that can be replaced in your turn if you happen to have the card it represents, along with making decisions on who to play your cards to, giving it an interesting pace, along with the alertness, strategy, logical thinking needed to win the game. 
Luck is not essential and even if it takes a couple of rounds for you to have the two matches that allows you to interact, you can turn it around with the correct cards and awareness, and win the game which is won by the player that gains the most points. Each card has a symbol which has to with how much that result is present in the game. There is a symbol score card with how many points each symbol on each card is worth. Value depends of how many times that result is available in the deck. Thus, prime numbers are worth the most. 
It may sound hard, but guys, this is a kids game...this is very easy to understand in a single 20 minute game play. 
The game ends when any player has no cards to play and no unsolved problems. There will be positive and negative points based on the symbols of every card. Simple to add up and to continue with another game or skip to the other 3 strategy games or the other 6 training ways to play HoliMaths X.

From the publisher:

Authored by three of the world’s leading rules designers and Napoleonic historians, Revolution & Empire marks an exciting new approach to grand-tactical miniatures gaming in the Age of the French Revolution and Napoleon (1792-1815). Refight the legendary battles of the period–Castiglione, Rivoli, Marengo, Austerlitz, Friedland, Borodino, Salamanca, Ligny, Waterloo–with this meticulously researched rules manual.

In Revolution & Empire players take the role of the great commanders of the period such as Napoleon, Wellington, Blucher, Charles, Suvorov, Masséna, and Lannes. Draw up your preliminary battle-plans in hopes of achieving your own decisive victory and watch as subordinates carry it to fruition. Revolution & Empire reflects the intense friction of war during this era, as billowing smoke and thunderous cannonades obscured the battlefield and increased the intense confusion and pressures of battle, and even the best plans can go awry by the accidents of war. Skillful management of reserves plays a decisive role in determining the outcome, as fresh troops must be carefully preserved to respond to the inevitable crisis of the battle.

At the tactical level, players performing as division and corps commanders are faced with mastering the interaction of combined arms. No other period of history presents such a balance, as each arm’s strengths and weaknesses are dramatically highlighted. Perhaps as no system before, Revolution & Empire demonstrates the evolution of tactics throughout the entire period, from the demise Frederick the Great’s linear style of warfare to the development of a new tactical system for a new age. With Revolution & Empire, players will also foster a new appreciation for the brilliant tactics of generals like Wellington and Davout, men who won astonishing victories against staggering odds.

Revolution & Empire is also a valuable historical reference, as hundreds of generals and units are rated on their performance during the period in order to reflect their historical capabilities. Comprehensive rules reflect the many salient aspects of Napoleonic and Revolutionary combat, including massed skirmishers, grand batteries, fatigue, savage town fighting, bridge assaults, weather, and ammunition supply. Whether re-fighting the famous battles, or recreating the scores of smaller actions from the period, Revolution & Empire elegantly reflects warfare in this fascinating and turbulent period of military history.

This fast-paced word game will strengthen quick-thinking skills and leave everyone wondering which way is up! Players roll the die to determine a category (such as "Games & Hobbies" or "In the Kitchen") and then draw a card.  The first player to decipher the jumbled letters shouts out the word to win the card.  Letters may need to be read from right to left, or from the bottom up, and mixed fonts and rotated letters add to the challenge!

The player to collect 20 cards first wins.
Includes 540 word cards and 1 large die.

boardgame: Don't fall in the Mead Hall
description:
Not all of the stereotypes about Vikings were true. They were more settlers than they were raiders, for instance. They bathed more than most of their European peers, and were quite fond of jewelry and fancy duds. One thing continues to hold true though. They loved to party, and would often hold feasts in mead halls (community longhouses) that lasted many days. The primary drink at these feasts was mead (honey wine or beer), and competitions were held to see who could hold their drink the longest while remaining standing and spouting poetry eloquently (yes, Vikings were some of the earliest poets).

In Don’t Fall in the Mead Hall, players emulate a host of Vikings drinking the night away in a mead hall. Although drinking while playing is encouraged, this is more of a party game with a fair share of strategy than it is a straight-up drinking game. Each player controls a table that starts with four Vikings (multi-colored pawns). A six-sided die is placed on the table with the number “1” facing up. This is the drunk die or “Odin’s Breathalyzer.” 
 After each player rolls a 20-sided die to determine initiative for the round, they then take turns rolling the action die. The results of the action die vary from taking a drink, to sending a drink to another table, to calling out a “skal” (toast) to cause all of the tables to drink, to throwing a chair at another table, to challenging another Viking at the battle mat. Each drink the Vikings at your table take causes the drunk die to move up by one. Taking a drink while the die is on six will result in a passed out Viking. The player lays that Viking on its side by the table (that Viking is out of the game), then sets the drunk die back to one. 
 Vikings are also out of the game if they lose at the battle mat. Battle is performed by rolling the attack dice. Either one player wins, both players get knocked down, or nothing happens (two blanks are rolled) because the Vikings are too drunk to fight. Additionally, Vikings will move from table to table for various reasons depending on the die roll. Players do not have ultimate control over any of the Vikings but rather control a table. Once all of their Vikings have passed out, been knocked out, or have moved onto another table, they are out of the game. They still have a chance to get back in the game if gameplay results in a Viking being sent back to their empty table. The player controlling the table with the last Viking standing in the mead hall is the winner.

Contains small parts for easy traveling (but not for swallowing!). Bring it with you wherever you go and play it anywhere.

From the publisher:

Hakkaa Paalle is the TSWW game dealing with the winter war. Inside the full sized box you will find:
1. 1680 beautifully die cut counters on 6 sheets, covering the forces involved in the Winter War, as well as Sweden and those forces the Germans considered invading that country with. In addition there are Western Allied Intervention forces and a bonus North Cape battle scenario for you to play.
2. 3 large maps - 1 at approx. 28" x 20" (70cm x 50cm), 1 at approx. 34" x 17" (86cm x 43cm) and one at approx. 21" x 15" (54cm x 38cm) covering Finland, Sweden and their environs in the usual high quality coverage you have come to expect from TSWW. The maps are laminated on the front for strength - and fluid resistance.
3. The very latest in TSWW Rules
4. The updated TSWW Charts - received to such good effect in Singapore!
5. Order of Battle and At Start booklets detailing the various modules included in the game (Hakka Paalle: The Winter War; Sweden 41, 42, 43; The North Cape; Suomossalmi, Karelian Isthmus 40 and possibly a wee surprise or two!)
6. 2 x High Quality D10 Dice.

This game utilised the modified rules presentation first rolled out with Singapore! and is the ideal way to learn the TSWW ground and air systems over time.

The "what if" forces you have come to expect in TSWW games are here in force, the Anglo-French intervention assets, the Germans for 1941, 42, and 43, various Finnish assets as well as the Swedish military for the entire 1939-45 wartime period in this box.

The Winter War as you have never seen it before. DE Ltd. are proud to release HAKKAA PAALLE - True Grit - as our next TSWW operation board war game.

Hakkaa Paalle: The TSWW War game of the 1939-40 Winter War. 15 miles to the hex, operational gaming at its snow bound best!
HP - The Winter War: TSWW's class leading operational board wargame system comes to snow bound Finland. Fight the winter war in all its gritty depths. Sick of that Swedish furniture company? Invade Sweden with the Wehrmacht, meticulously researched based on Bundesarchiv sources (amongst many others)....

Battleaxe was the first really serious British attempt to relieve the beleaguered fortress of Tobruk once Rommel's Afrika Korps had invested it. Operation Battleaxe is both an expansion to Mare Nostrum and a standalone game in the TSWW game series, handling not just the battle itself but a few other things.... read on!

description: The game consists of two decks of cards and, for each player, a timer that acts like a chess or Scrabble clock. One deck is of five letter words, the other of seven letter words. The active player looks at the word at the top of a card that is drawn from the deck of his choice. The rest of the card is hidden. He then either (i) begins naming the word's anagrams that may be hidden on the card, or (ii) says "Strudel" if he believes the word has no anagrams. He tips his timer to its side when he is finished naming anagrams or when he says Strudel and then re-starts it on his next turn. Players advance on a score track for each anagram they name and for each correct Strudel. They are penalized for incorrect Strudels. The winner is the first to the end of the score track or the furthest along when the timers all have run out.

This game is no longer in production.
If replacement pieces are needed, please contact SimplyFun. We'll do our best to replace game pieces as long as they are available in our warehouse.

Players vote for one of three countries that that they think best fits the category showing on the spinner, and are awarded tokens for correct picks.  To win the game, players must be the first with 10 tokens.

Global Ranking draws on a player's memory of facts or ability to logically eliminate countries that do not fit the spinner category to increase odds that a guess will be a successful.

boardgame: Herr Kompositor Card Game: Comporre è un gioco
description:
Description from the publisher:

Herr Kompositor Card Game. A composing game allows you to enjoy the thrill of musical creation even if you don't know notes and chords. In this strategy game, you challenge your opponent, gain chords, complete your song, then listen to it!

AIM OF THE GAME

Each player is a composer and has to compose a song of 16 bars before the opponent. Any direct fight with his/her opponent, if won, gives a player the right to draw one or more cards and win (or lose) one or more bars. The player who finishes all 16 bars first is the winner.

Do you want to write the best love song ever? Or do you want to become the greatest composer in the world? Each time you play, you will compose a new song in AAA, ABA, AAB, ABAA form... You have no limits!

From the publisher:
Arrr!!! is a non-collectible card game in which players compete against each other, taking the role of pirate captains, gaining fame and wealth while facing perils in the Seven Seas.
In order to gain fame and money players must face quests in the shape of Exploration cards. To win these quests player use their Crew, wich tell how many dices to use. Money can be used to "cheat", giving the player 2 more dices. When there are no more Exploration cards, the game end. the player with most fame and money wins.

From the publisher (Italian):
Arrr!!! è un gioco di carte non collezionabile in cui i giocatori competono tra loro vestendo i panni di Capitani Pirata il cui scopo è accumulare fama e ricchezza affrontando insidie nei Sette Mari. Per guadagnare fama e dobloni i giocatori devono affrontare avventure, rappresentate dalle carte Esplorazione. Per fare ciò il giocatore necessita di un equipaggio, il quale determina quanti dadi lanciare. I Dobloni possono essere usati per "barare", dando al giocatore 2 dadi in più da lanciare. Quando non ci sono più carte Esplorazione il gioco finisce e chi ha più fama e ricchezza, è il vincitore

description:
Dinostarkes Laufspiel

A simple children's game with a color die for ages 4 and up, themed to Jim Henson's "Dinosaur Train".

A simple card-based game, where dice and playing-wheel are only used to determine which cards can be placed on the table by all players. First player to place all cards wins.

To Make a Dollar is an economic game that is meant to teach kids about managing their money, making a profit, and the concept of supply and demand. The player is tasked with buying fruit when the price is low and selling when the price is high. Each fruit only has a shelf life of 7 days before it expires so they must decide if they are willing to risk waiting for the perfect selling price or if they need to settle for a smaller profit margin. The player with the most money at the end of the game is the winner.

boardgame: NHL All About Trivia Card Game
description: NHL All About Trivia Card Game is a travel-sized edition of the board game by the same name. It includes cards with 250 trivia questions and is officially endorsed by the NHL.

boardgame: 夏休み大作戦 (Summer Vacation Strategy)
description:
User summary
夏休み大作戦 (Natsuyasumi Daisakusen) translates to English as "Operation Summer Holiday".

Operation Summer Holiday is a bidding game that consists of two stages with different bidding rules. As a schoolkid, you make a picture diary about what you have experienced with your friends during your summer vacation.

The first stage is the First Trimester. You bid for Friend Cards using their Vitality Chips. The second stage is Summer Vacation. You bid for Diary Pages using your Friend Cards.

When the Summer Vacation is over, it is checked to see if you have completed your homework. You can't neglect studying even during vacation. Your Diary Pages constitute your own diary that describes summer events you have experienced, such as going to the beach, camping, fireworks, insect collecting, and most of all, your friends who share the memories.

Each page gives you some victory points, and the player who wrote the most pages gains bonus points. The highest scorer is the winner, who enjoyed the summer vacation most.

Publisher's description
「夏休み大作戦」は前半と後半でルールの異なる競りを行う2段階の競りのゲームです。プレイヤーは友達との夏休みの思い出を絵日記に綴ります。
前半の「一学期フェイズ」では手持ちの「元気チップ」を使って「友達カード」を獲得します。
後半の「夏休みフェイズ」では前半で獲得した「友達カード」を使って「日記カード」を獲得します。
後半の競りが終わると夏休みの宿題を済ませたかチェックが入ります。夏休みを満喫することも大事ですが勉強を疎かにしてはいけません。
海、キャンプ、花火、肝試し、虫捕り、様々な楽しみ……　そして何よりも一緒に過ごした友達との思い出！　たくさんの絵日記を描いたプレイヤーはボーナス点の「はなまるチップ」を獲得します。最も多くの得点を得たプレイヤーが夏休みを最高に楽しんだプレイヤーになります。

User summary
どっちの始末Show (Docchi no Shimatsu Show) translates into English as "Apology Showdown".

About 150 years have passed since the end of Edo period, and with emergence of civilization, Harakiri ended its role as a method of expressing apology. However, Japanese habit of apologizing is still alive over generations, and a new method of apologizing is invented, which is called Shimatsusho (Letter of Apology).

A Shimatsusho is created through a magical ritual in which you inscribe lengthy and complicated magic words onto a sheet of A4-sized paper, in order that you never cause such a disaster again.

Apology Showdown is a card game in which you, as a mid-level manager of a company, hand out a Shimatsusho to your boss. You lose the game if you can't get rid of most critical Shimatsusho. You should report the most urgent Shimatsusho first. With countless troubles, your boss won't accept less urgent Shimatsusho. As a competent business person, you know which one you should hand out first.

Publisher's summary
江戸幕府の終焉から150年。文明開化とともに日本社会からハラキリが姿を消しましたが、謝ることをDNAに刻んでいる日本人はそれに代わる新たな贖罪のメソッドを生み出しました。それが始末書(Shimatsusho)です。これはA4のコピー用紙に超大で複雑な呪文を綴ることで災厄の再発を防ごうとする魔術の一種です。
「どっちの始末Show」は始末書を上司に提出するトリックテイキングゲームです。危険な始末書を最後まで提出せずに抱えていたプレイヤーがゲームに敗北します。
緊急度の高い始末書は早めに報告するのが社会人の嗜み。しかし、トラブルに忙殺される上司は緊急度の低い始末書を容易に受け取ってはくれません。
ならば先に提出しなければならない始末書はどっちなのか……？　優秀な中間管理職であるあなたならきっとそれが分かるはずです！

Each player has a set of four cubes. A card that depicts an arrangement of four cubes is drawn. The first player to match his cubes to the pattern on the card gets the card. The first player to collect three cards wins.

boardgame: Crisis in Eastern Europe: Transnistrian Insurrection
description: This is the Second game planned to come out for our Modern Crisis Line of games. In Transnistrian Insurrection up to four players can take on the roles of the commanders of either; Transnistria/Russia, Moldova/Romania, Belarus, or the Ukraine. They will be fighting for control of the old Soviet sphere of influence as the Moldova/Romania your main goal will be to take out the break away nation of Transnistria before the Russian Army can break through the Ukraine to help them. The game works in phases where each player will switch off and have good opportunities to counter attack and stand there ground when need be. The game also uses an interest damage chit pulling system to simulate the unexpected nature of modern combat. This is a deep war-game that can make you think about the real possibilities of a war like this in the very near future.

Ninety-nine number tiles (eleven each of numbers zero through eight) are placed in Scrabble fashion onto a 13X13 grid. Each player plays from and replenishes a rack of six tiles, placing some or all of those tiles in a straight line so that they intersect or extend an existing line of tiles in the grid. The tiles in each line affected by the play must add up to zero or to a multiple of nine. The player scores a point for each multiple of nine and for each occurrence of zero in the affected lines. Placing on premium squares enhances scoring. The game ends when no tiles are left to draw, and one player has emptied his rack.

Players are archaeologists digging in rural areas and working together to unearth artifacts from different historical periods.

A dig area is formed in the center of the table by creating a tableau of face-down cards in 4 rows of 6 overlapping cards. Only the top card of each row is available for play action. Players are then dealt a hand of action cards that allow them to move cards in the tableau, search the dig field, dig in one column of the field, or transfer cards to the collection site (in a separate area). "No Trespassing" cards will block digging in a particular column and force a player to play a (limited quantity) "Permit" card in order to access any artifacts locked above that card.

The game ends when the 15th artifact or dirt sample card has been moved to the Collection Site or when players are blocked from moving any more cards.  Players count how many complete collections (groups of 3 cards representing the same historical period)they have managed to gather.

Briskars is a skirmish game taking place in the piracy ages. 
Each player builds his band of pirates and chooses among several factions of semi-humans pigs, apes, saurians and more...
The 54 mm miniatures are really beautiful and have to be painted.

Individual games of Briskars are quick and can easily be played in an hour or less , but you can connect them into an ongoing campaign. Many scenarios are given in the rulebook.

description:
An alternative opening for the Campaign Game of 1914, Offensive a Outrance, published in C3i magazine Nr.28. This scenario adds the German "Handstreicht" attempt and subsequent siege of Liège for the Grand Campaign Scenario of 1914: Offensive à Outrance (From the index of C3i # 28).

It contains a 4 page booklet (with additional rules and set-up information), 56 die-cut counters and a Game Turn Track extension. The Battle for Liege can be played as a stand-alone scenario.

Each sheet of a pad shows a 15X15 grid in which some square have been blacked out. The location of the black squares differs from grid to grid. Players take turns marking non-black squares, with the objective to get three in a row diagonally or orthogonally and thereby scoring three points. Subsequently adding a square to the row means scoring the entire row again. play until all the squares are filled in.

Munchkin: Sketch Edition is the same as the Munchkin base game, but all of the cards — and the cover — are art-free so that you can create your own munchkins and make the game look exactly like you want, depending on your artistic abilities, of course...

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Imperial Assault Carriers Expansion Pack
description:
From the publisher:

Coordinate your Star Wars: Armada fleet with the flotilla of Gozanti-class cruisers from the Imperial Assault Carriers Expansion Pack! With just one die in their left, right, and forward battery armaments, and no dice to fire from their rear arcs, your flotilla of Imperial assault carriers will not intimidate your opponent into surrender, but it more than makes up for its minimal firepower with its Fleet Support upgrades, which allow you to coordinate and support your fleet more effectively than ever!

The Imperial Assault Carriers Expansion Pack contains two Gozanti-class cruiser miniatures, two ship cards, eight upgrades, one rules reference card, and all necessary components.

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada –  Rebel Transports Expansion Pack
description:
From the publisher:

The Rebel Transports Expansion Pack introduces a flotilla of GR-75 medium transports to your games of Star Wars: Armada. In addition to its flotilla, which you can field as either a pair of GR-75 Combat Retrofits or GR-75 Medium Transports, the Rebel Transports Expansion Pack introduces eight upgrades, four of which feature the utilitarian Fleet Support icon. Relay commands to your larger ships with a Comms Net, or trail behind your largest ships with Repair Crews to keep them in the fight a bit longer. Clever use of the support your fleet gains from its Rebel transports can easily make the difference between victory or defeat.

The Rebel Transports Expansion Pack contains two GR-75 medium transport miniatures, two ship cards, eight upgrades, one rules reference card, and all necessary components.

boardgame: Iron & Ale: King of the Keg
description:
THE KING OF THE KEG AWAITS!
 The 1st Expansion for Iron & Ale -a Dwarven Drinking Adventure 
 The Dragon has been defeated! The Ancient Dwarven King of the Keg has been awakened. Grow yer beards and Grab yer ale! The King of the Keg awaits!
 New Ancient Relic Cards and King's Card! Fight new Monsters in the Mountain and experience new Meadhall Challenges.

As requested, Blank Meadhall Cards allow you to create your own epic Dwarven Challenges

boardgame: VERBA: Español Expansion 1 – Novice Sentence Pack
description: Expansion 1 - Novice Sentence Pack: This 52 card expansion pack extends VERBA: Español in the elementary classroom byproviding an additional 52 sentences, all containing high frequency verbs and novice constructions -perfect for scaffolding language acquisition among beginning learners.The Español Core set is required for play.

Expansion 2 - Additional Nouns: This 72 noun card expansion pack for the Español version of VERBA adds additional nouns to the core set for expanded gameplay. The Español Core set is required for play.

boardgame: VERBA: Latin Expansion 1 – Ablative Absolute & Indirect Statement
description: Expansion 1 - Ablative Absolute and Indirect Statements: This 50 sentence card expansion pack extends VERBA: Latin into the advanced classroom by providing an additional 50 sentences all containing ablative absolutes and indirect statements. Perfect for scaffolding the advanced clauses found in Caesar and Vergil. The Latin Core set is required for play.

Expansion 2 - Subjunctive Clauses I: This 50 sentence card expansion pack extends VERBA: Latin into the advanced classroom by providing an additional 50 sentences all containing subjunctive clauses; cum clauses, indirect commands, result clauses, and purpose clauses. Perfect for intermediate learners. The Latin Core set is required for play.

boardgame: Stockpile: Investor Card Promo Pack #2 – Moneymaker Mitt and Socialist Sanders
description: The Investor Card Promo Pack contains two new Investor Cards (Moneymaker Mitt and Socialist Sanders) that can be played with Stockpile.

boardgame: VERBA: Latin Expansion 3 – Additional Noun Pack
description: Expansion 3 - Additional Nouns: This 50 noun card expansion pack for the Latin version of VERBA adds additional nouns to the core set for expanded gameplay. The Latin Core set is required for play.

Expansion 4 - Latinitas Recens: This 72 noun card expansion pack for the Latin version of VERBA adds additional nouns to the core set for expanded gameplay. These nouns are all centered around objects fromthe modern world. The Latin Core set is required for play.

VERBA is the new language learning card game developed by The Pericles Group. The concept is incredibly simple -- one player (acting as a judge) draws a random card containing a fill in the blank sentence in the target language and the other players play a card containing a noun from their hand. The player who has played the sentence card then chooses the best card played and that player scores a point. Play continues with that player as the new judge.

All VERBA players benefit from seeing high frequency words, with many repetitions, in a context that fosters authentic learning and comprehensible input. As with all of The Pericles Group's educational games, the play objectives and the learning objectives are one in the same. Players win by making comprehensible sentences, scaffolded by the cards, the images, and the helpful hints included on the sentence cards. We always want to ensure that the learners are seeing correct forms and those forms are continually reinforced by the mechanics.

Core Set: Includes 50 sentence cards and 146 noun cards. Designed for the novice learner. of English as a second language. VERBA: English is also great for younger native speakers of English as they begin to expand their use of language.

From the publisher:

We developed a special cardgame: Passport Quartets! The goal is to win all the passports of your opponents by comparing their power indexes. By this game we want to draw attention to the global power imbalance and the discriminatory practice of visa issuance. Visa applications from people coming from the Global South are rejected above average and mostly on the grounds of the insufficient “intent to return”. Hereby, the visa allocation is used as a way of racist and power-motivated exclusion of people who want to come to Europe for professional or private reasons. The visa allocation manifests the power and privileges of the Global North.

The game includes 28 passport-cards and 6 action cards.

A critical and informative game for the whole family!

--------------------------------------------------------------

Wir haben ein Kartenspiel der besonderen Art entwickelt: das Reisepass-Quartett! Mit dem Ausspielen verschiedener Reisepässe gegeneinander, wollen wir die Problematik des globalen Machtungleichgewichts und der unfairen Visavergabepraxis auf spielerische Art und Weise in die Öffentlichkeit bringen. Visaanträge von Menschen aus Ländern des Globalen Südens werden überdurchschnittlich häufig und meist mit der Begründung angeblich fehlender „Rückkehrbereitschaft“ abgelehnt. Die Visavergabe wird so als Mittel zur rassistischen und machtmotivierten Ausgrenzung von Menschen, die aus beruflichen oder privaten Gründen die Grenze nach Europa überschreiten möchten, benutzt.

Das Reisepass-Quartett klärt auf, führt zu Selbstreflexion und macht vor allem eine Menge Spielspaß mit Freund*innen und Familie!

28 Reisepass-Karten – authentische Spielatmosphäre!
- Frei ausgewählte Länder
- Aufklappbar, handliches Format, doppelseitig bedruckt
- Außen: Abbild des Reisepasses
- Innen: Länderinformationen und Spielwerte
- Eingeteilt in 7 Ländergruppen zur Quartett-Bildung
- Werte aus fünf Kategorien: Macht, Visabewilligungsquote, Bürokratie, Auslandsvertretung und Schutz vor Willkür
- Trümpfe: individuell in bestimmten Duellen, erhöht die Chance auch mit einem „schwachem“ Pass zu gewinnen

6 Aktionskarten – bringen Schwung in die Partie!
- 3 unterschiedliche Aktionskarten je 2mal im Spiel
- Kreativität, Schlagfertigkeit und taktisches Ausspielen der Machtposition im globalen Kontext gefordert
- Beispiel „Rückkehrbereitschaft“: entscheide willkürlich über die Einreise deiner Mitspieler*in in dein Land

Iron & Brass: Duel is a 2 player, 9 card "nano-game" originally created for the 2016 9 card nano game contest.  The game is an infantry skirmish card game.  Each round of the game is a 3 vs 3 infantry encounter.

Each player will have 6 soldiers - 1 on each side of the 3 cards. These soldiers are labeled A, B, C, D, E, and F for clarity. Each soldier (card face) is also named alphabetically to give it more of a human touch.

Each soldier card has 2 actions per side, for a total of 12 actions per player.

All battle resolutions are: 
1) Determine # of dice to roll: Attacker's dice +/- Defender's modifiers
2)Roll the determined number of dice, 1 or more "6" = opponent card side (not the entire card) is defeated. So, in effect, the game will play as if each player has 6 1-sided soldier cards, but in reality, each will have 3 2-sided soldier cards. Hope that makes sense.

The game challenges each player to decide which soldier actions to play, where to play them, and when to attack.

T.E.G. Jr. is a children's adaptation of traditional T.E.G. It has a redesigned map with fewer countries, larger pieces and simplified mechanics.

boardgame: March of the Ants: Minions of the Meadow
description:
In March of the Ants: Minions of the Meadow colonies gain powerful new tools to aid in their conquest of the meadow. Major Workers invite players to explore, expand and battle, Aphid Farms grant majestic rewards, Parasitic Evolutions thwart the best laid plans, Tactics increase the allure and spoils of battle, and the fearsome Predators are drawn to the greatest colonies.

Major Workers - Each Colony can breed Major Workers, larger ants who evolve with your colony. Use their powers to scout the best territory or lead your armies to war.

Aphids - Send your ants to find aphids and form a symbiotic relationship. Herd them to new hexes where they will have space to grow and provide greater harvest.

Tactics - Play Tactics like Feint, Ferocious Expansion and Savage Cannibals to catch your opponent by surprise and turn the tide in battle.

Parasitic Evolutions -  Infect your opponent’s ants and corrupt their most powerful evolutions, forcing them to share their gains with you.

Predators - Summon forth the Centipede Broodmother and the Trapdoor Spider, terrible beasts who roam beyond the meadow.
Kändis is a quiz game about scandals and television tabs, b-celebrities and superstars. A player get points by giving right answers, and first player to a certain amount is the winner.

boardgame: France (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

There are special rules for ferry journeys.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Pax Pamir: Khyber Knives, an expansion for Pax Pamir, introduces new rules concepts and sixty unique cards. Now players can attempt to use their political acumen to secure game-changing capabilities. Imprison your opponent's spies in your dungeon or rely on piracy in the Punjab to fund your ambitions. Battle for influence over the six regional governments or attempt to do your own dynasty building. Players have never had this many routes to dominance. The fight for a new Afghan future has just begun.

Khyber Knives dramatically expands the game-to-game deck variance in Pax Pamir by adding 54 new game cards as well as six Wazir Cards.

description:
“Only remain slaves those who are afraid to die” - Zumbi dos Palmares

In Palmares, 2 to 4 players impersonate local chiefs of the Quilombo dos Palmares, a community of villages formed by runaway black slaves, spread out in the interior of Brazil. It grew so large as to become the biggest symbol of resistance to slavery in the Americas during the XVI and XVII centuries.

Frequent parties were sent to destroy Palmares, but always succumbed to their guerilla tactics. Palmares was only defeated after the largest army to date in the colonies was assembled to vanquish it.

Your job is to try to set another ending to this story. As a village chief, on each of the 8 turns you will secretly allocate your villagers among the 5 possible actions: hunt for food, harvest wood, raid farms, build structures and pray for the Orishas. Then you reveal what you did and compare with the other players, action by action. If you allocated more villagers than the other players, you'll get more benefits.

But beware of the attacking parties that each turn come closer to Palmares. When they attack, all players should be ready to defend collectively, or you may have your village sacked.

"The Question and Answer Game that is played like Bingo"

Game play is a lot like regular Bingo, with the exception that a question is asked prior to covering a space on the Bingo card.  There are 50 questions included in the game. (Example question: What large city is situated on Lake Michigan? The answer is Chicago, which is represented by the Bingo space "I-20".)

Object: Accumulate the most points by spelling words of 3 or more letters using the VWLS Craze cubes during three timed rolls.

Play: Each player in-turn rolls the five VWLS Craze cubes.  Using the consonants rolled, the player mentally adds vowels to form words.  Words are spoken and spelled aloud. Another player writes down each word as it is spelled.  Score is calculated after the time runs out (using a sand timer).

Score: words with 3-letters, one point each
       words with 4-letters, two points each
       words with 5-letters, three points each
       words with 6-letters, five points each
       words with 7-letters (or more), ten points each

Quadtriple Word Game (Two Games in One)

Triple: Each player simultaneously and secretly forms words using 3 randomly selected letter tiles. Words can be any length but must contain all three letters. After the time is up, players in-turn read their word lists aloud. Players cross off words from their lists that match the words being read aloud. Each player scores points for words only he or she wrote down on their word pads. After playing nine rounds, the game is over and the player with the highest score wins.

Quad: Same game as "Triple" only 4 letter tiles are randomly selected instead of three.

In the city of Rockopolis everybody wants to be a rock star, but reaching success is hard.
You've got to recruit members, trying to get the best ones before other bands.
You've got to train in Rehearsal Rooms, buy the best equipment for your rock genre and raise prestige playing in live events.
Don't forget to avoid fights in your band due to different tastes: a Metal guitarist should never play near to a Punk singer.
Be the first to record a Demo CD and win.

Each turn, players choose where to place their pawn: Pub (to search for new band members), Shop (to buy equipment), Rehearsal Rooms (to train and increase the Synergy) or Live concerts (to raise Prestige).
All considering the favourite Rock Genre of the Band Leader and of other Members.
Metal, Glam, Rock-Blues, Punk... Rock has so many faces that you've got to pay attention.

Players will experience all the struggles a beginning rock band have to face.
"It's a long way to the top, if you wanna Rock'n Roll", like someone sings.
Reaching the top is hard, doing it before others is even harder.

boardgame: Rum & Bones: Second Tide – Cutthroats Hero Pack
description:
This is a Kickstarter exclusive set of five Mercenary Heroes:

Captain: Bluebeard
Gunner: Selena
Swashbuckler: Jane Doe
Brute: Lu Bu
Quartermaster: Cierci

Bloodstone Frontier is a tabletop skirmish game using high-quality resin figures that's set in a pioneer-punk universe in which the card-based gameplay mechanisms remove the requirement for tape measures and dice.

Set in the technologically-progressive town of Hope, gangs fight over stashes that supply them with the assets they need to prosper and survive. Generations ago, they were sent to this world to guide the human race to a new future based on intelligence and craft, rather than history and politics, but not much from the past has changed in the future.

The game is played with a standard deck of poker-sized cards that players use to perform actions, make attacks and defend their characters. Although the basic system is simple, few decisions are simple and many have knock-on effects that must be considered.

The King's Shadow, his adviser, confidant and second in command has unexpectedly died. You are racing others to fill that position. 
   Using a worker placement mechanism combined with spatial maneuvering you attempt to gain the most support from the villagers to influence the King's decision on who will his next Shadow.
   You have 3 minions at your disposal. The first part of the game players take turns selecting villagers these minions will initially influence to your side. The second half of the game has the players moving the influenced villagers around in a grid based movement system to gather further influence from surrounding villagers.
   After all the villagers have moved, the player who gained the most influence will become new King's Shadow!

This new, fast paced, Russian roulette themed card game puts players in control of their own fates. This deadly game of chance will not only test your luck, but your nerves as well.

Each round, the dealer deals face-up a card to each player from the deck. If the card is a Click, the player will stay alive and continue in the round, but if it is a Bang!, the player has gotten shot and must leave the round with no points. Each Click card will also contain the number of points won by the player who posses it. The play continues in a clockwise fashion with each player deciding whether they will like to collect their points and exit the round, or risk their lives with a new card for the possibility of getting more points. Once all the players have either chosen to leave or have been shot, the points are tallied, the deck is shuffled, and the next round begins. The first player to 50 points is the winner.

You never thought it would come to this. When the dead rose, you thought that they would be the biggest threat. You were wrong. Your supplies are running low. Medicine and food are nearly gone. The group decided that you will make the next run. You thought that you had prepared for this. You weren’t ready for them but they were ready for you.

District-Z: The Cult is a stand-alone game and does not require the original District-Z to play. This is a Choose Your Own Adventure-style game that will take you from your first steps outside of your small compound and into a harried run from a mysterious Cult that is always on your trail. You will choose your own path. Will you take down the Cult or will you be converted?

The CULT GAME BOOK: This is the story that you must encounter. You will read a page and attempt to overcome the challenges presented. You may be presented with a choice of which test to perform on the card. The choices will be separated by the keyword “OR” and this allows you to choose one of the tests to
complete while ignoring the other test (unless the page states otherwise). Once the test has been performed, perform the appropriate SUCCESS or FAILURE result.

DICE
The dice in District-Z: The Cult are customized with symbols representing each of a SURVIVOR’S ATTRIBUTES as well as the dreaded AUTO-FAIL result. If you roll an AUTO-FAIL result you may not reroll that die during your reroll attempts.

CULT Cards represent the constant threat of the Cult and are used to change the game causing difficulties for the Player via increased challenges, reduction in stats, and even causing you to perform all tasks on a page.

Items and Companion cards represent the Items and Companions that you may come across during the game. These cards are attached to your character mat and you may use their special abilities to assist you in defeating the challenges.

The Survivor mats are where you will track the changes that your Survivor goes through. You will, through the course of the game, be able to increase the basic stats of your character, gain experience, and earn special abilities which are all tracked on this mat.

The game involves the player reading a scenario in the Cult book and then performing one of the tests on the page by rolling the number of dice equal to the abilities in the test. For example: Let's say that you are on a page that has you testing AGILITY and PERCEPTION. You would look at your mat and see that you have 2 PERCEPTION and 3 AGILITY. You would roll 5 dice and try to gain enough of each symbol to match the test's requirement. Let us assume that the test says AGILITY:2 and PERCEPTION:1. With your 5 dice, you would have to roll 2 AGILITY symbols and 1 PERCEPTION symbol. You do get to reroll your dice twice for a total of 3 rolls (unless an ability or card states otherwise) but you may not reroll the AUTO-FAIL dice.

The game ends when the players reach one of the different endings. Will you escape the Cult or will you fall into the cold embrace of death?

boardgame: Onore no Shinjita Michi o Ike!
description:
User summary
己の信じた道を行け! (Onore no Shinjita Michi o Ike!) in English means literally means "Go the way you believe!".

In this game, players explore far deep into a cave to collect gems. There are several forks in the cave. You must choose which way to go, or go back out of the cave. What you encounter depends on your choice, ranging from a gem to even the dreadful dragon. The deeper you explore, the more valuable gems you find, but the risk goes highter too. After three explorations, the player who has collected the greatest total value of gems wins the game.

Publisher's summary
プレイヤーは洞窟を進み、宝石をできるだけたくさん持って帰ることを目指します。洞窟には分かれ道があり、選ぶ道によっては、宝石ではなくドラゴンに出会うこともあります。洞窟の奥へ行けば行くほど価値の高い宝石がありますが、その分危険度も増します。3回洞窟に潜り終わった時に所持している宝石の価値の合計が最も高い人が勝者です。
designers: Monozuki Monoski (ものずきモノスキー)
artists: Monozuki Monoski (ものずきモノスキー)

boardgame: Rum & Bones: Second Tide – League of Extraordinary Pirates Hero Pack
description:

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Flame of the West
description:
From the publisher:

"Time is short. See! The beacons of Gondor are alight, calling for aid. War is kindled. See, there is the fire on Amon Dîn, and flame on Eilenach; and there they go speeding west."
     –Gandalf, The Return of the King

Brave the Paths of the Dead, battle the Corsairs at Pelargir, and stand fast against the Witch-king and the armies of Mordor during the Battle of the Pelennor Fields! The fifth The Lord of the Rings Saga Expansion for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game, The Flame of the West draws directly from the key events of the first half of The Return of the King as it returns our attention to Aragorn, the Grey Company, and the plight of Gondor, even while the fates of Frodo Baggins and the One Ring remain uncertain.

With its 165 new cards, The Flame of the West introduces three new scenarios with a decidedly epic bent. War is kindled, and your actions will determine whether or not Gondor stands. Fortunately, you will not ride alone. The Flame of the West introduces a new version of Aragorn to lead you, as well as two new heroes, two boons, and thirty player cards (three copies each of ten different cards) that represent the strength of the bonds that run between the Men of Gondor, the Riders of Rohan, and the Elves and Dúnedain of the Grey Company.

description:
Earth in the year 3200 is devastated and destroyed in post-apocaliptic environment, erosion and pollution have caused tectonic plates come together creating a single continent called "WOSOM".
4 clans of warriors have survived and have to rebuild their empires, metallurgy is the basis of development but resources are limited and must fight for being the biggest.

Wosom is a card game, where you use resources to build and earn points.
Each clan is led by an emperor (CARD), owners of large golden domes. which you can block a hacker attack.

There are also skilled warriors soldiers to ravage silver and copper mines. You can block with H3 virus.

And finally are androids, robots programmed servers to obtain iron and aluminum. which can be locked with an electrical failure. 
there is an interactive card that has a QR code, reading this code with your phone will get immunity in any of your armies.

The game is won by whoever gets in the end the most points in resources (iron, gold, silver, aluminum, or bronze)

Wosom is a fast game of interactive cards, based on competition and strategy.

From the publisher:
La tierra en el año 3200 se encuentra devastada y destruida en un ambiente post_apocaliptico, la erosión y contaminación han provocado que las placas tectónicas se junten creando un solo continente llamado “WOSOM”.
4 clanes de guerreros han sobrevivido y se disponen a reconstruir sus imperios, la metalurgia es la base del desarrollo pero los recursos son limitados y deben pelear por ser los mas grandes.
Cada clan está dirigido por un emperador, dueños de las grandes bóvedas de oro.
También hay soldados guerreros hábiles para devastar minas de plata y cobre.
Y por último se encuentran los androides, robots servidores programados para la obtención de Hierro y Aluminio.
Ganará quien al final obtenga la mayor cantidad de recursos.

Wosom es un juego rápido de cartas interactivo, basado en competencia y estrategia.

To D Island is a stick-men survival game, where the players (the stick-men) have to face the dangers of the island and escape. It can be played cooperatively, with all the players working together, or competitively, where it’s each player for himself and until the last man standing.

The dangers the players must face are the devious traps meant to hinder them on the way, the Monster trying to devour them and the volcano which will eventually envelop the island. But most importantly, the players have to deal with the Flip, an event which will change the perspective of the island, and move the play to an entirely different map, one where is it a lot more difficult to survive and get away from the Monster.

To help them, the players search through the island looking for items that boost their abilities, movement and overall make their job easier. However, the traps are hidden amongst the same treasures that they need to find items, so event this is a perilous task.

Welcome To D Island!

A 2 player game of strategy and luck. Be the first player to get 3 pieces from your base into your opponent’s base across an ever changing board.
The board is created from 9 cards which may be flipped during the course of the game to open up, or seal off, the paths to players' bases.

The word Kokono is derived from the Japanese word for nine.

Kokono was voted Best 2 Player Game in the 2016 9 Card Nanogame PnP Design Contest

Evil & Good is a two players game where each player represent a divinity ancient God.
In the game the players can see the progress of a civilization from prehistoric to modern times.
This civilization evolutions by automatic system (know by players) and the Gods (players) interact with the fate and future of this civilization using cards.
Each player has a secret objective for humanity's destination. The player who is the first to reach his objective wins! But beware! Playing with the humanity destination could have fatally consecuences and if the civilization collapses both players lose.


Evil & Good es un juego para dos jugadores en el que cada jugador representa un dios divino de la antiguedad.
En el juego los jugadores iran viendo el progreso realizado por una civilizacion desde la epoca prehistorica hasta tiempos modernos.
Esta civilizacion evoluciona mediante un sistema automatico (conocido por los jugadores) y los Dioses (jugadores) interacturan con esta civilizacion para influir en su futuro y destino mediante la utilizacion de cartas.
Cada jugador tiene un objetivo oculto sobre el destino de la humanidad, el primer jugador en completar sus objetivos ganara! Pero cuidado! Tensar demasiado la cuerda puede llegar a hacer que esta se rompa y si la civilizacion collapse ambos jugadores perderan la partida.

boardgame: Runebound (Third Edition): Caught in a Web – Scenario Pack
description:
From the publisher:

The sorceress Ariad once more schemes to conquer Terrinoth with the Caught in a Web Scenario Pack. Now, she commands her arachnid minions to spread insidiously throughout the realm, spinning webs, injecting venom, and preparing for their mistress to cover the world in darkness.

As we mentioned above, Caught in a Web is a Scenario Pack that introduces an entirely new scenario to Runebound. Throughout the course of this scenario , story cards and adventure cards will cause webs to be placed across the map. These webs restrict the heroes’ movement, but they can be destroyed if you’re willing to expend the effort. At the end of the first act, the sorceress Ariad transforms into her spider form and begins to move around the realm. If she places a web in each of the four major cities, all of the heroes lose the game. The only way you can win is by tracking down Ariad and defeating her in battle before another hero can reach her!

Along the way, you’ll find yourself face-to-face with new and hideous monsters or terrifying perils. You may encounter an Arachyura , a spider-like creature that can trap you in its webs and deal lingering damage even after it’s defeated. You may track a nest of spiders to the Smokeblue Hills, or delve deep into tunnels that riddle the ground beneath Terrinoth. Fortunately, a new hero also arrives to save Terrinoth with this expansion: Jonas the Kind. Jonas possesses many unexpected skills learned in a life of crime, but now he uses those same skills to defend the innocent from monsters.

boardgame: Runebound (Third Edition): The Gilded Blade – Adventure Pack
description:
From the developer:
Once a year, the merchants and civilians of Terrinoth come together in a time of celebration and commerce. The most talented craftsmen and the slickest charlatans alike operate under the sign of the Gilded Blade. As new wonders are unveiled and pickpockets work the crowds, even a noble hero can find much to interest him at this gathering.

The Gilded Blade Adventure Pack expands the world of Terrinoth in every game of Runebound with new sets of skill cards and asset cards that can increase your options for any scenario. New goods like Contraband vary in their return based on how good you are at exploring. Meanwhile, you could claim a skill card like Superiority Complex , which grants you a free trophy whenever another hero is defeated! These assets and skills can be swapped for other sets in your asset or skill decks, giving you the chance to customize your hero more than ever before.

Along with skill and asset cards, this Adventure Pack offers a variety of new adventure cards that can be added to any scenario. You may face off against a Highway Gang that attempts to surround and rob you, but thankfully for the heroes, a new hero also joins the game of Runebound in this expansion. Red Scorpion is a solitary wanderer, and her unnatural agility helps her easily outmaneuver her foes. Whether you take on the sorceress Ariad or battle Dragonlord Margath with Red Scorpion, her flurry of blades spells doom for any enemy of Terrinoth.

From Mewtwo to Hello Kitty, the Anime Deck 2 adds 100 new anime-inspired characters and powers to Superfight: The Game of Absurd Arguments. This deck can be played on its own or mixed with any other Superfight deck. So grab your friends, it's time to d-d-d-duel!
This is a set of six cards for Superfight that was released at PAX East 2016. Each swag bag contained 1 random card.

boardgame: Ghostbusters: The Board Game II – Get Real Pack
description:
Containing 23 minis inspired by the classic The Real Ghostbusters cartoon! And that’s not all: The pack comes with Ally Cards that introduce exciting new gameplay elements. The add-on is a CZE-exclusive.

Includes the Ghostbusters team—Peter, Ray, Egon, Winston, and even Janine—with the minis designed to resemble the characters from The Real Ghostbusters (RGB). They can be used as cosmetic swap-outs for the regular Ghostbusters in the game OR they can be used as Allies (a new rule).

Survey the city for paranormal hotspots with this 50mm vehicle. An autogyro that up to two Ghostbusters can ride, the Ecto-2 moves up to eight spaces uninhibited by map obstacles or other Entities. It has a fuel limit, though, and will become unusable if flown too many times.

The add-on pack includes Real Ghostbusters plastic minis that upgrade the cardboard tokens in the game: eight Goo Piles, five Gates, and four Traps. These minis can also double as figure stands for those of you who want to display your paranormal paint jobs.

Lulu Vroumette and his friends decided to make a race up to the house as quickly as possible to party. 
But the road is full of pitfalls ; it will be necessary to take risks by using the big die to go fast either be careful by using the small die. 
On the way, collect daisies which will help you to progress more or less fast in your race, take shortcuts. 
And attention not to be slid in the puddle or in the mud!

Travel Game is a card game that's easy to learn and quick to play!

From 2 to 5 tenacious travelers try to organize more trips than their friends to win the game. The type of journey (as well as the means of transport used) changes both the conditions of the departures and the assignment of victory points. In addition, a variant included in the rules provides a small memory component that makes the game competitive even for the greatest players.

Legends of the Colosseum is a gladiatorial miniature skirmish game for 2-3 players. You will be pit against other players in strategically meaningful combat rather than simply throwing dice at one another. Make a poor defensive decision and you could watch your best fighter be executed for the crowd's amusement; call an opponent's bluff and deal unbelievable damage as punishment!

Each quarter our fair city accepts four applicants to become part of our citizenry. These applicants will be given all the same connections and resources to prove to the leaders of our city that they are worthy to become one with the populous. Five hundred members of a random workforce and the ability to produce goods and services from different aspects of business. It is their goal to be as profitable as possible and succeed in creating a workforce that is focused solely on profit. There will be troubles along the way, from political insiders, government interference, and those seeking social justice. Our city is one of peace and profit driven by money and power. Are you worthy to be part of our community?
Welcome... to Capitalism USA.

Players are all billionaire industrialists that are taking on a new business in Capitalism USA, seeking residence, it is your goal to obtain the most money before one player reaches a completely their full potential of Profit and Progress. Each player is given five Workforce cards, these are laid in front of each player face up. displaying a human workforce residing in Capitalism, USA. As the game progresses they will become an automated workforce that is more profitable for you the billionaire.

Each player is dealt FIVE Workforce cards, these will represent your workers of your new company. Place them face up, showing the occupation, in front of you for the remainder of the game.

The game ends once a player has flipped all FIVE Workforce cards to Profit and Progress. The player with the most money is the winner.

A challenging maze game that combines elements of a maze and connect four.

Each player gets 9 chips. Insert your chips in the maze wheel. Turn the wheel to strategically move your chips through the maze wheel. Maneuver the maze wheel to get your chips in the slots. Get three chips in a row to win the game.

A push-your-luck dexterity game with cards. 
As a chef at TOSS pancake house, you flip pancakes in front of the customers whilst entertaining them with your frying pan skills. Get enough positive feedback and you could win the coveted 'Employee of the Month' trophy.

Each pancake has a different action/skill to perform. The more cards you decide to draw, the more actions you'll have to perform at the same time, but the greater the reward should you succeed in tossing the stack.

The cards feature frying pans, pancakes with different toppings, score tracker and trophy.
You'll also need 2 to 4 small cubes or similar (1 colour per player) to track scores.

TOSS was voted Best Dexterity Game in the 2016 9 Card Nanogame PnP Design Contest

description:
A quick historical family game for all ages.

The Exodus Of Israel is one of the most well known and preserved events of ancient times, being found in many sources, including the Old Testament writings acknowledged by the Islamic, Judaic, and Christian faiths. It follows the story of the tribes of Jacob, having relocated to Egypt during a severe famine. Initially welcomed due to the favor earned by Jacob's son Joseph, generations later forced into slavery by a Pharaoh who no longer remembered or cared for the growing nation of outsiders. The God of Jacob (re-named Israel before his death) hears the plight of the nation, and delivers them out of the hand of Pharaoh through a series of plagues which break down the hardened will of Pharaoh as well as show His power in the eyes of the region. Led by the prophet Moses, the Israelites are granted their freedom, and immediately make their migration (Exodus) back to the land promised them by God. The journey was not a pleasant one. Being in the desert with stressed resources, it often led to complaining, and sometimes outright rebellion against Moses and God Himself despite leading and providing for them.

This game is for 2-4 players, and at essence is a roll-and move game suitable for children. However there are also several elements of choice involved, and anyone with an interest in the Exodus story would enjoy following the path of the Israelites and being re-acquainted with the joys and pitfalls experienced along the way. The game can accommodate non-readers and readers alike, and has been designed to be playable independent of direct parental supervision. The winner of the game manages to make the journey from Egypt to the Promised Land with the most amount of points.

boardgame: Watchmen of Destiny: Bloody Mercenaries Expansion
description: They are savage, dangerous and covered in blood, but if you give them their money, you are on the safe side. Mercenaries are totally unique new cards that are different from the watchmen. Mercenaries can help you economically, they can harm your opponent and they activate global events! This expansion brings you a different strategic element and it offers new options. It also spices up and dramatizes a 2-player game. (Artwork is not suitable for kids and those who don't have stomach for it.)

They’re pretty useless on their own, but when combined with other cards, they can actually be pretty useful! Who knew?

An Intern may copy any one ability from any card in your hand. This can be a standard ability or a reactive ability and would be played as if it were written on the Intern card.

The search for brains has backfired! Employees are dropping like flies and taking their co-workers with them. Beware.

A Corporate Zombie has no ability, and can be played to the break room with no effect. If a Corporate Zombie is fired, it takes two more employees to the Unemployment Line with it.
5 Kickstarter stretch goal cards for You're Fired!. Includes 2 alternate Bosses, 2 additional Consultants, and a very special Company President card that comes in and fires everyone in sight.

An italian party card game, where you impersonate a journalist who wants to make a career to become the new head chief of the redaction OrgolioNerd ("NerdProud") and overthrows the current evil director Rinoceronte ("Rhino").

You can accumulate ego points in three different ways: with good manners, bad manners or appealing to your nerd culture. 
You can write news articles, or steal that from your opponents, each method is permissible for the win!

From the back of the box (Italian):

Grazie ad OrgoglioNerd: Area Stampa potrete vivere l'ebrezza di far parte della redazione nerdescha più nota d'Italia!

Quali redattori di OrgoglioNerd avrete 3 modi per chiudere i vostri articoli prima che vengano caricati su uno dei portali di nerdinformazione più cliccati di sempre: con le Buone, con le Cattive oppure semplicemente facendo appello alla vostra profonda cultura da fanboy di fumetti & co. Nessuno dei 3 modi di fare può definirsi migliore degli altri, bisogna sapersi adattare per soddisfare i capricci dell'incontentabile caporedattore, il Rinoceronte. Riuscire a soddisfarlo non sarà per niente facile e pezzo dopo pezzo accumulerete un Ego tale da farvi pensare che sia possibile scalzarlo dalla sua ingombrante posizione per reclamarne il posto... Ci riuscirete? Beh, in bocca al Rinoceronte!

OrgoglioNerd: Area Stampa è un gioco di carte in cui vestirete i panni di uno dei redattori del portale. Ciascuno di voi sarà caratterizzato da un potere unico legato alla vostra particolare personalità da nerd che durante il gioco potrete sfruttare per interagire con i vostri avversari. Per vincere dovrete riuscire a conquistare 3 punti Ego, ottenuti chiudendo gli articoli che il mazzo di carte al centro del tavolo vi proporrà di volta in volta, oppure fregando il pezzo ai vostri avversari. Entrambi i metodi sono più che leciti, starà a voi risolverli con le Buone, con le Cattive o a Modo vostro. Una volta raggiunto il terzo Ego otterrete il titolo di Rinoceronte, ma per vincere la partita dovrete dimostrare di esserne degni sfidando i vostri avversari e abbattendoli uno ad uno a colpi di mood. Il nuovo Rinoceronte sarà colui che riuscirà a dimostrarsi degno del titolo di predatore ultimo e di leader indiscusso della redazione!

Prodotto realizzato in collaborazione con la redazione di OrgoglioNerd.

Elementi del gioco:
55 carte
6 segnalini Ego
1 regolamento

Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface.
Each player has a flight of fighters, represented by two-sided counters. Aircraft counters move by means of curved “performance rulers” that represent the aircraft’s historical cornering ability and maximum speed.
Each performance ruler is unique to each aircraft, and contains all the speed, cornering, and weapon information needed.
TAC is designed for portability;  The entire game can fit in a plastic bag. Even the largest battles can take place on a fast-food restaurant table.

Each aircraft is sold separately. This set contains the Hurricane I, which was used in the Battle of Britain.

boardgame: Moomin's Card Game: Nyoro Nyoro Biri-Biri
description:
This is a Japanese press-your-luck game based on characters created by Finnish children's author, Tove Jansson. Each player is trying to collect the most Hattifatteners, mysterious, ghost-like creatures who grow out of the ground and are attracted to electricity.

The deck in the middle of the play area represents the mountain the Hattifatteners congregate toward, drawn by the lightning at the top. Each player begins with three plastic Hattifatteners. On their turn, each player will draw cards until they meet one of three conditions:

1) Collect 12 Hattifatteners represented on Nyoro Nyoro cards.
2) Draw a Biri-Biri card, causing them to discard all cards in their play area.
3) Choose to stop drawing, and pass to the next player.

Some cards allow you to take Hattifatteners from the field, or from other players; some cards cause you to lose Hattifatteners to the field. When the draw deck is exhausted, the player with the most Hattifatteners wins.

Achieve political dominance of known space!
Discover planets, aim the rockets, send envoys, protect trade routes, and meet political objectives in this medium weight strategy game.

Gain influence among the galaxy’s races

     Discover planets and visit them to build relations with the natives. Rivals might travel to distant planets with you, but only dominant factions increase reputation.
     First impressions matter, but rival factions can steal a planet’s allegiance.
     Patrol planets’ shipping routes to gain even more respect.
     React to changing goals. As political climates change, respond with flexibility to ensure your prestige among the peoples of the galaxy.


Key features

     A blend of worker placement, tile placement, and area majority
     Changing goals and special actions keep game play fresh
     Unique worker deployment mechanism
     Scales well from 2-6 players
     Moderate player interaction
