description: Our New Bosses expansion features 5 new Dungeon Cards to expand the game board further. New Mini Bosses means a new way to win and a boss prize that can give players another advantage. This expansion also features an completely new Underwater level with artwork not seen in any of the original dungeon cards.

The greatest warriors of history have been assembled for the ultimate battle to determine their legacy.

Warrior Clash is a capture and elimination strategy game that pits greatest warriors in history against each other. Players will draft teams of warriors, defend their land, collect resources with the goal of conquering their enemies. These battles are played atop modular gameboards which create hundreds of possible areas.

Each warrior has a different arrangement of trait scores between the categories of Grit, Agility, Ingenuity and Strength. As warriors are moved throughout the gameboard, the players will attempt to rely on relative strengths and exploit weaknesses of their enemies. When matching sides of two opponent warriors are aligned, they can do battle. The trait scores determine the handicap of the fight, but rolls of the dice determine the victor.

Will your team be the last one standing?

Together players grow the JellyBeanstalk and help Jack climb it, collecting their favourite flavour of Jelly Bean along the way. Be careful not to upset Jack's balance however, or he, and the beanstalk, will come tumbling down. And then there's the problem of that Giant.

The beanstalk is built using illustrated cards and cubes/dice. A player who causes the beanstalk to tumble is out of the game. Once the JellyBeanstalk is fully grown, the player who has collected the most Jelly Beans of their chosen flavour wins.

The game also has team co-op and solo play variants. The rules have been produced as a single sheet as well as a card-sized story book version.

JellyBeanstalk was placed 1st in the Most Innovative Mechanic category in the 
2016 One Page PnP Contest.

The Superbox is the ultimate storage solution for the discerning SUPERFIGHT collector. With space for over 1800 cards, you’ll be able to fit all of our current decks plus four expansions that don’t exist yet! The Superbox also comes with twenty exclusive “Super Cards” to spice up your game, twenty double-sided score keeping chits for when you’re feeling competitive, and six blank deck boxes to stop your cards from sliding around like peasants.

Meeple Maker is a 2-4 player light strategy game that plays in about 30-60 minutes.

Your goal is to get the most fans. To get fans interested, you'll produce games tailored to their demands. To meet those demands, you'll develop content with help from the internet.

Players take turns and either gain or spend content.

To gain content, they choose which website to move to from one of the next three clockwise websites (skipping over other players) and develop the content shown there (resources). Since each player's token never leaves the board and movement is limited, each move impacts the choices of others.

Players spend that content to grow their company, produce games, recruit people and get fans. All of these aspects are represented together on the cards in their hand or in the common market. These cards and their position determine the type and number of content that needs to be spent, as well as the skills (resource-gathering) and fans (score) the cards offer.

Those cards join their tableau, growing your company and helping players develop immersive worlds (theme), deep mechanics (strategy), valuable components, and wondrous artwork (the four types of content).

As players grow their companies to new sizes, they'll choose and flip tokens on the board. This changes how much resources are available and also acts as the game clock. When the last (eighth) token changes, the final round is played out. During final scoring, players gain resources based on who they recruited, and then spend them to acquire the cards in their hand. The end-game conditions are checked to see who has the most theme, strategy, component and artwork skills in their company and who gets the bonus points. The player with the highest score, i.e. most fans, wins.

The setup is highly variable, with resources being randomly placed on the board, different values for the end game conditions, and a constantly evolving, sliding market.  There are also several creators to choose from, an evolving board and many ways to manipulate the game - so each game is a new challenge to face.

Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface.  All you need are some six-sided dice and some coins to provide weight for the aircraft counters.  Everything else can be printed from this ruleset. 
Each player has a flight of four fighters, represented by two counters.  Aircraft counters move by means of curved “performance rulers” that represent the aircraft’s historical cornering ability and maximum speed.
Each performance ruler is unique to each aircraft, and contains all the speed, cornering, and weapon information needed.
TAC is designed for portability;  The entire game can fit in a plastic bag.  Even the largest battles can take place on a fast-food restaurant table.
Each aircraft is sold separately.  Only pay for the ones you want, and print out as many copies as you need to create your own epic WWII air battle.
This set contains the Spitfire II, which was used in the Battle of Britain.
family: History: World War II (Battle of Britain)

description:
User summary
Eat or Eaten is a two-player abstract game with a theme of hares struggling for feeding ground. On a 5x6 square grid board, players have five hare tiles each. Their goal is to capture four of their opponent's hares or to occupy their opponent's burrow for one turn. There are three types of hares, which determine their movement directions allowed, and each type has a flip side that alters its direction. You may flip your tile by consuming one turn, or moving into a special square.

This intellectual game is suitable for those who began to enjoy thinking.

There are two editions of the game. The original Version was produced for the Spring 2015 Tokyo Game Market (TGM). Each TGM, designers are invited to face a challenge, to design a game under certain conditions. This is where the ¥500 microgames came from, which included Love Letter. The Spring 2015 TGM challenge was '2種カードゲー企画' which was to bring games using only two types of cards. The first edition of Eat Or Eaten was produced to satisfy the 2種カードゲー企画 challenge. The components were only cards and the field was only 3 x 4 square. The Hares behaved differently between editions too. In the first edition, when a Hare was flipped over, it changed the power relationship with opponents' Hares, leading to a fourway stand-off. In the second edition, produced for the Autumn 2015 TGM, the Hares have equal power, but flipping a Hare changes the way it moves. In both Versions, the Hares are competing for food.

Publisher's summary
うさぎの餌場争いをテーマにした、二人用のアブストラクトゲームです。５匹のうさぎを使って争い、相手の巣穴マスに１ターン留まるか、相手のうさぎを１匹にすると勝利します。駒は裏返すことによって、動き方が変化します。頭をしっかりと使うゲームで、考えることが楽しくなってきた方におすすめです。
designers: Monozuki Monoski (ものずきモノスキー)
artists: Monozuki Monoski (ものずきモノスキー)

description:
Rustle: the Card Game is a card drawing farm animal themed game where the goal is to possess the most points by the end of the game. There are two decks - the Animal Deck and the Action Deck.

The Animal Deck contains only cards that give you points. The Action Deck contains cards that let you draw more Animal cards, steal Animal cards from other players or remove their Animal cards from the game.

Players start with two animal cards in their farm (in front of them) and four action cards in their hand. Each turn players draw an Animal card and an Action card. The Animal card is played in front of them straight away. Players then choose to play one of their Action cards or discard one.

Points are earned through collecting farm animals which are all worth different points. Game play ends when no more cards can be drawn from the Animal Deck.

This mini-expansion for Superfight: The Game of Absurd Arguments features 9 characters and 9 attributes from the minds of Geek and Sundry. Shuffle these cards into your deck to destroy your friends as Felicia and Wil, or arm yourself with magic missiles and awkward silences. Huzzah!

boardgame: Castle Panic: Tower Promo 2016 Tabletop Day
description:
Replaces one of the six standard tower sections with a piece of the same size but with the Tabletop Day 2016 logo on the front side and on the back, the instructions:

"Rebuild this Tower once by returning 3 slain Trolls to the Forest"

Each Expansion expands the game and focuses on a specific aspect of the war. We have designed the game from the start to include all the rules you need to use all the expansions!

Expansion box includes 56 cards.

This expansion features American hostile forces, more Action cards, more Weapons, more Equipment, more Skills, and more Mission, Objectives, and Locations!

Cards included are:
1x Location Card
7x Action Cards
2x Weapon Cards
4x Equipment Cards
6x Skill Cards
16x US Hostile - Frontline Cards
20x US Hostile - Elite Cards
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Each Expansion expands the game and focuses on a specific aspect of the war. We have designed the game from the start to include all the rules you need to use all the expansions!

Expansion box includes 56 cards.

This expansion features United Kingdom Soldiers and hostile forces, and UK Weapons!

6x PC Soldier Cards
5x NPC Soldier Cards
6x Squad Soldier Cards
7x Weapon Cards
15x UK Hostile - Frontline Cards
17x UK Hostile - Elite Cards
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Wooden game of the Maxis line, specially designed with big pieces, easy to grab, to allow kids and elderlies to play together.

Six up consists of a vertical wooden piece with six vertical tracks made of holes. Each player has six wooden tokens (red and blue). In turn, each player rolls a die and chooses a token to go up one of the tracks. If a token lands in a hole already occupied, it ejects the previous token. If a token reaches the top of its track no one can go up this track no more. Game ends when all tops are reached. In the case of a tie, count the highest positions of tokens that have not reached the top.

The game is a deck of cards, each with an adjective and three questions related to that adjective. The instructions include i) a Headbanz-style game in which a player tries to guess the word that is pasted to his forehead as the other players act it out, ii) an Apples to Apples-style game in which a player guesses which of the other players played which adjectives that describe him, and iii) a Balderdash-style game in which the guesser guesses which players wrote which answers to one of the questions on the card.

Imagine a world in which our technologic enhancements yield the powerful diesel-driven machines of the early 20th century. A world in which the discovery of an alien resource has changed the industrial evolution forever. Where the military arsenals have never been used to incite a world war, but for the very survival of mankind. It is the year 1948 and the world as we know it has ceased to exist. Welcome to Xibalba, where the remains of civilization fight for their very survival in an inter-apocalyptic dieselpunk world.

In the Xibalba - Generals Expansion you got to buy a general with paragon instead of regular ressources, that gives you a unique and permanent ability to manipulate the war for the ark to your favor.

Game features:

     Added four new headquarter player boards
     Added a direct player-interaction with raiding
     Added generals, powerfull entities bought with paragon
     Added elite soldiers as a powerfull household guard of your general

A reader reads a statement from a card, and the remaining players have one minute to debate it. Each debater then plays a card that indicates i) whether he believes the reader will agree or disagree with the statement and ii) the number of points (1,2,or 3) that the debater is willing to risk on his guess. Each correct debater keeps his card with its points. The next player becomes the new reader. The first player to 21 points wins.

A map expansion for Dorasure. This time with 2 dragons to fight, and additional terrain types.

The expansion comes with:

     A large expansion map board
     Rules sheet covering new additions to the game rules
     Reference sheet
     2 glass counters for tracking the green and black dragon meters
     A punchboard of additional tiles.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Caviar is an abstract strategy game played on 8x8 grid with each of the corner spaces missing. This results in the first and last rows having only 6 spaces instead of the usual 8. Each player has 6 tiles numbered 1-6 which are placed on the first and last row in numbered order.

On a player's turn, he rolls 6 dice to determine which pieces may move and by how many spaces. To begin with, pieces may only move sideways, forward, or forward diagonally, but may not move backwards.

If a piece is adjacent to an opponent's piece, it can be jumped in a Checkers fashion and removed from the game. Once a piece reaches the opponent's starting row, it becomes a king and can now move backwards.

The first player to eliminate all of his opponent's pieces from the game is the Winner.

The players have to move two cars from their garage at the corners of the game board around the track and back to their garage. Movement is determined by a die. Two traffic lights are operated by the roll of a 3 or a 5. Two event spaces (breakdown and restaurant/stadion) hinder the movement of the cars. The first player to return his two cars to his garage wins the game.

Similar to Rummy 500, you are trying to complete a goal and get rid of the cards in your hand to end the round. The game consists of five cards that must be completed in order.

Once you complete a goal, during the next round you work on your next goal while the other players are still trying to complete the previous one.

Goals:
1. Set of any 3 strawberries plus any 2 spinach
2. Set of any 3 tomatoes plus any 2 kiwi
3. Run of 5 in any suit (fruit or vegetable)
4. 2 runs of 3 in any 2 suits
5. Any 5 of one suit plus two 3's

Winner of the game is the first to complete all five goals in order.

The AufklärerLäufer KV 47 (r) "Otto" is a new addition to Axis forces on the Eastern front. After the sixth liberation of Kiev, they captured a whole battalion of brand new SSU KV-47 before these could even enter battle.
The “Otto” has been refitted with latest-generation Panzerfaust and Grenade Launchers. It can now destroy soft and hard targets with ease.

The Deluxe Edition models are not just “pre-painted”. What we offer is the exact replica of Vincent Fontaine’s work, Dust Studio’s Master Painter.

There’s not much money to be made on Mars, but there’s plenty to be made getting people there.

The population of Mars is just under 2,000 people ‐ mostly scientists, some entrepreneurs, and a handful of very wealthy tourists. And this population is growing every week.

Hauling Astronauts (or Hauling A’, for short) is a game that pits two space transport companies against each other as they try to make the most money transporting astronauts to Mars.

Players roll dice with the pips representing astronauts, then place the die one by one on a shared grid. By arranging the dice in a way that matches their Transport Ships ("5 or less", "6 - 10", "even", "odd", etc.), players attempt to maximize their profits. Additional bonuses can be obtained by delivering their passengers to destination docks that also have defined ranges ("3", "4 - 7", etc.).

Hauling Astronauts was recognized with the following awards from the PnP Game Design Awards 2016:
- Runner up "Best Art & Design"
- Runner up "Best Small Build"
- Honorable Mention "Most Innovative"

Welcome to the fastest growing suburb in the country. You and your opponents are cast as very irresponsible real-estate developers charged with the task of building houses, shops and factories to make quick money before larger interests notice the community.

New Jack City is a city-building game where players place tiles that contain residential, commercial, or industrial properties. Players must place tiles by matching the roads and construct them using workers known as builders. Builders start counting down when the property is connected to the electric grid. Constructing large properties offers bonuses which allow you to outpace all other players. To win a player must earn more money than all other players by constructing buildings before the city's’ electricity grid becomes maxed out.

A historical military strategy game set in the era of the Crusades. 2-4 players compete to occupy and rule the cities and castles of the eastern shore of the Mediterranean Sea. 
After setting up the armies, players take turns in moving their different units (infantry, cavalry, archers, sultans and kings, to name a few), on the map, across cities, castles and lands. 
Battles occur when two players occupy the same area. Battle and Strategy cards help add some strategy to this dice-based game.
As they occupy and rule these castles, cities and/or lands, players collect cards and accumulate points. Points are used to purchase new units or pay ransom for captured units in battle. 
The player with the most city and castle cards wins, after a predetermined number of rounds has been played.

Most Incorruptible Patriot is the sequel to Most Glorious Comrade. You are playing as notable democratic Cold War leaders vying for Voters. Whoever reaches 10 million first wins! This is an easy to pick up draw one play one style game.

Description from the publisher:

You're no longer looting and shooting on your own with Cash 'n Guns: Team Spirit. This expansion features rules for team play and enables a ninth player to take part in the action. It also introduces a deck of duplicitous mercenaries: twelve thugs-for-hire who can give your team a temporary advantage and who bring a deeper strategic dimension to the team game.

Seven new characters take seats at the table, including Boris, Natasha, the martini-wielding Maria, and the most awe-inspiring of all, Mama. They bring new surprises and powers with them, as well as a silencer that fits on your favorite weapon and three never-before-fired guns.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
シュレディンガー勇者 (Schrödinger Hero) is a partnership game from Japan published in Japanese with English rules.

Players belong to either the Hero's side or Satan's side, and their objective is to defeat the leader of the opposite side. However, no player knows any player's identity, including themselves, and their identities often change.

Each player is dealt one identity card: Hero, Satan, Hero's friend, or Satan's servant. During your turn, you can gain a clue by looking at another player's card or by exchanging your card with other player's. When a player looks at another player's card, that player's comment or expression can also be a clue.

Only the Hero can defeat Satan, so even if you know who Satan is, you cannot defeat Satan until you have the Hero card, and vice versa. Unlike Werewolf, this game doesn't require a moderator, and players aren't eliminated during the game.

Publisher's summary
プレイヤーは勇者陣営か魔王陣営に所属し、勇者陣営は魔王を倒すことを、魔王陣営は勇者を倒すことを目的としています。しかし、最初は誰も自分の正体を知りません。プレイヤーは自分の手番に、他人の正体を見る、もしくは他人と正体を入れ替える事が出来るため、それらの行動を活用し自分と周囲の正体を把握していきます。正体を見た際の表情や言動もヒントになることでしょう。
夜の襲撃フェイズや人狼同士の顔合わせもないため、ゲームマスターが不要ですぐに遊ぶことが出来ます。プレイヤーの途中脱落もありません。

boardgame: Boomtown Bandits: Sneakiest Bastard Promo Card
description:
Boomtown Bandits, celebrating game copies now lootable from store shelves in Spring of 2016, introduced this new bonus card, "Sneakiest Bastard", and branded shot glass promo. The bonus card provides a mid-game reward, claimed by the first player to win a shootout without a fight and call it. The card is worth $15 (points) at the end of the game.

--
The shot glass has no game effect, although filling it and emptying it repeatedly may alter dice rolling accuracy.

Email events AT breakinggames DOT com with your address to get your copy, while supplies last.

Bloody Broadsides – is a fast paced Napoleonic Naval game which allows you to Captain one or more massive ships of the line from the Age of Sail.  Most games can be played in 3 hours.  This game is intended to play with miniature ships on a large hex mat.  These rules were written specifically for Portsmouth Miniatures and Games 1:900 scale ships but may be used with any miniatures.

Games may be as small as one on one Captain Duels, up to massive 60 ships or more historical battles like the Battle of Trafalgar.  There is a point system to allow you to design your own scenarios.

Features:

Plotted Movement
Simultaneous Combat
Point System
Crew Grade
Morale Checks
Ship Classes (1st rate through 4th rate)
Critical Hits (including Ship Explodes)
Raking
Grappling
Capturing Ships
Drifting

From the publisher:

Earthos is a strategy-based board game with features rarely, if ever seen in most board games today. Your objective in the game is to defeat your opponents by increasing technology, making strategic invasions and occupying enemy planets. Other strategic aspects of the game include rotating planets and moons, as well as random wormholes which allow units to move randomly. Politics can also play a pivotal role in match-ups with more than two players.

One last important highlight is the fact that each and every game can be a new experience. With the ability to change the shape of the board, pair up for team battles, and do much more, every game should be packed with fun.

Chesscalation is a two player strategy game based off of Chess and Shogi. It's designed to avoid analysis paralysis and avalanching by favoring the defender and easing the pressure of each move - all while still keeping the strategic depth of Chess. It also has a bunch of fun unique pieces. The main difference from Chess is that players can chose to upgrade pieces or even put new pieces on the board.

There is a risk versus reward mechanic as higher value pieces limit the player's ability to upgrade and make new units. On the flip side, losing a high value piece may actually be an opportunity in disguise. It frees up the player to be able to place new pieces, which makes recovering easy. Also since you can place pieces anywhere on your own side, you can really easily block a checkmate. The increased complexity introduced by upgrading and placing new units makes it essentially becomes impossible to predict your opponent more than a few moves in advance. Each move is important, but not crushingly so. Each player feels like they have a chance to win up until the very end.

There are two modes of play. The default mode plays the most like Chess. In it, the goal is simply to force a checkmate on the opponent. When pieces are captured, they are removed from play, so each player only has a set amount of pieces to work with. In the other mode, players earn points for each piece they capture. Captured pieces go back into the player's pool. The goal of the game is to capture 100 points worth of their opponent's pieces without losing their King.

This is a Micro-game with 18 cards some cubes and some dices!

In the game each player control their pack of robotic miners and send them to planet to work and back with resources ( The Victory Points ).

This game is a simple and eficient Area Control, Action Select and Dice Selection in a small package.

Each round consists of two phases. The first is to roll and select, the first in turn rolls the dice and select 3 of them for you and passes the rest to the next player. In the second phase the players used the dice selected to prepare actions in the mother ship and / or send your miners to planets paying the landing value so that the miner can land on the planet that will mining until there is the scoring event that calls the miners back to the player.

				
				
					Game Components
     18 Cards
     13 six-faced dices
     24 cubes ( 6 of each color )

OfficeDungeon is a card RPG for 2 to 4 players taking a humorous poke at the stereotypical office environment. Choose one of four Heroes and collect Promotion Points to crawl your way to the promotion you've always wanted. But be careful! Your co-workers and Bosses will want to knock you down a peg.

The main goal of the game is to have the most Promotion Points (PP) after either the defeat of the required number of Bosses or when the deck has been played through once. PP has another purpose, however: it is also player's currency, allowing them to purchase equipment, items, and companion creatures to boost their stats and stand a chance against whatever stands in their way.

Players choose their Hero Card at the beginning of the game, each representing an entry level position in the Office. From there, they encounter creatures, loot, and the dreaded Instant cards as they work their way through the 90-card deck. A simple, stat-based combat system and the ability to make your own shady backroom deals between players facilitate quick, competitive play.

Inspired on Lewis Carrol's 'Alice in Wonderland'.

Players have to build roads using hexagonal tiles, move their figures across them and interact with the scenes and characters from the book in order to complete their goals.

boardgame: Birds of a Feather: Undomesticated Owl Bear Promo
description:
Promo card from 2016 Table Top Day.

The Undomesticated Owl Bear has an appetite for eggs, but is easily confused. Try to spot this mysterious creature!

Setup: Place this card to the side. Right now, no one controls the Owl Bear.

Each Round: After revealing cards, count the egg cards played this round. If zero or two or more players played an egg card, the Owl Bear is confused and doesn't move.
If one player played an egg card, and she didn't already control the Owl Bear, she gains control of it! She can immediately mark one uncommon spot from any habitat. Place this card in front of her.

boardgame: Five Tribes: The Thieves of Naqala
description:
Naqala is now a prosperous place. Gaining the favors of the different tribes was not easy, and your rivals have not been discouraged by your success. In fact, some tribes have now abandoned your cause and rallied to your rivals instead, and you'll soon discover that these tribes follow influential leaders that your rivals hired against you. Every man has his price, though, so perhaps you can return the favor to your rivals — should you have what it takes to recruit the thieves of Naqala.

Five Tribes: The Thieves of Naqala is a mini-expansion of six thief cards and one new djinn for Five Tribes that introduces a new element to the base game to create a real thorn in your opponents' sides. The djinn is shuffled into the deck with the other djinns and protects you from the effects of thieves. One thief card is revealed at random at the start of the game, and whenever someone would buy a djinn, they can purchase the thief card for the same price as the djinn. Each thief is associated with one of the tribes, and whenever you take an action with that tribe, you can choose to activate and discard the thief. If you do, everyone else must get rid of something — two resource cards, one tile they control, even a djinn or palace — after which you get to choose to keep something from all the discarded things.

Introduction
				
				
					It’s the Wimbledon finals, and you’re facing off against an opponent that’s every bit your equal. Will you play your best, smartest game? Know when to play aggressively? Keep momentum and use your entire bag of tricks to win the match? Big Time Tennis is the only board game that plays like real tennis. You’ll experience long volleys, dribblers off the net, service aces, fatigue-- and hit great lobs and other strokes. You’ll even be able to challenge the referee’s calls!

Object of the Game
				
				
					======Use your skills, wits and a little luck to win the match and become the champion of Wimbledon!

Components
				
				
					1 Game Board
  1 7-Point Tiebreaker board
  1 Scoreboard
  1 10-sided Die
16 Action cards (2 sets)
48 Momentum cards
  8 Player markers (4 gold, 4 silver)
 1 Tennis Ball token
 1 Momentum marker (double-sided)
 1 Advantage marker
 1 At-the-Net token

(I only rate my own games so they'll appear in user searches.)

boardgame: Xia: Embers of a Forsaken Star
description:
This is the first expansion for the game Xia: Legends of a Drift System.

In its meandering across the galaxy, the drift system of Xia has come into orbit with a dying star. Once habitable worlds now sit desolate waiting to be ransacked by opportunists, and an unknown cold fills the void. New dangers, hidden treasure, and adventure await in this abandoned system. Uncover the mystery in: Embers of a Forsaken Star

This expansion adds:

     New Sector tiles
     New Mission cards
     New Ships
     A solo campaign
     And much more!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Game: Xia: Legends of a Drift System

description: Just like the classic game play of Connect 4, but instead of a flat game grid, it is a 5 layer tower - and each layer twists.  Players take turns dropping a piece in the tower and then MAY twist any layer exactly one space. By doing so, at least 1 piece typically drops to a different layer adding an extra level of skill to the game. First player to get 4-in-a-row wins.

Hoshi is a playable hero with a miniature and card for Arcadia Quest and Arcadia Quest: Inferno. She is a kick-starter exclusive character gotten by having backed Arcadia Quest: Inferno and additionally having made the optional purchase for the Arcadia Quest: Fire Dragon expansion.

Conquer one Enemy card held by another player before someone conquers yours (2-4 players).

1)Shuffle 9 Enemy cards and give two to each player (3 if a two player game). Each player places his enemy cards face up in front of him. Remove leftover Enemy cards from the game. Shuffle the rest of the cards and deal 5 to each player. The rest become the draw pile.

2) A player begins his turn by discarding as many cards from his hand as he wishes and places them face down near the draw pile in a discard pile. He takes the same number of cards from the draw pile, always having 5 cards.

3) In time the player tries to collect any 5 that form a set listed on the Enemy cards. Divine Intervention is wild but can only be used once and never on Satan.

4) A soon as a player discards and draws, the next player takes his turn. If a player assembles a victory combination, he places those on the proper Enemy card (owned by an opponent) and declares "The battle is the Lord's" and is the winner.

Players race their vintage rocket ships through a random and mostly unexplored galaxy, encountering asteroids, UFOs, wormholes and more as they collect Star bucks to buy fuel and rocket parts. The first one to collect 3 Flags is the winner.

A deck of space cards is drawn dealt out in a conveyor belt system that creates the board as you play. Players must ration their money and fuel cards to avoid calamity. Ship upgrades can be purchased to give them an edge over their opponents. The game is random enough to be challenging without being dependent on the whim of dice.

A light racing and hand management game intended to fill a sweet spot between Sorry! and Merchants of Venus.

boardgame: I See What You Did There
description:
I See What You Did There is a game about convincing your friends that you're clever. Convince them by relating the cards using any means of interpretation necessary.

Every card has a word on the top half and a game instruction on the bottom half.  Players either use the abilities detailed on the bottom half card (ex: While this card is in play, players must repeat the last word of their sentence sentence) or try to connect 3 of their cards together (ex: Penis, Kindness, STD).   Successfully convincing your fellow players that 3 of your cards are connected earns you a stack; first player to 3 stacks wins.

I See What You Did There is NOT responsible for any shenanigans you and your friends get into. (Including prank calls or murder)

Rally On is a rally racing tabletop game that uses a risk and reward dice system to play out events during each rally stage.

Rally On has been a dream of mine for the last five years. I have always been an avid rally and all around race fan. I started to design a game based on what I wanted a table racing game to be and something that I had never played before. Once, I got together with Ben M, the game really started to pick up steam. He brought the same passion I had for making a fun and exciting table top rally game. When we decided to bring on randomly laid out stages every time you play, dice driven events and a driver confidence system really brought the project to life.

Playing a different track varies the game dramatically. Never again will you have to have to play the same board over and over again or are you forced to spend money to buy a new track, just to add variety. The confidence system allows the player to push their luck with a smaller risk of an event happening, but the catch is that you only have a limited number of confidence points to start the entire rally with. The points are also carried over stage to stage, so you have to be careful not to use all yours up at the beginning or you won't have any left for later stages. This really helps to determine how hard you want to attack a turn or how fast you want to push your driver. You also get penalties for running out of confidence, so that is always on your mind. There are various ways to regain confidence points and also many ways to lose them. During Event rolls you might gain more back by taking a turn perfectly or lose a point for botching it.  At the end of each Stage the players each get four driver points to spend to repair drivetrain or add confidence points. You can also use those to buy more Pace cards. Pace cards are various cards that the players can use to benefit themselves or play against their opponents. There are six, six sided dice that the players use to play out events throughout Rally On. Each die side has a unique symbol that corresponds to an event that could happen to player. Some are good and some are bad. Depending on how hard you are pushing your driver determines how many dice you will be rolling for the event. You could come out a head and gain a few extra movement spaces, shave some time off your current run, damage your car, lose time or wreck. Don't forget you always can use your precious confidence points to re-roll an event die if needed.

The goal of Rally On is to complete each stage of the rally with the least amount of time ticks. It is meant to be played as a three stage rally, but you can do less. At the start of each turn the player decides if they will up shift or downshift their gear. Once that is determined they roll the corresponding gear die. They then move that number of spaces on the stage. Each time the player comes up to a turn tile they have to decide which lane to take the turn in. Each lane has a maximum gear the player can enter in. The player has the option to attack the turn in the inside lane or outside lane. The inside lane is usually safer, but slower. The outside lane will be faster but adds a higher chance of rolling a bad event, due to rolling a higher number of event dice. This makes the outside lane a higher risk and you could potentially take damage, slide, spin off track, lose time or even gain confidence for a perfect turn. At the end of each stage the players tally their time. The player with the least amount of time wins that current stage.

In a war torn land, you find yourself trying to start over, but war seems to follow you.

The world has been all but destroyed by war. You are the leader of your people, looking for untouched land that hasn't been destroyed by war. After a long journey you find this new land and you start to build a new home for your people. Other Leaders have the same idea, and the new land breaks out in war.

War Torn is a resource management game with Drafting and Building of your new city. Each player starts with a limited amount of resources to start their new home. Craft your City to match your play style, and be the first player to gain enough Victory Points to claim the land as your own.

Play as unique characters like a Lava Ape - The Fire King, or a Necromancer Crow that raises the dead. In this 2-4 player game you will find yourself building, fighting and deceiving other players to claim the victory.

Billy and Mandy really want to set up a garden in their backyard. The problem is Billy wants a garden of carrots and Mandy wants a garden of Broccoli. The only way a garden can grow is if all of the plants group together. Otherwise, the weather, animals and other gardeners can destroy the garden. In this war over the garden, there is no compromise, no surrender. You either win or become rabbit food.

This game uses the Looney Pyramids, some dice and a checkerboard to recreate the sense of total victory of your crop surviving while others fail.

Included with the Kickstarter shipment of Pyramid Arcade as one of the twelve bonus Looney Pyramid cards created for $333 level backers.

Number of Players: 2 or 4

Equipment: Timer (optional), 8x8 board, a rainbow stash per player (up to four), a Pyramid die and a Color die

Who Starts: The player who has spent the most time in a garden goes first. If it is a tie, rock-paper-scissors to see who goes first.

Setup: For a two player game, set up the a chessboard with one red trio and one yellow trio on one corner of the board just off the side of the board (they represent the carrots) & one green trio and one blue trio on the opposite corner of the board just off the side of the board (they represent the broccoli). Place the remaining red, yellow, green, and blue pyramids off the board at a neutral corner.

On the diagonal of the board in between the two players, form a line of random black pyramids. One pyramid per space. There should be an empty square at the corners of the board. The black pyramids represent the garden wall. For a four player game, set up the chessboard with each color at a corner just off the board. Form an x of black pyramids in the middle of the board. One pyramid per space. There should be an empty square at the corners of the board.

Each of the open spaces represents a part of the backyard. Place two of the same color trios off each side of the board near the opening of each of the x sections, touching the edge. The remaining trios should be accessible to the other players off the side of the board.

Gameplay: The first player rolls the Color and the Pyramid die. The player selects the pyramid based on the color and size rolled on the dice. If the dice roll shows two pieces, select one of the two pieces. If the dice roll shows the atom (*), select any color. You may place that pyramid any square on the board that does not have a pyramid already. This represents planting the seed in the garden.

If you roll black, you may move one of the black pyramids from the wall that matches the pyramid from the other dice. The black pyramid can only be moved one orthogonal space and must be no more than two squares away from the other black pyramids.

If your roll matches a combination that is already on the board, you may move one of the pyramids one square orthogonally on the board. If there is already a pyramid on that square, a player can not move into that square. If the pyramid was moved by the previous player, it can not be moved this turn.

Winning: During a two player game, the player wins when they have ten of their pyramids in their corner of the chessboard. All of the pieces must be orthogonally touching with one of the pieces in the corner of the board. In addition, all of the pieces must be on the same side of the wall. If a piece in on the wall line, it does not count as being on your side of the wall.

During a four player game, the player wins when they have ten of their pyramids in their side of the yard. All of the pieces must be orthogonally touching. In addition, all of the pieces must be on the same side of the wall. If a piece in on the wall line, it does not count as being on your side of the wall.

Scratch is a dexterity/skill game with a hint of strategy, for 1 to 5 players.

The aim of the game is to score points by getting your fleas to jump onto Scratch the dog. You do this by skimming cards through the air to try to land on Scratch's card.

The fleas each have different abilities which can affect the points scored and there are other card types which also effect the game.

All the rules are on the cards themselves.

From the Cards Against Humanity Website:

There's nothing nerds love more than branded references that they can consume. Remember Batman? What about Firefly? Bazinga!

24 white & 6 black cards about video games, D&D, Game of Thrones, and all the other bullshit you like.
    
Contains cards previously released as part of sets at PAX East & PAX Prime in 2013 & 2014.
    
Artist's Edition wrapper by Jessica Paoli.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: Draw Blood: Remix is exactly that - a remix of the Original Draw Blood, which was a Rummy-style card game. This revisit to the Draw Blood concept, brings back discarded elements from the earlier versions of the game, while also creating a completely new game with cards already familiar to Draw Blood fans. The result is a fast-paced game where players draw and discard their way to an empty hand, laying down a variety of Monsters that can hinder your opponent’s progress. Strategic hand management, card awareness and fun ways to stop Monsters (and your opponent) are the essence of Draw Blood: Remix.

boardgame: Frostgrave: The Hunt for the Golem
description:
From the publisher:

The Hunt for the Golem is a mini-campaign for Frostgrave: Fantasy Wargames in the Frozen City.

Over the course of three scenarios, the wizards and their warbands will learn about the Granite Golem, a unique monster rampaging through the ruins. They will then go on the hunt for the Golem, first to study it, then in an effort to capture or destroy it.

They will also have a chance to find some unique new treasures.

From the publisher:

Sellsword is a digital-only mini-expansion for Frostgrave: Fantasy Wargames in the Frozen City. This expansion gives wizards the option to hire a captain, a special type of soldier, to help lead their warbands. Unlike other soldiers in the game, captains may choose their own weapons, have a few special abilities known as 'tricks of the trade', and gain experience. Of course, such experience and versatility comes at a price, so captains are going to want more than the retainer given to most soldiers – they want a cut of the treasure! Also included in this expansion are three new scenarios designed to give soldiers more of a chance to shine. All feature some type of limitation or obstacle to spellcasting that will force wizards to think a bit more about how much they rely on their magic.

From the publisher:

Dark Alchemy is an ebook only mini-expansion for Frostgrave that includes a three-scenario campaign which can be played by either solo by one player or by two players working co-operatively. It also includes an expanded potions table, with numerous new magical elixirs, as well as new rules for the buying, selling, and creation of potions.

boardgame: Poor Bloody Infantry: Somme – Campaign Expansion
description:
From the publisher:

Poor Bloody Infantry: Campaign Expansion provides more scenarios, more rules, more counters, and more ways to enjoy the standard PBI system.  Fully compatible with PBI: Somme.  In fact, you must own PBI: Somme to use this expansion.  Here's what's included...

You get an eight-page illustrated rule booklet that contains seven additional historical scenarios; two new counter sheets with more men, more markers, and two new counter types (Captains and Shell Holes); a Reference Card; and additional optional rules (such as Heavy Mud, Variable Reshuffling, and new rules for shell holes, among others).  There is also a grand campaign game, utilizing 14 of the scenarios linked together, as well as a Company Game (play a single scenario four times, one per platoon in your company).  There are enough individual soldiers--using men from this expansion, along with those from PBI: Somme--to form an entire company of 180 individual soldiers (with men left to spare).  Finally, notes about British Army organization in 1916, and a section devoted to Battle Plans and Combat Details provide you with background information to help bring the game (and history) alive.

With this expansion, you will have scenarios covering each of the fourteen British divisions involved on the first day of the Big Push.  To quote from the Designer's Notes: "After years of contemplating this offensive, I still cannot come to grips with this, largely, citizen army making such a frontal assault into the face of an entrenched defender possessing modern weaponry.  If PBI helps bring the magnitude of the staggering losses of July 1st home to you, it will have achieved one of my design goals."

Designer Summary

Treehouse Dreams is a card game about imagining a giant, impossible treehouse built together by the people playing the game. Each card represents a room or feature in this treehouse, and they all contain elements of who you are and the connections between you and the other players. The game is designed to ask simple questions that can reveal the deeper (or sometimes funnier) elements of who we are.

Play with people you already know, but want to know better.

User summary of play
Depending on the length of game desired, 10-20 cards are dealt among the players and everyone arranges them into a central 'tree' shape.

Taking turns, players flip cards - revealing new rooms in the treehouse and questions for the current player or all to answer. This is a storytelling game without objective win conditions.; play continues until all the players agree that the treehouse is done or all cards on the table have been revealed and answered.

User summary
タコツボ (Takotsubo!!), themed on sea creatures, is an abstract game similar to Chess, Checkers and Shogi. You win the game by catching your opponent's Octopus piece or moving your Octopus into the trap pot (Takotsubo). Like checkers, catch your opponent's pieces by jumping over them. Try to come up with a clever tactic to lure and catch your opponent's Octopus.

This game was originally designed with its illustration in 1996 by Kohei Higashi, a former Japanese Chess champion and Shogi journalist, when he was 60. After twenty years, in 2016, it was reissued with the artwork by Maiko Fujita. The re-issue is called タコツボ 復刻版, meaning "Takotsubo!! Reprint".

Publisher's summary
海の生き物たちによる、チェス・チェッカー・将棋に似たアブストラクトゲーム。相手のタコをつかまえるか、自分のタコがタコツボに入れば勝ち。生き物はチェッカーのように飛び越せるときは必ず相手を飛び越してつかまえなくてはいけない。うまくおびきよせて、タコをつかまえるには？
1996年、チェス元日本代表で、将棋記者の東公平が日本に将棋やチェスの楽しさを伝えられるように60歳のときに考案・イラストデザインしたゲーム。20年後の2016年に、ふじたまいこのアートワークでリニューアル出版した。"

Are you ready for a pop-culture trivia game where you have to think up the questions, your opponent provides the answer, and we rely on the Internet to settle the bet?

The handheld 69-card deck features the question prompts from popular categories like movies, TV, sports, corporations, famous people, places, and things.

You versus me, let's go.

If I played the card "US Politicians, current age," and said to you, "Donald Trump, 67 and a half," would you say "Over" or "Under"?  Once answered, use your phone to look it up and settle the bet.

Switch roles, play to 9.  That simple!

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Heroes of the Resistance Expansion Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

Rey, Finn, Poe Dameron, and the heroes of The Force Awakens fight for freedom with the Heroes of the Resistance Expansion Pack for Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game. Featuring new sculpts for the Millennium Falcon and Poe Dameron’s black-and-orange T-70 X-wing, Heroes of the Resistance also expands your fleet with three unique X-wing pilots, as well as new versions of Han Solo and Chewbacca. Meanwhile, the pack’s eighteen upgrades introduce new elite pilot talents, Tech upgrades, and unique Title upgrades for both the Millennium Falcon and Black One.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description:
The circus world is full of glamour, magic, and... intrigue. Who is the best, who is the most talented?

Each player gets pair of performers and must bring both of them performers into the circus tent, avoiding the
wicked magician, so they can perform first.

Player in his turn rolls die and move accordingly avoiding the magician. Sometimes He may choose paths - shorter or longer but with ability to find useful items against magician.

The first player who reaches into the circus tent is the winner

Players are princesses who want to charm the Prince.
For this they must create the perfect love potion by collecting the most secret ingredients with the same color in the player's cauldron, while spoiling the potions of the other players with differently colored ingredients.

Players roll die and may choose and put ingredient with the same color to the cauldron with the same color so that other players can´t see the color of this ingredient. If someone thinks the ingredient is incorrect She may cry out: Charming. If You were correct You may put any of Your ingredient to the cauldron of this player. If You lied She may put any of Her ingredient to Your cauldron.

Player who runs out of ingredients may announce that the love potion is ready.  The player with the most ingredients matching the color of her cauldron wins.

From the publisher´s website:

Which fairy tale hero would you like to be? The Dragon or the Princess? Lets make it a surprise instead! 
Pick a card and let the magic begin! Only the others can see who you have become, don't even try to look into the mirror! Your task is to ask clever questions and guess who you are based on the answers of the other players.

In order to guess who or what he/she turned into (what creature the card depicts) the first player has to ask questions from the other players but they can only answer with “Yes” or “No”. 
For example: 
- Am I human? Boy? Girl? Etc... The well known fairy tales can help you in coming up with the questions. If the first player successfully guesses who or what he/she has been turned into, he/she can look at the card and keep it and it is the next players turn. If the first player is unable to guess the card, you should set it aside. Before you start the game you should agree upon how many times you want to change (how many cards one player can pick during the game) and the player with the most cards at the end of the game wins!

From the publisher:

Once upon a time in a far away kingdom, there lived a beautiful princess. One day an evil witch, out of shear jealousy, bewitched her with a deep and empty slumber. The witch ripped apart the princess' magical necklace and scattered its forty pearls in the garden. 
You have to gather the forty pearls of the necklace before the last grain of sand falls in hourglass.

Player rolls die in Her turn and either pick a rolled color and place to the necklace or pick a hourglass card if die shows the hourglass.
Players win if they find all 40 pearls.
Players lose if the all the hourglass cards are drawn.

Be a fan of one of the colored contestants on the game board! You can also guess who will be the first to swim across the pool first!

On your turn, roll the color die and move the swimmer with the same color one step further. If one of the contestants acrosses the pool the game ends. Who guesses the winner wins also the game.

Pick three birds of the same color and place them on the game board! We marked their places with three colored tiles for each
color. That is where you start!

The player who can get all three of his birds to the princess by rolling die and moving before the other players, wins the game. The remaining players continue until all the birds reached the goal.

It´s raining and players race to home and can only move on from hideout to hideout so they don’t get soaked.
The youngest player goes first, then take turns clockwise! When
it’s your turn, throw the die and step forward onto the colored field shown on the die. If you threw pink, then step on the next field with a pink mushroom, if you threw green, step on the next field with a leaf, if you threw blue, go to the next field with a rock! These little drawings show where you’ve hidden from the rain. If you’ve reached the tree stump cottage at the end of the path first You win the game.

Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface.
Each player has a flight of fighters, represented by two-sided counters. Aircraft counters move by means of curved “performance rulers” that represent the aircraft’s historical cornering ability and maximum speed.
Each performance ruler is unique to each aircraft, and contains all the speed, cornering, and weapon information needed.
TAC is designed for portability. The entire game can fit in a plastic bag. Even the largest battles can take place on a fast-food restaurant table.

This set contains the Ju-88, which was used in the Battle of Britain and the remainder of World War II.
family: History: World War II (Battle of Britain)

This set contains the Ki-84 Hayate. Known as "Frank" to the allies, the Ki-84 design was a departure from the agile and delicate Zero. The Frank was less maneuverable, but was much faster and had pilot armour and self-sealing fuel tanks.

This small expansion introduces 6 "envoy" cards, each of which provides a special power.  Similar to Alien cards in the base game, these cards are acquired by assigning a particular die to the card. However, unlike the Alien cards, the envoys provide a recurring power (once per turn) to the owning player.  The envoy cards stay with a player until another player takes it away (by assigning the appropriate die). The 6 different cards are marked with the "blue" die, numbers 1-6, so players may only take these cards by assigning the blue die of the appropriate value.

boardgame: The Institute for Magical Arts: Ethereal Powers Expansion
description: This 12 card pack provides 12 unique Institute cards for use with the base game. The new cards provide a variety of powers, some of which affect the Portal and/or the Ethereal Realm -- in contrast to the base game. In addition, there is a recommended rule change regarding the game ending condition; players may now win the game by having 3 more character cards than his/her opponent.

This 5-card expansion pack to Capo dei Capi includes 5 "apprentices".  At the start of a turn, a player chooses one of the 5 cards to use for that turn. Each card offers a unique power.  After the turn, the player returns the card (face-down) to the supply. Once all 5 cards have been used, they are turned face-up and may again be chosen by the player taking a turn.

Big Bad Overlord is a card and dice game for 3-6 people in which players assume the roles of evil Dukes vying to take over the world. Players gather minions great and small, ranging from Riff-Raff to Horrible Monsters, battle each other, and fend off attacks by occasional do-gooders (such as adventuring bands and small children). The game takes about an hour to play.

The goal is to eliminate all other players and become the Big Bad Overlord. Players start with six cards, half of which they can keep face-down. The face-up cards serve as the players' armies and can be used to attack other players. Each turn, players draw a new card and add it to their army. (Unless they draw a do-gooder, in which case the player has to fight it off!) There are three combat abilities - Mind control, Magic, and Melee. Most cards have at least one ability, and some have more. A successful Mind-control attack allows you to capture an enemy card; magic blows things up (especially cards weak against magic, such as the otherwise powerful Machines-O-Death); and melee allows you to chop things up.

Players fight each other using all available attacks, rolling 2 six-sided dice to determine the winner of each battle. Die rolls are modified according to the relative attack and defense strengths of each side's army in each combat category. But even the most lopsided of battles isn't in vain - a natural 12 always wins, no matter what the modifiers, and a natural 2 always fails.

In addition to the evil minions and do-gooders, the deck contains two magical artifacts. They're important because players need them to survive the endgame. When all the cards are drawn from the deck, only players who have an artifact survive. At that point, if two players are still in the game, there's a final battle in which the evil Dukes use the artifacts to smash each other's troops.

Players can gang up on each other, help each other defend against attack, backstab each other, and more. Defeat your foes, play them against each other, hoard a precious artifact or obtain one at all costs - Big Bad Overlord is a game where it's good to be bad.

Typically given away at conventions as a set, these promotional pieces for Stratego Legends add two new units with unique abilities to the game. Each piece comes with a game piece sticker and a rules reference sticker.


    207 Celams (10) - Dragon - light army - Valorians (gold)


Ability: Reveal Celams when a piece flies over her to destroy the piece that flew over Celams.
Action: Flying. Reveal Celams to jump her over any number of pieces or water spaces vertically or horizontally. Celams must land on the first empty space when flying.


    208 Noslrac (10) - Mutant - dark army - Qa'ans (purple)


Ability: Reveal Noslrac and an adjacent Mutant to give that Mutant +2 until the end of turn. Use this ability only once per turn.
Death Curse: When this Death Curse ends, chosen opponent destroys 5 pieces under his control simultaneously. Then choose and destroy 3 pieces under your control simultaneously.

boardgame: Dark Souls: The Board Game – Darkroot Expansion
description:
The Darkroot is a lush but foreboding region of Dark Souls™. Scarecrows, giant stone warriors, and the very plants of the forest stand sentinel to hinder any heroes foolish enough to brave this realm. Darkroot is the final resting place of Artorias, a deadly revenant and relentless swordsman who is guarded eternally by his companion, the Great Grey Wolf Sif.

The Darkroot Expansion is intended for use with Dark Souls™: The Board Game.

New Dark Souls™: The Board Game adventures await in the Darkroot Expansion, with new enemies, new encounter cards utilising those enemies, and new treasure cards for characters to discover along their adventures. Components for two new main boss encounters further enhance players’ options and provide fresh challenges.

The CopyCat is a word-based, hidden-identity psychology card game. Players secretly assigned the CopyCat role must pretend they know the word or phrase that everyone else carefully describes based on hints and descriptions, while the other players try to identify and eliminate the CopyCats!

At the beginning of the game, players are assigned the roles of Human or CopyCat randomly without knowing each other's identity (minority CopyCat). A Topic Card will be randomly selected for each game. The Topic Card consists of a Topic and Hints. The Humans will be able to see the Topic while the CopyCats will only be able to see the Hints. After everyone have seen the Topic or Hints, players will take turns describing the Topic (or what CopyCats think may be close to the Topic) using a word or phrase. The Humans will have to give good descriptions to prove their allegiance, but vague enough as to not give CopyCats too much information. The CopyCats will have to give clever descriptions by combining the Hints with what they've heard in order to blend in with the Humans and prevent their elimination.

The Humans must eliminate the CopyCats before 2 rounds to win, or else the CopyCats win!

Things can get pretty heated, and there is limitless potential for creativity in how to strategize.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Arthur et la Vengeance de Maltazard: le Jeu de Course
description:
Help Arthur, Betameche, Selenia and Max reach the passage room as fast as possible ! The future of the Minimoys is in your hands !

Playing in turns, roll a direction and a speed dice to move your vehicle as fast as possible while avoiding Maltazard's soldiers and collecting bonus cards to help you be the first to reach the end of the race.

Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface.
Each player has a flight of fighters, represented by two-sided counters. Aircraft counters move by means of curved “performance rulers” that represent the aircraft’s historical cornering ability and maximum speed.

This set contains the Ju-87B Stuka, a German dive-bomber which was used in the Battle of Britain.
family: History: World War II (Battle of Britain)

description:
Detective Bullshit is a game for 3-5 players you win by making shit up.

Think of Clue meets Cards Against Humanity, sort of. It's a competitive storytelling game centered around a fictional criminal case. Unlike Clue, there's no right answer, but like Cards Against Humanity, the fun is in getting your friends to pick your ridiculous story.

For each case, one player is Chief Bullshit and other players are detectives.

The Chief draws a victim card and decides the location of the crime. It's then up to the other detectives to play suspect cards from their hands and convince the Chief that their suspect is guilty. But things get complicated in the 3 rounds of each case as additional evidence, complications, and bribery keep even the best bullshitters on their toes.

—description from the publisher

A mini expansion of six cards, released exclusively to backers of the spiele-offensive crowdfunding campaign.

This contains three Planned Work cards, and three Order cards.
A mini expansion for Lignum that consists of 1 wooden disk in each player's color and a special rules supplement. This expansion allows for more accurate planning.

From the publisher:

Flint and Feather is a 28mm, individual figure, tabletop skirmish game system of tribal warfare between the First Nations of the Great Lakes region in the legendary Pre-Contact era. Unleash your Great Warrior and assemble your Warband to raid and plunder your opponent and grow your Orenda.

Attack and Defense Card System: This unique combat system gives you the ability to choose the attack or defense maneuver of your key characters providing a critical decision point in the game during each and every melee you may fight.

Medicine Roll Initiative System: This creative initiative system allows interesting aspects of the Native American Indian mythology to be added into the game play and provides the game with more of a feel for the Legendary Pre-Contact period.

Orenda: This word is the Iroquoian name for the spiritual power inherent in people, animals and the environment. The rich Iroquoian mythology introduces aspects that allow us to creatively add magic, spells and spirit creatures to the game. Of course the wargame purist does not have to add these rules to the game if your inclination is for standard combat.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
New York Bop is small scale skirmish game created by Ghetto Miniatures where you command a gang of local hoodlums looking to make a name for themselves in 1970’s New York.

Suitable for 2 or more players you will each take turns at activating your gang members to perform a number of actions. You will test your brawn against your enemies in a one on one fighting or will your gang up on the weaker enemies to take you on the path to glory.

The gang characters are represented by a detailed range of 28mm gang miniatures that each have a baseball style stats cards. All the gangs and their members have special attributes that will set them apart from their rivals who they want to get the better of.

A standard game of New York Bop is played with 8 gang members on a 3’ x 3’ surface. Games are fast paced and exciting and you can have a number of enjoyable games in one evening between friends.

Gang Warlords take control and can issue command orders to their street soldiers. You can tell your gang member to cause havoc in the streets by vandalising shops, breaking into cars and spray painting your gang logo on enemy turf in the ultimate 'Up Yours'.

Event cards in the rules add an element of unpredictability to the game as you never know when or what cards your rival is going to mess up your plans.

The rules will contain full campaign rules and map, which will allow you to fight over of New York’s Boroughs of Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens and The Bronx. You will start the campaign with a small gang and a number of turfs with the aim to expand your territory to increase your income and gang size.

As you battle through the campaign you will be able to expand your gang and upgrade member’s ranks and weapons with the income from the local shops protection money.

The poleis were the city-states of ancient Greece. Each state was like a political entity ruled by their own citizens. Your goal is to form the greatest Greek alliance, build wonders and be favored by deities.

COMPONENTS:

There are three types of cards:

POLIS CARDS, the backbone of the game, which provide resources, some abilities, Victory Points, trade routes, deity worshiping.

WONDERS CARDS, which give Victory Points. These require certain resources and once build they belong to that player.

DEITIES CARDS, give Victory Points. These require faith, they belong to the player that worships a certain deity the most - during turns these can switch among players.

GOAL:

The goal of the game is to win the most victory points.

SETUP:
The deities and wonders cards are placed face up in the middle of the table. The starting player will get two polis cards, the rest will form the draw deck.

GAMEPLAY:

During a round, a player, must draw until he has three cards in his hand. Then must choose one card and place it in front of him - once doing that he will acquire faith and resources symbols.

He then checks if he meets any deity or wonder requirements.

The round ends with the cards passed to the next player.

END OF GAME:

The game ends when all players have five polis cards in front of them, in a row.

SCORING:

Players add the Victory Points from Wonder Cards, Deities Cards, the longest trade route and any extra victory points from the polis cards.

According to the publisher, this educational card game is "designed to improve vocabularies, improve spelling and provide profitable entertainment". The game consists of a pack of 72 cards, each of which bears a single letter of the alphabet in large print. The deck has between one and six cards of any given letter.

Six cards are dealt to each player. The dealer begins by playing one of his cards and announcing a word starting with that letter. Subsequent players must play the next letter in a word on the board if they can, or start a new word if they cannot. They then draw a card from the deck to replace the one played.

After each player has had a chance to play on a word that has been announced, subsequent players may change the announced word to any other that word that includes in order the letters already played.

Players get points for completing words, ranging from 5 points for a 2-letter word to a maximum of 1,000 points for a 10-letter word. Each time a player takes a trick by completing a word, he may also "declare" another word for which he has all the necessary letters in his hand. He scores for that word as well, and then ends his turn as usual by drawing a card. Winner is first to 2,000 points.

boardgame: Spanish Civil War Battles: Belchite & Teruel-Alfambra
description:
Spanish Civil War Battles: Belchite & Teruel-Alfambra is an Operational level simulation of the battles of Belchite (August-September 1937) and Teruel (December 1937-February 1938). The game uses an adaptation of the Fire & Movement system.

Each hex represents one kilometer. Each turn represents two days. Units are brigades and regiments of 3-4,000 men, and battalions of 400-800 men depending on type. Tank units represent Battalions or groups of companies of 30-40 tanks or armored cars. Support markers represent artillery battalions or squadrons of assault and bomber planes.

World at War #62 August 2018

boardgame: Slaughterville: The House on Haunted Hill Expansion
description: For a long time no one was even aware that this place existed. This place is one filled with horror and mystery. The House on Haunted Hill is an expansion to Slaughterville allowing you to discover this Location into your town!

You hear stories of people dying in their sleep and think nothing of it. Then more and more start dying in gruesome ways that seem to defy logic and physics. Your friends warn you not to fall asleep but exhaustion takes over and you drift off. Can you make it back before death claims you? The Dream Demon is an expansion to Slaughterville allowing you to introduce this Villain into your town to cause havoc on the citizens whether they are awake or asleep!

The Horseman stalks Slaughterville. He is taking heads and leaving death and mystery behind! The Headless Horseman is an expansion to Slaughterville allowing you to introduce this Villain into your town!
family: Books: The Legend of Sleepy Hollow

description: The circus has come to Slaughterville. There will not be pony rides, games, or clowns. Instead you will have murderous strongmen, torture devices, and well, ok there are clowns but clowns are evil! Walker's Carnival is an expansion to Slaughterville allowing you to introduce this Villain into your town!

The spirit of Jack the Ripper has returned and he has come to Slaughterville! Jack has possessed one of your friends and it is your job to find him. Unless you are Jack, then it is time to relive your infamy. Jack the Ripper is an expansion to Slaughterville allowing you to introduce this Villain and Player vs Player gameplay into your town to cause havoc on the citizens whether they are awake or asleep!

This is a promotional game released by the software company "Stackify" to entice people to demo their product.

It is a Cards Against Humanity clone, with all of the cards referring to software development and/or computer geek humor.

The rules are even expressed in scrum/agile terminology.

From the rulebook:

Occyourpation is a game of Financial Management, simulating the normal day to day costs of owning your own home and car, while at the same time running a business. The object of the came is to own your own $30,000 car, $100,000 house and have a nest egg from your businesses totaling $1,000,000!

Occyourpation is an 1978 Australian made board game made by Cassa products and is an Australian themed economic game.

In Occyourpation, much in the same vein as Monopoly, players throw dice and move around a board while purchasing objects they land on. Players however are gated on which paths on the board they are allowed to take, each path with different penalties and rewards, by their level of vehicle and income. Players are allowed to purchase shares, businesses, houses and cars aiming to earn more and more money and eventually reach the Victory condition.

Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface.
Each player has a flight of fighters, represented by two-sided counters. Aircraft counters move by means of curved “performance rulers” that represent the aircraft’s historical cornering ability and maximum speed.
Each performance ruler is unique to each aircraft, and contains all the speed, cornering, and weapon information needed.
TAC is designed for portability. The entire game can fit in a plastic bag. Even the largest battles can take place on a fast-food restaurant table.

This set contains the P-38L Lightning. This twin-engine fighter was fast, and maneuverable. P-38s were used in both the European and Pacific theaters.

Stay Out of My Dungeon! is a semi-cooperative 4-6 player worker placement, hidden identity, building game that plays in about 60 minutes.

In Stay Out of My Dungeon! players take on the role of goblins trying to defend their dungeon from the greedy king Flumperdump. Each player, playing as 1 of 7 unique goblins, takes turns visiting and building various locations in and around their dungeon. From flame throwing turrets, to sharkodile moats, to poison traps, players must work semi-cooperatively to build each location to perfection before the King's royal guard shows up.

If all three primary defenses are constructed before the King's Royal Guard arrives, the Loyal goblin players win the game. If the Royal Guard reaches the dungeon before that happens, the hidden Spy will win.

Players achieve global and, more importantly, individual rewards for completing construction phases of a particular defensive structure. But beware! Your fellow goblins may not be as loyal as you! Each player has a hidden identity card and plays as either a Loyal Goblin, a Spy, a Selfish Jerk, or an Anarchist.

Each Loyalty type has its own win conditions. 

     If the Royal Guard shows up before the defenses are built, the Spy player wins
     If the defenses are built before the Royal Guard shows up,
         The Anarchist wins if the Anarchy Track is high enough, otherwise
         The Selfish Jerk wins if he/she has accumulated enough individual glory, otherwise
         The remaining Loyal players win.
    


Stay Out of My Dungeon! combines the fun of hidden identity games and the camaraderie of cooperative play into one strategically rich yet easy to learn board game.
