If you play a Draw It, your friends must sketch whatever uncomfortable or absurd scenario the card describes. For both Write It and Draw It cards, you will choose your favorite response, so your friends must create something that stands out in order to win. There are also Challenge Cards, which you can use to try to force your friends to play the most horrible cards from your hand.

A promo card handed out at BGG.CON 2015.

Text of card:

Play Ernie at any time to done of the following:
1) Randomly draw up to two (2) cards from any player's hand.
2) Place up to (2) cards into another player's hand.
3) Randomly draw one card from any player's hand and place one card into their hand.
This CAN be played as a player is declaring victory to stop them.
Discard this card after it is played.

It is fun and easy-to-play board game , appropriate for all ages, which aims to become a modern classic. The 2-player version is a real duel, while the 4 player one is a great family contest! There will be 4 gaming methods for 2-3-4 persons, You can vary them to provide entertainment for a quick-paced game as well as a longer family fun session. The game will also be suitable for championships.

The main target is to find pairs and place tiles from hand to reach 2048.

Is is a simple game, but carries a lot of opportunities to improve strategic thinking. So game by game you will find, this game is not as simple as it looks...


Nagyon lelkesek vagyunk, tudjuk hogy mekkora potenciál van ebben a játékban. A legnehezebb feladatunk az volt hogy kommunikáljuk a szenvedélyünket és a víziónkat, meglátásunkat. A tesztek során több, mint 95%-ban pozitív visszajelzést kaptunk ami teljesen különlegesnek számít a társasjátékok világában.

Tudjuk, hogy nagyon vicces-mulatságos a játék: jól bemutatja, hogy milyen rövid az agyunk illetve rövidzárlatos…

Játszunk még egyet?

Ez volt a leggyakrabban ismételt kérdés a tesztelőktől és emiatt is van most itt ez a projekt.

Amikor elkezdtünk játszani nagyon sokat nevettünk, főleg saját magunkon. Reméltük, hogy ezt jókedv másoknál is előjön játék közben és így is lett. Most pedig meg szeretnénk osztani a világgal ezt az élményt.

Azzal tudjátok támogatni ezt a projektet, hogy vásároltok ebből a készségfejlesztő és szórakoztató társasjátékból, egyet vagy kettőt vagy négyet… vagy akár 2048-at! :)

Master criminals are freely walking the cobbled streets of London town. The scuffers are clueless, but the reliable old Chief Inspector is on the case and making good progress. That is, until he turns up dead in a ditch.

Any of you desk jockeys want to be the new Chief Inspector?

Chief Inspector is set in the Gaslight Empire and follows the events of Ruse and Skyway Robbery. In Chief Inspector, each player takes on the role of a junior inspector walking the streets of 1800's London, hot on the trail of a Master Criminal. The King wants results, and the player to make the biggest arrest will become the new Chief Inspector.

To set up the game 5 suspects are placed in a line up and beneath each of them face down clue cards of four types: Intelligence, Reputation, Status and Prior Arrests. Hidden on the other side of these clues are truths about the suspects and related numbers.

Players must use their hand of three Inspector cards to uncover clues about the five key suspects, procure the necessary evidence, and arrest the suspect with the highest total value (the most notorious criminal). But beware, certain actions corrupt, and the player with the most corruption automatically loses!

boardgame: Clusterfight!: And Whose Army? Expansion Pack
description:
From the publisher:

Clusterfight is a party game of hypothetical battles for 3-12 players. If you've ever stayed up into the wee hours debating the finer points of Freddy vs. Jason, or Kirk vs. Picard, this game's for you. And seriously, who really thinks that the landmark case of Alien vs. Predator settled anything? Nobody even stopped to ask, "What if Predator had laser eyes?" Each round in Clusterfight, players throw combatants into the ring, then change the dynamics of the fight with "battle cards". Sure, Batman could beat up the Pope, but what if Batman was "Vomiting Uncontrollably" and the Pope was "Fighting Mad"? Everyone has a chance to bet on the outcome, then a judge decides the winner and bets are settled. When all players have judged twice, the player with the most points wins!

50 card expansion to the popular party game Clusterfight!  New Battlegrounds, Items, Actions & Statuses and Fighters.  All cards created by our Kickstarter backers.

boardgame: Malifaux Second Edition:  Bound By Law – Lucius Box Set
description:
Box Set for Lucius Crew.  It contains Lucius, The Scribe (Lucius's Totem), Captain Dashel (Henchman), Two Guild Lawyers, Two Guild Guards.

Lucius is a Guild/Neverborn master who concentrates on using and manipulating minions.  He works well with guard and mimic models.

Set differs from first edition box set in being made of plastic instead of metal, with 2nd edition cards, and additional guard and lawyer, and the Scribe and does not contain Ryle.

boardgame: Malifaux Second Edition:  Burning Revelation – Kaeris Box Set
description:
Crew box for Kaeris for the second edition.  The box set contains Kaeris, Eternal Flame (Totem), Firestarter (Henchman), and three Fire Gamin.

The box set is for second edition and contains the plastic figures and associated crew cards.

boardgame: Malifaux Second Edition: Hog Wild – Ulix Box Set
description:
Box set for Ulix's Crew for Malifaux 2nd Edition.  The Box contains, Ulix, Penelope (Totem), Old Major (Henchman), Three Wild Boars.

All figures are in plastic with associated character and upgrade cards.  Figures come unpainted and unassembled.

Ulix is a master for the Gremlins faction.  He specializes in pigs and almost all his abilities, actions and upgrades are pig related.

boardgame: Malifaux Second Edition:  Take Back the Night – Molly Box Set
description:
The Box Set for Molly.  Contains Molly, Necrotic Machine (Totem), Phillip and the Nanny (Henchman), and three Crooligans.

Miniatures are unassembled on plastic sprues with associated character cards and upgrades.  It differs substantially from the first edition in that, there are new plastic sculpts, 2nd edition cards and it contains Phillip and the Nanny and the Necrotic Machine, but does not contain a rogue necromancy.

Molly is a resurrectionist master and former belle of Seamus.  She works well with Belle models and can summon them and either Horrors or Spirits with the appropriate upgrade.

The most mysterious ninjas, mystics focus internal energy to tap into magic powers.

Includes a custom kage master die and character card.

Ninja Dice core game and Ninja Dice: Kage Masters expansion needed to play.

What gunpowder ninjas lack in precision, they make up for in destructive force!

Includes a custom kage master die and character card.

Ninja Dice core game and Ninja Dice: Kage Masters expansion needed to play.

User summary
AHHBANG! ("AHH!" is sound of a zombie growling. "BANG!" is sound of a cop shooting a gun. The name is created from these words.)

AHHBANG! is a speed card game where cops fight zombies to protect civilians.

Each player initially has seven cards (one civilian, two cops, three zombies, and one randomly-dealt special card). Each round, players play one card face down, then reveal them simultaneously. If number of zombies exceeds that of cops, all cops and civilians played are killed. If they survived, players gain five points for the civilian played, and one point for the cop. Play seven rounds and the highest scorer wins the game.

Publisher's summary
To "BANG!" of "AHH!"
警官がゾンビを撃ち殺すか、ゾンビが警官を食べるかを、数で決めるカードゲームです。

マタドーン (Matadoon!) is like a bull-fighting game, but the players aren't fighting the bull; instead they try to control the bull in order to hit the other players.

On the square grid of the game board, players take turns placing three of their four tiles face down, after which the bull starts running. The bull always goes straight, but when it steps on a tile, the tile is revealed and the bull's new direction is determined. When the bull hits a wall for the second time, it stops moving.

Use the tiles wisely to hit your opponent. A player falls prone and can no longer move once hit and is eliminated when hit for the second time. The last survivor wins the game.

Publisher's summary
牛「と」戦うのではなく、牛「で」戦う闘牛！
闘技場にマントチップを配置して、牛の動きを操り、対戦相手にぶつけろ！！

Roll, build and win! The gold from the All That Glitters expansion continues to add wealth to your city in Dice City: Crossroads. That wealth can now be used to invest in the arts, music, and new buildings. The citizens of Rolldovia are finding new ways to get around your city, and in turn making it a more thriving community, with taverns and guilds sprouting up everywhere to support their needs. Which way will your city turn next? It is fair to say you have reached a crossroads...

But which is the right way to once more roll, build, and win?

A game of thought and artistry. Players are bonsai tree gardeners competing for the title of "Master Gardener."

Bonsai is a beautiful pattern matching tile-laying game where players use tiles to grow their tree and compete for the best overall submission. The game is played over the course of 12 turns depicted on the turn track.

Players take turns selecting and adding a branch tile to their flower pot. Each tile has a color: red, green, or yellow. When players have created a pattern that corresponds to a pattern card in play, they obtain that pattern card. As the game progresses players are given the opportunity to add foliage to their tree, but only to certain branches. This foliage can also count towards color patterns.

Trunk tiles and Large trunk tiles are placed on top of branch tiles. Trunks are worth VP, and build your tree "up" visually, which is very satisfying.

In the basic rules, players have a one-time use special power to trim their tree, or change the position of 1 tile. This happens more often in Advanced Play.

Scoring happens at the end of the game, when players easily count points for every pattern card completed, the number of visible points on tiles, and community goal cards. But watch out! If  your tree has any spare branches, you're going to lose 1 point for each one!

Gem Hunter is played on a 10x11 board but only the squares forming what looks like a capital E with 4 horizontal bars are used. Each part of the E is 2 squares wide and the horizontal bars are connected by pairs of "Crossing" squares. The other squares are Start, Barren, Miner/Prospector, Opal, Sapphire, Ruby, Emerald and Diamond.

There are 50 cards of each gem type with values 100-500 and 3 special decks -Prospector, Miner and Questions. Players start as a Propector and can graduate to a Miner by drawing the appropriate card from the Prospector deck and this increases their options - at a cost.

It is essentially a "throw die, move, follow instructions" game and even movement is restricted to a single path that winds all round the E.

The winner is the first to collect one of a number of specified gem sets.

boardgame: Alien vs Predator: USCM Sentry Guns Expansion
description:
From the Publisher:
Automatic and mathematically efficient - that's what matters most when it comes to automated Sentry Guns. They can be placed anywhere in matter of few minutes and will keep firing at terryfing rate with inhuman accuracy until there is no movement in their line of sight. If you are not friendly - you are dead.

Famous robot sentry from "Aliens" movie is finally available for AVP players as an add-on. Automated gun nests can be placed anytime in any place which extends game in whole new direction.

Box consits of:
2x resin miniatures
2x plastic bases
1x stat card

From the Publisher:

Facehugger is terrifying parasatoid which only purpose is to implant an embryo in victim's body by attaching itself to its mouth. While its strong tail and bony finger-alike limbs grip your throat, all you want is to grasp for air. And that's your last mistake you will ever make, when creature will get inside your body and turn you into Xenomorph's host.

Absolute classic monster from horror and sci-fi genre - the infamous Facehugger is available now as an add-on to core AVP The Hunt Begins game. Miniatures are Single Piece ones, that means no assembly is required.

Box consists of:
5x resin miniatures
1x card

boardgame: Alien vs Predator: Predator Young Bloods Expansion
description:
From the publisher:

Too young to be adult hunter, too old to not to try. Every Yautja needs to prove himself among other clan members as skilful fighter before can became respected warrior. Every Young Blood goes through same rite of passage - marking itself by acidic blood of killed Xenomorph. Many dies trying, but those who succeed are the ones recognised as equals by other clan members.

Straight from the Alien Versus Predator movie comes new squad of Predators. Each of them is different miniature and unique by design. Almost all are Single Pieced miniatures with no assembly required.

Box consists of:
3x resin miniatures
3x plastic bases
1x stat card

boardgame: Alien vs Predator: Weyland Yutani Commandos Expansion
description:
From the publisher:

The best what Company has to offer - at your disposal.

Box contains:
5x 32mm scaled miniatures
5x plastic bases
5x ping token
5x stat cards

boardgame: Stockpile: Investor Card Promo Pack #1 – Opulent Oprah and Royal Robins
description: The Investor Card Promo Pack contains two new Investor Cards (Opulent Oprah and Royal Robins) that can be played with Stockpile.

boardgame: Marvel Dice Masters: Spider-Allies Promo Cards
description:
This promo card set for Marvel Dice Masters contains three cards:


    Luke Cage: Power Man (Alternate Art for card from Marvel Dice Masters: The Amazing Spider-Man)
    Gwen Stacy: Spider-Ally
    Mary Jane: Spider-Ally


All three cards use the dice from Marvel Dice Masters: The Amazing Spider-Man.

boardgame: DC Comics Dice Masters: Classic Legion of Doom Promo Cards
description:
This promo card set for DC Comics Dice Masters contains three cards:


    Black Manta: Deep Sea Deviant (Alternate Art for card from DC Comics Dice Masters: Justice League)
    Lex Luthor: Legion of Doom
    Scarecrow: Legion of Doom


Black Manta and Lex Luthor use the dice from DC Comics Dice Masters: Justice League, Scarecrow uses the die from DC Comics Dice Masters: War of Light.

Dumpster Diver is a game of glorious trash digging, and turning that trash into treasure. This fast and easy card game is for 2-4 players and can be played in about 20 minutes.

Players search, buy, sell, draw, and dive to create hands. The goal is to have the most victory points at the end of the game. VPs are earned during each game round by playing rooms and assortments.

With colorful art and easy game play, Dumpster Diver is fun for the whole family.

This two-player game says on the board that it was "approved by United Airlines" and appears to have been developed as marketing tool. The back of the board features a large picture of a United Airlines two-engine "Mainliner". The front of the board features a UAL logo. And the playing surface - a map of the United States overlaid with a grid of squares 13 wide and 9 deep, seems to include UAL's entire route map in red lines - even though these lines have nothing to do with the game.

Each player has five planes and starts from either New York or San Francisco. Each player must also pick up 10 pieces of baggage en route to the opposite base city. At the start of the game, each player puts the other player's baggage in the most inconvenient pattern possible, since the goal is to be the first player to reach the destination city with all five planes having picked up all 10 bags.

A player may move each plane only one square, either directly or diagonally ahead. Moves North and South are only allowed in the home airport bands on either coast, and planes may never move backward toward their starting airport. If a plane of either player is adjacent in a legal direction, the moving player may jump over the other plane as in checkers but without removing it. Jumps are free moves, and multiple jumps are allowed, followed by a final square forward as usual.

Success depends on artful placing of the opponent's bags, careful planning of a five-plane route that will both reach all bags and enable as many jumps as possible.

Panda Craves Danger is a game all about eating weird, and often dangerous, things that more intelligent beings would steer clear of. Why you ask? You are a panda, an adorable, achingly cute ball of fur with only one problem: You and your fellow pandas have grown tired of bamboo and are craving something more exciting. The trouble is, you don't know much, so you have a hard time telling what's food and what's not.

Enter the the Danger Buffet! A line-up of some of the most exciting, er, "foods" that you have ever seen. Throw caution to the wind and eat everything in sight, but be careful — the world is full of dangerous things, and you may end up biting off more than you can chew.

Using a set of 12 custom danger dice, each panda takes turns alternating between eating dangers from the buffet in front of them and resting to heal up and to digest (score) the dangers they've consumed with the goal of digesting a combination of dangers worth the most prestige by end of game.

boardgame: Apex Theropod Deck-Building Game: Exotic Predators Expansion Deck
description:
The Exotic Predators Expansion Deck released with the ATDG Collected Edition Kickstarter (2020) is playable with the rest of the Collected Edition as is with no changes.

Quetzalcoatlus is a very specialized predator relying heavily on critical ambushes or swarming the skies with Drones and the Commander. Planning ahead is a requirement for this Apex to stand a chance against bigger predators.

     Apex Deck Difficulty : Brutal


Sarcosuchus is a nasty, strictly-ambush predator. Ambushing is an absolute must along with hoarding every bit of food to expand your species. Ambush, hibernate, and grow is the key with this Apex.

     Apex Deck Difficulty : Brutal


The Exotic Predators Add-on Expansion Cards released with the second Kickstarter (2016) included additional cards and Alphas for the Quetzalcoatlus & Sarcosuchus decks that were part of the Limited Edition (1st) Kickstarter and could not be used without the decks from the Limited Edition. It allows you to use the cards from the first game together with those from the expansion, to bring the total number and types of cards in the two species deck up to the same as all the other species in the 2nd edition game.

According to the rules, Hurry Home was designed by J. W. Patterson, whose most famous game was the 1939 hit Contack. Described as "72 miles of hilarious enjoyment", this race game has three twists.

First, the circular course from Home to Town and back again is laid out in three lanes: Red (inner), Yellow (middle) and Green (outer). Second, the game uses a very large 12-sided die with color-coded numbers: 1-4 are red; 5-8 are yellow; and 9-12 are green. Finally, players may only move counter-clockwise on the yellow and red tracks, and must move the opposite direction on green.

Players choose one of seven vehicles: train, plane, bus, car, scooter, bicycle and horse-drawn buggy, but their mode has no bearing on their speed. Each player roles the die and must move on that color track in the direction indicated. If this requires moving sideways to get to the right track, each such move counts against the total: so a player on the red track who rolls green 9 must first spend two points to go sideways to green and then 7 points clockwise.

Player counters block movement by other players, whether sideways or around the track. First player to pass town and then home wins. A variant allows each player three rolls per turn, with the first two being optional and the final one compulsory (even if one or two previous were accepted and moved).

boardgame: New York World's Fair Children's Card Game
description:
This very simple children's card game was published as a souvenir sold at the 1964/65 World's Fair in New York.

The 36-card deck features photographs of some of the fair's pavilions and exhibits. Players deal out the deck face down on the table and take turns turning over two cards. Cards that match are captured; those that don't are turned face down again. Winner is the player to capture the most cards.

It’s the last hour of the school day, on a Friday, before a vacation, and there’s a substitute teacher in study hall. Of course a spitball fight breaks out … Players will buy classroom combat troops, set them up on the study hall battle grid, and wage furious pencil driven warfare!

Players take turns spending 12 troop points, buying and placing a single troop each turn. Troops are represented by unique symbols, cost the number of squares they cover, and each have an action as detailed in the troop chart. Troops can never move once they are placed.

Once all 12 points of troops have been placed, players will take turns using the actions of all of their available troops. Some troops are only available on some rounds, and destroyed troops can never take an action. The start player is whoever was most recently in detention. Rounds are tracked on the round chart with each player drawing half of an X in the current round box after they have acted, making a full X after both sides have gone.

To use an action, place your pencil tip on the acting troop, close your eyes, and draw a line - an action line – toward another troop. An action is successful if the action line touches another troop, affecting the first troop it touches (unless the troop’s description says differently). An action line doesn’t have to be ruler straight, but it must travel in what is generally a straight line. You can’t scribble or go all ziggy-zaggy.

When a troop is damaged, shade in one of its squares. It doesn’t matter which square gets shaded. Repairs work the same way, but you erase the damage from the affected troop. When a troop has all it’s squares shaded in, it is destroyed. If a troop has been destroyed it can still be repaired.

The first player to destroy all their opponent’s troops wins! If no one has won by the 24th round, whoever has taken the least damage wins.

boardgame: Nordguard: Tribes of the White Land
description:
Nordguard: Tribes of the White Land contains 36 cards (including 2 new canon Nordguard characters from the graphic novels).

An expansion to Nordguard: The Card Game, this pack not only allows for additional players, but balances out the impact of larger teams.

This U.S. election game is played with two decks of cards. The Rollcall deck, backed with stars on blue, contains a card for each state, showing an outline of the state, the cost of campaigning for the state, the number of electoral votes it provides, and one of eight symbols representing different power blocs. The Campaign Deck, with red and white striped backs, consists of five types of cards used to capture states: Campaign contributions (money), Splinter Party, Favorite Sun, Endorsement and Party in Power.

The state card deck is shuffled, and the first six states are laid on the table. Each player receives a hand of seven Campaign cards. On each turn, he uses his hand to take states either from the table or from other players. Once he has made as many moves as he desires and can afford, he ends his turn by drawing two more Campaign cards from the deck.

States on the table can be obtained by discarding campaign contributions greater than or equal to the campaign cost of the state. No change is provided. States can be taken from other players by using the other cards. Using Endorsement, a player must pay 1.5 times the normal cost to take a state from another player; using Favorite Son, he pays only the normal cost; and using Splinter Party, he pays only half the normal cost. The most powerful card is the Party in Power, which allows the taking of one state from each opponent for half their normal costs - but the player must take and pay for a state from each player.

The final element to strategy is the assembly of power blocs. When a player manages to assemble three or more states with the same symbol, he pays $1M per state and places them in front of him as a group. After that, only the top card is vulnerable to attack, and the attacker must pay double the normal cost.

Once the last state has been taken, electoral college votes are tallied and the player with the most votes wins.

boardgame: Xia: Print and Play Mini Expansion
description:
From the publisher:

This mini-expansion features:

     3 New Ship Models (Available as STL files)
     3 New Ship Ability Cards (Available as PDFs or PNGs)
     3 New Ship Mats (Available as PDFs)


Updated versions of these three ships are included in the Embers of a Forsaken Star expansion.
family: Game: Xia: Legends of a Drift System

description:
Played by the same rules as Fanorona but on a 5x5 grid of an Alquerque board instead of the 5x9 grid used by Fanorona. On this smaller board Fanorona Dimy is played with 12 black pieces and 12 white pieces.

Capturing is compulsory and a series of captures may be made by the same piece on a turn. Unique aspects of the game include its method of capture (either approaching or withdrawing your piece from a line of enemy pieces), and the "vela partie" (debt game) after winning a decisive game (the winner must let the loser "eat" one man each turn for 9 consecutive moves). Because of the density of the board (only one open spot at the games beginning) and the unique method of capture, strategy is complex and enigmatic, with fewer pieces often beating more pieces.
family: Traditional Games: Alquerque / Fanorona (Indian War Games)

boardgame: Schmovie: Table Top Day 2016 Felicia and Wil Schquid Trophies
description: This is a set of two schquid trophies featuring Felicia Day and Wil Wheaton. These promos were released for Table Top Day 2016.

2-4 players race to the red planet of Mars.  This game comes packaged in a deluxe wooden crate.  Most game materials are wooden, along with some hand silk-screened cards.  Each player has a rocket with several wooden parts, including a capsule, fuel stages, tail section, and drive.

In a twist, the playing area is vertical.  Three long poles for the trajectory to Mars, which is a red sphere placed at the top.  Play cards to blast off from the launch pad, and race to Mars.  But you must contend with your fellow voyagers, asteroids, meteorites, and even a black hole.

Rhyminitions is a party game. Players get questions about words that sound alike such as "Rabbit" and "Habit". If the player can correct can identify the answer the player gets one point. The player with most points at the end of the game is the winner.

boardgame: Are You Ready For The 4th Grade?
description: Are You Ready For The 4th Grade? is a educational children's game. The players gets questions about math and language, and also gets "challenges". There is three levels of difficulty, giving the player $50, $100 or $200 in-game money. The player with the most money at the end of the game is the winner.
It's quiz game for whole family about history of Poland since first Prince Mieszko the first till modern times.

海賊殺 (Killer of Pirates) is a game based on the game Legends of the Three Kingdoms (a.k.a. San Guo Sha), and shares many rules with BANG! The players play cards to try to eliminate the other players, and win if only players of their own team is still in play. The game has the theme of One Piece.

This is a table card game suitable for 4-10 people. The shapes of the characters and props in "One Piece Kill" are all set according to the style of the original Mr. Oda. There are 162 cards in the game (104 game cards, 38 character cards with special abilities, 10 strength cards, and 10 identity cards). The character cards are the collective wisdom of 3 painters. The game cards have been revised several times, and the drawing is exquisite. At the same time, the game cards are laser printed with high-quality copperplate paper to ensure the quality of the cards.

Killer of Tokyo Ghoul is a game based on the game Legends of the Three Kingdoms (a.k.a. San Guo Sha), and shares many rules with BANG! The players play cards to try to eliminate the other players, and win if only players of their own team are still in play. The game has the theme of the Tokyo Ghoul anime.
Space boardgame: modular, creative and strategical design and use of spaceships, fleets and space stations for mining or fight.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: 7 Crowd Favor Gladiator chips, made in the likenesses of the first reviewers of our earlier Hoplo games. These chips can be used in the Crowd Favor Battle Royale mode of Origins and also work with our other two games in the series!

4 Crowd Favor Gladiator chips, attributed to others who have been extremely influential in our Hoplomachus series of games. These chips can be used in TLC and RoR as well as the Crowd Favor Battle Royale in Origins!

The game bau cua tom ca is a traditional Vietnamese dice game primarily played during lunar new year/Tết/Chinese new year celebration. The game set consists of a gameboard and three six-sided dice. Each side of the dice displays one of the six characters of the game (Deer, Gourd, Rooster, Fish, Crab, and Prawn). The game board also displays all six characters that correspond with the dice.

Bau Cua Tom Ca is also known as Bau Cua Ca Cop or simply bầu cua in Vietnam, but its also played in China known as Hoo Hey How. Variations of this game also exist in other cultures such as Britain and France.

How to play:

To play, you will need a dealer (the house) and 1 or more players. There is generally no limit to the amount of players that can play which is one of the reasons it makes for a great party game. Each round starts after the dealer shakes the dice (covered in a dice bowl/plate. Once the dice have been shaken, players are allowed to guess the at the dice and place wagers on 0 or more character spots on the game board. Once all wagers have been placed, the dealer reveals the dice, by uncovering the dice. If the 1 or more of the dice match the player's wager, the house (dealer) must pay the player the amount equal to the player's wager. If the multiple dice match the player's wager, the dealer must pay the player his wager times the number of dice that match. If none of the dice match the player's wager, the dealer takes the wager.

This cooperative game is based on a classic austrian children's book "The grandma in the appletree" by Mira Lobe. In the book Andi is sad, because he doesn't have grandmother. He retreats to the appletree, where he imagines all kind of zany adventures with an equally imaginatory and zany gandmother. Later on he meets his new neighbor Mrs. Fink, an elderly woman without grandkids, so in the end, he has two "grandmothers". In the game the players try to help Andi tell Mrs Fink about his adventures with his grandma and then tell his mother about how he helped Mrs. Fink.

The game is played over 8 to 10 rounds, depending on playercount. Each player has a hand of three cards. In each round, the active player flips an apple token from the tree, revealing either grandma (green) or Mrs Fink (blue). Depending on the colour he tries to fulfil as many of the cards of that colour until a timer runs out. To fulfil a green card the player has to roll a certain combination with four coloured dice as fast as he can. To fulfil a blue card the player has to stack the dice in a certain way. Whenever the active player finishes a card in front of him, the next player in turnorder lays another one out in front of him and the active player continues.

Once the timer has run out the active player has to shut his eyes immediately. On each green card is a word, on each blue card a chore. So the active player either has to tell a story with the words on the green cards or recount the chores he did on the blue cards. He has to do both in order. Each card correctly remembered goes into a scoring pile. Once a mistake was made, the rest of the cards are put away, and the next player becomes active player. After the last turn all the saved cards are counted up, and the group can rate their score based on a scale. The goal is to save as many cards as possible for scoring.

boardgame: Back to the Future: An Adventure Through Time – Einstein promo card
description:
Promo card that was included with pre-orders of the Back to the Future: OUTATIME dice game from the IDW Games webstore.

The card, featuring Doc Brown's dog Einstein, is awarded at the end of the game to the player who has the most Doc character cards in their ripple pile. (In case of a tie, Einstein is lost in time and awarded to no one.)

TennisMania simulates a tennis match with dice and cards for the individual players. Choose your favorite champion among the four available, studies the opponent's weaknesses, choose whether to play on the ground, grass or concrete.

Each player receives 1 tactical card and 10 actions cards (service, volée, back, smash, etc) of his champion, then shuffle each deck of Reaction, Challenge and Event cards.

Before each match determine who goes first by rolling the D12. The person serving gets 3 ball cards, these determine which direction(or lane) you are attempting to hit the ball into. The other player gets two ball cards. Whoever is serving then chooses an Action card to attempt and rolls the D12. The card will tell you what each Die result will mean remembering to take into consideration any modifiers the tactics card grants you. You might fail by missing the ball, hitting the net, or even hitting it out on the opponents side. Or you might succeed by merely hitting it in, lobbing it in or ACE smashing it in. you then drag the Ball token to the appropriate spot on the board and the other player gets a chance to respond based on the type of success was achieved, attempting to hit it back in a similar manner. The Player go back and forth like this until some one misses or all 5 balls are used. Points are awarded the same way they are in real tennis and win conditions are also identical.

Phone Phever is a new, fun, fast-paced, family-friendly party board game that perfectly blends the wonder of smartphone technology with the simple fun of a traditional board game.  Players/teams match both wits and smartphone skills in a FRANTIC RACE to answer fascinating trivia questions and complete hilarious challenges, all of which cleverly explore how the world's favorite invention -- the telephone -- has shaped music, movies, TV, history and technology, law and politics, and the rest of Western pop culture.  Feeding your smartphone addiction, and cravings for social interaction, Phone Phever will provide countless hours of laughter, excitement, conversation, and learning.

Drawing upon the unique knowledge and abilities of individual players, Phone Phever is the ideal gift for smartphone users and board game lovers.  It’s also easy to play:  roll the die, pick a card, and tackle one of 1,200 questions or challenges.  Best of all, to play Phone Phever, only your phone needs to be smart!  Will you be the first to reach the Phone Phever store?

Phone Phever is the first hit game of Bear Mountain Games, a company committed to creating high-energy and intellectually challenging games for today’s modern family. This one-of-a-kind family and party board game has already won several board game awards.  Phonetastic!

In Kahoots is a party game. Players draw a card, and when roll the die to see how many clue words they get. The players then guesses for hidden phrases on the card. The players can score up to 3 points on each card. The player in the end of the game with most points is the winner. The game comes with 6 card racks, a die, 500 phrase cards (1 000 phrases), a timer, and 60 point chips.

Manatech is a 1-4 player, cooperative, action-adventure game that features real-time elements, modular levels and dice-assignment.

In a world of destined kings, daring rogues and scheming evils, mages and magic creatures were once hunted to near extinction. Through rebellion, struggle, and fate, the mana-laden were able to overthrow their oppressors and, over time, build a sprawling metropolis. Now, with these past events all but forgotten, their ancient oppressors have risen again in the form of the Iltheria Corporation. It's up to your band of rebels and warriors to infiltrate the Iltheria Corporation and foil their plans once again. Manatech is played by moving standees through a series of room tiles and rolling dice to pass challenges. The game is won when your scenario specific objectives are completed. Your objectives will take you all around the corporate tower, and force you to face auto turrets, laser walls, and the corporate security team. This modular, corporate espionage game uses a combination of timed player turns and dice assigning to create tense, strategic gameplay.

Jeg har aldri (I have never) is an entertaining and fun party game that reveals wild stories, embarrassing moments, and interesting secrets about you and your friends.

The game contains more than 200 weird, unlucky, and unexpected revelations, and this is a fun way to better get to know both new and old friends. You're guaranteed much laughter, fascinating stories and embarrassing secrets.

The rules are simple
All you need is a group of friends and some beer, wine, or drinks. The first player draws a card and reads aloud "I have never ...". For instance the first statement can be "I have never had sex with someone whose name I can no longer remember." Then everyone who has had sex with someone whose name they no longer remember, must drink. The game can also be played with some coins, or some other way of counting points, or just by a show of hands.

In Dungeon Crawler ECG your party of adventurers face off against terrain, traps, events and characters using equipment, magic, skills and tactics to try and complete 2 out of 3 quests or just to survive the dungeon!

Each round cards are drawn from the dungeon to face off against, and the player must strategize the best way through each encounter. This game starts off in Story Mode, but can expand for Multi-player.

The Thorn really adds some brutal combinations to the mix! This expansion focuses on an undead lich named Zhuldar that has awakened. The heroes are on a quest in to the elven forests to face him in his tower; the Thorn.

The Thorn introduces a new Adventurer (Keldornan Druid), a new Quest (Seen It All), 16 new crawler and dungeon cards.

The expansion totals 82 all new cards with 34 new pieces of art and two Resource tracking cards.

This is not a game in itself, but an expansion pack.

Hedbanz: Headrush is a family party game, based on the original Hedbanz game. The quick question game of “what am I?” that leaves everyone guessing. This version is played in two teams. The fastest team wins. Contents include 25 Picture Cards, 2 Headbands, and Rule Sheet.

From the publisher:

Enjoy the great game of Sequence featuring a unique collection of cuddly canines. Be the first player to get 5 chips in a row by matching the pooch pictured on your cards to the same ones on the game board. Each card also has a brief description or fact about the dog shown. So go 'fetch' your family and friends for a fun strategic game of Sequence Dogs.

As in traditional Sequence, the object of the game is for one player or team to form a line of five or more consecutive chips, either orthogonally or diagonally. Players take turns drawing and playing cards, using a card from hand to claim one space on the board matching the played card. 2 or 3 players can play; higher player counts are supported via team play.

Superhero Solitaire integrates super powers into a mix-and-match multi-deck twist on the classic game of Klondike/Patience. Take on the role of a superhero and choose which villain you will face. Shuffle your deck of 52 cards together and you are ready to play!

Power cards showing your hero give you positive abilities while power cards showing the villain hamper your game play.

Win by playing all cards from your deck (HQ) and tableau (City).

A single deck provides hours of single player enjoyment. However, multiple decks allow players to mix-and-match characters as well as unlocking the ability to play a two-player variant.

One hero can make a difference!

The games aim is to gather his sheep on a hill or a pasture.

The sheep are looking for a new king for their new home.
Each player starts with 30 sheep and 6 crowns.

Who 's on turn , rolls all dice :
The number of diced sheep come to graze .
A sheep per arrow (according to number of dice ) migrates to the next pasture .
The first player to 5 sheep in a pasture , places one of his crowns in the cloud . Are all the sheep to the plan , so that the player per round 3 sheep can move one square . Are then eventually all the clouds covered with crowns , so the one who had been elected king , who has the most crowns in the clouds .

The game is designs to be completly in wood.

Zomburbs is the zombie apocalypse if it were to happen in your idyllic neighborhood. It is also an interactive deck-building game in which you shoot zombies, build your fort, buy loot, and ...ahem... "help" your neighbors, in order to thrive during the zombie apocalypse. We felt that the deck-building genre too often felt like a 4 person game of solitaire. Our aim is to evolve the genre and create a game where you might be scratching your opponent's back...just to stab it later.

The objective of the game is to accumulate the most victory points in both your deck and your fort by the end of the game. Cards will have varying number of victory points and weapon cards are worth no victory points since you use them to earn higher victory point cards called cure cards. The game ends when either the wound or cure cards are exhausted.

At the end of each turn an increasing number of zombies will be added to a player's walls. Creatures stay behind your walls until they are either shot or if they overcome the strength of your wall. When creatures come over you walls you can end up taking wound cards which are worth a negative one victory point and they will hinder your deck a bit. There are ways to remove wound cards and there are not that many in a game so your deck will not be adversely affected by them.

There is a resource in the game that you can collect called scrap. It can be used in one of two ways. One you can use it to reinforce your walls, therefore preventing zombies from coming over and giving you wounds. You can also use it to construct buildings. Buildings are essentially abilities that you can use at least once per turn since they don't have to drawn from your deck, they are also worth a good amount of victory points. An example of one building is the zombie catapult which you can use to fling zombies on your walls towards other players.

If you help other players you can earn two different types of cards. One type is called consumables and they are one time burn cards that can either help you or mess with the other players. The other card you can earn are cure cards which are worth a decent number of victory points and provide the ability to easily kill creatures on your walls, therefore allowing you to continue to "help" other players.

The game is a "take that" game so players who prefer coop games might not enjoy the mechanics of messing with the other players. I spent a significant amount of time in making the cards that affect other players in a mild and fun way versus king maker or knocking somebody out. The game was intended to be very interactive so most abilities are centered on that. Alliances will be formed and broken throughout the game which makes it very dynamic each time.

There really is no trashing in this game so you only start with 5 cards. This allows a player to get to making fun card combos much sooner in the game. This also allows you to focus more on the type of strategy that you are going for in the game.

The cards for purchase in the tableau area are fairly unique with a maximum of 2 copies versus 10 of the same card that normal deck builders have. This gives each player a unique feel/identity each game. It also makes the replay value fun and fresh each time you play the game.

A ruthless struggle for power in a small Spanish village. Accusations will fly as knives, impossible projects will come true and the sickening stench of corruption will be clearly noticed. The meanest face of politics in a card game for three to six players. A self-published free print and play game.

The goal of this game is prevail in the struggle for power in a small village. The winner will be the one who win the mayoralty in two elections.

The village is represented by seven scene's cards, different in every match. They represent activities and places that give votes to players. Sometimes, they will have the chance of change them by their own projects for strenghten their own voting base and weaken his opponents at the same time. When it happens, players must negotiate which of the scene cards will be substituted by the new project. If necessary, they vote if each project is accepted or rejected.

Players take cards of a deck and use them by turns for attacking, defend themselves and take new votes and cards. If they obtain the Opportunity card they will have the chance to execute one of their projects. If they have (and decide to use) the Temptation card, they will choose one of the corruption cards, which purpose is to buy votes or councilors.

After the third round, it's time to elections. Players count their votes and assign to each one the members of the town hall. If a player is elected for second time, wins the match.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
While this 1906 question-and-answer game from Milton Bradley might seem to be a response to Parker Brothers' 1893 Komikal Konversation Kards, it actually owes more to the much earlier 1784 Comic Conversation Cards (unknown publisher).

While the former provided questions and answers to be distributed randomly amongst a mixed group, the 18th century game specified that answers must be given by someone of the opposite sex from the questioner. Comic Conversation Cards is even more specific: the Gentleman asks the questions and the Lady provides the answers.

There are 24 Question cards in black and 24 Answer cards in red. Here is one example. One question: "Has mamma bought you a rubber dollie yet?" One answer: "I'll ask mamma, and let you know next Sunday afternoon". A bit forward for a first date, wot?

boardgame: Japhet Jenkins' and Sally Jones' Visit Tu Bosting
description:
The rules say the full title of this 1868 game from Milton Bradley is: "A Side-splitting Account of Japhet Jenkins' and Sally Jones' Visit Tu Bosting with the Old Meere and a Load Uv Pro-Duce", subtitled "A facetious fund of funny fancies from fact and fiction".

One part of the game consists of a booklet with title page, publishing information and preface, followed by a 20-page story told in a kind of country-bumpkin dialect and containing many blanks. The other part of the game is made up of 162 small (2.5" x 1/2") cards each printed with a noun and one or more other words (e.g. "A rusty rasher of bacon" or "A fighting parson").

One member of the party is designated as the narrator, and the small cards are distributed equally among the rest face down. As the narrator reads the story, he points to one of the players as he gets to each blank, and that player turns over his top card and reads it aloud. Merriment ensues.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Fox Cities of Northeastern Wisconsin.

Lone Star-Opoly hits the highlights with some of the province’s most interesting landmarks. Full of buffalo, crops, and parks, this game is truly about the Lone Star state. So choose your token and roll the dice. You may be competin’ in a Chili Cook-off... or wheelin’ and dealin’ in the big city.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Words and a wild atmosphere!

Each round, a theme is revealed. Race the other players to find letter tiles that make a word related and be the first to ring the bell to place it! The longer the word, the more points you win... But be careful your opponents do not take the wind out of your sails ! What about the theme ? It is up to you : anything is possible as long as you can justify !

Demonstrate your imagination and speed to win the game !

Includes several modes to vary the pleasures.

Players: 2-6. Age: 8 and up . Duration : 20 min.

Contents: 120 cardboard tiles, 90 cards, 1 bell and 1 rule set.

A complete remake of the original game. Tiny Battle Publishing's game has new art, refined rules, die-cut counters.

---

AMERICAN TROOPS ADVANCE ON MONTREAL

MARINE LANDINGS THREATEN HALIFAX, COUNTERED BY ROYAL NAVY

MONTREAL, September 1, 1939 (Roto-Reuters News Service, Earl Dixon reporting):

American President-for-Life Hugh Johnson's threats over securing unfettered access to the St. Lawrence River became deeds yesterday as troops of the American 1st and 27th Divisions lurched across the border into Canada. Elements of a United States Marine Corps brigade are reported to have landed at the small town of French Village, west of Halifax, and are advancing on the city.

Canadians were summoned to defend their homeland by the newly-appointed Defence Minister, John E. Mitchell.

"We will not permit these American scoundrels to pave our paradise and put up a parking lot," Mitchell warned. "All true Canadians will rise to defend our unique culture and way of life in this, our country's hour of need." (Mitchell was shortly thereafter the subject of a Vote of Censure by the Legislative Assembly of Quebec for having failed to make his remarks in French.)

Meanwhile, all units of the Non-Permanent Active Militia in all Militia Districts have received mobilization orders, though transport and equipment are lacking. Volunteer battalions have spontaneously formed in Montreal and Halifax as university students, local police, townsmen and farmers band together to resist the invasion. And the majesty of the British Empire has begun to make itself felt: a battalion of British troops are reported to have arrived at Quebec City; even more encouraging are sightings of Royal Navy and American ships engaged in heavy exchanges of fire off Halifax.

---

WAR PLAN CRIMSON is a simulation game of a hypothetical invasion of Canada by the United States some time between 1935 and 1939. The game is for two players, one representing the leader of the armed forces of the United States that could be deployed against Canada (the US player) and the other representing the commander of the Canadian and British forces that would defend against a foreign invasion (the Commonwealth or CW player).

In reality, both countries have enjoyed cordial relations but it is also historical fact that the United States Army did make plans to invade Canada, in the context of the ‘Rainbow’ war plans where potential enemy countries were referenced by colours (Japan was orange, Britain red, and Canada crimson - whence the title of the game).  In most cases these plans were created, studied and maintained in order to keep staff officers sharp, but the strategic priorities of an actual plan submitted for consideration in 1935 (seizing Halifax and Montreal in a quick campaign before Britain could intervene decisively) are the obvious ones and have been used as the basic framework for the action in this game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Rugby à 2 is a card game. Each card can be used in attack (information on the top of the card) or defense (on the bottom of the card). In total, there are 110 cards and 24 different actions.

Each player has a hand of eight cards. The attacker player can play suites of cards in order to score (try or penalty). When the player plays a card in attack the information in the top of this card tells him which card it is possible to play now.
The Plague Inc: 5th Player Expansion allows you to add a 5th player to the base game: Plague Inc.: The Board Game.

The Patient Zero Pack was included as a Kickstarter exclusive to backers of Plague Inc.: The Board Game.

This small expansion introduces two exclusive Trait Cards (Mass Hysteria and Strain Injury) and three exclusive Event Cards (Infect Board Game, Internet Shutdown, and Crowd Funded Soap).

WeQu is a fun card game to get to know people in minutes while revealing the best qualities about yourself – for relationship and work. Players win the game by obtaining higher WeQu Scores between players based few factors. They are common qualities they share together and the level of understanding between each players. Players use WeQu app to unlock scores and record the score.

In weQu app there is a leaderboard where player can compare scores and see the development of one's play history.

The WeQu deck is composed of cards describing essential virtues and characteristics. Players take turns drawing two cards and selecting the card that best fits their playing partner. As you draw cards and learn about others, you also discover how the world sees you, all in a positive game.

When players completed the card deck, you will get a unique arrangement of cards that represent your personality and characteristics. Use WeQu app to capture the results and unlock the WeQu score.

Cotto & Frullato is a game for only cool people. You've been recruited into a team of legendary action movie stars in the most epic competition in history. Finally the world will see who will be the best badass of all. Your task will be to taste different dishes that have been blended to pulp, recognize their nutritional benefits and assign them to each star of your team, in order to bring him / her to their highest physical supremacy.

All the stars will face 4 Challenges: Strength, Resistance, Coolness and Charisma. But as we all know, physical strength is not enough. To win the Challenges you will need to let your stars drink cooked and blended dishes, so that every fiber of their organism can be pervaded by the right nutrients. Carbohydrates increase the strength, Fats increase the resistance, Proteins increase the coolness and Vitamins increase the charisma. Pay close attention, because the final victory will depend on your choices.

First of all each player gets 2 or 3 stars each (according to the number of players), revealing them.
There are 2 stages: in the first one each player get some cards that represent dishes that have been blended with a drink. Each dish is divided into 5 ingredients. Players will place on the table 6 tokens (5 ingredients and 1 drink). All tokens are true (so they have a correspondence with those indicated on each card) but one, that is false. The opponents should guess whether an ingredient is true or false. They bet on one of theit choice thanks to their own double-faced token (1 side indicates TRUE, the other side indicates FALSE). Clearly betting on the false ingredient is harder, but it gives you a lot of rewards.

Betting on ingredients allow players to obtain nutrition cards, that can improve the basic values of the stars. Carbohydrates improve Strength, Fats improve Resistance, Proteins improve Coolness and Vitamins improve Charisma.

After all players played their dish cards allowing the opponents to guess ingredients and collect nutrition cards to assign to their stars, the stage 2 starts.

All stars will face 4 challenges: Strength, Resistance, Coolness and Charisma. Players reveals all cards of each challenge assigned to the stars. The character with the highest score gains 3 victory points, the second only 1. In case of tie all characters at the first position gain 2 VP, while all characters at the second one gain 1 VP.
Then pass to the second challenge and so on. Each character, however, has a skill that can influence several moments of the game, making the results of the challenges always unpredictable.

At the end of the game, the team's player with the highest rank (calculating the total amount of rewards of each star of his team) is the winner. In case of tie, the player with more nutrition cards assigned to his team is the winner. In further case of tie, players share the victory.

Adorable to Horrible is an adult party game for 2-4 players. It's a game that's easy to learn and play, so you can quickly get to the fun and debauchery! Choose one of the four "Adorables," do your best to keep them safe in their Happy Place cloud, while you push your opponents' characters down the stony steps, toward "The Nasty Void!"

Each player has three pawns, which are moved along a track. Each turn, a player plays a movement card, which moves a specific type of pawn along the track. Alternatively, he can play an event card with some random effect.

The game ends when all three pawns of one player have reached the end of the track - the nasty void. The other players get points depending on how far along the track their pawns are. The player with the lowest score wins the game.

From time to time, blacksmiths from all around the world gather in a competition that shall decide who is the world's best blacksmith, the only one entitled to the blessing of Hephaestus, the god of the forge. But not all are worthy of such honor, and only those artisans able to create the best weapons, armor and shields have a chance.  
Hephaestus is a simple, yet very involving game that brings all the strategy from the worker placement mechanics using only cards. Each blacksmith has a special skill and a single specialty. On her turn, the player must choose one of the actions of the game and pick up the card of that action:


     Buy resources  
     Purchase contracts  
     Buy improvements for weapons  
     And Install Improvements  


Each contract describes how to create an equipment, which can be either a weapon, an armor or a shield, and shows the resources and the amount of work needed to create the equipment. For each piece of equipment you will need to heat the metal, cool it down and then smash it with your hammer! The required time for each step depends on the equipment, and the time you will actually take to forge it depends on the action cards you will take during the game. Each time you complete a contract, you receive the number of victory points described in it, earning bonus points according to their powers and their improvements! The game ends when the contract card deck is over, and the blacksmith who has the most victory points is the winner!

Game Components:
- 104 Cards  
- 4 players boards  
- 26 Resource tokens
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Star Realms: Admiral's Tabletop Promo Card
description:
Promotional card given out at Tabletop day 2016.

Admiral's Tabletop
Event
Each player may shuffle their discard pile into their deck. If they don't, they gain 5 Authority.
(When an event enters the trade row, do what it says immediately, then scrap it.)

Elevenses for One is a set of two solitaire games, each previously released on their own. Only Elevenses for One is noted on the boxtop, but the back of the box notes that Bowling Solitaire has been included.

In Elevenses for One, the player must load their tea trolley with all the accoutrements required for a splendid morning tea before the clock strikes 11!

The game consists of only eleven cards — each with specific actions the player must choose to follow or ignore, possibly to their detriment — with the goal being to load the tea trolley correctly within fifteen actions.

In Bowling Solitaire, standard playing cards are used to simulate a round of bowling.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Chitty Chitty Bang Bang Switcheroo Card Game
description:
This 1968 card game is visually a spin-off from the hit movie, but the mechanism is essentially the same as Crazy Eights. This deck has 44 cards in four suits, each numbering 1 to 11. Each number has an illustration of a character, with the 8s being Chitty Chitty Bang Bang the car.

Each player is dealt a hand of five cards. The rest are face down as a draw deck, and the top of the draw deck becomes the card to match. Each player in turn must play a card from hand that matches either the number or the suit. Chitty Chitty Bang Bang cards are wild, can be played on any card, and allow the player to specify the suit that must be matched by the next player. First player to empty his hand wins.

There are no other special or "pick-up" cards that usually figure in Crazy Eight games.

A fast-paced dice game that simulates the most exciting part an of American Football game, the final 2-minute drive.

The Offense player is down by 4 with two minutes to play. With simple play calls and fast-rolling dice, the Defense player guesses the play call for chance at a big play while the Offense player works to fill up the board with successes and drive down the field to score the winning touchdown!

A perfect two-player filler game for tailgating, halftime, road trips, and more, Hurry-Up Offense offers the flavor of live-action American football for both casual and die-hard football fanatics.

boardgame: Malifaux Second Edition: Smoke and Mirrors
description:
Second edition box set for Colette.  Contains Collette, three Mechanical Doves (totems), Cassandra (Henchman), two Performers, two Mannequins.  Box is in plastic and must be assembled.  Contains relevant character and upgrade cards.

Collette is an Arcanist master who concentrates on dropping scheme markers and manipulating markers.  She is a showgirl model and works well with showgirls.

Second Edition box set for Kirai.  Box contains Kirai, Lost Love (totem), Ikiriyo (only summonable by Kirai, two Seishin (only summonable) and two Onryo.  Box is in plastic and must be assembled.  Box contains relevant character and upgrade cards.

Kirai is a ressurectionist master who specializes in summning spirits and manipulating spirits.  The box contains three models that can only be summoned, so it contains a lower soul stone count than is normal for a crew set.

boardgame: Switching Gears (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description: The scenario is set in 1882 and contains an average amount of combat (but nothing gory). It is suitable for most older kids and families. There are a couple of tricky puzzles that may stall progress until they are solved.

Here's your chance to play America's favorite game show on DVD with the all-new Family Feud DVD game!

Just like the actual game show, you'll now be playing double and triple money rounds, with the chance to steal the money and the round.

With the updated game play and hundreds of new questions, it's hours of fun for the whole family!

The game deals with 2 players at home, trying to match all the Top 5 answers on the survey, with a twist. Both players must try to out score the opponent by scoring the most points on each question round. With a pair of duel question rounds where both must answer the same survey question, and the fast money question round where both are given seperate questions to answer, and match the surveys.

"A uniquely edifying game" where 2 - 6 players move around a Monopoly-style board collecting Word, Revival and Treasure cards and trying to avoid being under Attack.

Players take turns to roll dice and collect cards depending on the space that is landed on:

Word: Collect a Word card which has a Biblical verse on it.
Treasure: Collect a Treasure card
Revival: Collect a Revival card, once you have collected a full set, you are in "Revival" and can collect two cards when you land on a Treasure space.
Attack: Draw an Attack card and play the matching Word card to counter it.
Jesus: You can "Minister" to another player under "Attack" by drawing a Word card for them.

The winner is the person with the most Treasure when the Maranatha card is drawn. The Maranatha card is in the Treasure deck.

The box promises "a game of spiritual insight for all the family."

Riddle Moo This is a children's game. All the players has a button to press, that makes a Moo sound, and gives them a chance to answer the riddle. The players draw a card. On each card there is three clues to what the riddle's answer is. Players read one clue after another. And them someone want's to take a guess they press there Moo button. The first player to guess right get's one point. The player with most points at the end of the game is the winner.

Sums in Space is a children's game. The game has a board with a start and a finish. The winner is the player first to reach the goal. To get there the player roll three dice, two with number on then, and one with - or +. The player then organise the dice to get a sum, and then move the sum on the board. On some spots on the board the sum must be even or odd.

All Around the Playground is a children's game. The player spin a spinner that shows different symbols, like cube, triangle, heart or star. The player then move his/her pawn to the next spot on the board with the matching symbol. First player around the playground on the board and back to the school, that is the finish, is the winner. There is three shortcuts on the board. Includes a game board, 4 game markers with stands and a spinner. Game board measures 16” x 16”.

This game was crowdfunded but Grublin Games dissolved before the game was published.
__________________________________________________________________________

Perfect Crime is an asymmetrical, modern day, tower defense-style bank robbery game with hidden information and tons of tension that's based on heists both real and imagined.

To set up, one player builds the perfect bank using a double-sided modular game board that's designed to give the banker maximum control over how they want to play. Aside from building the bank itself, they set up patrol routes for guards to ensure that the robbers never face the same bank twice. Once the bank blueprint is set, it needs a state-of-the-art security system that will prove impossible to defeat...or so the bank hopes.

The robber's job is to pick apart those carefully planned defenses by assembling a crew of specialists and equipping them with the tools and weapons they'll need. Robbers can choose to scout out the bank's security or go straight in for a quick smash-and-grab heist. Robbers choose which game mode to play: co-op (working together against the bank), semi co-op (kind of working together against the bank), or competitively (pretending to work together, but shooting each other in the back).

Picnic Party is a children's game. The game has a board, and is card driven. The players play as ants and the winner is the first one to the picnic basket, the goal, on the board. All players draw a card, they then compare their numbers, the player with the highest number moves the number of steps that he/she has more than the card is the second highest. There is four shortcuts on the board. Includes a board, 4 ant game markers and 48 cards. Game board measures 16” x 16”.

High Seas ADDventure is a children's game. The player draw cards until the numbers on the cards is more the 10. He/she then sums how many cards there is and moves on the board (eg a 4 card and a 6 cards makes 2 cards, and the player moves 2 steps). The first player to the goal is the winner. The theme of the game is to sail the seas. Includes a board, 4 game markers and 56 cards. Game board measures 16” x 16”.

Dice Nutz Pro Football is a realistic fast-paced, simulation game played using dice and charts. Pro player's offensive and defensive abilities directly impact the action on each & every roll of the dice. Play head to head or solitaire and coach your favorite team to victory by maximizing your team's strengths while exploiting your opponent's weaknesses.
 Will you grind down your opponent's defense with a beastly, smash-mouth rushing attack? Or will you spread the field and allow your future hall of fame quarterback to lead a lethal, down-field aerial assault? Will you implement a 3-4 zone blitz defensive scheme, or do you have a shut-down secondary that excels in man to man coverage? DICE NUTZ PRO FOOTBALL features dozens of offensive & defensive plays & formations which allow you to craft a gameplan that best suits your personnel. Breakaways, audibles, play action passes, fake punts & field goals, multiple blitz packages, double teams...you name it, it's in here! Play the advanced version for the most realistic gridiron simulation, or try a basic "quick play" version for a fun, casual, yet statistically accurate experience.

Hoop Nutz Pro Basketball is a realistic fast-paced, simulation game played using dice and charts. Pro player's offensive and defensive abilities directly impact the action on each & every roll of the dice. Play head to head or solitaire and coach your favorite team to victory by maximizing your team's strengths while exploiting your opponent's weaknesses.
 Find yourself immersed in a world of momentum changing 3 pointers, fast-breaks, steals, soul-crushing blocks, clutch free throws and last second buzzer beaters. Your decisions are dictated by your team's style of play. Are you built to shoot three's, or do you have a dominant big man to feed in the paint? Do you thrive in transition, or are you better off patiently working the ball up the court? You'll need to know your team if you want a shot at championship glory!

Ice Nutz Pro HOckey is a realistic fast paced, simulation game played using dice and charts. Pro player's offensive and defensive abilities directly impact the action on each & every roll of the dice. Play head to head or solitaire and coach your favorite team to victory by maximizing your team's strengths while exploiting your opponent's weaknesses.  
 Ice Nutz features precision passing, skating, shooting, checking, rebounds, breakaways, glove saves, line changes, penalties, power plays, shootouts, injuries, even fighting...It's all in here! Play head to head or solitaire. Play a full season, or just the playoffs...maybe with the right breaks you can claim the Cup as your own!

Soccer Nutz Dice Futball is a realistic fast-paced, simulation game played using dice and charts. Pro player's offensive and defensive abilities directly impact the action on each & every roll of the dice. Play head to head or solitaire and coach your favorite team to victory by maximizing your team's strengths while exploiting your opponent's weaknesses.  
 Soccer Nutz features everything that makes the "Beautiful Game" beautiful! Amazing crosses, incredible headers, unbelievable saves. Perfectly executed free kicks, corner kicks & goal kicks. Fouls, yellow cards, red cards and penalty kicks. Dribbling, breakaways, rebounds, offensive & defensive strategy...it's all in here!

From the publisher:

"A Four Player Abstract Strategy Game with Goblins"
Comprises a leather board 15" x 15" and wooden pieces; all hand-finished. Each player controls an army of Giants, Ogres, Trolls, Dwarves, Hobgoblins and Goblins, battling for land in the prized turnip-growing valley of Ogre's Knee.

At the start the four armies are positioned at the corners of the board. The initial aim of the armies, of which each constituent has a different numerical value, is to capture opposing pieces. Capture is custodial, captors having to have a higher combined value than those being captured. Players can combine in capture. Capture is rewarded by gold, which, when sufficient has accumulated, allows an army to build a monument. Further gold allows players to build walls around the territory they dominate, enclosing both men and monument.

The primary aim of the Battle is to capture territory on the board; and the winner is the player who captures and encloses the largest territory.

My Friends are Weird is a meta party game. When friends come by for a picnic, movie night, party, game night, or just general hanging out, they each pick a card and keep it secret. Each card has an action detailed on it that is out of the ordinary. The players’ goal is to perform that action without anyone catching on that it is being done for the game. People must notice you are doing the action (unless the card says otherwise). The trick is to try to incorporate it into the context of whatever it is you happen to be doing at the time. If, at the end of the night, you’ve pulled it off, you get points based on how tricky the action is to get away with. If you get caught (if someone guesses that you did something weird for the game), you lose any points that you have acquired from that card. You cannot take another card until (1) you are successful with the first one or (2) you get caught doing the first one. If there is debate over whether or not you accomplished something, the group as a whole decides at the end of the night if you pulled it off; partial credit is permitted at the group’s discretion. If the action is something that you would do anyways; congratulations, you are a natural at this game and you are weird.

Green Thumbs is a tile placement and deck building game.  Build a competitive deck of resource cards to purchase plant tiles to add to your vegetable garden.  Points are earned based on the difficulty of growing that particular vegetable and where it is placed within the garden.

Barony: Sorcery, the first expansion to Barony, brings magic to the world, with a sixth action now available to the players that allows them to cast powerful spells. Before they can cast spells, however, they need mana, and only a few places on the board allow them to collect those precious mana points. The battle for control and access to these places will be hard!

Barony: Sorcery stays true to the base game as the new elements add no luck to the game, instead opening up possibilities for players to bend the rules, thereby adding even more tension to the board. This expansion also adds components for a fifth player: new tiles, new wooden components, new player aid.

Welcome to the Intergalactic Skateboard Championship!
Competitors from various ghettos around the universe are gathering in the radical lanes of Drop Flip  Zone.
Are you prepared to skateboard in the universe?

Sk8 Pro is a cardgame with dice. In the game, each player will have to be the most radical skater of the universe, trying to carry out their maneuvers and accumulate victories in a thrilling tournament. But be careful! Not everyone plays fair in this competition.

The game is about choose the right moves at the right time that may (or may not) be made by the skater using the dice result, choosing the best sponsorship and style, and the best time to disrupt your opponents.

Wins the game the player who accumulate three wins first.


O campeonato intergalático de skate esta começando!
Competidores de vários guetos do universo estão se reunindo nas pistas radicais em Drop Flip Zone.
Você esta preparado para encara o mundo do skate interestelar?

Sk8 Pro é um jogo de cartas e dados onde cada jogador terá que ser o skatista mais radical do universo, tentando realizar suas manobras e acumular vitórias num torneio empolgante. Mas cuidado! Nem todo mundo joga limpo nesta competição.

O jogo consiste em escolher as manobras certas no momento certo que poderão (ou não) ser realizadas, a partir da rolagem de dados, escolher o melhor patrocínio e a melhor hora de atrapalhar seus oponentes.

Vence o jogador que acumular três vitórias primeiro.

A psychological teaching game that teaches how to deal with Rage and properly Reason.

From one of the rules sheets:

"Randy is in his late teenage years. He goes to school and also has a job. In the game, we will follow Randy through challenging situations that make Randy very angry. Randy's anger is not unusual, because these situations would make most people angry. It is the job of the players to give him advice on how to deal with these problems and achieve his goals. Players earn lots of play money for giving good advice.

The object of the game is to earn the most money. Play until the last Spark card is used or the instructor says that time is up, whichever comes first. Players choose a banker to give out the money. The banker receives no extra pay period

Nature of the game

This game is different from most board games. Players do not go around the board with their pawns. In fact, there are no pawns or dice. It is played more like a card game than a board game. Players play certain of their cards on each round, and the player who has played the best cards wins the "pot" (all the money on that round). The board serves as a playing surface and a presentation of the skills used in the game."

2-6 Players (More with team play)
Doomtown: Reloaded – A Grand Entrance is an expansion for Doomtown: Reloaded.

Mage Wars: Academy – Warlock Expansion is an expansion for Mage Wars.

A new student has enrolled at the Academy! The nefarious Warlock seeks to prove the way of darkness is unequaled as he summons shadowy denizens and casts secret enchantments to curse his enemies! Compatible with both Mage Wars Academy and Arena, this character expansion comes with 72 Spell Cards, game markers, and complete rules.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
You're trapped down here and the only hope is to escape!  The gates are the way out, if only you could remember where they are in this twisting maze of moldered passageways.  You'll need the Silver Key to get through them as well; it must be here somewhere, waiting to be found.

Suddenly there is an inhuman screech, a yowling, ululating expectation of madness that paralyses your body and frays your psyche... from somewhere, out there in the dark.  You pant as your breath returns and you stagger to your feet; you must escape before they can find you!

Game Pieces:

23 Hex Tiles
1 Hex Rift Tile
12 Horror Cards
12 Lost Cards
24 Item Cards
4 Player Tokens

How to play:

The game is played between 2-4 players, the ultimate goal being to escape.  This is done by finding two things; the tile of the gates, and the card of the silver key which opens them.

Each player starts with a random character card and three item cards - maybe even the silver key, which makes the game much more cut-throat, much more quickly.

The players explore, revealing new hexes as they do so, expanding the board out, revealing the unknown.  Sooner or later a player will succumb to a trap, or an attack from another player, or just the bad luck of finding the brain parasite item card.
