When this happens, that player is removed from play.  They will come back on their next turn with a Horror card.  They are now an eldritch beast from another dimension, and the only way to recover their humanity is to devour one of the human players - reversing their fates.

The game is heavy on strategy; all the cards affect each other in one way or another,depending on the situation and positioning of the tiles themselves.  Despite this, the rules are simple enough to pick up and play in five minutes.  Games can be over quickly, but the more experienced the players, the more devious and complex their strategies become.

boardgame: NYC IQ: The Trivia Game For New Yorkers
description: The ultimate test of city smarts for the Big Apple! This trivia game allows diehard natives, new arrivals, and nostalgic transplants to prove they know the city better than anyone. The game comes with 400 multiple-choice questions spanning all five boroughs; correct answers are counted as IQ points, and the winner is granted NYC insider status. From Harlem to Hell’s Kitchen, the Guggenheim to the Gowanus Canal, Fashion Week to Yankees-speak to Knickerbocker lore and more, this game rewards those truly in the know, while revealing hidden secrets that will surprise even the most knowledgeable New Yorkers. Sleek graphics and game components ensure that this simple but sophisticated package will be displayed on the cityphile’s coffee table, seized at dinner parties, and lingered over for serious fun.

boardgame: London IQ: The Trivia Game For Londoners
description: This one-of-a-kind game offers diehard Londoners the chance to prove their insider status while exploring the city in a whole new way. Multiple-choice questions quiz players’ knowledge of London’s historic nooks and crannies, hotspots, and favorite pastimes. Correct answers are awarded IQ points, and the team with the highest IQ wins! From the Tower to the Tube, Annabel’s to Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese, London IQ promises hours of fun and makes a great gift for any resident or fan of this beloved world capital.

Simple roll-and-move Board game with penalty and bonus spaces, set in the bushranging days (1840s-1850s) of the Australian colonies of Victoria and New South Wales. The board depicts the pre-railway horseback mail route route either from Melbourne Post Office westward to the Town of Ararat, or from the Sydney Post Office westward over the Blue Mountains to Bathurst. Hazards include broken bridges, bushrangers and bushfires.

At least three versions appear to have been published. 
The first, apparently from the 1890s, is set in Victoria. The Melbourne Post Office is depicted anachronistically as is was after the completion of its clock tower in 1887, and the race route is via Melton, Moorabool, Bacchus Marsh, Mt. Egerton, Lal Lal Creek, Mt. Buninyong, Lake Burrumbeet, and Mount Cole to Ararat, with an alternative route via Laverton, Werribee, Little River, North Shore and Geelong. Ararat asylum is shown as two small single-storied cottages. Hazards include bushrangers and bushfires. This version was specified as being for 2 to 4 players.

A second version, published c.1900, also depicts the Melbourne-Ararat route, but without the alternative route via Geelong. The quality of artwork and printing is improved, and the Ararat asylum is depicted as three large two-storied buildings. It was also billed on the back of the board as being for 2 to 4 players.

A third version of unknown publication date features the Sydney to Bathurst route and is subtitled "a great entertainment". This version came with at least 5 playing pieces, so presumably was for 2-5 players. The rules-sheet includes an adventure story of a mailman's adventures along the route, intended to be read before playing the game, and which included footnotes relating the events of the story to the various penalty and reward spaces.

Angels vs Devils is a party/social deduction cardgame for 3-6 players where players can pick which team they're on, try to detect who's not and score points depending on their choices.

Each round players secretly select the team they'll be on - Angel or Devil - or join the neutral team - but switching straight from Angel to Devil is not allowed, which should sow some doubt.

Then players decide to let "the mission" succeed or fail. Devil players can play blue "succeed" mission cards to avoid suspicion and Angel players can play red "fail" mission ones to draw suspicion.

Players each then select who they think joined the Devil team.

Angels score if the mission succeeds, they managed to select a Devil or if they successfully drew suspicion upon themselves.

Devil players score if the mission failed and if they avoided suspicion.

Neutral players always score, but only 1 measly point.

First to an agreed number of points or most points after x rounds played wins.

Publisher's ad copy: "This game from designer Arrigo Velicogna recreates the Malayan Campaign in WWII. A motley assortment of Indians, Australians, and Brits are trying to hold Singapore and Malaya against the Imperial Japanese war machine.

Clever fatigue rules simulate the mounting exhaustion and dread. Nuanced air rules-- that's right, baby!, Air rules!-- and the assignment of tank and anti-tank assets deftly emphasizes their importance in modern warfare. The Commonwealth is in a desperate situation, but the game's just as tough for the Japanese, who must win a total victory, or no victory at all. It's WW2 gaming at its finest, and it's coming in the sixth issue of Yaah! Magazine.

The game uses 84 counters, one 11x17 inch map of Malaya, and an 8.5x11 inch inset map of Singapore. Extensive designer's notes and historical background material is provided as well."
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
From the publisher:

The game comes in a cloth bag and includes:

1) A game board, silk screened on heavy fabric 
  2) 4 packets each with fifteen game tokens
  3) Instructions and rules of the game

The object of the game is to capture five squares in a row, horizontal, vertical or diagonal!

You can play with more than four people by having people play in teams sharing a marker set.

Each turn, the player (or team) marks a square. Then, he drinks a sample of wine (as indicated on the square) or eats a sample of food (as indicated on the square). Each player starts by placing a marker on a white wine space, and from then on, he has to alternate wine and food space. You can either stay with white wine or change to blush/rose and after that to red. At a minimum, it is recommended to bring at least 3 kinds of wine, with at least one white, one blush/rose, and one red. Think of this as similar to a Pente' wine party.

There are several different wines listed on the game board:

     White, Blush, and Red Wine
     Riesling, Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, White Zinfandel, Merlot, and 


Port

     Sweet, Dry, Fruit, and Dessert Wine


Take as little or as much wine as you want to drink on each turn.
Drink responsibly.

boardgame: The Secret Life of Pets: Home Before Dinner
description:
This is a game for children based on The Secret Life of Pets from Universal Studios. One player represents Max (the dog) trying to get home after spending a day in the city. The other player represents Snowball (a grumpy rabbit) trying to block any path chosen by Max. Players take turns laying pathway tiles around a 3-D board representing a park, city buildings, an apartment complex, etc.

Max wins if he gets home before all of the pathway cards have 
been placed.

Designed for two players age seven and older. Includes a plastic pet miniature depicting Max along with the 3-D display and 44 cards.

User summary
未確認スパイ (Mikakunin SPY) is Unidentified SPY. Part of the artwork in this game comes from public domain sources.

In the game of Unidentified SPY, players try to identify a spy, a player who belongs to a group with the least members.

Someone raises a topic, and each player secretly writes down an answer to the topic. Spies are the players whose answers are shared with the least players, but no one knows if they are actually a spy or not. After 3-minute discussion, each player votes a player who they think to be a spy. A player who gets the most votes is executed. When all answers are revealed, the spies win if the executed is not a spy, otherwise the non-spies win.

At the beginning of a game, each player looks at the answer of the player to their right. Deduce a spy from what you saw and what you heard.

Publisher's summary
お題に対して各自自由に回答を書いて伏せます。そこで少数派回答になった人がスパイ（いわゆる人狼）になります。3分間の相談時間の後、ゲームの終わりに一斉で投票し、最多票を集めた人が処刑されます。全員の回答を公開し、処刑された人が少数派回答者（人狼）であれば多数派（村人）の勝ち、そうでなければ少数派回答者（人狼）の勝ちです。ゲーム開始時に全員右隣りの回答は見れるので、それをヒントに互いの回答模様を推測しましょう。

User summary
ギルティブロッサム (Guilty Blossom) is an Uno-like card game, but this is a co-operative game. All players win if any one player gets rid of all cards in their hand. All players lose if the apocalypse number reaches 13, or players take total of four damage.

You may play a card if that card matches its color or number to the top-most card of the discard pile, or draw a card if you cannot or don't want to play. The apocalypse number fluctuates according to the cards played, so sometimes you have to draw a card even when you have an eligible card. But you cannot draw too much, because you take damage if you have more cards than your hand limit. All of you should discuss who will be an attacker who gets rid of cards, and who will be a healer who draws cards and keeps away from apocalypse.

Publisher's summary
協力型のウノです。誰か１人でも手札を出しきれば全員の勝利となります。手札の出し方のルールはウノと同じで、直前に出したカードと色か数字が同じなら出せます。しかしカードを出すたびにダメージが溜まっていきます。時にはダメージを回復すべく、あえてカードを引く必要もあるでしょう。誰をアタッカー（手札を出し切る役）にし、誰をヒーラー（限界まで手札を溜めてダメージを回復させる役）にするのか相談しながらゲームを進めましょう。

This is one of a series of educational games published in the early 20th century by Cincinnati Game Co. They were intended to be used for group play and class exercises in ways that would be fun for children as well as sound in terms of pedagogy.

The deck is made up of 56 cards, each featuring a fraction with a denominator between 2 and 12. On 15 of the cards, the numerator is a "?", which can stand for any number less than the denominator.

The rules booklet describes four group games and 13 class exercises. In the first game, for instance, the cards are dealt equally among the players, with any odd cards placed face up on the table. Play proceeds with each player turning over one card and by combining it with any other face-up cards, achieve a whole number. If successful, all the cards used are gathered up, and the player with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

This is one of a series of educational games published early in the 20th Century by Cincinnati Game Co. The 52-card deck is made up of two each of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5; three each 6 and 7; four each 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13; and two each 14, 15, 16, 17, 18 and 19.

The rules booklet describes two group games and a class exercise for Addition; three group games for Subtraction; and a game and additional exercise for Addition and Subtraction.

In the first game, two cards each of 1-9 form a table deck, with three cards laid out and a fourth face up on top of the deck. The rest of the cards are dealt to the players. Each player in turn must try to use two or more of the table cards to equal a card held in hand. The player takes all of the cards in the set, new cards from the deck are turned over to fill any gaps, and play continues until the deck is exhausted. The player collecting the most cards through sets wins.

Word Game is one of a series of educational card games published at the turn of the 20th Century by Cincinnati Game Co.

The game consists of a deck of 111 half-size cards, each bearing a single letter, with the number of each letter varying from 1 (j, k, q, v, x, y, z) to 12 (e). The rules booklet describes a Primary Exercise for first-year students, three Group Games and four Class Exercises.

In Group Game 2, for instance, one of each vowel is placed face up on the table, and the rest of the vowel cards are distributed randomly to the players and also placed face up. The rest of the cards are distributed face down as equally as possible. Each player draws a hand of five consonants, and then in turn try to form words using cards in hand and vowels on table. Players may also play cards from hand that add to existing words and make new, longer ones. The first player to empty his hand wins.

The rule booklet concludes with rules for using this deck to play Niloe, a word game published separately by Cincinnati predecessor Fireside Game Co. in 1896.

This release includes rules for Yacht Sea!, a maritime-themed game (specifically the scoring sheets and gold "treasure" dice) based on Yahtzee and additional rules for playing Ship, Captain, Crew.

From the publisher (both descriptions):

Yacht Sea!: The object of the game is to collect as many points as possible by rolling certain combinations with five dice. A player may throw the dice up to three time during their turn. After each roll, the player chooses which dice to keep and which to roll again. The player must put a score or a zero into a score box after each turn. The player with the most points at the end is the winner!

Ship, Captain, Crew: Played with five dive, the object is to roll a Six (Ship), a Five (Captain, and a Four (Crew) while trying to get the highest score possible by adding together the sum of your remaining two dice (The Ship's Cargo).

Heist to Paradise - a Dice Game where Crime does Pay! Play the roll of a Criminal and your goal is to rob banks, steal cars, hack computers, and do other mischievous missions to get $1 Million and flee to Paradise!  Compete against other players playing as individual criminals or gang together in up to a 9 player game with 3 teams with 3 players on each!

A perfect game for those who love rolling Dice in a fast paced, roller coaster ride of cops and robbers. It's easy to learn and has advanced play features with a timer element adds only more drama!  For those who's want to increase the difficulty or just to avoid your slow friend sucking all the fun out of the game taking too long every turn you're now timed on your missions! Besides... who's going to really give you all day to rob a bank anyways?

—description from the designer

Stack Tactics is a abstract strategy game for 2 or 4 players where the goal is to capture the other opponents chips. This is done by stacking more chips than your opponent on a square.

Using 4 dice per turn, you execute your strategy by choosing what chips to move, where to move them and how big to make your stacks. 4-player allows unique co-operative attacking.

boardgame: Tiny Epic Galaxies: Beyond the Black
description:
Your galaxies have expanded to the extent of known space.  You overlap with your rivals and fight tooth and nail for fertile planets.  Now it is time to extend the horizons.  Now it is time to upgrade your ships to the limits of today’s technology, and ready them with the brave pilots. Now it is time to go beyond the deep blackness of space into the truly unknown universe…

In Tiny Epic Galaxies: Beyond the Black, players have access to four new ships that they can upgrade their original ships to. To do this, they will need to hire specialized pilots. There are many pilots to hire, each possessing a unique special ability. Pilots are also specialized in what ships they are trained to use. Hiring the right pilot for a ship reduces that pilot's cost. The more ships a pilot is specialized in, the weaker their special ability is. Pilots are hired by spending unused dice that correspond with the four different ship types, and are worth victory points during play. Pilots are shuffled into a deck and splayed out like planets.

Additionally, Tiny Epic Galaxies: Beyond the Black introduces a press-your-luck exploration option for players. This allows players to venture beyond the planets and into deep space where they might encounter dwarf planets worth victory points, or rare materials that yield resources... or they may encounter cosmic threats like solar storms, black holes, and even supernovas. Either way, exploring deep space provides a player with exploration badges. These badges are tallied up at the end of the game and players are rewarded bonus victory points based on who has the most of a certain badge type. They are four types of badges to collect.

Tiny Epic Galaxies: Beyond the Black also comes with additional planets and new and exciting secret missions to explore!


For a deluxe edition of this expansion, see Tiny Epic Galaxies: Beyond the Black – Deluxe Edition
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The idea of Elevator is to get rid of all your cards. All the cards are dealt out at the start. You start with five cards and take cards from your own stack as you play. 
Save your wilds for when you need them, try and stop your neighbor from going out by sacrificing and taking the pile, or change the elevator to "going down" if you know your neighbor has high cards.

What is in the game:
A sturdy box with 3 decks of cards, (marked for easy separation when playing with less players). 
Instructions for gameplay

A simple roll-and-move game about Finnish folk costumes. The goal of the game is to collect a set of five cards from the same folk costume. One card may be replaced with a wild "trader" card.

On their turn, players roll a D6 and move along a board. Depending on the square landed, player may either take a card from the deck, trade cards with another player or take a wild card. The first player to collect a complete five-card set of the same folk costume wins the game.

The Dutch game association Ducosim publishes the game magazine Spel!. With this magazine they will publish several 'Spel!enkaarten' (gamecards). These are games which fit on a A5 card. #0020 is 'Maurice vs. Niek', and was published for the 40 year anniversary.

This game is an area enclosure game by Martyn F for 2-4 players. Each player controls 3 committee members on a 12x9 board. Each turn, a player moves one of his committee members in a straight line, and then places two blocking pieces (Ducosim members) on a spot where two of his committee members could move to. A player controls an enclosed area if he has the only committee member there.
The person with the most enclosed squares (most are worth 1, some 11) wins the game when a player can take no more moves.

IntroductionSquer is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It's played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square board. The suggested board sizes are between 5x5 and 8x8. The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

				
				
					DefinitionsTo flip a stone means to replace it with a stone of the opposite color.

The flipping direction is horizontal for Black and vertical for White.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, you must perform exactly one of the following actions:

- Maneuver: Place one stone of your color on an empty point and flip all straight lines of contiguous enemy stones that are both parallel to your flipping direction and adjacent to your placement in the same direction.

- Sweep: Flip a straight line of contiguous enemy stones along your flipping direction, provided that said line touches the two opposite board edges of your opponent's color and that you haven't swept that same line on any previous turn of the game. As a reference, a swept line can be marked by placing a stone next to it outside the board.

				
				
					ObjectiveYou win if, at the start of your turn, there's a chain of orthogonally contiguous stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color. You also win if, at the end of your turn, there's such a chain on the board and your opponent has no moves available.

The King is holding a great banquet and demands the kingdom’s best entertainment possible. Therefore he has invited all Troubadours and Fools around to audition their best show. The winner will become Fool of the Feast and well rewarded.

Fool of the Feast is a light pocket card game for 2 - 4 players where you try to put on the best show for the King. Only three cards are played in a game round, but with split cards and special actions there is a more complex strategy to explore.

The second time a players show gets the king’s favour - that player wins. A show consist of three rounds. Each round a card is played. First the entertainer presentation, then a fist act and lastly a second act. During the game the King reveal what he likes to see - and players now have to use the different card actions to make their show fit the Kings wishes.

The show with the most points total wins the king’s favor that round. The crown icons on a card is basic points you will score for act. So more crowns means a better act. This will be modified by what themes the king like and don't like. So if the king laughs at your rivals "juggling monk" - you might need to mess that show up.

The goal is to become the king’s fool - so basically everyone wins.

This game was originally a submission for the 18 card contest 2015. It was one the 18 finalists.

This game is an express dice game adaptation of Eldritch Horror similar to the Arkham Express dice game. It borrows ideas from Eldritch Horror, Arkham Horror, Arkham Express and Elder Sign. The goal is to make a short and small game that could fit into a small fantasy flight expansion box.

As all lovecratian games, the goal is to prevent an ancient one from awakening by moving around the world to close gates and solve mysteries. You use the “dice express” mechanics to perform your actions and encounter hazards. You will gather clue, items, spells and artifact to make your quest easier and give you a chance to fight monsters. The combat system is deterministic, so you know for sure if you are going to die or become insane. So you might consider an injury or a madness instead, it's hard to live with it, but at least you remain alive.

Dice City: Game Store is a promo card for Dice City with the following power:

Roll a Die:
1: Get 1 star
2-5: Get 2 stars
6: Get 3 stars

According to designer, promo should include 3 copies of the card.

User summary
奇面村へようこそ! (Kimenmura e Youkoso!) means "Welcome to the funny face village!" in English. Artwork in the game comes from public doamin works.

A player give a question by expressing two randomly determined feelings in one facial expression. The other players answer the combination of feelings simultaneously. They gain one point if correct. The questioner gains one point if at least one player was correct. Play several times and the highest scorer wins the game.

Publisher's summary
毎回１人が出題者になります。それ以外の人は回答者になります。出題者はランダムで決定された２つの気持ちを１回の表情で表現してください。回答者はそれを見て、どの気持ちの組合せか一斉に回答します。組合せに正解した人と、正解者が１人でもいれば出題者にも点数が入ります。誰かが７点に到達するか、４ラウンド過ぎるとゲームが終了し、最も点数の高い人が勝者として奇面村に取材に行きます。

User summary
酔いどれメデューサ (Yoidore Medusa) means "Drunken Medusa" in English. Artwork in the game comes from public domain works.

In the game of Drunken Medusa, players are assigned a role card as a Medusa or a Medusa Hunter, but they may not look at their own role card; only other players can see it.

You hold your card in front of your forehead so that everyone except yourself can see your identity, and look down the table. Then the game begins. You may look at any other players' role card, but once you happened to see a Medusa card, you must leave the game with a dying scream.

The game ends in 20 or 30 seconds, depending on the number of players. At the end of the game, surviving players may discuss, then vote a player who they think to be a Medusa. The Medusa Hunters win if the most voted player is a Medusa, otherwise the Medusas win.

Publisher's summary
プレイヤーにはメデューサカードまたは、メデューサを退治しにきたハンターカードのどちらかが1枚配られます。しかし自分のカードを見ることは出来ません。ゲーム中、自分のカードを他の人に向けて遊びます（インディアンポーカー形式）。ゲームはリアルタイムで進行し、他の人のカードを自由に見ることが出来ますが、メデューサカードを視認してしまうと石化してそのプレイヤーは即座に脱落します。30秒のプレイ時間後、全員カードを伏せて、生き残ったプレイヤーの一斉投票を行います。最多票を集めた人が処刑されます。処刑された人がメデューサであればハンター達の勝利、逆にハンターが処刑されればメデューサ達の勝利です。

Due to a telecommunication snafu, all of the pizza places in town are receiving the same orders from the same customers! The first delivery person to the house gets the tip, but did you remember the order correctly? Which pizza did the zoo order? Mushrooms? Pepperoni?!? You have to race your opponents to get customer’s tips first, but make sure you complete the order accurately or you get nothing!

In Pizza Pronto, 3-5 players compete to deliver pizzas to locations around the table by flicking discs and tossing tokens. Each round, an order is read aloud. “Hello! I need a pineapple and pepperoni pizza at the zoo and a mushroom and pineapple pizza to the firehouse, please.” Players flick their delivery disc in real time, racing to each location. Once there, you can pick up a pizza and try to toss it into the fenced area. When a player has delivered both pizzas, they place their disc in the highest spot on the garage board. Players score points for delivering the correct pizzas to the correct locations, with bonus points awarded for speed and throwing accuracy. Once the deck of cards runs out, the player with the most points wins!

User summary
双六丸忍法帖 畳地獄 (Sugorokumaru Ninpoutyou TATAMIJIGOKU) means "NINJA girl in TATAMI INFERNO" in English.

Sugorokumaru Ninpoutyou TATAMIJIGOKU is roll-and-move style board game for two players.

There are 18 cards placed on the table, forming a triangle. Each of these squares represents a tatami, Japanese flooring material made of woven straw, and has two sides: black and white. Players take turns rolling a die and moving their Ninja pawns along the triangle. If the white pawn lands on a black square that is forming a line of three or more black squares, the white player takes damage, and vice versa. The longer the line of cards, the more damage that player takes.

Before a player rolls a die, that player may play one card from their hand to flip a square specified in the card, and may spend one tatami power to play an additional card.

Each player has a Ninja character who can use their special abilities spending tatami power.

The game ends when a player's life is reduced to zero, in which case the survivor wins, or when any player goes around the triangle three times, in which case the player with the most tatami power remaining wins the game.

Publisher's summary
カードをマス目に見立てた二人用対戦双六。
畳の色を連鎖させて呪いで相手のニンジャを殺す。

User summary
In the game Last Earth, the planet Earth has been ruled by gorillas, and the humans preserve their positions as gorillas' slaves.

One day, dolphin-like aliens invade the Earth. The Humans have to choose their way. Do they continue to be slaves of gorillas, or welcome dolphins as new rulers? Which would you do?

Publisher's summary
ゴリラに支配された世界。人類は奴隷として生き延びていた。
ある日の事、そんな地球へイルカの姿をした宇宙人が攻めてきた。
人類はゴリラの奴隷として生きる道を選ぶのか、それとも
新たな支配者としてイルカを選ぶのか、
あなたならどちらにつく？

Too many sheriffs have arrived in Rhymetown and there's only one way to find out who rhymes the best in the wild wild west.

Turn over a card and take it in turns saying words that rhyme with the one on the card. Can't think of a word? Play a sheriff card to put somebody else in the hot seat.

If you don't have a sheriff card left to play and can't think of a word, you're out. The last player standing becomes the sheriff of Rhymetown!

Simple roll-and-move race board game played on a map of Australia. Players race from Adelaide to Darwin, with various penalties, bonuses and detours along the route. Line illustrations of animals, birds and Aboriginal artifacts within the map are keyed to a list of their names in the top left corner.

boardgame: Ascot: A Day at the Races
description:
This is a complex horseracing game that includes the purchase, sale and training of individual horses, choosing the best horses and jockeys for each race, establishing betting odds for each horse and trying to win money both by having a horse finish well and by winning bets.

Players each start with GBP250,000 and one horse, all with the same form rating. While at the Auction Ring each turn, a player rolls a D6 three times, to determine the mix of actions he may take: 1=Sell a horse for Form rating times current Auction Price; 2/3=Buy top horse on the deck on the same basis; 4=Read the Ascot Bulletin (draw event card); 5=Change the horse up for auction; 6=Change the Auction Price by drawing a new Auction card.

Then the players take the next card in the Race deck to see the distance, entry fee and prize money. Some races are restricted to horses of a certain Form Rating, or apply handicaps that help low-rated horses. Each player chooses a horse for the race and hires a jockey (better ones cost more). The combination of horse Form and jockey results in a number of tokens for each player. These tokens allow a horse to be moved extra spaces during the race.

The Form ratings of the horses are totaled and each horse uses it rating against the pack to establish betting odds. Players then place their bets and the race begins. Each move is based on an 8-sided die. Horses can move forward or diagonally, but not into a space with another horse. Tokens add one space, but only one can be used per die roll.

After the race, the prize money is paid out, bets are settled, and the game moves to the next Auction phase. The game may be ended after any number of races, with the player holding the most money combined with horse value (10,000xForm) being declared the winning trainer.

A simple roll-and-move race game along a 30-space track circumscribing a map of Australia. Starting from any space, players roll a die and move that many spaces clockwise around the track. Each space contains a hand-painted image of a location on the map in the centre of the board. When a player lands on a space, another player identifies the location, "googles the location on a smart phone" and asks the moving player three questions about that location. If all three questions are answered correctly, the moving player gets to remain at the new location, otherwise he returns to his previous space. 
The first player to return to his starting space wins.

Storyteller Cards: Fantasy are a playing card deck, designed to inspire creativity in gamers, role-players, writers, artists, teachers, parents and children. They were designed by Jason Tagmire and illustrated by Campbell Whyte.

Each card features an illustration with 4 unique elements. There is a character, holding an item, completing an action, in a location. Each of these elements can be used to create something new, get you out of a mid-project slump, or just to have some creative fun with family and friends.

The cards also feature additional icons in the corners to help you dig a little deeper into storytelling, creating, and gaming. The icons represent a rank, suit, mood, season, letter, and color.


The Storyteller Manual: Fantasy edition includes rules for different ways to play. From strategic coloring to push-your-luck storytelling, there are game variations for everyone. Many variants are story based gaining points for each card played, best poker hands and matching card elements, but others include strategic coloring (where players must complete illustration quadrants to earn points), and endless possibilities (where the players create and change the rules in a race to 30 points).

Crossword dice game. 13 cubes with one letter on each face, except one blank face on one cube, with a plastic cup. Letters have values similar to Scrabble.

The box says "No. 143" Kohner probably released this at the same time as their Tumble Words (which has dice in three colors, without values, and has "No. 142" on the box). The company address has no ZIP code, so it's probably from the early 1960s if not older.

A snakes-and-ladders like game, using palm trees as snakes and packets of Guest's biscuits as ladders, to promote the products of the publisher's Melbourne-based biscuit factory. A metal spinner is built into the board. Counters were provided at the side of the board ready to be cut out, and were differentiated by colour and the name of one of Guest's biscuit products.
The back of the board featured a pirate galleon to be coloured in with paints or crayons. 
A player spinning a 6 got a second spin (presumably adding the results together). It was necessary to land on the final square on an exact spin; otherwise the player moved backwards a number of spaces equal to the number spun.
publishers: T. B. Guest & Co. PTY. LTD.

description:
Simple roll-and-move race game themed for the 1934 MacRobertson International Air Race from from Mildenhall, England, to Essendon airport in Melbourne, Australia. The race was sponsored by confectionary magnate Sir MacPherson Robertson as part of the Melbourne Centenary celebrations. This game published as a promotion for Dutch biscuit company Verkade, is based on the heroic efforts of the Dutch team, flying a KLM Royal Dutch Airlines DC-2, who came second in the race overall but won the handicap prize.
The board is a map of the eastern hemisphere from England to Australia, with 63 numbered circles (unusually, they are not contiguous nor connected by lines) marking cities both on and off (Peking, Algiers) the official race route. 12 of the circles are blue, and have penalties or bonuses described in the lower left of the board. 
The game is described as for "een onbeperkt aantal personnen worden deelgenomen" (an unlimited number of participants), and players much pay an ante of five collector cards from packets of Verkade biscuits. Some of the penalty spaces require payment of more cards into the pool. Although not specified, presumably the winner takes the pool of cards.

French 19th century roll-and-move board game, similar to the Game of Goose, with 63 numbered spaces spiraling into the centre of the board. Players roll 2 dice and move that total number of spaces. Bonuses and penalties are incurred on some spaces, as detailed by text in the middle of the board. The theme of the game is a naval expedition. Spaces represent various French admirals, sea battles, and other naval and nautical themes.
Landing one one of three lighthouse spaces (every 9th space) gives you a double move (i.e. if you land on a lighthouse with a move of 5, move on another 5).

From Box: Alexander the pink bunyip is hungry again. He decides to visit his four favorite shops. But the Bunyip Catchers have other ideas and set out to catch The Bunyip before he returns to the safety of the billabong. Watch Out Bunyip - don't get caught!
[note: a Bunyip is a mythical Australian Aboriginal creature].

Object of the game: The winner is the player who finally catches The Bunyip OR the Bunyip who has visited all four shops and returns safely to the billabong.

One player is the Bunyip, the others are Bunyip Catchers. If 6 play, the 6th is a second Bunyip. The Bunyip(s) starts in the Billabong in the centre of the board, the catchers in the corners of the board. Each player spins a spinner (mounted in the box) and moves that many spaces (1-2-2-3-3-4-5-6). 
The Bunyip(s) must pass over (they don't have to stop on) each of the 4 shops in the centre edges of the board. The shops and the pink spaces either side of the shops are prohibited to the catchers. The catchers attempt to land on the Bunyip when he is not in a safe area. If they do so, they spin the Bunyip Escape spinner (a second spinner mounted inside the box): either the Bunyip is moved automatically to another named space, or he is caught (and the catcher wins). If the Bunyip visits all 4 shops and returns to his starting space (the Billabong) he wins. Catchers landing on another catcher send the latter is sent back to his starting space.

The same game system, with different artwork and creatures was used in "Watch out wombat" and the New Zealand version "Catch that kiwi" both issued the following year.

boardgame: Digit Dick's walkabout on the Barrier Reef
description:
Based on the best-selling Australian children's book "Digit Dick on the Barrier Reef".  Set in Queensland, the book details the adventures of Digit Dick , a "little boy only as big as his mother's big toe", who smuggles himself on holiday with his parents in his father's coat pocket.

The game is a simple roll-and-move race game using marbles as pawns on a board mounted in the game box. The track is a connected sequence of 45 holes. Most holes are small enough to hold the marble in place, but 9 of the holes are large enough to swallow the marble (and represent the adventures Dick has in the book).

Players must throw 1 or 6 on a six-sided die to being, then get to roll again for a proper move. If the player's marble lands on a big hole, it rolls down the box and he must start again. If he lands on an already-occupied small hole, he places hi own marble one space behind (which might be a big hole). He must throw an exact number to finish, otherwise he moves backwards the excess amount.

A second version "for older children and grown-ups". In this version, a big hole causes you marble to move backwards 5 spaces (instead of starting again). If the countback takes you past the start space, you must start again.

Wizard's Tower Solitaire is a Tarot card game based on the playing card solitaire game Spider. The game begins by laying out all 78 cards face up in 13 rows of 6. Cards of the bottom column are exposed and may be placed below any card of the next higher numerical value or overlapping the next higher numerical value of the same suit. Overlapping cards may be moved as a unit and removed from play when it is complete. Trump cards may be placed under any other Trumps of a higher numerical value or overlapping a Trump of the next higher numerical value. When the Fool is exposed it is transferred to the top of the tableau to form the foundation of the Wizard's Tower and other Trumps are placed on it in ascending numerical value. Any exposed card may be transferred to an empty column and weaving may be used to overcome a blocked game. The goal of the game is to remove all but the Wizard's Tower of Trump cards which remains mysteriously suspended in the air after the cliffs below it have been blasted away by magic.

boardgame: Warhammer Quest: Silver Tower – Mighty Heroes
Warhammer Quest: Silver Tower – Lord of Plagues
description: An additional promo player character, the datacard for which was published in White Dwarf 121 (21 May 2016). Does not come with represetational miniature.

An expansion pack of 40 new building cards, and 2 new Reputation cards.

Metropolis cards have an orange sunset background. When you play with this expansion, you set up an extra row of cards above the normal building cards. Only three cards are available to purchase, and they require you to have one, two, or three books (depending on where they are in the row). You also must have one normal building for every Metropolis building you own. Because they're a little harder to get, each Metropolis card is worth +1 renown.

The Resistance: The Plot Thickens includes the Plot Cards module from the second edition of The Resistance, plus the Rogue, and Sergeant modules originally released as promos. It also includes a full set of replacement Character and Mission cards and new Mission tracking tokens.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – For Family Honor
description:
Description from the publisher:

The War of Five Kings has already begun to shape the future of Westeros. Robb Stark marches on Lannister holdings, even as Tywin Lannister menaces the Riverlands from Harrenhal. To the south, Stannis Baratheon lays siege to Storm's End and Renly Baratheon calls upon the might of Highgarden to break his brother's armies. Yet in the midst of these kingly conflicts, another drama unfolds far to the north. From Castle Black, the Night's Watch rides forth in a great ranging and ventures beyond the Wall to confront Mance Rayder and brave the fury of the wild. If they fail to defend the realms of men, the consequences could reach far beyond the icy heights of the Wall.

A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – For Family Honor is an upcoming Chapter Pack in the "War of Five Kings" cycle that continues to follow the storyline of A Clash of Kings. Throughout this Chapter Pack, you'll find plenty of new cards that support the major themes of the cycle. New Kings and Crowns invite you to stake your own claim to the Iron Throne, even as other cards heighten the importance of loyal cards: the characters you can truly trust, the locations that would never fall, the events and tactics that you utterly control. Finally, For Family Honor introduces iconic characters to join the game, including Ser Edmure Tully, a House Tyrell version of Renly Baratheon, and Hot Pie.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
This game was dated by the vendor at 1939, but the board bears no publisher or date marks, only a proud "Made in England". Certainly it was published just pre-war or early war, since it's objective (other than fun) is clearly to familiarize players with all of the people and equipment needed to deal with the bombing of London.

The game features a striking black board with an outline of the west end of London and its major streets. The objective is to move one's car from one corner to the opposite corner, either between Marble Arch and Charing Cross or between Hyde Park Corner and Holborn. There are four cardboard cars with wooden stands enabling one player to start from each corner.

The game is played with a deck of 52 cards, four each of 13 different people, signs or pieces of equipment: Red, Amber and Green Crossings; Road Refuge; Fire Alarm; Auxiliary Fire Squad; Air Raid Warden; Policeman; War Auxiliary Policeman; Fire Fighting Equipment; Sandbags; Fireman; and Sand Buckets.

The streets are littered with barriers, each of which requires one of the 13 card symbols to get past. Plotting a winning course across blacked-out London therefore requires not simply finding the shortest route, but the best route available with the cards obtained.

From the publisher's blurb:

This product provides 6 extra wound cards you can use together with Extra Wounds from Blood-Soaked Plains and the wounds from the base set. With these extra wounds, you can make a 30-card wound deck consisting of 2 copies of each type of wound. With two copies of this product, you can make two such decks.

Useful for just that bit more variety in your wound deck.

In The One True Goblin King 2-4 players play as a Goblin family vying for the Goblin throne. Players use a press your luck dice mechanic to buy oil, water, sticks, and stones with which to craft tools of murder to kill the Goblin King. Kings have four specific weaknesses that must be taken advantage of. The player that scores the coup de gras becomes the new King according to Goblin law.

The goal of each player is to have the greatest legacy or be the first to seat three of their family members on the throne.

The One True Goblin King uses a modular naming mechanic to humorous effect, and increases the game's replayability with hundreds of combinations.

boardgame: Iron Fists of Our Fathers: Korean War
description:
“Iron Fists of Our Fathers: Korean War” is a war simulation system covering the period from MacArthur’s triumphal advance towards North Korea to the disastrous retreat of the US Army in December (referred to as the First Phase and Second Phase Offensive by the communist side). The game includes the tactical and strategy concepts of this war, and has special written Optional Rules to simulate some particular characteristics of this war.

The game as a base package including only the "Chungchon campaign” part. It is to be followed by expansion packs on Chosin Reservoir, the recapture of Seoul, the Triangle and 1950 North Korean offensive.

In this game, each turn represents 3 days in real time; each hex equals 4.5 km, unit size ranges from regiment to division in general, each SP equals 1000~1500 men for UN side, 3000 men for PVA side.

The game includes the rule book, one A0 sized map, 192 counters and 3 Player Aids.

Schism is a fast paced 'collect or kill' game of celestial warfare for 2-6 players.

Players take on the role of celestial beings, claiming Souls and influencing Believers, in an attempt to be the first to raise their very own army of the damned or legion of followers. Using grid-based movement over two different realms, Schism utilises an action-point system giving players access to a range of moves, options and abilities each turn, but decisions must be made carefully as some actions will cost you your life force and energy; lose too much and you may find yourself banished to one world or the next, edging you ever closer to the end of your existence. So, which path will you follow?

Action in Schism is spread over two separate board sections, one representing 'Life' - the world as we know it; and the other representing 'The Other Side' - the celestial world where the souls of the dead roam free. Players may travel freely between these worlds, or play an entire game in one of them, exploiting the resources available there and playing event/item cards to shape the way the game progresses.

Be the first player to collect 10 Souls/believers or be the last player standing to be declared the winner.

FROM THE BOX:

There is a war that’s raging, a war between celestial beings living among us on earth. No one can be sure whether there’s a heaven or a hell, but we can be sure that there’s a divide - a divide that will separate us all. Some of them care for us, watch over us and protect us, while others corrupt, kill and manipulate. We assume they have always been here, but now they are making it known. The world as we know it has been changed forever. ‘Angels’ are revealing themselves, gathering followers and building an army of believers to defend against the threats that emerge from the darkness. ‘Daemons’ are stealing souls, building rank after rank of damned warriors, preparing to ravage and consume one realm or the next. These beings have begun tearing each other limb from limb, wielding weapons of fire & light, spreading their wings and exerting otherworldly powers. So, which way will you turn? Influence believers? Claim souls? Slaughter your opponents? Regain your humanity? You decide.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
We have all been there. An endless queue of people rather desperate to relieve themselves, and only one toilet cubicle! Roll the custom dice and see who can hold onto their dignity the longest, and who will fail.

Clench is a speedy dice game of player elimination. Players take it in turns to roll the two dice to see if they will be clenching, losing some "dignity", or giving another player the stink-eye and thus causing them to lose a point.

A fun and hilarious game for two to twelve players. Who will be the one to triumph and avoid incidents?

In Immortal Warriors, players take on the roles of warriors cursed with immortality. They fight forever against the hidden evils of the land, playing throughout the ages of history and into the future.

In this turn-based game, players roll their dice up to three times in hope of matching the enemy before them, in the guise of a symbol on the card. They are fighting through battlegrounds in different time periods, to face off the boss at the end of each era in a final confrontation. If the boss is defeated, the player is awarded extra battle prowess (in the form of a reserve die) that they take with them to the next era.

With each foe the player manages to defeat, their score increases handsomely. With each loss the player takes, they are weakened and a die is removed from their pool.

This 1897 educational card game consists of a deck of 48 plain undersized cards, each printed with either a question or an answer in French. The 24 questions and 24 answers are divided equally among the players.

The first player calls out a question, and the player to his left must offer a suitable reply. If he cannot, the next player has an opportunity and so on round the table. When a question is answered, both the questioner and the answerer get to discard those cards, and the player providing a correct answer gets to ask the next question.

The winner is the first to empty his hand unless a question is called out that no one can answer, in which case the game ends immediately and the player with the fewest cards in hand wins.

The rule card says the cards include a vocabulary of more than 200 words and introduce "the various changes of the Articles, Adverbs, Pronouns and Verbs. It can be used with success as a novel and pleasing way of studying the French language."

boardgame: Here, Kitty, Kitty!: Milkshake Promo Card
description: Here, Kitty, Kitty! Milkshake is a single promotional card for Here, Kitty, Kitty! that allows you to move all of your cats from your neighbors' yards and to move all cats from your porch to your yard.

User summary
世界監獄からの脱出 (Sekai Kangoku karano Dasshutsu) means "Escape From The World Prison" in English.

In near future, the entire World was unified and outwardly peaceful, but there still were skirmishes among old nations.

Players take a role of an agent from various nations, and their goal is to let out prisoners of their own nation or their ally. In a prison (playfield), 20 prisoner cards are placed in a 5 x 4 square grid. Each turn, you may switch two prisoners following some rules.

Each prisoner has a nationality, and when three or more prisoners of the same nationality form a line, you take one of them out of the prison and gain victory points. But if you take out a prisoner of your enemy nation, you suffer negative victory points. Then a new prisoner fills the square.

The game ends shortly after the prisoner deck runs out, and the highest scorer wins the game.

Publisher's summary
世界が統一された近未来、表向きは平和な世界だが水面下では旧国家の小競り合いが続いていた。
プレイヤーそんな世界の諜報員となり、世界監獄に収監された仲間を救い出す任務に就きます。
監獄に見立てたカード上で、看守を操り、他プレイヤーの目を欺きながら自国の囚人を脱出させましょう。
3人以上同じ国家の囚人が隣接した場合、彼らは協力して自力で脱出していきます。
敵対国の囚人を逃がさぬように細心の注意を払いつつ、自国や友好国の囚人を多く逃がすことがゲームの目的です。
publishers: 758 Board Game Circle (758ボードゲーム会)

description: This pack contains a 5-card Adventure Objective Set for Space Movers 2201. The pack continues the story of the stolen time module and picks up right where the Adventure Objective set included in the base game ends. The pack also includes 3 trading cards with game artwork.

Pants Up!
Who will be the first to fully dress his or her character from top to bottom?
Pants Up! A cheeky card game for the whole family!

You start off the game with a butt naked character. The goal is to be the first one to dress your character, starting with the pants and then by stacking 5 different types of clothing of the same season.
At the beginning of each turn, players will draw - from the clean laundry pile - clothes cards, joker cards to help them, attack cards to slow down the other players, or event cards to wreak havoc… before discarding one card and putting it on the dirty laundry pile.

The first one dressed all the way from the pants to the hat wins the game!

Contents: 108 cards

The burglars arrived at the same day at the same bank to steal the diamonds. Everyone is trying to stop everyone else from stealing the diamonds that they want for themselves, so naturally they're also trying to rob one another. They're here to get rich, not make friends...

Your goal in the party card game Burglars is to have the most diamonds at game's end, which occurs when all diamonds have been stolen from the bank. To create that bank at the start of play, place the 25 diamonds in the middle of the table. Players shuffle the cards, then each receives four cards.

At the start of a turn, the active player declares whether they'll rob the bank or another player, then they roll the die to determine how many diamonds they'll steal. This die roll can be modified by the cards everyone plays — whether positive (green) cards or negative (yellow) cards — after which the player receives from (or gives to) the bank or an opponent the appropriate number of diamonds. The player takes three more cards from the deck, then ends their turn.

It's time to use your skills!

The Grim Reaper is calling it quits! Humans are dying at alarming rates, and he just can't keep up. He's challenged a few individuals to take the burden from his bony shoulders, promising that the most efficient reaper will take a place beside him to become the next big boss. To test themselves, the reapers — each with their own powers and secret missions — embark on six different locations and times to capture dying victims, but these potential victims will put up a fight, then bargain for their life, offering valuable help in exchange for mercy. The reapers must decide which victims deserve to die and which are indispensable sidekicks — well, indispensable until plans change since in the end the Grim Reaper will accept only corpses as proof of your work, so the clock, as always, is ticking.

The Reaper Game is a dice-rolling game with strong set collection and press-your-luck elements. During the game, players roll their dice, then try to fulfill the requirements of the available victims to capture them. In turn, those victims will grant the players unique abilities, such as extra dice or dice manipulation, to help them capture even better victims.

Each victim belongs to a specific location, and at the start of the game players receive three secret objectives that require specific victims in order to grant extra victory points. During the game, the Grim Reaper itself tries to sabotage the tasks of these chosen souls by taking one location out of play, and once a location has been visited three times, it's removed from play completely. After the second location has been removed, the game ends. The Grim Reaper also has special powers, one of each triggers at the start of each round, causing chaos and heartache to the players.

The Reaper Game features an introductory mode suited for new players and for those looking for an easier and even faster experience.

The standalone game 12 Realms: Dungeonland expands the fairytale world with a dungeon-crawling game in which powerful magic has mutated the lands of the 12 realms, invading their tales and attempting to change them forever. Evil spreads everywhere, twisting and mixing the tales in sinister stories where the ending is never good. Horrific villains have come back for their revenge on the heroes of the tales, this time more dangerous than ever. The source of this magic is unknown, yet whispers of an ancient evil spread through the lands. The once green fields and lush forests are now dark and dead as nothing grows anymore and all the beauty of the realms is gone. With more tales being affected by the dark magic every day the heroes of the tales combine their powers to save the 12 realms and banish this evil once and for all.

In the game, the players take on the roles of famous folklore characters like Little Riding Hood and Prince Charming, each with a unique ability, then attempt to travel across the tales to stop the great villains that have returned by dark magic to change their endings to bad ones and cast the realms into eternal darkness. In their quest to fight this great evil, the heroes face great challenges, but they will have at their disposal powerful weapons, skills, and ancient magic. They will travel across many tales gathering clues about the source of this mysterious evil, while fighting for their lives using a unique combat system that features a set of completely custom dice. Through their adventures, the heroes will gather experience, hone their skills or acquire new ones using an innovative skill-tree system offering tons of customization and options, collect gold, and find powerful artifacts to aid them against the foul magic that has spread through the lands.

The heroes will face strong villains and their minions with a new A.I. system that allows the bosses to react to certain circumstances while utilizing amazing abilities that will challenge the heroes' might and resourcefulness. The battle for the 12 realms has just begun, yet time grows short as more and more tales are being corrupted by this foul magic. Will the heroes be able to find and stop the source behind it before it's too late, or will the tales be lost forever into twisted evil versions of themselves?

Lost Star is a cooperative/competitive hybrid card game about the internal power struggle of a star ship crew adrift in the galaxy.

The Lost Star's captain is dead, and her crew is lightyears from home. One player in command and up to four other players acting as the crew must cooperate to return home safely. However, the Commander is untrustworthy as he will take credit for the success of the mission.

Play proceeds with drawn encounters of various difficulty. Four decks of cards feed the players' hands based on the their role. The commander takes cards from each of these decks, and plays orders based on his exclusive knowledge of the encounter's potential effects.

In the following phase players are encouraged to dissent and replace their given orders for ones they prefer. Orders affect how the encounter resolves, and successful orders increase the players trust. With which they can use on challenging encounters, boost their fellow crewmembers, or mutiny and take command.

If the crew makes it through all of the encounters the Commander takes credit for the success of the mission and is declared the winner. There is only one winner, but no one wins unless the Lost Star makes it home.

Regions is a game about territory fight, using the same board game set as Go. It has one basic concept: region. The players increase their territory by making regions:

The rule of the Regions is very simple: If the player's current stone and the other 3 stones on the board form a 4-side region (which is formed by vertical lines, horizontal lines and lines of 45°), and the region contains no enemy stones (including edges), then the whole region belongs to the player and will be filled by the player's stones.

When one player has occupied more than half of the territory, then game ends, i.e., whoever takes 181 points wins.

Notes:
    * If there is no restriction to the 4-side region, the shape can be weird and against the common sense. So the region cannot be concave, and any 2 sides of the region cannot be crossing or overlapping, which means, only regular quadrilateral are valid. Actually the shape can only be rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram, trapezoid, and a kind of right-angled quadrilateral. 
    * In some special situations, one played stone can form multiple regions
    * Since black moves first and has advantage, so it is ruled that the first move of black cannot be in the center, because center also has advantage. At the end, maybe black needs to pay back some points to white. Currently we assume handicap of 4.

boardgame: Harap Alb Continuă... un joc Love Letter
description:
Neris the goddess has left Earth a long time ago and quickly after forces of evil have started fighting for power over all mortals. Can Harap Alb, our last hope, collect the magical gems needed to open the portal that can bring Neris back? With the help of his friends, who all have super powers, he heads into battle against gods, sorcerers and other supernatural creatures.

Harap Alb continuă… is based on the mechanics of Love Letter, but its action is placed in a whole other universe and the characters are borrowed from one of the most popular Romanian stories of all times: the story of Harap Alb, the youngest son of a small ruler who proves to be smart, strong, and fearless.

Players start with only one card in their hand, drawn from a deck of sixteen cards. In each turn, a player draws another card from the draw pile – containing the cards that have not been dealt, and then plays one of the two cards from his hand, trying to expose his enemies and knock them from the game. Will you rely on powerful cards and jump right into battle, or are you building a plot using the weaker cards? But more importantly, do you have what it takes to help Harap Alb open the portal for the goddess Neris?
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: This pack contains 14 new Objective cards to add to your Space Movers Random Objective Card Deck in the base game.

Need some extra challenge in Space Movers 2201? This pack features 10 more difficult UO Pursuit cards and 4 more difficult Event cards. These cards can mix with or replace the UO Pursuits and Events provided with the base game to make the game more challenging.

boardgame: Space Movers 2201: Bounty Hunter Expansion Pack
description: Now for Space Movers 2201. A bounty hunter has been sent after the Liberty crew! This expansion adds a new element to the game with 3 new Objective cards to add to your Random Objective Card Deck. Will the bounty hunter catch you? The pack also includes 7 wanted poster cards that work with these new Objectives and 2 trading cards which tell the bounty hunter's story.

In The Orchestra players take upon roles of a starting conductor. The aim of the game is evolving to the best one there is. You complete your musical works and control as many musicians as you possibly can. 
You have to collect musicians for you to play (music) your music pieces.
You gather your musicians by combining cards, place them on the right spot in the Orchestra and collect extra bonuses by placing them on important places.
So, who wil be there first on the right spot?

There are many more possibilities to gain extra points, block your opponent and steel points. There is also the possibility to gain points (or to lose them) on the timeline.

The artwork for this fantastic game is designed by Joris van Beusekom and will be crowdfunded august 2016.

Seven is an easy to play boardgame for children from 7 to 14 years old. The aim of the game is to reach the center of the board by placing tiles and making sums of seven. The tiles contains numbers with values between -5 to 12 in all sorts of combinations.
Each player has to lay down tiles on the board. When you lay down a tile you can activate a marker with different opportunities to impede your opponent. 
Before you reach the center of the board you have to cross a markerline within a special way.

Best played with parents! This game is tested on many schools. All teachers were very impressed of the learning opportunities for the children and the children were very impressed by the fun within the game.

A fictional wargame (WW2 themed) for two players in which quite a few things are foggy at the start of the game. To have a better chance of winning you will try to find out what kind of units the enemy has, how many are fielded and where they are located. And hopefully, you will find out your opponent’s objective before it is too late.

The hearth of Mists of War is a true double-blind game system without using an extra player as an umpire. You might call it a block wargame because of the cubes, but it goes much further into hiding information than the standard games in this genre. The cubes in Mists of war are not just hidden units, but are used as a system of fog of war which comes and goes according to what your units can actually see. Besides the cubes, Mists of War also gives the players a secret objective to win the game. Furthermore, a player receives a number of force points unknown to the other player which he can spend on buying troops. The more force points you get, the harder your objective will be.
Activation of units is done by selecting zones. Selecting more zones gives your opponent more benefits.
It uses a randomless (no dice) combat outcome in which all the units in the selected zone can act together and add their strength.

A cheeky game about ludicrous tall stories for 2 up to 6 players. Life writes the craziest stories. But what can we believe, and what is just a pack of lies? Who is lying his head off? Find it out – with the game for know-alls, wiseacres and lovers of unnecessary knowledge.

Aim of the game
Find out which stories are lies and collect points. That is the only way to win this game.

The Question-Cards
Every question-card contains one question, three wrong answers and one correct answer. The correct answer is marked in colour. Also, the correct answer is always either the first, or the last answer on the card.

Although TaRocca employs hexagonal cards that look like cubes both to extend the traditional French card game, Tarot, and to enhance the layout and experience of using the deck for divination, most importantly, the game comes with instructions to play TaRocca Castle (タロッカ・キャッスル). As with a number of games from Rocca Spiele, the goal of the game is to empty your hand of cards. In this game, however, the minor and major arcana cards have distinct rules dictating how they must be played to build the titular castle. For a minor arcana example, cards of the same suit must be laid out to form a path, but cards of the same number must be laid out as if you were stacking the depicted cubes atop one another. Major arcana cards are placed as rooms at the ends of such paths. Additionally, each minor arcana suit has cards that provide special effects, such as forcing the next player to draw, or allowing you to forcibly give cards to another player.

As is traditional with Rocca Spiele games, the rules are fairly simple, with the focus being on producing an artistic 3D layout on the table.

CleptoMania is an exciting game of memory and concentration for the whole family. 
Play it solo or as a gang of thieves – at the various crime scenes, you are looking for loot and demonstrating your skills. You need to be careful and quick, because if an alarm goes off or the site is surrounded by police, the game is over. Only if you master all the challenges will you be a true master thief.

In CleptoMania, every player is a thief. You can play on your own or in a team. The cards are laid face down on the table, are then mixed and arranged. The complete array of cards represents the scene of a crime. One player takes the first turn and flips over 2 cards. If two cards are turned over that are the same, something is activated, acquired, opened or the turn ends, depending on the cards. If two different cards are turned over (the exceptions are the alarm and the handcuffs), both cards are placed face down again and the turn ends. Once a player has finished his or her turn, the police, who are on the game board, move one space closer to the thief or the team. Then it is then next player's turn moving clockwise. If all the treasure and lock cards have been taken and the scene of the crime abandoned before the police have caught the thief or the team, the raid has been successful.

boardgame: I Hate Zombies: Spyke and Geek
description:
I Hate Zombies: Spyke and Geek are two promotional cards for the I Hate Zombies base game.

• Spyke — which depicts the familiar Munchkin face — allows a player to throw both hands during play, resolving the right hand normally and dealing one wound to a zombie card if the left hand ties.

• Geek — which depicts the BGG mascot Ernie — lets a player throw tentacles, which beats rock and scissors while losing to paper.

The King has fallen! With no heir to the throne the task of governing the kingdom falls to former King's advisers until a new King can be crowned.

Move your favor to different characters in the city to utilize their specialities in gaining popularity, gold or improve policies. Discuss and pass the best policies to become the next ruler.
La Recave is the first expansion for Blanc Manger Coco. It contains 40 question cards and 160 answer cards.

boardgame: Latvia (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards: some are for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city: others give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 6 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are some opportunities to overcome this.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

The leaders of the planet ECHO are showing interest in the conquest of the solar system and offers attractive subsidies for all entrepreneurs who want to join the race. In Elites, players assume the role of entrepreneurs who try to take the control over the solar system.

Elites is a competitive game played in team of 2 players in real time over a period of 5 minutes exactly. During the game, players move their units on different points of interest in order to carry out missions (mini- games). There are 3 types of missions, hacking missions, infiltration missions and trade missions . Each mission will bring a certain number of points. The objective of Elites is to be the team with the most points at the end of the game.

A simple spin and move game similar to Pachisi. The game has a four color spinner, and used 3 small metal airplanes for each player's pieces to note position on the circular track on the playing board.

There was only one version of the game and it has an mostly orange cover with an airplane in the center over the game name Crash.

Contents:

     12 metal airplanes in 4 colors
     Game board
     4 color spinner
     Instruction booklet

Born of Titans is a game of heroism in the world of ancient Greek mythology. Two to four may play . Each player portrays a hero from legend who undertakes quests to battle with fierce monsters and retrieve epic artifacts.

Each player controls the actions of one Hero selected at the start of the game. Hero counters are moved on the map along from Sites to linked Points, Points to linked Points, and Points to linked Sites. A Hero's Ally, if she has one, and Crew remaining sit aboard the Hero's Ship and lend their skills to the Hero as needed.

Heroes, Allies, and Crew have three attributes: Battle, Navigation, and Wits. During the game, players must make die rolls against the combined attributes of their respective Hero, Ally, and Crew. Monsters and other game effects can kill Crew. A Hero begins with six Crew. Players record the current number of Crew by placing the Crew marker on the corresponding shield number. As a player's Crew are killed by monsters and other game effects, the owning player moves the marker to a shield with the appropriate lower number.

A Hero with no remaining Crew is essentially alone on a raft. Her crew is dead or has run off. If a Hero's Crew counter is on the "0" Shield on her Ship mat, the Hero may not add the Crew's skill numbers, and may only take one Action each turn. Also, when a Hero's Crew reaches the "0" Shield, she loses any Gear or Ally counters she had. Turn those counters face down and shuffle them back into their respective piles.

In the fashion of good mythology, BoT relies on a generous amount of Prophesy. The amount of Prophesy collected by a Hero is represented by a track on her Ship. A player may have no more than six Prophesy points at any time. Prophesy has two primary uses. A player may spend a Prophesy point during her turn to look at all counters at any Site on the map. This is important so a player can know what sort of challenge she faces on her next Quest or what a particular Sea Monster is. A player may dedicate Prophesy markers to provide bonus die rolls, if needed, during a Challenge. Each Prophesy committed allows the player to roll an additional die during the Challenge roll. Any Prophesy committed are lost at the end of the Challenge, whether used or not. Neither of these uses costs the player an Action.

Each player begins the game with a randomly drawn Quest Marker. The first player to gain a third Completed Quest wins the game!

Zombicide: Black Plague – Huntsman Pack contains all the Kickstarter exclusive stretch goals received by those who backed the Zombicide: Black Plague funding project at Huntsman or Knight-level.

This pack contains:

     the Dice bag
     custom Black Dice
     12 N.P.C. miniatures and white counter bases
     extra Zombie miniatures (5 Walkers, 4 Runners, 4 Fatties, 3 Wolfz, 1 Necromancer, 1 Abomination, 1 Wolf-bomination)
     2 special Necromancers and 12 Spawn cards for special Necromancers
     Metal First Player Coin
     Survivor miniatures and ID Cards


Included 17 Survivors with ID Cards:

     Lucas
     James
     Hitch
     Mortimer
     William
     Danton
     Troy
     Evil Troy
     Falstaff
     Montalban
     Xuxa
     Morgan
     Paul
     Bob
     Lady Faye
     Gilbert
     Piper


Included special Necromancers:

     Queen Medea
     Necro card for Evil Troy
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Zombicide: Black Plague – Knight Pack contains all the Kickstarter exclusive stretch goals received by those who backed the Zombicide: Black Plague funding project at Knight-level. In addition to survivor ID cards, spawn cards, and a rules leaflet, this pack contains:

Six survivors:

     Gowan
     Scowl
     Gregoire
     Beauregard
     Doctor Stormcrow
     Chauncey


One necromancer:

    Grin


Two abominations:

     Abominalpha
     Abominarat

The gamebox contains two Maestro games based on the theme of classical music. You don’t need basic knowledge to play, but can acquire it during these games! You will get to know the instruments of the orchestra, learn the names of famous composers and experience the sound of their music.

Maestro Ensemble is the simple variant of the game Maestro Symphony. The players must walk around a circle of 60 minutes. That means they need to collect 60 minutes playing time of tracks and ' play '.

Every turn one gets the chance to tackle a piece of music, a tool to buy or a kanskaart. The turn can also be used to ' perform a piece of music. To do so, you need to have the right music instruments.

On the map of the music piece which instruments are required and what the playing time of the piece is. Knocking out the map also provides money and/or a change cart, which offers opportunities to buy new instruments.

Musical instruments that you don't own, you can rent another player, who earns money again. At each played music piece you go forward one or two sizes on the staff. First at the end, the Maestro!

The chance cards bring tension in the game. They provide unexpected with it-or setbacks. You can also disturb other players. Each player possesses also a Maestro card. This you can bet once for an extra turn. Convenient to just before the end of the game a fellow player to pass through.

To play this game, you don't need knowledge of classical music. You just learn a lot of knowledge. The players are introduced to various instruments in all sorts of compositions. They learn names of composers and music pieces. By a QR-code on the piece of music-map, you can listen to the track directly on your smartphone or tablet. Because the money is running in 'notes' is that a playful way to learn.

For the version Sympony, you have to earn points. Now it's nog only the time that counts. You need also collect differtent intstruments, soloists and make extra money. Also you get three job tickets to collect extra points. You need to play stategic, like Agricola and Rail Baron. The player with the most points will be the Maestro.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This game is comprised of a deck of 100 cards, each of which bears the name of a country, colony, region or body of water along with 10 questions about its subject.

Each player is dealt a hand of three cards. The first player asks the player to his left one of the questions on one of his cards. If the second player answers the question correctly, he takes the card and puts it face down in front of him. If not, the next player in line gets a chance and so on. Whoever answers takes the card. If no one answers, it is placed into an Ignorance Pile on the table. The asking player then replenishes his hand to three, and the turn to ask moves to the left.

Every four cards collected by correct answers make up a "book" and victory goes to the first player to collect an agreed number of books. The rules recommend four books for 2-3 players, three books for 4-5 players and two or even one book for larger numbers.

The rules suggest two ways for adapting the game to classroom use, either by forming teams or by spelling-bee style elimination.

This book introduces a brand new faction to the DZC universe - The Resistance. This diverse and colourful army is made up of the ragtag but hardened survivors of Scourge occupation. They have lived their entire lives at war and while their equipment may not be state of the art, each and every fighter can be considered a veteran. Their armies contain an eclectic mix of machines from antique armoured remnants of a bygone age to weaponized civilian vehicles in a bewildering array of designs. This expansion also introduces several new rules mechanics and scenarios to DZC as well as a range of characterful Famous Commanders for all five races. The definitive versions of the rules for all new units released for the original four factions are also included. [From the Publisher]

Overview

IBETCHA is a highly interactive mix of Cards Against Humanity and Never Have I Ever. Guess what your friends have done, hear their stories, then laugh your face off. You're gonna love IBETCHA, we guarantee it. The game is basically truth serum for people that aren't in the CIA (and some that are). See which of your friends can read people, dazzle them with your past mistakes, then crush them.

Game Rules

To start the game, each player draws eight random Betcha Cards that say things your friends may have done or are willing to do. All the cards are ranked by explicitness (1 to 3 points).

Each round has a different Victim. Everyone puts down a card (face-down) they think the Victim has done. Then, the Victim has to respond "yes" or "no" to each one. The group then screams which story they want the Victim to tell. Whoever submitted the chosen card, gets to keep it and receives the number of points on it.
