boardgame: Garbage Day: "Will You Marry Me?" Promo
description:
Promo card given out during the Kickstarter for Garbage Day.

One player asks another player to marry them.

If they answer YES:
Share your room and your life with your fiance!  Your room Max Size is now 20 and you now clean your room together.

If they answer NO:
Place this card into the Garbage Can.

Get out of my room, NOW!

This expansion is all about sharing your love (or hate) with your annoying siblings.  Tidy up that spare guest room because this expansion takes Garbage Day to 6 players.

Includes 15 new cards!

Synergy! Delegate! Buzzwords!

This expansion is all about decreasing that workload so you can enjoy that nice corner office.  Also, take on a new employee as this expansion takes "Garbage Day" up to 6 players.

Includes 15 new cards!

Shhhhhh! Don't tell the RA!

This expansion packs your college dorm rooms so full you may want to sleep in the commons area.  Clear off the top bunk as the expansion takes Garbage Day up to 6 players!

Includes 15 new cards!

Src:Card 3-4 player expansion adds 60 additional cards to the game to allow 3-4 player play. The pack also contains extended instructions on how to play with additional players.

This is not a stand-alone product.

The Bass Fishing Board Game involves moving around the board to different fishing spots with your boat pawn. At those spots you can use a combination of lures, action cards, and dice for the chance to land a big bass. The object of the game is to fill your boat with five of the largest fish. At the end of the tournament, each player's total fish weight is calculated and a winner is determined.

Visit www.bassfishingboardgame.com for more.

Viernes 13: Jason en San Juan ("Friday The 13th: Jason in San Juan") Board game is a fan made tribute mostly to Viernes 13, a song made by urban songwriter and singer Vico-C and Friday The 13th franchise. 
In this coop game the goal is get to Joseph Café before Jason does. Every player starts at "El Morro" and after choosing who's going to be the 1st player at random; each player takes turns rolling dices and running thru the streets of San Juan trying to survive the Jason's Hideouts spaces and the Event spaces. The first time a player ends his/her turn on Jason’s Hideout space or an Event card states an action that includes Jason, this “personaje de viernes 13”(Friday the 13th character) enters in play. Jason will play on the last turn (after the last player). Whoever wants to can play as Jason (even your mom who hates horror films). Try to stay alive while everything on the board is depleting those important Group Points. Every one wins if one of the players reach Joseph's Cafe. Everyone loses if Group points reaches zero or Jason gets to Joseph's Cafe before any player.
The Pax Renaissance Expansion introduces 59 new cards with new abilities to Pax Renaissance, new "Renaissance Man" card types, and two new one-shots: Apostasy & Victory Inactivation.

This children's game from Corey Games is a very simple roll and move race. Players each get two tokens and must move them along a path that includes four circles and rectangle. They must spin the exact number needed to reach the exit (red dot) square of each of these.

The only element of judgment is over which token to move with a given spin so as to make it through the exit points. Movement is eased by a "move ahead seven" space in each circle that just happens to put a token on the exit dot.

This children's game combines a deck of 54 small cards and a playing board. The board has outlines of four large clown faces, each containing 12 spaces for cards. There also are six green spaces for individual cards. Each of the cards has a number and either a piece of clown face or an animal.

Players are dealt eight cards each, with the remainder forming a draw pile. In turn each must try to play a card on one of the spaces. The first player must play either a 1 card on one of the clown noses or an animal card on a green space. If he can do neither, he draws a card.

Each clown face must be built up in number order, from 1 (nose) to 2 (eyes), 3 (mouth) 4 (eyebrows), 5-6 (ears) and finally 7 (hair). There are two spaces for eyebrows, eyes, mouth, and upper and lower ears, and the right and left numbered spaces must have precisely matching parts on each face.

Each player either plays a card or draws until one player managers to win by being the first to empty his hand. The rules recommend continuing the game until all the clown faces are filled out.

Based on his abstract L-Game, this is a themed election simulation game by the same designer, Edward de Bono, using a British general election as a model.

While the original L-Game can be played with the pieces and board which are the same dimensions as the original, this game adds descriptions and effects to the 16 board spaces which influence the standing of political parties on an included "Poll Indicator" chart.

At the end of each player's move, the uncovered spaces on the board influence the standings of the two Main Parties (players) and the non-player Other Parties. For example, if the "Diplomatic or military success" space is left uncovered then the government party gains 1% in the polls at the end of their move, while the the opposition party would lose 1%.

Unlike the original L-Game, this version does not end if a player is unable to make a move. Instead the game ends after a time limit, fixed number of turns or if the "Snap election" space is left uncovered the active player chooses to end the game.  At this point the support for the non-player Other Parties is subtracted from that of the leading Main Party to determine the winner.

A game for the gays by the gays. The first expansion pack that will make your main package bigger, harder, and better performing!

100 unique cards that is updated with the latest LGBT lingo and even includes some of your filthy ideas!

Promotional tile from the Spanish edition

Roll 1 die (specials included) and 1 instant die.

Remove 1 scarab to each player

User summary
In the game of BANDITS!, players are bosses of bandit bands, and try to steal unearthed treasures. The player with the most treasures wins the game.

Each round, a treasure is unearthed from each of four sites. Treasure value ranges from 1 to 10. Then each player plays a thief and reveals them simultaneously.

A thief card has a specific color that indicates which site to go to. If you go into a site alone, you steal all the treasures there.

If two or more players chose the same site, they can negotiate to split the treasures there, or fight to grab all the treasures. Treasures not taken are held over to next round, heating up the game. Players hire thieves using treasures they have stolen.

The base set has special cards: Knights and Golems. And there is an expansion that has Dragons, Princesses, and Witches, adding more excitement to the game.

Publisher's summary
プレイヤーは盗賊団の親分です。プレイヤーが雇っている盗賊で発掘された宝を盗みます。
盗賊団を強くするために盗んだ宝で盗賊を雇います。
勝利者はゲーム終了時に宝を多く持っていたプレイヤーです。
発掘場所では毎ターン１つずつ宝が発掘されます。その価値は１〜１０点のランダムです。
宝を見た各プレイヤーは盗賊を1人選び、同時に公開します。
発掘場所と盗賊はそれぞれ4色あります。盗賊は自分と同じ色の発掘場所の宝を盗みに入ることが出来ます。
同じ発掘場所に盗賊が集まった場合、交渉によって宝を分配する事が出来ます。
もしくは、戦争して勝つ事で、全ての宝を入手出来ます。
発掘場所に残された宝はキャリーオーバーします。ゲームはヒートアップします。
基本セットには騎士、ゴーレムが含まれます。さらに、拡張セットもあります。ドラゴン、姫、魔女、が登場し更にゲームが盛り上がります！

This game requires good skills!

Every player in his turn play as a mad scientist who'll try to create some heroes by a mix of potions while the other players will try to steal them creating the best super heroes team.

The Scientest player is the master who has got in his hand some cards to mix in groups of three cards matching with the three components useful to realize every Super Hero.

Some component cards are covered and other ones are discovered, so everybody can use the discovered informations to understand if the superhero has been created or not.

The Master must realize more super hero as possible for receiving award to delete his malus at the end of the game.

Every other player must block (with the hand), before the other players, the super heroes realized, receiving victory points.

If you block a not realized super hero you'll receive the same points as malus.

The winner is the player with more victory points at the end of the game.

User summary
100万人死んだ部署 (Hyakumannin Shinda Busho) in English would be "The Department That Claimed a Million Lives". (In Japan, there is a picture book named "Hyakumankai Ikita Neko" (The Cat That Lived a Million Times), and the game title is derived from that).

Welcome to the department that claimed a million lives!

You are assigned to a department that is said to have killed a million workers, and have four job cards to work on.

You cannot finish your jobs without the part-time worker Miya-chan's help, so everyone competes to get her. When a player finishes all their jobs, her working time runs out and everyone else has to work over-time to finish their remaining jobs by themselves. Add up the face values of cards in their hand, and that's the over-time hours this week.

If a player's total over-time exceeds 300 hours, that player suffers Karoshi (death from overwork) and loses the game. All other players win because the department gets investigated and all survivors are liberated from overworking.

If all players survive four weeks without exceeding 300 hours, all players win the game.

There are four types of cards in the game.
(1) paperwork: Playable while you have control of Miya-chan to finish a job
(2) snack: Playable in response to paperwork to gain control of Miya-chan
(3) toy: Playable while you have control of Miya-chan to make others discard snacks
(4) paperwork with candy: Playable while a toy is active to gain control of Miya-chan

Use them wisely and finish all jobs with Miya-chan.

Publisher's summary
100万人死んだ部署へようこそ！
あなたたち社員（プレイヤー）は『100万人死んだ部署』と呼ばれる部署に配属され、仕事（4枚の手札）を渡されることになります。
手札は、アルバイトの『ミヤちゃん』に手伝ってもらうことで減らすことができます。
社員同士で「ミヤちゃんに手伝ってもらう権利」を奪い合い、誰よりも早く自分の仕事（手札）をゼロにしましょう！
手札が残ってしまった人は、カードの数字を合計して「残業時間」として記録していきます。
勝利条件は2つあり、①誰かの残業時間が累計300時間を超えて『過労死（負け）』した際に生きていること、　または　②全員で4週間生き残ること、です。
ゲームのフェイズは4段階あり、それぞれ「準備」「スタートプレイヤーの決定」「権利を奪い合うメインのゲーム」「残業時間の計算と勝利判定」になります。
この準備～勝利判定までの流れを1週間と呼び、誰も死んでいなければ手札を配りなおして再開します（1週間を終えるのには3分もかかりません！）。
メインのゲームでは、権利を持っているプレイヤーが手札から場にカードをプレイしていきます。カードに描かれた4種類の絵柄で権利を奪い合います。
権利を持っているときにプレイできる書類、書類が出た時に出せて権利を奪える「おやつ」、権利を持つときに他全プレイヤーのおやつを捨てさせる「おもちゃ」、おもちゃが出た時におやつの代わりに出せて権利をうばえる「おまけつき書類」の4種類があります。

Delving! is a fantasy themed family style card game.

Take on the role of both dungeon crafter and seasoned adventurer. Explore the dungeon, slay the monsters, grab the treasure all while trying to thwart the efforts of your rivals.

The game is playable with one to five players. Play time is about 30 minutes.

Search and Survive is a cooperative, zombie apocalypse deck building game for 1-4 players. In Search and Survive players must work together to rescue survivors, gather resources, and complete objectives; all while attempting to keep the growing zombie threat in check. The zombies have unique abilities to challenge players with, and the Horde moves towards the players with the potential of moving off of the play area and damaging them.

Unlike many games within the deck building genre, Search and Survive offers a unique combat system that blends card values with dice rolls. Failed attacks, zombie advancement, and zombie abilities can all cause players to take Damage cards. These cards not only dilute a player's deck, but are also a finite resource. If any one player takes their 10th Damage Card the game ends in defeat.

In Search and Survive players must decide how far they want to push their luck on their turns. As ammunition depletes attacks become more dangerous. Melee attacks must also be considered carefully as close combat is riskier than ranged, but also has some powerful rewards. As combat resolves players take the spoils of war and add them to their decks, increasing their strength.

Players must work together to complete the objective of the session. Rescued survivors, locations, useful items, and powerful weapons must be combined to ensure the survival of the human race.

Spiral consists of two games:

The Gift: A two-player abstract strategy game with a dose of luck; cards contained in a trick determine where you must play. It's a a mashup of David Parlett's Tantony and Cameron Browne's Cross with a unique way of ensuring that you don't know precisely the contents of your opponent's hand.

Grazing: A solitaire game played with a formidable virtual opponent. It's a mashup of the objectives of Jorge Gomez Arrausi's Unlur with a simple solitaire game. You are trying to win a game against a virtual opponent that plays two pieces per turn.

From the designer:

This is an even simpler, air group version (for me and my 8 year old son to play) of my squadron level 1990 game Deadly Reckoning, which I am in the process of revising/updating.  It nonetheless seems to have all the essential elements and decision points of a depthy Midway game.

90 miles per hex, 3 hour turns, 13 Japanese combat units, 11 American.  All icons crafted pixel by pixel by me.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
3 complete games for pre-schoolers: circuit game, memory game and balancing game.

Walking The Animals: The 15 shape blocks are placed to form a circuit with colors organised in repeated series of
red/green/blue. The children throw the dice in turn. Their animals go forward to the next block with the color
that has come up on the dice. The player who arrives the first on the last block wins the game.

Hide And Seek: The 6 shape blocks are placed in the centre of the game area. The adult hides the 6 animals at random under the 6 shape blocks. The first child throws the dice, tries to find the animal that comes up on the dice by lifting up the shape block of his choice.

     If he finds the animal he is looking for, he has won it and places it in front of himself.
     If he does not find the animal he is looking for under the block or if there is no animal there,


the next player throws the dice.
And so on…
The player who finds the most animals wins the game

Bang, Crash, Wallop - Poor Animals!: The blocks and animals are placed in front of the players within arm's reach.
Playing the game Version 1:
The children choose in turn a block or an animal and place them
on top of each other. The player who makes the pyramid fall over loses the game.

Playing the game Version 2 :
With 1 color dice and 1 animal dice, the children take turns to throw the 2 dices. The color dice determines the block that the child must take and place on the pyramid. When it is his turn the player chooses a block or an animal corresponding
to what comes up when the dices have been thrown.
The blocks and animals are placed one on top of the other or one inside the other to form a pyramid. The player who makes the pyramid fall loses the game.

boardgame: Halo: Fleet Battles – UNSC MAC Platform Upgrade
description:
From the publisher:

Magnetic Accelerator Cannons of unsurpassed lethality were used to defend the planet of Reach. These devastating orbital defence platforms will add a new element to the game of Halo: Fleet Battles in the form of fixed fortifications that form defensive lynchpins behind which players can rally their fleet. A cool feature of these models is that it can be mounted both horizontally and vertically.

Societies in the 28th century roam the galaxies in continent-sized starships. Captains of these starship societies gather to negotiate peacefully in the Starships Assembly. While the peace holds you will recruit captains to your side and deploy a fleet of starships. But after a declaration of war there is no course left except to engage your opponent's fleet and triumph!

Starships Assembly is a two-player strategy game played with 20 cards, each with a captain on one side and a ship on the other.

The Assembly phase is a peaceful political arena. A card from your hand could be played as a recruited captain, where captains cast votes and have special abilities. Spend your votes to recruit more captains and also to play cards from your hand using the other side: a deployed ship.

Eventually one player or the other will cause a declaration of war. Then every card that is not a ship gets shuffled together and your fleet of ships is all you take with you to the final Open Warfare phase.

War has been declared! There are no reinforcements! From here on out you must defeat the enemy fleet, ship by ship. Be the last player with a ship to win the game!

Lin is a drawless territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an odd-sized, initially empty square grid (board).

Lin was inspired by Rin and Loops.

				
				
					DefinitionsA domain of one color is a set of orthogonally interconnected points that includes no points occupied by the opposite color. Thus, a black domain can include black stones and empty points but not white stones.

A domain is alive if it includes a line of six orthogonally adjacent points in the same row or column. When the game is played on tiny boards, the required line length can be reduced.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point in a live domain of your color, then remove all enemy stones that are part of no live domains of their color.

The game ends when every point on the board is part of a live domain of exactly one color. The player with the higher score in the final position wins. Your score is the number of points in live domains of your color, plus komi in the case of White. The button is used to avoid ties.

				
				
					Komi and buttonKomi is the whole number of points that is added to White's score at the end of the game as compensation for playing second. Before the game starts, the first player chooses the value of komi, and then the second player chooses sides. Alternatively, experienced players may agree on a standard value for all games.

The button is a special token that is placed next to the board at the start of the game. It is only used when komi and the board size have the same parity (i.e. they are both odd or both even). On your turn, if neither player has taken the button yet, you may not pass, but you may take the button instead of making a board play. At the end of the game, a half point is added to the score of the player who has taken the button.

"Dream up your next reality, and we'll take care of all the details!"

In Interdimensional Travel Agency, each player plays as a travel agent responsible to manipulate an interdimensional gateway to create alternate realms explicitly tailored to various travelers' designs. At the beginning of the game, each agent must choose two Travelers to attempt to satisfy, in secret. On each turn, a player plays cards from their hand in one of three ways: As a dimensional "trait" that they configuring the gateway with, as an action that manipulates the gateway or players' hands, or as a discard to be replaced.

Once a Traveler is satisfied, it is revealed and added to the discard pile. The game is over when one player has satisfied both of their assigned Travelers!

This Depression-era party game is really a gambling game, but green wooden disks are provided as a game currency. The board has a central pot with a buff corridor from each of the four sides. In between are three concentric octagonal rings (Green outer, Grey middle, Orange inner). On these rings are spaces with 8 Up elevators, 8 Down elevators and 16 black circles.

There are 16 numbered wooden tokens and matching cardboard disks. Each player takes one of the tokens randomly from the cup and keeps it secret. The cardboard disks are distributed randomly onto the black circles on the board. The goal of the game is to land on and "collect" one's own disk number.

Each player antes one chip and moves onto the board via one of four Entrance Elevators. Players roll a red and a white die and may choose which to use (red goes left, white goes right). Elevators, dots and the buff corridors must be reached by exact throw. In a buff corridor, a player immediately tries to reach the pot by exact count (1,2 or 3 depending on ring); if successful, he collects the pot, and everyone antes again. In either case goes back outside the building.

When a player lands on and captures a numbered disk other than his own, the owner (if in play)must say "That's Me" and pay his captor five chips. Landing on another player "bumps" them and requires a fine of one chip to the pot and another to the bumper. The winner is the first to land on and collect his own numbered disk. The winner collects the pot plus 10 chips from each player.

This is a game about the life choices made during young adulthood. You’re all grown up and leaving home, but making good life choices can be harder than it seems! Your parents don’t think you can make it on your own but you’re going to prove them wrong. You have a job and an apartment, but you need furniture and your new friends love to party. You want to impress everyone but your parents are visiting for Christmas and you only have nine weeks to get ready.

Each turn is three days: Friday, Saturday and Sunday (the only days that matter!). Fridays are payday. You get paid, and life happens. Saturdays and Sundays are up to you. On each day you can: (a) Shop for furniture (b) Assemble furniture or (c) Throw a party

The game features a number of furniture stores (decks of cards) which you can choose to visit and browse but with limited stock it’s important to visit the right store to get the items you need. Remembering what you see in a given store is half the battle.

The winner is the player with the most Life Points at the end of Christmas. You can score two types of life points: Parent Points and Party Points. Most Parent Points are scored at the end of the game when your parents visit. Your parents are impressed by matching furniture and grown up décor. Party Points are scored throughout the game. You can choose to throw a party, but sometimes the fun happens when you least expect.

boardgame: Mobile Assault Card Game "Mao vs Chiang: Struggle for China"
description:
A fast action card game that combines strategic planning and tactical combat all in one. The best thing about the game is the portability. It has a small footprint, and can be played in 30mins to 60mins.

Mobile Assault Card Game "Mao vs Chang: Struggle for China" pits the nationalist forces of china against the communist forces, the game allows players to recreate the tension and stress of the Chinese Civil War post ww2. Each player starts with their five major cities on the table, and a hand of five cards. The cards in the game can either be unit cards representing regiment, and brigade sized units. Factories and population center cards that are used to produce two types of currency production, and manpower. By exhausting production, and manpower you may bring more combat units to bear against your enemy. the ultimate goal being to strike major blows to the enemy players five major cites and render their nation combat ineffective thus winning the game. But be ware the character cards such as Mao and Chiang these cards give all their units special buffs in combat and morale. But to counter this the player has one more type of card the political event cards, and propaganda cards these are used to assassinate the other players characters call on a major strategic bombing offensive, and boost a nations national will to fight. Good luck and may the best leader rule China.

The game consists of double-sided letter tiles and a bag. Each turn, the players draw four letters out of the bag and try to form a word. However, a word with four letters counts no points, unless it contains an "exotic" letter (marked with a . after the letter, as in the title of the game). But if you manage to form a very short word, you can keep tiles for use in later rounds. Each letter beyond the fourth in a word counts one additional point. 
After a certain number of rounds (depending on the number of players), each used exotic letter and each letter beyond the fourth in word counts a point, each unused letter tile means minus one point. The player with most points wins.

Advanced Platoon Leader (APL) takes the basic game Platoon Leader to a whole new level. Gone is the single map which is replaced with terrain cards that will allow you to configure a ton of map variations.  The detail and number of options have also been ramped up such that this game is guaranteed to be better than ever!

Description from the publisher:

For twenty-three seconds, the world's largest bank and backer of the world's most important currency goes dark. Trillions of credits are lost, stolen, or simply erased. Each corp blames the others. Economies, industries, lives collapse. And that’s just the beginning.

At the beginning of the Flashpoint Cycle, Android: Netrunner – 23 Seconds provides us our first glimpses of the Android world after the Twenty-Three Seconds incident. Wounded and desperate, the corps act more aggressively than ever. Even as Runners try to take advantage of the chaos, the corps begin to implement extremely powerful—often hostile—ice and operations that come with trash costs. And as the bodies start piling up, they unleash new Terminal operations, like Hard-Hitting News, that are so powerful they immediately drain the Corp's remaining clicks once they're played.

Trillions of credits, millions of lives, and the survival of the world's largest megacorps—the stakes in 23 Seconds and the Flashpoint Cycle are as high as they've ever been. Amid the chaos, who will be the first to emerge with a semblance of control? Will it be you?
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
In a competition to acquire favors from the Elemental Kings, players bid and secure influences under the framework of a shrine.

Components required for this game:

STANDARD DECK (DECK):    1
OINKS:    10 from each element for each player (Default)

The objective of this game scenario is to build a shrine with the highest influence among the 4 Elemental Kings. Influence is measured in terms of INFLUENCE points. Different hands of cards acquired during the game will score different INFLUENCE points and the player with the highest number of INFLUENCE points wins at the end of the game.

Players start the game by each drawing a card from the top of a common shuffled DECK. The player with the highest number starts first and the players get their turns in a clockwise manner. The drawn cards go back to the bottom of the DECK. In the case of ties, the tied players draw again till the turns are resolved.

At the beginning of a cycle of turns (from player back to the same player), the top card from the DECK is drawn and placed face up. Players can then take turns to bid and acquire this card using Oinks of the same element. The lowest bid is 1 Oink and players who pass will not be eligible to bid again in the same cycle of turns. Players may not bid for more than what they actually have in Oinks but the number of Oinks each player has can be hidden from other players during play. Each subsequent active bidder must offer a higher value than the previous one. The highest bidder gets the card and the next card is drawn from the DECK.

While the rest of the unsuccessful bidders get back their bids, the amount of Oinks from the successful bidder is divided equally among the rest of the players. This is so UNLESS one of the players is holding on to a card whose FACE VALUE corresponds with the value of the bid or the unit value of the bid E.g. In a bid of 28, 8 is the unit value. If more than one player has cards with the corresponding FACE VALUE, they share the bid amount equally. If any player has more cards of the same corresponding FACE VALUE than others in this situation, he/she will get the entire amount from the bid. After dividing among the players, any remaining amount will be pooled to be accumulated for the next bid. If any division of the bid amount cannot be resolved, it will remain in the pool.

Each time a player bids an amount with a unit value of 0 (E.g. 10, 20, 30), another card will be drawn in addition to the first one in the next bid as a set. Cards in a set cannot be bid separately. Players can bid using a mix of Oinks belonging to any element corresponding with the cards within the set. All acquired cards are placed in front of the players in clear view for others and a player may not bid an amount which has a unit value that corresponds to one of the FACE VALUE belonging to one of his/her cards. The game ends when there are no more cards to bid.

INFLUENCE points are calculated in terms of sets. For each single card, 1 point, each set of 2 cards of the same FACE value, 4 points, each set of 3 cards of the same FACE value, 9 points and a full set of 4 cards of the same FACE value, 16 points. A set of 5 cards of sequential numbers belonging to the same element will receive 25 points, for 6 cards, 36 points, 7 cards, 49 points, 8 cards, 64 points and 9 cards, 81 points. In the case of a tie, the player with more Oinks wins.

Mochizuki is a mercenary ronin for Ninja All-Stars. Ronin bring potent new abilities to a player’s team that are entirely unique to them. As mercenaries, ronin may be used by any player, on any team. This makes ronin popular choices for every team, regardless of clan, since they may be used to supplement a team’s weaknesses in battle or further augment their strengths.

Mochizuki is a potent ranged threat, able to lay down an uncommon amount of fire for a single model. Mochizuki provides excellent benefit to teams lacking long range firepower or as an answer to opponents with uncommonly fast, and difficult to pin down, ninja.

Mochizuki eschews her school’s preference for subtle poisons or the concealed tanto in favor of the shock and power of paired pistols. Preternaturally swift and silent, Mochizuki is famed for appearing an instant before she turns her guns upon her target in a blaze of flame and thunder, and then vanishing again into the smoke.

Miniature expansion for Ninja All-Stars.
Includes 1 additional character and Reference Card.

Heuler, an outstanding warrior with a temper, and the small child he calls Fieps are traveling over Kagejima and living the hard live of Ronin.
It is told he fell in love with a Kunoichi in his past.
He didn't know about her engagement with the prince of her clan until the birth of their son.
To keep his honor he forsake his name and swore vengeance to the prince.

From the publisher:

Stack them if you can! A clever game of quick thinking with numbers - race to be the first to stack all your number tiles in sequence.

1. Distribute all the tiles, face down, evenly amongst all players.
2. All players look at their own tiles and when they turn over a "1" place it face up in the middle of the players.
3. Players all play at the same time to stack them into piles of 1 to 10.

The aim is to get rid of all your tiles as quickly as you can. Players race to put 2 on a 1, 3 on a 2 and so on. Stacks complete from 1 to 10. The winner is the first player with 3 tiles or fewer left.

From the instruction manual:

Royal Gambit is the exciting new game of skill that combines the color of medieval pageantry with the thrills and fascination of ancient warfare.

Designed for two, three or four players, Royal Gambit is played on a board representing four ancient mountain kingdoms joined in the center by lowland meadows. Combat takes place among the kingdoms for possession of the entire area.

Each kingdom consists of a castle and an army of 10 mobile pieces: A King, four Nobles and five soldiers. Royal colors for the kingdoms are orange, black, white and yellow.

During the course of battle a King may be elevated to Emperor by capturing a rival kingdom whose forces have not been severely reduced. Title of Grand Emperor is bestowed on the winner of the game.

Welcome to Glimpse, a fun, family game of friendship!

In the game, two teams compete to make friends through acting, miming, singing, dancing, describing, and generally being silly. Each team consists of a "Glimpser" and the rest play their "Helpers". In front of you are sixteen character cards, each with different colored tokens. Unfortunately, your Glimpser is wearing special "Glimpse Glasses" which render them colorblind, so their Helpers must guide them to choosing friends that score points and avoiding those that cost points.

The Glimpser's role is to ask the right questions and pick the best friends. Helpers must sing, dance, describe or mime their way to victory, perhaps by doing "an impression of them at a party" and while they are there, describing "what they would order at a pizza restaurant"...

boardgame: Nemesis: The Battle of Abbey Ardenne, June 7 1944
description:
Nemesis: The Battle of Abbey Ardenne, June 7, 1944,  The battle of Abbey Ardenne was marked by a level of ferocity and savagery that would be continued for the rest of the war whenever these combatants met. The massacre of Canadian prisoners by the SS, who viewed such measures as rather common-place when they fought on the Eastern Front, was never forgotten or forgiven by the Canadians. The conduct of the fanatical 12th SS Hitlerjugend unit made it very clear what the war on the western front would become, and what the stakes were in this conflict.

Each copy of Nemesis  is comprised of the following:

--One 11 by 17 inch hex map with game charts and tables 
--52 double-sided, un-mounted counters 
--4 pages of rules with designer notes

Players use a standard deck of playing cards to activate their units and resolve some game events. A six-sided die is also needed to play the game. A complete game takes about 1.5 to 2 hours to complete. Designed for two players, Nemesis is very interactive, and is also very solitaire friendly. The game is designed by Paul Rohrbaugh with graphics by Bruce Yearian.

A cardset for Nemesis, that replaces the standard deck of playing cards called for in the rules, is also available. It features appropriate graphics and rules reminders on pertinent cards.
family: Series: Standard Deck Activation System (High Flying Dice Games)

description:
In Deus: Egypt, you are now the head of an extraordinary ancient civilization: Egypt. Can you lead this dynasty to expand and impose itself upon the surrounding lands?

This first expansion for Deus adds 96 building cards: 16 for each area of development. Players can use this deck in place of the one in the base game, or swap in one or more groups of 16 cards for those in the base game. Each group of 16 cards delivers a new set of rules and strategies.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Entour is a roll and move travel game with an educational objective. The board is a linear track of 38 destinations starting from a Port of Departure and ending in a Port of Arrival. There is no rhyme or reason to the order of the destinations, which are literally all over the map.

One player or non-player acts as the Tour Director, and holds the pack of Destination Cards (which list a variety of information about each destination. There are three piles of Itinerary Cards labelled Day 1, Day 3, and Day 6. Each player starts with $3,430. Each destination has a landing fee, starting at $300 for the first destination, Antigua, and rising to $1,000 by the final one, Tokyo.

Each player rolls two dice and advances that many destinations. First he pays the landing fee to the bank. Second, the Tour Director asks him what he knows about that destination. If the player supplies any of the information on the destination cards, he gets rewards. Third, he may buy the destinations Investment Certificate, which pays dividends each time a player lands there. Finally, he then takes an Itinerary Card, from the Day 1 pile on turn 1, Day 3 on turn 2, Day 6 on turn 3 and rotating from there.

When they reach the Port of Arrival, players must pay $500 and start over. The game ends as soon as one player runs out of money, and the winner is the one with the most money left.

boardgame: Runebound (Third Edition): Fall of the Dark Star – Scenario Pack
description:
The coming of the dark star has long been foretold among the dark prophets of Terrinoth. When it falls, they say, an age of darkness will overtake the world, and its coming shall be heralded by a rain of dark shards that corrupt every stout and honorable soul. This dark and corrupted Terrinoth becomes the focus of your struggle to survive with the Runebound (Third Edition): Fall of the Dark Star – Scenario Pack.

Like other Scenario Packs, Fall of the Dark Star introduces an entirely new scenario to your games of Runebound, offering a substantially different experience with thirty new adventure cards: ten for each of the three archetypes. You may battle a Starborn Specter or travel to the Howling Valley in search of a fallen dark shard, but no matter where you go a new deck of story cards continues to raise the stakes as you search for the dark shards that fall from the heavens. Finally, you'll find an entirely new hero, Zyla, a being from the Aenlong realm whose otherworldly powers have led her to become a hero of Terrinoth.

boardgame: Runebound (Third Edition): The Mountains Rise – Adventure Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

In the wilds of Terrinoth, strange beasts have begun to gather. Innocent townsfolk hurry home at night, barring their doors behind them, as bloodthirsty, savage creatures venture farther and farther from their lairs in search of their next meal. The realm has called upon its greatest heroes to venture into the hills and mountains to confront this threat. Only the most stalwart will return with Runebound (Third Edition): The Mountains Rise – Adventure Pack!

Like other Adventure Packs, The Mountains Rise offers new cards that can be incorporated into any existing scenario to dramatically change the flavor and feel. Fifteen new adventure cards (five per deck) introduce new enemies, events, and quests while twenty new asset cards and ten new skill cards give you dozens of new ways to customize your hero. Many of these new assets come with new combat tokens, some of which introduce new combat symbols to the game — ensuring that your Runebound battles will never be the same. Finally, you’ll gain an entirely new hero: Nanok of the Blade, a warrior who eschews armor in favor of the pure thrill of battle.

The brightly colored board makes this game look simple, but it is not. There are one red and one white dice, and a single 47-space track spiralling counter-clockwise and inward from the Start. The track includes 10 spaces where players draw Situation Cards that provide benefits or penalties. But the key to the game are the 21 spaces labelled Log Book and numbered 1, 2 or 3.

When landing on a Log Book space, the player rolls the dice twice more. The first red-white combination determines the section of the Log Book to consult (e.g. a 5 red, 2 white = section 52). A second roll, adding the two dice, gives the question number to be asked (e.g. a red 4 and white 3 = question # 7)

These are not simple questions. The easier ones are True/False or multiple choice, but some ask for detailed knowledge of flying rules (e.g. "What is wrong with a pilot flying a heading of 200 degrees at an altitude of 3,500 feet?", aeronautics ("Vfc/Mfc means ?") and even history ("What date did the airforce take delivery of the first P47D?").

To win, the player must make it to the second-last square and answer two questions correctly, then roll doubles and answer two final questions. The rules say that Fly-In "can be a learning tool to help the aspiring pilot be more aware of the world of flying".

This early American card game is a festival of puns for gardeners. It consists of an 8-page rule booklet and 72 small (3.25" x 1") yellow cards, each of which has a number, a phrase "Plant ....." and the question: "What will come up?"

The rules say it is best played by "a large and mixed company", with one person chosen as Keeper of the Pool, or more precisely holder of the answer book. The cards are distributed equally among the players along with an equal number of tokens. Players take turns asking the player to their left the question on one of their cards, the answer to which is always some kind of plant or flower. The Keeper of the Pool judges the answer against the book and if the answer is wrong, the responder pays the asker one of his tokens.

Some examples from the book: 
Plant a shepherd. What will come up? Phlox.
Plant a river. What will come up? A Currant.
Plant a man who won't pay his debts. What will come up? Willow.

There is one exceptional rule. "7. By vote of a majority of the players, rewards shall be paid from the pool for unusual brilliancy, and fines imposed for ludicrously erroneous answers. For example, when this game was first played, a young lady drawled out, in answer to the question 'Plant a menagerie. What will come up?' the single word 'Grapes'. Upon being made to realize that she intended to suggest the idea of 'Gray apes', the Keeper of the Pool voluntarily paid to her the sum of ten counters." Yes, that entire passage is in the rule booklet.

There is a girl in a hospital with only seven days to live. Can you save her?

Hourglass of Life is a roll-and-move game cooperative game with a traitor, and variable board consisting of cards that start face down. As players land on cards, they pick up the card then put a card from their hand in its place, optionally activating the card placed. Every time the turn goes back around to the start player, the days remaining ticks down.

For the "good" players to win, one of them needs to make it to the end of the track before time runs out — but the traitor wins if he makes it to the end first, or if time runs out.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
ABXY is a competitive 2-player simultaneous action selection game.  Players use hand-picked teams of extraordinary creatures to battle until one player has no one left.

Learn the game in 3 minutes, play a match in 5-15 minutes.

Players begin a standard match with a team of creature cards and 4 button cards representing A, B, X, and Y actions.  A single creature card is the active combatant for each player while all others sit on the side awaiting use.

All creatures in the game possess types, a weight, a passive ability, and a special action tied to one of the four button cards.

Each round, players secretly and simultaneously select a first and second action card.  Players reveal and resolve their first action and then reveal and resolve their second.  When directly contested actions occur, the lighter weight creature acts first.

By default, the A action deals damage, the B action prevents damage and then deals damage back to the attacker, the X action switches out a player's active combatant for a different one, and the Y action prevents future blocking or takes control of an opponent's attempt to switch.  A creature's special action entirely replaces the default effect of one action button.

Types have no innate effect on gameplay (so players don't need to remember special rules based on types) but are sometimes referenced in passive abilities or special actions.

The most important effect in the game is damage.  Rather than dealing with health totals, chits, dials, or dice, a creature is either damaged or it isn't.  When a creature becomes damaged, it is flipped over to its damaged side.  If a creature that is already damaged becomes damaged again, it is defeated.

When a player's last creature has been defeated, so has that player.

boardgame: Batman: Year One (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description:
New Job: Preserve the temporal continuity of an alternate universe.

New Boss: Step aside Bob. Oracle is in charge of this team.

New Mission: Remove every corrupt member of the Gotham City Police Department.

(No previous knowledge of Batman is required)

In a time of crisis, will you choose to remain calm or fall into the panic? This is a game about a doomsday prophecy. Players’ goal is to become the Prophet and brainwash everyone into believing that the world is ending. In this mind game, Everyone starts out with the same amount of sanity points. Once your sanity points are gone, you fall into a state of madness. The player who plays the role of the Prophet will spread his prophecy(madness), and convert every other player into the state of madness, thus becoming his follower. Once he turns everyone into his follower, he wins.

The rest of the players need to not only keep themselves from losing their sanity but at the same time, expose the Prophet’s lies. The player who destroys the Prophet’s last bit of sanity will become the next the Prophet. There is no trust in this game, as anyone who fights on your side might be your enemy next round; because the only way to win the game is to become the Prophet and eliminate the nonbelievers yourself.

Our game features a handful of controversial historical figures that had made the significant impact through the course of the world history, whether it is good or bad. Players will be able to play the role of a historical character of their choice, use their unique ability to either fight against the Prophet or be the Prophet themselves.

Now stay sane, and spread the madness.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Yagyu Jubei had heard tales of the Moonlight Tournament for years before finally daring the passage to Kagejima to experience the tournament for himself. He has informed the Sun Court that he will not be returning soon; there is simply too much to see, and Jubei has sworn to discover all he can.

Boer'n Bluf is a stunning tricky game for 2 to 10 players where it's all about trickery and deceit. Whose bluffing is best and who can outsmart the other players. But watch out! when someone caught you bluffing, you can start all over again. Try to get rid of your handcards as soon as possible. Be smart and challenge your opponents!

In the game you try to get rid of all the cards in your hand by laying them down on a pile. the first player starts with the Dog card with the golden bone. After that the second player puts his 2 on the pile and the next player his 3's. If you don't have the card that needs to be put down, you "bluff". If someone catches you, you take to whole pile of cards. The first one with no cards in his hand wins.

Devotion to duty is a hallmark of many on Kagejima. For Benkei, devotion to duty is a defining characteristic. While his martial skill serves him well, it is his utter devotion to the Moon Throne that imparts his famous resilience and fearlessness. They say that the gods could not move Benkei when he takes a stand in the Princess’s name.

The life of Momotaro is as strange as Kagejima. He was found as an infant, floating down the river in a giant peach, by an elderly farmer. Growing up on stories of predation by the Ijin lcan and their oni allies, he turned his interest and his tetsubo toward confronting that threat. His legend, strength, and resolve grow with every mission.

As a young boy, Ryu showed a talent in alchemy and was able to apprentice in this profession with his father. One fateful trip, however, saw them fall victim to a howling group of bandits. He survived, and wandering monks found him. As he grew in their monastery, Ryu soon became a strong and focused fighter with an understandable hatred for those who preyed on the weak.

The Ika are more than just fishermen and traders. They are charged by the Princess herself with watching and protecting Kagejima's coasts. Serious in demeanor, strategically clever, and ruthless in battle, Mizuchi's ship, the Honor of Ishika, and a hail of fire arrows are often the last things would-be invaders ever see.

Behind Football is a board game for 2-4 players over 9 years old.

It's a game designed for the family, football players and anyone who loves board games.
The game covers everything behind the scenes, i.e. the business of buying a club, hiring training staff, building a stadium and investing in club shares.

The ref's card and 3rd half card keep the atmosphere continuously tense, with lively comments and orders to be carried out.

On your way to success you’ll meet sponsors and the fan club. You will go through nerve-wracking matches and once in a while, will have to suffer the indignity of a touchline ban.

You will be taken through the entire emotional spectrum with Behind Football.

Who supports the best club?
How will MY club perform?
And “if only they’d bought him” or “fire the manager!” are familiar comments from any professional sofa manager.
The passion and belief that anyone can run a football club from the sofa has made a whole range of computer-based football manager games highly popular.
These two concepts are now finally brought together in the Christmas present of the year: “Behind Football”!

The game can be played by 2-4 “clubs”. The winner will be the club which gets the most from its initial investment in the form of the best manager, the best team and the biggest stadium!

PLAYING THE GAME

     Players place their pieces on the beginning square (1)
     players roll a dice or dices and move his piece as per the numbers indicated on the dice, clockwise. 
     when landing on a field, events are triggered and followed.


THE FLOW AND AIMS OF THE GAME IS TO

     control one or more clubs by buying, renting or selling them.
     develop stadiums.
     build team spirit.
     buy and sell shares in the club to make a profit.
     draw a "3rd half" card, with comments or good/bad instructions to be followed.
     draw "Rank" cards, allowing the manager/trainer to rise or fall in ranking.
     draw a "Referee" card with comments from the referee.
     lead the club through exiting matches.
     experience pressure from the fans or the press.
     become manager of the month.
     serve bans.
     participate in meetings with sponsors.
     complete your mission
     LET THE MONEY MACHINE FULFILL YOUR POTENTIAL AND DREAMS.

A combination memory and race game. Make sure you find the path through the jungle the fastest.

The 36 tiles are randomly placer face down on the board, and the 4 playing pieces are placed in each their own corner. Players take turns trying to find pairs. If no pair is found the tiles are turned back face down, otherwise the tiles are removed and a path through the jungle is formed. After each turn a player may move his playing piece as far as possible through the cleared paths. First to reach to opposite corner wins the game.

There is also an advanced game where snakes on the tiles can be place to block opponents from moving.

This game is based on the Dutch TV series "Baantjer".

From the publisher:
There have been a number of killings in the city and you are put on the case as a detective. The case consists of a suspect, a motive, a weapon, a corpse and a trace. Gather evidence at the scene as quick as possible, and trace the fingerprints of the suspect. Subject your opponents with some hard cross-examination and try to discover important information. Write down your findings with precision and finish the crime.

If you succeed as the best crime detective, you can call yourself "baantjer".

Both players play the role of a detective and a murder. The murder that everyone did consists of five hand cards. Those five cards are a suspect, motive, weapon, corpse and a trace. Every player tries to find out which cards the other players have in their hands. In a players turn, a player is allowed to ask a question to another player that can only be answered with "yes" or "no". If answered with "yes" you are allowed to ask another question, if answered with "no" the turn moves on to the next player. 
Asking questions is done with the cards laying face-up on the table with all the case items in a pattern. Every player writes down what he thinks is helpful for his investigation and tries to score points with the right questions.

The game ends when there is only one player left with cards in his hands and a murder that has not bin solved.

An educational boardgame about dinosaurs, made with the consultation of Cristiano Dal Sasso, a well-know italian paleontologist.

The game takes place in two phases: the hatching eggs and the battle of the dinosaurs
During the phase one you spin the wheel to get the cards of that particular type (i.e. quadrupedal, bipedal, carnivorous, herbivorous, etc.). At the end every player take the corresponding cards forming its own deck.
During the phase two you play the dinosaur cards to make them fight comparing the values written on the cards. The last player with a living dinosaur wins the game.

User Summary

Voennaya Taina ("The Military Secret") is a Soviet-era game for children aged 7-9 that is pure propaganda. It combines a snakes-and-ladders type track with a story in the rule book that plays out through the illustrations along the track.

It has its roots in a Russian fairy-tale, and shows strapping young lad starting life placidly farming with his father, seeing his father go off to war on horseback and come back wounded, and then following in his footsteps.

He goes off to war, has his comrades in the lines betrayed to the fascists, survives capture and prison and eventually triumphs over both the fascist trio and the cigar-smoking capitalist giving orders behind the scenes.

From the designer:

Dungeon Dealer generates random maps with enemies, traps, and obstacles. Draw a card, roll a 20 sided die to see which position the new map section goes, and see if you've been surprised by the enemy. For both free drawing and 3D tiles, every card is different. There are literally billions of ways to lay out a map using these unique cards. Both decks can be combined to create massive dungeons.

For solitary, cooperative, and competitive play, Dungeon Dealer is a flexible game with a number of rules:

Solitary gameplay

a.    Initially pulling a starting map card from the deck 
b.    hand drawing or placing three dimensional tiles in the shape of the map shown on the card
c.    placing character within that drawn grid map
d.    progressing from 1 inch square to 1 inch square within the perimeters of the tabletop roleplaying experience
e.    drawing a new card once the player is on an unexplored/open edge of the map
f.    rolling a 20 sided die to determine the north, east, south, or west orientation of that card 
g.    hand drawing or placing three dimensional tiles in the shape of the map shown on the new card
h.    discarding that used card
i.    reacting to the random placement of each asset on the card like enemies, obstacles, and traps
j.    repeating these turns in a cycle as the map grows
k.    ending game when all cards are drawn or all players perish.

Cooperative gameplay

a.    Initially pulling a starting map card from the deck 
b.    hand drawing or placing three dimensional tiles in the shape of the map shown on the card
c.    placing character or characters within that draw grid map
d.    progressing from 1 inch square to 1 inch square within the perimeters of the tabletop roleplaying experience
e.    drawing a new card once the player is on an unexplored/open edge of the map
f.    rolling a 20 sided die to determine the north, east, south, or west orientation of that card 
g.    hand drawing or placing three dimensional tiles in the shape of the map shown on the new card
h.    discarding that used card
i.    reacting to the random placement of each asset on the card like enemies, obstacles, and traps
j.    alternating these turns within a group of players until every player has had a turn
k.    repeating these turns in a cycle as the map grows
l.    ending game when all cards are drawn or all players perish.

Competitive gameplay for 2-10 players

a.    dealing out three cards to each player or team of players which they keep secret, no player or team of players can have more than three cards at a time
b.    starting on opposite ends of a blank grid map that is either a dry erase board or paper, or on a tabletop for three dimensional tile placement
c.    progressing from 1 inch square to 1 in square within the perimeters of the tabletop roleplaying experience
d.    drawing a card from the player’s hand once the player is on an unexplored/open edge of the map
e.    rolling a 20 sided die to determine the north, east, south, or west orientation of that card 
f.    hand drawing or placing three dimensional tiles in the shape of the map shown on the new card
g.    discarding that used card
h.    replacing used card with a new card from the deck
i.    alternating these turns within a group of players until every player has had a turn
j.    repeating these turns in a cycle as the map grows
k.    trading in an unwanted card in the player’s hand for a new card from the deck when the player rolls a 20 with the 20 sided die when orienting the card
l.    giving the unwanted card to a rival player
m.    progressing through the map, avoiding obstacles and vanquishing enemies 
n.    linking up the map in the middle so that all players meet 
o.    battling each other to see which player or team wins 
p.    ending game when all but one player or team survives.

Knowing that thousands of players have game components that require a game master to make playable, we wanted to create a system for randomly generating dungeons that does that work for you.

Keeping Dungeon Dealer simple was very important to us. One of the best things about tabletop roleplaying is how DIY the culture is. It’s a community built on making and sharing. By keeping this deck simple, we’ve allowed it to be adaptable in countless preexisting systems.

boardgame: Braves to Warriors: American Indian Rite of Passage
description:
INTRODUCTION
In America’s “Old West,” the young male braves of many Indian tribes faced difficult trials in order to become warriors. In Braves to Warriors, you and a friend will compete to win five such tests and gain the coveted feather of success in each. At day’s end, only the best brave in the tribe will henceforth be called: “Warrior”!

OBJECT OF THE GAME
Win the most Victory Points (VPs) during the five trials – swimming, tracking, riding, hunting and fighting – and finish the game with a higher score than your opponent.

COMPONENTS
36 cards (7 cards in five suits + one “Big Chief” card)
10 Feather tiles (2 tiles in five suits)

THE CARDS
There are five suits, each reflecting one of the five trials in the game—swimming, tracking, riding, hunting and fighting. Each suit has seven cards valued 1 to 7, which is used during trials. Your goal is to obtain 7 or more of the 13 Success Points (SPs) available during the trial. One SP is scored for each card in the trial suit you take, plus one SP for each value 7 card, during a round of quasi-trick taking.

Lonely Bears is a 2-4 player casual strategy card game that will have you arming Billy-No-Balloons, Drunk Frank and Scaredy-Cat Matt to the teeth, with Ridable Nukes, Horny Nordic Helmets and all manner of other crazy weapons and armour to defeat your opponents... if you don’t get caught by a game-changing event!  
The aim of the game is to kill your opponents bears by equipping them with cards drawn from the deck, balancing an offensive or defensive strategy tailored to the items you pick at random. Attack too soon and you'll be the target, but leave it too late and you'll be the victim!

Roar of War is a tactical card battle game for 2-4 players. You will play as one of the four race leaders and try to locate and defeat your rivals’ King or Queen. Each race has asymmetrical Attack / Defence / Counterattack Powers; therefore, a different strategy applies for each player. Orcs and Humans have balanced Attack and Defence Power whereas Elves have high Attack Power and Dwarves have high Defence Power. With each turn, players will have the chance to try different strategies and cover their King/Queen to win the game.

Roar of War packs pure Strategy Game mechanics and beautiful art in one box. Each additional copy of Roar of War adds 4 more players to the game and enables large team plays.

The goal is to eliminate your rivals by attacking and defeating their King or Queen cards. The last player with the King/Queen card wins the game.

The object of the game is for each player to visit two Lakes & Tarns, two Mountains & Fells and two Places of Interest before returning to their starting position before any other player.

Along the way players learn about this fascinating part of the UK whilst testing their map skills and local knowledge.

Fences add a tactical element to the game as players try to obstruct their opponents from making progress on their ramble, while the use of both dice adds a portion of luck! Ramble cards add unexpected problems and challenges into your adventure, putting a player’s best-laid plans to the test.

This is essentially a throw dice and move game with a few bells and whistles.
 The game consists of a 26x26 gridded map of the Lake District, 5 decks of cards (Mountains & Fells, Lakes & Tarns, Places of Interest, Ramble and Gate), 18 fences, coloured counters and 2 dice, one red, one white.
 Each player is initially allocated two each of the Mountains & Fells, Lakes & Tarns and Places of Interest cards. The winner is the first to visit each of their allocated destinations and return to the start.
  Movement is governed by the dice, the red die determining NS movement and the white die determining EW movement. The Ramble cards help/hinder movement, sometimes depending on the answer to a question.

boardgame: Massive Darkness: Heroes & Monster Set – Noble Warriors vs The Cockatrix

description:
Hvem finder det først? ("Who Finds it First?") - an action Board Game for all ages.

Very easy to learn - using four picture tiles at time, just match up each picture tile with photos of the same object on the board.  There are 500 different objects to choose from so it is not quite as easy as it sounds!

The first player to spot and shout out the item, wins the tile........however......the fun does not stop there.  Some of the tiles have "Action Flags" on them so during each round you can:

     Steal picture tiles from your opponents
     Win extra tiles from the big bag, to add to your pile
     In addition, some tiles are worth extra points at the end of the game - so keep them safe


This is a loud game and requiring fast actions and reactions!  Keep your wits about you and don't take any prisoners. Brothers and sisters you have been warned!

From the publisher:

How do you exceed the spectacle of DreadBall Ultimate? That was the question that the DGB asked themselves ahead of the new season of DreadBall, and it turned out that there wasn’t any one answer, so they decided to include them all! New teams, new MVPs, new coaching staff and a whole new set of rules mean that this isn’t an update – it’s a whole new era for the greatest sporting spectacle in the galaxy! While the new guys find their feet on the neodurium, the old guard find that they have to re-evaluate their tactics to take advantage of new rules and rewards that fundamentally alter the flow of the game. Coaching assistants can make or break a game, and there’s always the possibility of a fan or two getting more directly involved in proceedings.

The message to every player in the league is clear – go big or go home!

48 Page A4 Full Colour Rulebook
    Stats and background for four new teams – the Sphyr, the Rebs, the Hobgoblins and the Brokkrs.
    Stats and background for 7 famous MVPs
    Expanded rules for Fans, Coaches and Cheerleaders
    A new achievement system to push your players a step further
    A host of new abilities to add to your games.

From the publisher:

Forget what you think you know! The Xcitement of Xtreme comes to the DGB!

New teams, new rules and a whole lot of fresh havoc can only mean one thing – it’s new season time for the Galaxy’s Greatest Sport

The regs get relaxed for the first time ever to allow modified organics, tailor-made robots and even experimental limb grafts in the ongoing search for the perfect DreadBall player.

Exhibition Matches allow coaches to go wild, drafting in players of any shape, size or species for one off matches where anything goes. Think you could create the perfect combination of Guards, Jacks and Strikers? Now’s the time to prove it.

Free Agents auction their arms, tentacles and teeth to the highest bidder.
    New teams bring the Xtreme edge – or do they? (Mechanites, Mutants, Kalyshi, Convicts)
    New MVPs arrive to tip the balance for whichever team can afford them. (Brickbat' Vognar, Kailasa, Brute Force, 'Dead Man' Davitz)

Tear up the rules you think you knew – this is a whole new ball game.

From the publisher:
If there's one word that we can use to sum up the latest season of the Galaxy's Greatest Sport then it would be more! More weird and wonderful alien races join the team roster. More MVPs arrive to hire out their talents to the highest bidder. More Giant players add increased carnage to your matches. More is revealed of what goes on when the teams are off the pitch. And even more dirty tricks appear which a team can deploy to get ahead, both on and off the neodurium.

There's so much more packed into this season, the DGB issued a health warning! So what are you waiting for? Get out there and be the best you can be, rookie!

This expansion features the rules for GIANT players in your games, rules for hacking into the DGBs computer systems, a new Reputation rating for your teams, and a host of new abilities and MVPs to add to your games. It also introduces four new teams – the Crystallans, the Tsudochan, the Koris and the Ada-Lorana.

48 Page A4 Full Colour Rulebook
    Stats and background for four new teams – the Crystallans, the Tsudochan, the Koris and the Ada-Lorana.
    Stats and background for 4 famous MVPs
    Rules for enhancing your players
    Stats and background for Hector Weiss - Speedball MVP - and Judge Dredd DreadBall Referee

In Lairs, players play strange creatures that are excavating strongholds in the same mountain. Buried in this mountain  are powerful artifacts and treasures, that grant their finders great power.

​In the first portion of the game, players build long winding Lairs towards the center of the mountain.  All competing to to claim one of these prizes.

Once one player has taken the prize they become the Enemy of the other players. The remaining players become the Allies and must fight their way through each chamber of the Enemy’s lair, and duke it out in a climatic finale.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This Australian game is an early example of asymmetric warfare, pitting two Dingoes against a herd of 28 Sheep that are fleeing from their Paddock across a Wild Belt to the safety of their Sheep Fold.

Movement is akin to checkers, with a cross-shaped grid of orthogonal and diagonal lines. Sheep may move forwards or sideways, but may not move backwards or into a previously occupied position. The goal of the Sheep player is to fully occupy the Sheep Fold square across from him with nine Sheep - or to completely block movement by the Dingoes.

The two Dingoes can move in any direction and can capture a Sheep by jumping over it into an unoccupied square. The Dingo player wins if he can prevent the Sheep from making any further moves or if he captures 20 Sheep.

boardgame: Summoner Wars: Swamp Orcs – Second Summoner
description:
From the publisher:

We will grow, we will attack, and we will choke out our enemies' source of life. Natazga joins the Summoner Wars as the Swamp Orcs Second Summoner!

This Second Summoner deck is a full faction deck with its own Summoner, set-up card, Champions, Commons, Walls and Event Cards. It requires a Summoner Wars Master Set or Starter Set to play.

Set Contents:

Natazga - Summoner
Skrub - Champion
Sklursh - Champion
Zur Lak - Champion
7 Skull Takers - Common
6 Zealots - Common
5 Swamp Archers - Common
3 Erosion - Event
2 Battle Champion - Event
2 Fury of the Fen - Event
2 Quagmire - Event
3 Wall Cards
15 Vine Wall Cards
1 Summoner Reference Card

family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

boardgame: Summoner Wars: Shadow Elves – Second Summoner
description:
From the publisher:

The blood of our enemies will be the seed by which we conquer. Saturos joins the Summoner Wars as the Shadow Elves Second Summoner!

This Second Summoner deck is a full faction deck with its own Summoner, set-up card, Champions, Commons, Walls and Event Cards. It requires a Summoner Wars Master Set or Starter Set to play.

Set Contents:

Saturos - Summoner
        Zhant - Champion
        Shiiq - Champion
        Uriel - Champion
        6 Shadow Warriors - Common
        6 Vindicators - Common
        6 Blood Drakes - Common
        3 Revenge - Event
        3 Greater Blood Summon - Event
        2 Vanquish - Event
        1 Relentless Advance - Event
        3 Wall Cards
        1 Summoner Reference Card

boardgame: Summoner Wars: Sand Goblins – Second Summoner
description:
From the publisher:

An esteemed leader gathers a new crew of Sand Goblins. Scraven joins the Summoner Wars as the Sand Goblins Second Summoner!

This Second Summoner deck is a full faction deck with its own Summoner, set-up card, Champions, Commons, Walls and Event Cards. It requires a Summoner Wars Master Set or Starter Set to play.

Set Contents:
