description:
UNO Wild Jackpot is a spin on traditional UNO that incorporates a slot machine-style device and re-writable custom rules cards.

Before the game begins, players come up with their own unique custom rules and write them on the dry erase custom rules cards.  These are shuffled and inserted into the back of the slot machine.

During the game, which plays exactly like standard UNO, you can play a special Jackpot card to pull the lever on the slot machine.  This spins a random result on the center wheel, such as dispensing one of the custom rules to a player at the table who must then follow that rule.

Think Iron Chef and Apples to Apples, only you're playing with the intent to really eat those apples!

Fast paced, party style, game of cooking and socializing. A judge reveals a secret ingredient for a dish. Cooking players create different dishes using ingredient cards they're dealt. Players with the best mixes, a dish the judge enjoys, win. Food items used in dish are collected as points for those who played them. At the end of all rounds, the player with the most food cards stored as points is the winner.

Social Cooking is picture based and has been successfully played by kids as young as 3 years old. If you eat food and have an opinion on what taste good or bad, you can play.

Game is 3-4 players but scales up +4 players per deck. Can be played with 2 players with the use of a couple dummy hands.

Changeling is a hidden-identity game in which a small team of Changelings has infiltrated Ponyville to prepare the way for the invasion of Queen Chrysalis. The Pony team attempts to keep Ponyville intact through 5 Episodes, using teamwork, while the Changeling team wins by causing disasters and sowing 10 tokens worth of Mistrust.

Changeling's twist on other mafia-style games is the use of Character cards, which serve as both pass/fail votes and as special abilities. Players can call upon more than 60 of My Little Pony's most well-loved characters to aid them during negotiations. Even outed Changelings can swing the outcome of the game if they can use these powers to cause chaos.

Get ready to build an evolving structure full of twists and turns and snappy decisions!

In U-Turn, players take turns snapping together U-shaped bricks in an effort to surround one of the dots in their own color. Each side of the structure is considered as a flat plane when determining whether a dot is surrounded, so don't be deceived by depth! Turn and snap until you trap your dot.

Rat Race is a fun, fast, easy card game for all ages (10+) that combines single-step gameplay with memory and process of elimination to create an experience that everyone can enjoy. Each player is assigned a rat card with a rule on it that you—and only you—have to follow. The object is to win either by collecting in your hand all the game cards featuring your rat, lining up 3 of your rat on the board, or eliminating all the other players by correctly guessing which rat card they were dealt—or getting them to incorrectly guess yours! Pay attention, watch for tells, and don’t run out of cards as you work to outlast the others! The action is quick, the cards are plenty, and the race is on!

You're a newbie vampire in the most vamp-hostile town on Earth! A fast-paced competitive card & dice game for 3-6 players ages 10 & up.

Vampsylvania is a card and dice game set in a lighthearted world of monsters and mayhem. In Vampsylvania, you’re a newly-risen vampire ready to take the world by the throat. Unfortunately, you’ve risen in the most vampire-savvy place on Earth. Hunting here isn’t gonna be easy. The villagers are tough, and they’re loaded with anti-vamp weapons, skills, and allies. And to make matters worse, your fellow vamps keep trying to sabotage you, too!

Game play is pretty simple. Each round—which we call a Hunt—a new Villager is revealed. Players compete to capture the Villager by playing bonuses on themselves to raise their Vamp score, which increases their chances of capturing a Villager. But players can also complicate things for other each other. Spoiler cards can be used to lower a player’s Vamp score, or even take a player out completely…for now, anyway. Some spoilers make Villagers tougher, which makes things harder for everyone, but if you think you alone have the cards to pull it off, it might be just the edge you need.

Once everyone has played their cards, everyone rolls dice and adds the result to their Vamp score; if you get a high enough score, you capture the villager! Capture enough Villagers, and you win the game. Don’t get cocky, though. Villagers will often escape the Hunt without being captured, and end up in the Angry Mob pile. If enough Villagers end up in the Angry Mob, all the players lose and the Villagers win! Like we said, winning here isn’t gonna be easy.

Bolster is a turn-based, abstract strategy game where two lords meet on the battlefield; both maneuvering to eliminate the other's most powerful units. Players move their units of various strengths to constantly improve their own position, while weakening their opponent.  All units share the strength of adjacent allied units when resolving attacks/defense, and charge movements allow long chains of units to be moved to trap, manipulate, and eliminate their opponent’s units.

Each player takes one move per turn, and based on positions of allied and enemy pieces, attack and defense scores may be bolstered by adjacent units. Players may choose to have any number of allied units follow behind the piece moved.

The object of the game is to eliminate each of the opponents 3 most powerful units; when only one of the 3 most powerful units is left, that player must choose one of his remaining pieces to remove from play at the end of each turn.

(Note: This listing covers only the compilation versions of Last Will that include both the base game and the expansion; for a listing of the expansion-only versions, go to Last Will: Getting Sacked).

In his last will, your rich uncle stated that all of his millions will go to the nephew who can enjoy money the most. How to find out which nephew should be rich? You will each be given a large amount of money and whoever can spend it first will be the rightful heir. Visit the most exclusive theatres or eat in the most expensive restaurants. Buy old properties for the price of new ones and sell them as ruins. Host a huge party in your mansion or on your private boat. Spend like your life would depend on it. Spend to become rich! If you're the first to run through the money on hand, you'll receive the rest of his inheritance – oh, and win the game.

In Last Will, each player starts with a certain amount of money, an individual player board, two errand boys and two cards in some combination of properties and helpers. At the start of each round, lay out cards from the appropriate decks on the offering boards; the four regular decks are properties, companions, events, helpers and expenses, with special cards forming a deck of their own. The particular mix of cards varies by round and by the number of players.

Each player then chooses a plan for the round, with each plan indicating the number of cards the player draws (drawn immediately from the four regular decks in any combination), how many errand boys he can use later (one or two), the number of actions available to him that round, and his spot in the playing order that round. In the playing order for that round, players then take turns choosing an action with their errand boy(s), with those actions being:

Take a card on display and add it to your hand.
    Draw a card from any regular deck – This can be chosen only once by each player.
    Visit the opera and spend $2.
    Adjust the value modifiers in the property market.
    Take a player board extension, thereby giving you room to play more cards.

Players then take actions in the playing order for that round, with each player having as many actions as indicated on his plan. Actions let you play one-time events (which have a cost, possibly variable); helpers and recurring expenses (which are placed on your individual player board); and properties (which cost money and may depreciate over time). You can often play companions with events or recurring expenses – of course you should bring a date to the opera or a horse on your yacht! – to increase their cost. You can also use actions to activate cards on your player board, possibly with one or more companions and always with the goal of spending money. Helpers and special cards can provide you with unique powers to further boost your profligacy.

At the end of each round, you must discard down to two cards in hand, and properties that can depreciate do so; this is good as a player cannot go bankrupt if he owns properties, and the only way to get rid of properties is to sell them, which regretably puts money back in your hands unless the depreciation was intense or you manipulate the market.

If a player has no money and no properties, he declares bankruptcy and the game ends at the conclusion of that round; otherwise the game ends after seven rounds. The player who has the least money (or even who is most in debt) wins.


In Last Will: Getting Sacked, the lawyers have found a hidden clause in your uncle's last will. Spending money faster than all your relatives will not be enough. To inherit your uncle's fortune, you must also lose your job! After all, your uncle's heritage can't go to someone who's employed!

With this expansion added to Last Will, each player begins the game with a job card that provides him with a regular income each turn and details a number of things the player must do in order to get fired. Unlike in real life, it can be difficult to persuade your employer to give you the old heave-ho. When you miss work, he's inclined to be charitable because he liked your uncle and he's sorry for your loss. To make him angry, you need to get caught living it up when you should be working. He won't sack you straight away, but he will at least dock your salary.

Once per round, you are able to reduce your income by completing one of the things listed on the job card, earning you a demotion. Even your kindly old boss has his limits, though; when you earn enough demotions to cancel out every line of income, you can discard your employment card. Congratulations: You have been sacked and are one step closer to winning the game!

You will also have a chance to find yourself a good, demanding wife. A wedding is such a romantic way to spend piles of money in a hurry, right? The expansion has six nuptial cards, so get down on one knee and ask the question!

Also, with this expansion, each game can have a different planning board to bring additional variety to the game. When you set up the game boards, you randomly select the eight plan tiles that will be used in the game.

Driven from their Ancestral Homelands in the frozen north, four Orc tribes have assembled as a mighty horde.

Spring has arrived and the ice passes have melted.  You must lead your people to carve out a new home in the rich realms of Man. Scout wisely, avoid enemy patrols, capture settlements and duel rival Champions to prove your worth to the tribal Shaman.

A card based invasion game for 2-4 players, with a choice of 8 Champions amongst the tribes.  Gather units into your Warband and assault Settlements in a diceless combat system that uses Orcs as a resource as your throw the might of the Horde against the walls of human Settlements.

Duel your fellow Champions for Honour and Glory and establish Dominance in the Horde to prove your might.  Once the Winter snows come the Shaman of the Orcs will proclaim one Champion as LORD OF THE HORDE!

boardgame: Vampire: The Eternal Struggle – Heirs to the Blood
description:
White Wolf's seventeenth expansion for Vampire: The Eternal Struggle is Heirs to the Blood, which focuses on the bloodlines and infernalists.

Each booster pack contains 11 cards randomly selected from a set of 168 all-new cards consisting of 54 rares, 58 vampires, and 56 commons.

The set also features 4 non-random preconstructed decks: Gargoyle, Kiasyd, Salubri antitribu and Samedi, which contain even more cards and are designed to get beginning players started right away. All the new cards in the preconstructed decks are also found in the boosters, and naturally each preconstructed deck contains reprints of vampires and library cards from earlier sets.


New Stuff

Research Area: Some cards may move cards to a special staging area called the research area. The cards in your research area are face down and out of play; they can be affected only by cards and effects that say so explicitly.
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The City of Corsairs
description:
Description from the publisher:

"Looking thither they cried in dismay; for black against the glittering stream they beheld a fleet borne up on the wind: dromunds, and ships of great draught with many oars, and with black sails bellying in the breeze." –J.R.R. Tolkien, The Return of the King

The City of Corsairs is the thrilling conclusion to the Dream-chaser cycle of Adventure Packs for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game! Its new scenario grants your heroes the opportunity to board the Dream-chaser one last time, set sail for the lands of Umbar, and conclude your business with the Corsairs once and for all. But will you truly be ready for the dangers that await you? Featuring not one, but two new encounter sets, The City of Corsairs challenges you to navigate Umbar's coastal waters and battle past Corsair Ships before you set foot on land to pursue the Númenorean artifact that was stolen from you. Don't expect this task to be easy, though! In their lands, the Corsairs you'll face are deadlier than ever, and the Númenorean artifact grants them a strange and corrupting power.

Fortunately, you don't have to undertake this journey alone. Not only does The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game allow you to adventure with up to as many as three of your friends, but it allows you to fill your deck with numerous allies and as many as three heroes. And you find new options for both within The City of Corsairs, including a host of Gondor allies and one of the noblest and most notable names in The Lord of the Rings, Prince Imrahil, who arrives as a Tactics hero, ready for battle.

description:
Tower defense game themes of Three Kingdoms.

The player become a strategist of Shu. player need to defend player's country from the Wei and Wu army that appear one after another. Protect the base from which the enemy comes to attack, the player has to fight off the enemy if the country will have to decline.
 Really, the player defeat all of the army, or will be able to pass through to protect the country?
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
In The Racing Line you steer a car token around a course in a succession of straight lines. The length of those lines is determined by your current speed as measured on a special ruler.

You're limited in how much you can change your speed between turns so approaching corners can be very tricky unless you get them just right. 
 The game also encourages you to pick-up various "Power-up" tokens scattered through the course:  Missiles, oil, nitro gas, emp bombs and more!Use these against your enemies, or just bump them off the track in your quest for victory!

Motherland is a game of suspicion and accusation, paranoia and groupthink.  Citizens must stay alert to the Spies among them, while Spies collectively conspire against the Citizens. All in the name of the glorious Motherland.

The dreaded Gulag awaits!

Motherland is a hidden role game where players secretly take on the role of either citizen or spy. Under the watch of the Block Captain, each round takes place in three phases: Gossip (players discuss who should be sent to the Gulag), Troika (a team of three players votes on who to send to the Gulag), and Darkness (Spies that have not already been sent to the Gulag secretly notify the Block Captain of an additional player that gets sent to the Gulag by force in the night). Gameplay continues repeating rounds until only two players remain outside of the Gulag. If only citizens remain, the citizens win the game. If even one spy remains free, the spies win.

Ashes: The Roaring Rose, which features new Phoenixborn Leo Sunshadow, is a full, ready-to-play Ashes expansion deck including three conjurations. Leo requires ten Charms (pink) dice to play, which are included in the Ashes: Rise of the Phoenixborn base set.

description:
User summary
ログズ (LOGS) is an abstract game for two players. Each turn, you may place your pin on a 9x9 grid of intersections, or make a special move using your large pin. You can play two types of game with it. The game was originally published in 2015, then revised and re-issued in 2016 with the "bloom" rules also added.

(1)LOGS connect
The goal is to build a complete route on the board from your end to your opponent's end by only your own pins. You may place your pin adjacent to any of your existing pin, or remove your opponent's pin by placing your large pin if adjacent to any of your existing pins.

(2)LOGS bloom
You score victory points by orthogonally surrounding an intersection with your pins, then placing your large pin on that intersection. You may place your pin on any vacant intersection.

Publisher's summary
"LOGS"はアブストラクトゲームで、2つのゲームが遊べます。
1つは"LOGS connect"。プレイヤーはお互いの対辺を先に繋げることを目指します。大きなコマと相手の普通のコマを取りかえることができるルールが特徴です。
もう一つは"LOGS bloom"。陣取りした場所に、多くの点数を得ることが目的です。普通のコマで囲まれた場所の中心に大きなコマを置けば得点になります。

Ashes: Orrick Gilstream & Gobi Sunshield is a set of four cards that can be added to the Ashes base game, with Orrick Gilstream being a Phoenixborn who has three copies of Gobi Sunshield to be used in a deck of your own creation.

4x Nature dice

description:
The conflict between King Charles I and Parliament had come to an irreparable breach by the time the King raised his royal standard at Nottingham. There was now war between the King and his Parliament and the country was divided in its loyalties. The Royalists, who favor the King’s cause, begin the war with many excellent troops and an advantage in cavalry troops, but have initial limited financial support from mostly towns and castles. Parliament starts with a somewhat reliable force in the London Trained Bands and a more solid financial backing from most of the kingdom’s cities, but initial cavalry and leaders do not inspire confidence. Of course, the fortunes of war can change these circumstances over time.


     Two Player game refighting what is often referred to as the First English Civil War. This Renaissance conflict is often identified in the “Pike and Shot” category, since musketeers fought around blocks of pike men for protection from cavalry. 
     Troop Types are represented with abstract counters. Infantry counters denote up to 1500 men and cavalry units represent up to 1000 men. There are units of dragoons and artillery trains as well. Special and elite units are also represented, such as the Northern Horse, Cornish Infantry, Lifeguards, and Hasselrigg’s Lobsters. Both sides can also press Levies into service when necessary. 
     Game Boards display a map of England and most of Scotland. It is hex based with terrain within each and many prominent towns, cities, manor houses and castles are portrayed. Some of these locations have high fortification ratings and some do not. Necessary playing tables are located on the board for ease of play and necessary reference charts are provided to assist the players.  
     Noteworthy Commanders are represented in the game, with holding boxes for leaders of larger armies. 
     Multiple Scenarios to play depending on when players want to begin and how long they want to play.  


Game Features


     Leader Driven - Play is divided into turns, but players move alternately, sometimes one going before the other a few times depending on circumstances. Leaders are the core to the game system with all actions being mostly conducted by them. Leaders have ability ratings for strategy and battle. Some also have special characteristics peculiar to them such as Impetuous, Financial Resources, Cavalry Commander, etc.  
     Multiple Events Combination - These continuously have an effect on the game. This is provided by a combination of the turn chart, a random events table, and event counters. Event counters are played immediately on locations, leaders, or units and at times are deployed by the choice of a player. 
     Strategy Counters - These can be rolled for by leaders, and the larger their rating the better. This provides some uncertainty as well as advantages and disadvantages as appropriate to each case. Strategy counters can be played immediately or held for the duration of the turn for opportune moments.
     Variable Combat System - Each side has a respective Battle Deck to add more panache to the Combat Result Table (CRT). The CRT gauges results based upon the size of the battle, so it is not one size fits all. 
     Scotland Shown - Many games on the English Civil War focus solely England and Wales. The necessary parts of Scotland are shown here to include the effects of Montrose’s campaign on the war.
     Attractive Coin Counters - Provided to help track paying for the cost of war.

boardgame: The Daring & the Doomed: The Elder Dooms
description: Prepare yourself for the mind-bending madness of The Elder Dooms! This Cthulhu/Zombie inspired expansion adds more tentacles and craziness to your game with 74 more cards!

Description from the publisher:

It may be possible to learn the alphabet without the dungeon-delving excitement of Munchkin...but why would you want to? With The Munchkin Alphabet Coloring Book, you'll delight in reading aloud the 26 alphabet verses written by Steve Jackson. Then your little munchkins (or you!) can color the 26 all-new illustrations by Munchkin fan-favorite John Kovalic.

The book also includes ten new Munchkin cards, so you can surprise your friends more easily than jumping out of a closet and shouting, "Abcdef!" From now on, ABC stands for Attack, Backstab, and Color!

From the publisher:
A broom, rotating slippers, meatloaf explosives and aggressive grannies!
All this is Angry Granny!!!
A game up to six players where you have to drive your grandmother in a fight full of explosives and grandchildren of Alzheimer attacks with purpose of winning the coveted old-age pension!!

Angry Granny is an easy game to learn but more and more challenging as they play! You have to choose very carefully the grandmother to attack because it might hide some surprises ...
in short, this is a game in order to put an end to even the most solid friendships!.
Grandmothers of Angry Granny will change forever!!
There are no more grandmothers of a time!

IN THE BOX: 
18 Grandma cards
100 action cards
6 staples scorecard

The goal of the game is to defeat other players reducing their grandmas' score to zero! The last player who survives with his grandma is the winner.

Each turn consists in three phases:

Supplying phase: The turn player take a card from the fighting deck. He  reveals it to everyone and then he holds it among his cards.

Main phase: The turn player can play cards of his hand respecting a sequenze of colors in the use of the cards (first yellow, then red and finally purple cards).

End Phase: Every player can place a covered defence card and then he respects the number of three cards in his hand drawing new cards from the fighting deck or discarding redundant cards .

boardgame: UNO: Batman v Superman – Dawn of Justice
description: Similar to regular UNO except there are a few special Justice cards. Based on the 2016 movie Batman Vs. Superman.

boardgame: Kill Doctor Lucky: The Secret Lair of Doctor Lucky
description:
Description from the publisher:

Kill Doctor Lucky: The Secret Lair of Doctor Lucky is an alternate board expansion for Kill Doctor Lucky set in the Spy Game universe! On this board, you are all henchmen of the arch villain Doctor Lucky, chasing a random spy around the secret lair, and trying to kill him.

Players play cards and move colored hares on the board. Until the very end nobody knows which hare belongs to which opponent. The game has simple rules and victory is decided by tactics and the ability to bluff.

In Zające na łące ("Hares in the meadow") every player controls two out of four hares. It may happen that some hares are controlled by two players. On a turn a player chooses one card from hand and moves proper hare.

The game ends after 3. round. At the end of each round hares are awarded points. Hares can also earn points during the race by landing on a carrot. The player who has the most point at the end of the game is the winner.

Too Schuss is a boardgame that takes place in the Ski Resort of Tignes in France. The board represent the map of the ski resort.

Goal: Be the first player to end your skying day with the higher score.

Game is a race where you need to finish the day as quick as possible while optimizing your path down the ski trail to score points.

A lighter version of the rules is available for kids or player that want an easyer game.

Sure, playing with the sponsors and teams that are presented in the rulebooks is fun, but Xtreme is all about breaking the mould, and what better way to do that than by creating your own sponsor and their own band of misfits from the ground up and throwing them into the fray?

This 80 page full colour book will show you how, and set you on the path to true Xtreme greatness!

Use any DreadBall Team in DreadBall Xtreme
    Build your own custom DreadBall Team from ANY player in the DreadBall range
    Rules for 4 New Sponsors, including: Kraato Gon, Dr Kain, The Shojuun and Rathmar.
    Build your own custom Xtreme Sponsor

Xtreme’s lack of rules and referees means an infinite amount of variety for the fans, not just in terms of the makeup of the teams, and the scattering of obstacles on the pitch, but the very shape of the playing area itself and even how many teams take part. Xtreme multi-team games make DreadBall Ultimate look like a Zee tea party. Play them out on some of the most infamous layouts ever devised, or come up with your own. Just remember, this isn’t a game, it’s war on a pitch.

This expansion includes:

24 Page Full Colour Xpansion Rulebook, which includes rules for:

Customizable Pitches
    Dangerous New Traps
    Multi-player Games
    Medi-bots

A complete multi-part pitch, made of thick card tiles.

Xpansion Counters

Four Giant Players

1 Plastic Krastavor Giant miniature
    1 Plastic Chovar Giant miniature
    1 Plastic Sann-Gar Giant miniature
    1 Plastic Iron Ancestor Giant miniatures

The ultimate tour for the Galaxy’s Greatest Sport, as teams venture across the quarantined Hazard System, vying it out for the Challenge Cup Title. From games played in utter darkness to matches dodging deadly lava flows, it isn’t just the other team that presents a challenge in this contest! Four brutal new MVPs complete the excitement. Your campaigns will never be the same again!

This set contains:

40 Page DreadBall Challenge Cup Rulebook
    Challenge Cup Map
    Challenge Cup Card Deck
    Challenge Cup Counter Sheet
    MVP Alo-khan
    MVP Blitz
    MVP Eko'o
    MVP Hexan

Copies of DreadBall, DreadBall Xtreme and the DreadBall Xtreme Xpansion will be required to use the various parts of this set.

Two to five players race to the hive in the center of the board with both their characters. The active player rolls two color dice. If they show different colors, the player picks one of them and each player can move their pieces to an adjacent flower of that color (if there is one). If both show the same color, only the active player gets to move two fields. If one of them shows a spiderweb, the player can place one of the spiders into another player's field to block the path. Two spiderwebs mean the player can move both spiders and move by two flowers on top of it. 
First player who gets both pieces into the hive is the winner.

Diamonds: The Thief, a mini-expansion for Diamonds, consists of a 60mm circular token ("The Thief") that moves around the table whenever the clubs suit action is taken. Note that this action is different when playing with Diamonds: The Thief rules.

NOTE: This new clubs suit action is mandatory for all official Diamonds Tournaments starting after the Gen Con 2016 tournament.

This is the first expansion for Backstab.

The Danse Macabre expansion include 4 completely new characters with unique abilities and introduce the new "events" card mechanism to push the plots and double dealing at a whole new level!

From the publisher:

Prepare to top up your troops, call in reinforcements and commit your reserves for your favourite Airfix Battles forces, with this brand new set of  24 bonus Force Cards for the Airfix Battles wargame.

With a huge variety of British, American and German troops, it features specialists like the Allied Bazooka team, the 6-pounder anti-tank gun, a ten man squad of Tommies with anti-tank Piats, as well as experienced leaders like the Commando Sergeant, Allied Captain and unit of Veterans.

You'll also find a huge variety of specialist Allied vehicles such as the Sherman Calliope tank with its rocket launchers, the M8 Greyhound armoured car, or the Churchill Crocodile battle tank, plus a host of classic world war two vehicles.

For the Axis forces you'll be able to deploy an array of fearsome vehicles and armour like Michael Wittman's Tiger, featuring the ace German tank commander, the Panther battle tank, the SDKFZ 222 armoured car and the deadly Tiger II King Tiger!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Kiddie Cards is described on the box as "Colorful, Educational, Entertaining Cards for Children. The 55-card deck has backs in five different colors (red, orange, yellow, blue and green), while the front of each card has an illustration of what the rules call "items which surround every child in his everyday life".

The cards are clearly aimed at what the rule card calls "small fry", and are primarily educational and aimed at very young children.

The game does not come with fixed rules, and suggests that children may wish to devise their own games. The one game suggested is to deal the cards equally to the players, and have each child discard in turn. The next child may not discard unless he or she identifies the image on that card.

Alex G. Malloy (American Games: Comprehensive Collector's Guide; Krause 1999) dates this as circa 1970s, but the illustrations of household appliances and vehicles have more of a 1950s look to them.
Roll-and-move where players are trying to reach the Princess with both their characters, Mario and Luigi. Player pawns return to start if their square is reached by another player piece or Koopa.
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
Each player has his own game board and four pawns. The goal of the game is to get all meeples off his board, either by moving them past space 49 or by getting them on the board of another player. Movement is determined by two dice. The player may choose to move two pawns with the result of each die, or one meeple with the added result of both dice. If a meeple gets on a space of it's own color it stays there, but if it gets on the space of another color, it moves to the game board of this color onto the space of the same number and is now controlled by the player of this color. Instead of rolling the dies, he may move a meeple of his own color ten spaces ahead, if it lands on a space of his own color.

The first player to get all pawns off his board, wins the game.

From the series of quiz games that tests players' knowledge of different Czech regions.

In this game the players will try their knowledge of Prague in four different categories: History, Culture, Places of Interest and Miscellaneous.

Players roll the die and move around the board, landing on a spot where they will answer a question from a card. If they answer correctly, they get to keep the card. Whoever collects cards from all categories first, is the winner.

boardgame: Creative Differences: The Video Game Development Card Game
description:
Take the role of a Video Games development Studio. Employ designers, artists and programmers to make the best game you can.

2-4 Players hire staff, give them ideas, then get those ideas into the game - whilst avoiding industrial espionage from your opponents.

Also, use the free app after the game to see how your game comes out.

Gameplay:  The game uses multi-use cards so that all of the staff that are available to hire can also be used as money - to pay for the staff - or as ideas - to increase the likelyhood that they will be able to add something to your game design.

On a player's turn they use their 2 actions to either draw new cards, put an employee into play (paying with cards,) add an idea, or roll the dice to get that employees idea into the game.

This is augmented by a wide variety of action cards that will help or hinder your and your opponent's progress respectively.  For instance, hire an employee into a rival player's company to employ some corporate espionage!

You can also gain benefits from reaching communal and private milestones / achievements as the game progresses.

Project Nos is a real time dice drag race that attempts to capture the high performance rush of street racing.

1-4 Players start the game with a car and 3 dice and modify their cars by selecting modification cards and taking the associated dice.

A six card track is laid out and, on go, players roll two dice at a time trying to match the number on the track. Once a number matches, player places it in their space on the track card and grabs a new dice.

First player to complete the track wins!

The Big Apple is a “building” game for from 2 to 4 players.  Players recreate the “history” of New York City, from its earliest years up to modern times, by slowly constructing more and bigger buildings, parks and cultural centers. Each player is looking to amass wealth and political power, all leading to his being elected Mayor of New York.

Players win by earning Victory Points.  There are several paths to gaining VP - wealth and political power are the two main roads - and players must choose which of these they will take to win.

The Big Apple requires players to master various types of games all at the same time. The building portion of the game requires players to adapt spatially to the changing environs for in each of the three eras of the game. In addition there is a population management aspect where new immigrants are constantly coming ashore and need to find housing. This leads to a constant friction between the building of housing to hold voters... I mean population versus the creation of lucrative businesses versus the migration of the populace into the boroughs of Brooklyn, Queens & The Bronx. Finally the game is full of dirty political tricks that can set your opposition back and create opportunities for you.

The Big Apple is for 2-4 players and plays in about 45 minutes per player. It is easy to learn but full of nuances that make mastery slightly more difficult.

boardgame: Are You Chicken? (腰抜けやろう!!)
description:
Publisher's summary
腰抜けやろう!! (Koshinuke Yarou!!),
 Are You Chicken!! is a game of chicken with simple rules. 
(The publisher is the designer of the game).

Each Player takes 1 card from the DRAW pile and lines it up in front of themselves 
to form the Chicken Road.

Compare the TOTAL COUNT of the numbers on the cards with other players.

The player who gets most number of cards lined up with the TOTAL COUNT stated 
on the cards within [5] wins the round and scores points.

Play multiple rounds.
The first player to get to 20 points wins the overall game.
designers: Tsuyoshi Hashiguchi (はしぐち つよし)
publishers: GALLERY OUCHI (ギャラリーおうち)

description:
Royal Steward is a medieval period strategy board game.  The purpose of the game is to impress The King so that he will appoint you as his Royal Steward. You impress him by performing various actions efficiently, building your wealth, and showing you are a knowledgeable and have great diversity.

You get to choose among 55 Occupations to play (Baker, Miner, Locksmith, Shipwright, etc.). Some Occupations obtain raw resources (cotton, gold ore, oak, etc.), while others convert resources (gold ore->pure gold, oak->spear, gold necklace->gold diamond necklace, etc.). Some even just perform services for prestige points (bard singing, physician curing the sick, etc.).

The game takes place over a year period, with 1 Occupation being used per month. Therefore, you have 12 months (turns) to impress The King and prove he should appoint you as his royal steward.

This expansion for the board game of Broom Service is contained in Broom Service: The Card Game.

It contains 16 special cards which you shuffle and divide into 2 piles. If you play a role card cowardly, then instead of executing the action, you may take 1 of the visible special cards or activate 1 of your special cards you took on a previous turn.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Nov 2016: On this page, you find a Print and Play prototype version of my barbarian card game. I recently found a publisher, signed a license agreement, the game will be published at some point in 2017. There'll be changes in artworks, rules and layout, so you're not looking at the "final version" here.

From the designer:

Grab some friends and compete on an epic quest for glory.
With 108 cards and 6 dice, Barbaria fits in every pocket and provides a wonderful beer and pretzel experience.

It is my take on the dungeon crawler genre: I stripped off all complicated and redundant components, like game boards, miniatures, long rulebooks, hundreds of markers. 
It is designed to be a quick, easy and fun game, while still mainstaining the core: you control a hero, get stronger, fight monsters and find treasure, and you roll a lot of dice.

All 100+ illustrations are hand drawn and colored in aquarel.
I'd appreciate feedback! :)

This game by Technical Term Game Company of Japan comes packaged with Iron Lady's Fleet 2, a remake of the company's 2007 game on the Falklands war. Like others in the series (including Antarctic Fleet and 4th Fleet), it is based on the Victory Games Fleet System of tactical and operational naval warfare, using individual named naval ships, airplane squadrons, and smaller coastal patrol combatants.

This game deals with potential naval conflict between Ecuador and an allied Colombia and Peru. As a result, it has only a single sheet of counters, and splits an Antarctic Fleet-size map in half, with ILF2 on the other half.

Each player in Zosimos is given four gems of five different colors (20 gems total) to start. The 24-card deck of potion cards sits in the middle of 2-4 players. On a player's turn, they peek at the top card of the deck and make a bid using gems of the same color for that card. Each card represents one of the colored gems and a number between 1-6. Numbers 1-4 have one potion on a card of a single color; numbers 5-6 have two potions of a single color.

When a player makes a bid for a card, they are bidding with the gems they currently have. When all players but one has passed, the final bidder reveals the card and either pays the difference if they bid over the number, or gets paid the difference if they underbid the number. The card is free if their bid is exactly the same as the number.

At the end of the game, players multiply the number of potions on their cards by the number of gems they have of that color, then add the colors up. It becomes very important to try and bluff your opponents into losing all their gems or allowing you to grab a card for cheaper than you should have gotten it.

The Anagram Word Game.

Expand your vocabulary and find out how anagrammatically correct you can be!

The title of this early 19th century game translates as the Forfeit Game. It comes in two parts: a set of 47 cards and a 32-page booklet. The booklet has rules for 20 different party-style games. These include Mufti-spiel (like Simon Says), Das Klingel-Spiel (Musical Chairs), Das blinde Ruh-spiel (Blind Man’s Buff), Die stummen Spieler oder Pantomime (Charades), and a set of terrific Teutonic tongue twisters.

What these games have in common is that at least one player may or must fail, and that’s where the cards come in. The company selects one of their number to act as the warden and choose which card to inflict on each loser. Each card includes one to three challenges. Card 39, for instance, orders that the player either “have one or two persons of the opposite sex kiss your eyes” or “confess which Commandment you have sinned against most often” or “kiss the most beautiful person in the room”.

Described on the box as entertaining and funny, the booklet takes pains to assure readers that the games and forfeits include “nothing insulting, much less offensive, but only for the humorous maintenance of youth”.

The cards and booklet are printed in a pre-umlaut Fraktur script, and the cards appear to have been hand-cut, since they vary in width and length by as much as 1/8 inch, and by as much as 3/32 inch from top to bottom of a given card. The publisher was an art dealer as well as publisher based in Stuttgart in the early to mid-1800s.

Prohibition has settled over the United States. Liquor is outlawed.  But people still want it.  And there are people who can supply it.

2-4 Players are bootleggers running whiskey from stills in the Smokey Mountains to thirsty people in towns and cities below.  Each bootlegger is competing to build a bootleg empire by controlling the highest dollar value of towns and cities across 4 states in 15 turns.

Working against them are Sheriffs and Federal Revenue agents whose cars move around the board using Movement Cards and, when a bootlegger is caught, taking actions against them by using Action Cards.

Make money selling your whiskey so you can buy more cars, stills, and vehicle improvements.  Each car gets a driver with a skill and cars can be upgraded to go further, carry more whiskey and protect themselves against Sheriffs and Revenuers or other bootleggers.

boardgame: Tease: The Liberating Game for Couples and Groups
description:
Tease combines a classic drinking game with an array of sexy and titillating features, such as massage, lap-dance and sexy fantasies. Designed to seduce the senses, this experimental game can be played between you and your loved one; or if you are feeling more adventurous it can also be played with up to two other like-minded couples or between 2 to 6 adult players. Tease warms you up with five levels of rewards and forfeits, releasing your inhibitions and removing your clothes as you progress around the board. Tease includes: 1 game board, 6 playing pieces, 150 Rewards, 150 Forfeits and 50 fantasies, including Fetish, Role Play, Massage, Voyeur and Threesome. Adults only.

Play consists of roll & move around a board marked with spaces representing various actions such as Drink, Massage, or Strip. Some spaces instruct you to draw from a deck of cards and perform the action described. These are sorted by intensity during setup and go from "truths" like describing your last crush, through physical and mental challenges, up to and including sexual acts with the other players.

Your prize for reaching the end first is first pick of the Fantasy cards. Each card is a scenario to played out by you and the other players after everyone has finished the game.

The rules have well thought out variations for different numbers and combinations of relationships between the player, from 2 to 6 players. The wide variety of action cards let you select an intensity level appropriate to your group.

Alligators, Dice & Doors is similar to Snakes & Ladders in game play, although generally faster.  It's made to be a simple dice-rolling game to help familiarize youngsters with larger numbers and simple arithmetic, and the goal is simply to get from Start to Finish across 69 squares between them.

It uses polyhedral dice (4, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 20 sided) and a rolling mechanic for backwards steps - if a player lands on an alligator square, they roll the 6-sided die and move backwards.

The "Doors" in the game refer to portals similar to the ladders in Snakes & Ladders. Under basic rules, these are one-way and treated the same. Alternatively, each door has a number. Upon entering, the player may roll an eight-sided die to determine the output location.

Ludo Quiz is a combination of the classical game Ludo/Trouble/Frustration/Mensch ärgere Dich nicht/Pachisi and a trivia game.

Pawns are moved by rolling a die, and if landing on a question field a question of the relevant color will be read aloud. If answered correctly another move (roll of the die) is allowed, otherwise the other players gets a chance to answer in turn, and earn a one space move.

Each player controls two pawns, which have to make it round the board and into their goal, and then correctly answer 3 out of 6 questions.

Contains 3888 questions in 6 different categories, each split between questions for adults and questions for children. Children can choose to answer adult questions, but not the other way round. Categories are: Sports, Music, Movies & TV, Litterature & History, Geography, and Chance.

From the publisher:

Space Swarm is a fast paced game of miniature spaceship combat that sets 2 players against each other in an easy to learn, quick to play tabletop game!

With rules that are designed to be simple and easy to learn, out-shoot, outmaneuver, and outnumber your opponents to secure victory. Use nimble but fragile Frigates, sturdy Cruisers, or powerful but slow Battleships. With access to powerful technology, The Alliance is able to stem the never-ending tide of of The Swarm, but it is only a matter of time before The Swarm overwhelms them.

Play with just the Core Game Set, or use multiple to play even bigger games, the Core Game Set provides you with everything you need to play, including the rules, range ruler, tokens, dice, scenery, and 24 un-assembled ship miniatures (12 Swarm, 12 Alliance).  The core game set is made from 3mm MDF.

One expansion set available.

boardgame: Czech Republic (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Czech Republic is a fan-made expansion to the Days of Wonder, Inc. Ticket to Ride series created by Alan R. Moon. This expansion is designed for players who are already well familiar with Ticket to Ride’s standard gameplay and mechanics. Standard, Big cities and Mega variants are available.

Published by permission for non-commercial use. All rights to the Ticket to Ride name, gameplay, mechanics, and imagery are reserved by Days of Wonder, Inc., who have not endorsed or playtested this expansion.

This horse racing game by John Jaques of London was a follow-up to its successful 1910 offering Minoru (named after the horse that made King Edward VII the first reigning monarch to win an Epsom Derby in 1909). Manifesto is the name of another famous horse that raced in the Aintree Grand National a record eight times between 1895 and 1904, finishing third three times and winning twice, the second time carrying a record weight of 12 stone 13 pounds. (Wikipedia, Grand National Guide)

While Minoru focused on betting the odds on horses that had different lengths to run and equal chances of advancing, Manifesto says on its box that "players win on their judgement".

There are eight horses, each running a straight 20-space track. Each turn, players must choose one of three cards for each horse: Careful, Risky, and Reckless. By playing Careful, a horse will move safely between 1 and 3 spaces on 2D6. Playing Risky means moves of 2-4 spaces, but failing to move at all on rolls of 5, 7 and 9. And Reckless offers the chance of moves from 3-5 spaces, but failure to move on 4, 6 and 8, and a fall (elimination from race) on a 10.

The odds are set in a way that the average outcome of each card is identical, so "judgment" really comes down to "How lucky do you feel?"

Described on the cover as being designed by the (unnamed) "inventor of Minoru, the box has cover art by British equestrian painter George Dervill Rowlandson.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
From the publisher:

The goal is to be the first player to use up or deplete his/her pile of chips. Chips are played when a Set of three or more cards is played in a row (vertically, horizontally, or diagonally), placed on top of each card in the Set.

From the publisher:

We are in the English county of Devon in 1830. The rivers Plym, Yealm, Erme, Avon and Dart, along with its many creeks and estuaries, lead to the rise of river transportation by building canals; but also it helps the steam railway expansion across all England. Tycoons and investors will struggle to impose the conveyances most prestigious (and, of course, valuable to them).

In this context, several companies are founded: the Tiverton Canal Co. strive for boat transport and canalization, the South Devon Railway Co. puts its efforts on the railroads and Dartmouth Steam Co. (also known as 'the Independents') diversify in building railways and canals at the beginning of this exciting race to dominate the transportation of passengers and goods in the region.

Both players start with a pile of 9 tiles each one and a common starting tile. They will expand a modullar board (made up alternating tiles and hollows). Each hollow closed by four tiles will be awarded with a victory point at the end of the game to the player with the most connections pointing to this that gap (all vertical and horizontal projections of the board count!). In addition to its own meeple, players have the valuable help of an external company that may be outsourced... but be careful of not to abuse this option because it can stop working in the area permanently.

When the last player places his last tile, representing the end of construction of the region of Devon, victory points will be distributed following the scoring criteria. The player who gets most victory points wins the game.

From the publisher (Spanish):

Nos situamos en la región inglesa de Devon en el año 1830. Los ríos Plym, Yealm, Erme, Avon y Dart, junto con sus numerosos arroyos y estuarios, propician el auge del transporte fluvial mediante la construcción de canales; pero el vapor también ayuda a la expansión del ferrocarril por toda Inglaterra. Magnates e inversores pugnarán por imponer el medio de transporte que más prestigio (y beneficios) les puedan reportar.

En este contexto se fundan varias compañías: la Tiverton Canal Co. apuesta por el transporte en barco y la canalización, la South Devon Railway Co. pone toda la carne en el asador por los caminos de hierro y la Dartmouth Steam Co. (también conocida como ‘The Independents’) divide sus esfuerzos en la construcción de vías férreas y canales en el inicio de la contienda por dominar el transporte de pasajeros y mercaderías de la región.

Los jugadores, con un mazo de 9 losetas cada uno y a partir de una loseta inicial común, irán expandiendo un tablero escaqueado (formado por losetas y huecos). Cada hueco cerrado por cuatro losetas otorgará un punto de victoria al final de la partida al jugador que más conexiones de su color estén apuntando hacia ese hueco (todas las proyecciones verticales y horizontales del tablero cuentan). Además de sus propios meeples, los jugadores disponen de la valiosa ayuda de una compañía externa que podrán subcontratar... pero mucho cuidado de no abusar de esta opción, ya que pueden dejar de trabajar en la zona definitivamente.

Cuando el último jugador coloque su última loseta, que representa el fin de construcción de la región de Devon, se repartirán los puntos de victoria siguiendo los criterios de puntuación. El jugador que más consiga ganará el juego.

DC Comics Dice Masters: Batman is an expansion for any Dice Masters starter set, with the entire set featuring more than thirty iconic heroes and villains from Batman's career fighting crime in Gotham City, including a number of characters that are new to the DC Comics Dice Masters game. Each foil pack includes two cards and two matching dice for the game.

description:
The year one promos are 12 additional cards which will be released to the general public periodically over 2016/17 as event support and prizes. They were available as a complete set during the Hero Realms Kickstarter campaign.
2x Afterlife (Action)

description:
The things that make men's blood run cold. The danger, the darkness, the destroyer, the big bad guy. Yup, that's you!

Play as a mighty Dragon or an undead Lich and take on an entire group of your friends and their pathetic, puny, mortal characters. Or, if you're up for a real challenge, you and a friend can battle these boss decks against each other!

Boss decks include 30 cards. Each boss is unique, having different numbers of cards in their starting decks, different ability cards, and even brand new card types.

Each match-up is a unique challenge which will require different strategies. When playing a cooperative campaign, your Character Pack represents the starting point for your character that you will customize over time.

Each 15-card Character Pack includes:


     A custom ten-card starting deck featuring multiple unique character-specific cards
     A character card with your starting health
     A class-specific minor ability card
     A class-specific major ability card
     Two score cards
     Rules


Thief [Hero Card]
Pick Pocket (Ability)
Heist (Ability)
Throwing Knife x 3
Ruby x 2
Gold x 5
"Ones Digit" Score Card
"Tens Digit" Score Card

description:
At 200 MPH only inches separate your car from the car behind you. Anywhere else in the world and you would be cursing the driver. But this isn’t just any joy ride. This is the world championships and as frightening as it may be, he’s giving you the speed you need to win it.

Draftcar is a 2 to 10 player drafting & racing game. Use clever card play to reposition race cars and get your car in the lead.

In Racing, drafting is the term used when two or more cars drive close together at high speeds to reduce the effect of drag on their car so they can go faster.

In card games, drafting is a term used to describe players having to choose from a subset of choices and potentially leaving the other choices for other players.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Rise of the Runelords – "Tup" Promo Character Card Set
description:
This is Paizo's Free RPG Day 2016 promotional character card set for the Pathfinder Adventure Card game.

Tup, like his compatriot's Ekkie and Ranzak, likes to eat, stab, and steal, not necessarily in that order. He comes with a character card, a token card, and a role card, and he can be played with any Adventure Card Game Base Set.
Additionally, there's a card included for Tup's cohort Deliverance.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Mummy's Mask – Character Add-On Deck
description: Expand your Pathfinder Adventure Card Game adventures with the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Mummy’s Mask Character Add-On Deck. This 110-card expansion deck includes four new Pathfinder Adventure Card Game characters—Ahmotep the magus, Channa Ti the druid, Drelm the cleric, and Mavaro the occultist—along with all the additional cards you need to expand your Mummy’s Mask adventure to accommodate 5 or 6 players. This deck is stocked with new weapons, spells, items, and allies, as well as additional copies of some of the game’s most useful gear—everything you’ll need to face even the deadliest challenges.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Mummy's Mask – Adventure Deck 2: Empty Graves
description: Your Mummy’s Mask adventure continues! You’ve barely started exploring the tombs of Wati when the dead began to rise throughout the city. You’ll need to calm the panic in the streets before you can cross swords with the cultists behind the undead uprising. The Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Empty Graves Adventure Deck is a 110-card expansion that adds 5 new scenarios to the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game, and also includes new locations, villains, monsters, curses, traders, loot, and more.

description:
Tuscany Essential Edition is an expansion for Viticulture which features the three most popular expansion modules from the original Tuscany expansion: 

     Extended Board: New game board featuring four full seasons for worker placement.
     Structure Cards: New cards that players can build to supplement their existing structure figures.
     Special Workers: New workers with their own special abilities, which players can train.


All other expansions from the original Tuscany expansion will go out of print.

Tuscany Essential Edition is recommended for use with Viticulture Essential Edition, but it is compatible with older versions of Viticulture as well.

description: Legends of Andor: Dark Heroes is an expansion for Legends of Andor: The Last Hope that includes four new hero boards — along with dice, cards and stand-up figures — so that you can confront the final part of the Andor story with up to six heroes at once.

The Artillery is a new type of unmovable ground unit that has a massive firepower and a strong armour.

During the setup phase, agree on the number of units of each type that each player will control. Set up the board, units, and cards as usual, including the Artillery Units.

Artillery have 6 endurance points, so when setting up the cards, place a disc on the number ‘6’ of every Artillery card. They also have a strong armour (indicated by the shield icon).

The Artillery has some special rules.

Components:

-    3 terrain tiles of 1×5 squares each
-    20 square Destroyed Floor cards
-    6 Artillery Units in 2 colours (unassembled)
-    6 Artillery targeting tokens in 2 colours
-    6 Artillery cards in 2 colours
-    6 black discs

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Nightmare Deck: The Lost Realm
description: 60 card print-on-demand deck containing the cards needed to play the three scenarios from the Lost Realm expansion in nightmare mode. A copy of both The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game core set and the Lost Realm boxed expansion are required to play.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
The Holy Grail is a highly strategic card game where players take turns drawing and playing cards until someone wins the game by whittling down their hand so the only card they're holding is the holy grail itself. The deck is made up of action cards that let you steal cards like the grail from other players, force your opponents to pick up multiple card and shuffle up everyone's hands to name a few.

The game gets more and more intense as each player battles it out to be the one in possession of the grail, all the time trying to work out how to get rid of the rest of their hand without the grail eluding their grasp.

boardgame: Iron Lady's Fleet 2: Modern Naval Combat in Falkland '82
description:
This game is a remake of Japanese publisher Technical Term Game Company's 2007 title, Iron Lady's Fleet. Like its predecessor and others in the publisher's series of modern naval conflict games, it is based on the Victory Games Fleet System of tactical and operational naval warfare.

Iron Lady's Fleet 2 takes a fresh look at the Falklands War conflict between Britain and Argentina, with a new mapboard, a new strategic movement grid, some new units and counters, and rule updates to bring this nine-year-old game into line with the company's more recent titles.

The game comes in a ziplock package with Japanese rules, and Iron Lady's Fleet 2 is packaged with a new, smaller game, Andes Fleet, which looks at possible naval conflict among Peru, Colombia and Ecuador.

description: The players have to herd 3 oxen (meeples) from the village (space 1) to Chief Wampo at the cave (space 53). Movement is determined by 3 dice (one for each oxen). On some spaces oxen are lost to predators or enemy tribes. If an oxen is lost, the player uses 1 die less.  The player may choose  which die roll to use with wich oxen, to avoid losing one. The first player who brings the most oxen to the cave wins the game.

Life Sucks is a party bidding game where players expend future potential to avoid or embrace the worst life has to offer. In the end, only two players win: the one with the most points and the one with the fewest.

Each player begins the game with 15 points and 7 success cards. These cards are the best things a player might achieve if not for life getting in the way. They range in level of achievement and can include having once gotten a B+, becoming a local celebrity, writing a bestseller, or owning a private island.

During each of the 5 rounds, a number of life event cards will be revealed (one for each player). All life events are bad and they can manipulate a player's point total, cause on-going problematic effects, or effect end game scoring.

Players then secretly bid with their success cards (numbered 1 through 7). Once revealed, the players choose life events from the pool from the player that spent the highest card to the player that spent the lowest. When there's a tie, success card suits will determine the winner. The suits are Intelligence, Charisma, Wealth, Health, Creativity, Looks, and Charm.

Life events and which suits break ties will change across the rounds as the players get older. Young life events appear during the first and second rounds, adult life events appear during the third and fourth rounds, and elderly events appear during the last (fifth) round.

At the end of the game, life events permitting, each player adds the value of any unused life events to their point total. The player with the most points wins. The player with the least points shares in an equal (and often more difficult) victory.

boardgame: Robotech RPG Tactics: UEDF Tomahawk / Defender Destroids
description:
From the publisher:

Prepare for invasion with Robotech RPG Tactics! Take command of the deadly mecha fighting forces of the United Earth Defense Force, the Zentraedi Armada, or the Malcontents. Robotech RPG Tactics is an epic 6mm scale battle game where players control whole armies from the Robotech universe. Take to the battlefield to crush your friends in a dynamic tactical game where the outcome is always hanging in the balance. 2 Tomahawk Destroids which are the main battle tank of Destroids, brimming with powerful particle beam cannons for arms, and wielding a battery of missiles and an array of other weapons. 2 Defender Destroids which are a long-range, anti-aircraft juggernaut capable of shooting down incoming Zentraedi Battlepods and Gnerl Fighters.

From the publisher:

Commonville. A dangerous place to be. Especially since a callous murderer has gone into hiding within its maze of streets. With a warning given to police that if they don’t call off their manhunt within an hour, another innocent civilian will pay the price. It’s up to you and your team to discover which of the 12 suspects is the offender, where they are hiding, which weapon was used on the victim, and arrest him/her before the time runs out.

In this game, each member of the team gets to play as one of Commonville's finest, exploring the city, consisting of tiles. Each tile contains either a suspect, evidence that can be used to eliminate suspects, or something to assist you in solving the case in time.

During each round, each player will roll a varying amount of dice up to 3 times. You may then use the dice for movement (by matching the number on the destination tile), for gathering evidence (with any 4 consecutive dice), and for getting assistance (with any 3 matching dice). Donuts can be used to perform special dice actions (such as passing a die to another player). There is also a special blue Investigation die that can be used to perform actions rapidly (but only by the lead detective). After performing any actions, the team must move the time marker down a number of spaces equal to the number shown on the time die plus 1 minute for every wild 6 that was used for an action.

There are 12 possible suspects in the game. Once you have collected enough evidence to eliminate 11 of them (and found the weapon that was used on the last victim), you must collect an Arrest Warrant from the Police Precinct and arrest the remaining suspect. If you can do all of this before time runs out, the team wins the game.
Addition to base game, with five new packs of cards and notes sheets.

User summary
In the game of 渦巻くキャンペーン (Uzumaku Campaign), players are candidates for the next US President, and struggle to win the election.

There are four influence types: Popularity, Economy, Military, and Religion.

They go on a stumping tour to States such as New York, Florida, and Pennsylvania. In these States, they build influence of specific type, gain supporters spending influence, control votes per supporter, and obstruct opponents by negative campaigns.

At the end of game, number of votes is calculated according to number of supporters and votes per supporter ratio for each influence type, modified by undecided votes. The player with the greatest number of votes wins the election.

With little card text and no special powers, this gamers' game requires you to adopt various influence management tactics according to opponents' moves.

Publisher's summary
プレイヤーが大統領選挙に出馬して、選挙を勝ち抜くゲームです。
ニューヨーク、フロリダやペンシルバニアといった都市を回って
サポーターを増やしながら、相手にネガティブキャンペーンを行い、
浮動票も獲得して勝利を目指します。
テキストや特殊能力はほぼありませんが、駆け引きとリスクがとても強いゲーマーズゲームです。
publishers: Alhara Systems (アルハラシステムズ)

boardgame: Blood Before Richmond: Beaver Dam Creek – Battles of the Seven Days
description:
Beaver Dam Creek is the fourth game in the Blood Before Richmond series, but the first battle chronologically.

The first major engagement of the Seven Days set the precedent for the fighting that would follow. You have a small contingent of Union soldiers occupying a strong defensive position; Lee's plan calls for multiple divisions to coordinate their assaults so as to outflank and destroy the enemy; one or two divisions do all the fighting, abandoning the plan and settling for costly frontal assaults. At least this time, Stonewall Jackson shows up - sort of, anyway: having just marched from the Valley with orders to attack the Union rear, he hears the fighting and promptly sets up camp instead, because of course he does.

The fourth game in our series goes back to the beginning. Though this battle has relatively few special rules, it's very challenging. It's much harder for the johnnies to activate more than a single division at a time, while the yankees find their line stretched precariously thin...

description:
Stashed - A Solitaire Card Game

The Story So Far...
You step inside your collection room to admire your lovingly collected treasures when you notice that instead of them being neatly organised into their trays they are scattered all over the place. Your precious items are in a mess and you need to get them sorted, luckily you have a system!

You have a deck of item cards that must be organised into designated spaces. If you can complete them all you win!

You also have some special actions that may be used to help you achieve your goal. Stashed is designed to be played by a single player, and can be played in around 15 mins. Co-operative variant rules are also included so you can play with a friend (or three)

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Forever Evil – Bane Super Villain
description: The text of this oversized Super-Villain Character card reads "If the first card you play during each of your turns has cost 1 or greater, you may destroy it. If you do, +2 Power."

The Outlaws expansion introduces 3 bandits to the Station Cards deck. These jerks spend a round robbing players of one of their dice if they happen to roll the train car the outlaw wants to rob! But to counter this, 6 new Station Cards are added and each has the passive U.S. Marshal ability to protect the player from the bandits. The bandits are smart enough to move on to easier prey.

From the publisher:

“This world shall be my hunting grounds for all eternity, and your souls my prey.”
   –Vha’shaelhur, Daemon Prince of Slaanesh

The Warp Unleashed is the final War Pack in the Death World cycle for Warhammer 40,000: Conquest.

A massive portal into the Warp has opened in the skies above Nectavus VI. Blood rains down, drenching the ground, and every drop holds a tiny piece of the Immaterium that will gradually overwhelm and possess the entire world. As the power of Chaos grows, distinct forces converge to destroy the servants of the Warp before they can utterly corrupt this world. Before they can triumph, however, these unlikely allies must fight their way through the Chaos warlord—a Daemon Prince of Slaanesh.

The Warp Unleashed is the sixth War Pack in the Death World cycle for Warhammer 40,000: Conquest, and as with the other War Packs in this cycle, you’ll find the focus on the terrifying Death World of Nectavus VI. Plenty of new cards with the Deep Strike keyword give you ways to surprise your opponent with unexpected tactics or reinforcements. Elite units continue to become more dangerous and viable for every faction, and finally, a new Chaos warlord invites you to call upon the Daemonic power of the Warp!

boardgame: Masmorra: Dungeons of Arcadia – Adventurers Set
description:
Adds a whole new group Heroes, as well as new Bosses and Tiles, adding new challenges and strategies to the game!

This set features 5 new Heroes eager to brave the Masmorra. Each of these Heroes comes with their figure, and hero board and cards featuring their unique abilities. Each of them also comes with an Arcadia Quest hero card, if you want to bring them into that game.

The Adventurers Set also introduces 2 brand new Bosses represented by tokens: Rhodus Mk1, the Mad Construct, and Gorka, the Orc Chieftain! And finally, this sets contains 2 brand new Medium Dungeon Tiles, one for Level I and one for Level II.

boardgame: Masmorra: Dungeons of Arcadia – Monsters Dice Set
description:
The Monsters Dice Set adds a whopping 12 new Monster Dice, as well as new Bosses and Tiles, and the much requested Dice Bags!

There are 8 copies of the new Minor Monsters Dice, adding Harpies, Gargoyles, Rust Insects, Kobolds, Zombies, and Dire Rats to the dungeons of Masmorra! There are also 4 copies of the Major Monster Dice that will bring more diversity to the second level of the dungeon, with the threat of Beastmen, Hydras, Death Knights, Lizards, Medusas, and Manticores!

These dice are mixed with those of the core box and drawn randomly when a tile requires a monster spawn of that given type. To help you out with the process of drawing random dice, and also to help store all your dice, this set comes with two custom Dice Bags, one for Minor Monsters, and another for Major Monsters!

But The Monsters Dice Set doesn't have only dice, it also features a couple of new Bosses, represented by tokens, to haunt your dungeon! Get ready to test your valour against Cun-Ha, the corrupted pharaoh, and Lord Blinkerton, the... whatever that thing is.

boardgame: Masmorra: Dungeons of Arcadia – Luke, the Lucky
description:
Kickstarter Exclusive Optional Buy. Luke the Lucky, a young and enthusiastic Hero that will come with his figure, hero board and cards, as well as a Hero Card to play him in Arcadia Quest!

This exclusive Hero is based on Luke, the Rodney Smith's son from the Watch It Played Youtube channel.

boardgame: The Good Dinosaur: The Great River Adventure
description:
Arlo, the young Apatosaur, and Spot, the lively human child, have been swept away by the big river. Can you guide them on their journey back home? Help Arlo and Spot on their adventures up the river. The player who receives the most river cards along the way will be the winner.

Each round, the Arlo & Spot standee is moved to the next river tile segment, which is flipped over.  The picture on the tile indicates what challenge Arlo & Spot must overcome this turn; all players get a chance to participate in the challenges, which could involve throwing their character's 'stone' at a bad guy standee trying to knock it over, trying to throw their stones closest to a good guy, trying to get across the rapids first by rolling the two custom color dice and moving the characters matching the rolled colours one step further, etc.  Whoever wins each challenge gets to keep that round's river tile.

When Arlo & Spot reach the end segment of the river, whoever has won the most river tiles is the winner!

Thanks to the Cinderella setting cards and the new intriguing characters and magic items in Dark Tales: Cinderella, an expansion for Dark Tales, you will be able to experience different plots and surprising endings!

description:
A card game using the same art of Dreamworks Eye Found It, but with different rules, goals, and mechanics. The card show detailed illustrations on one side and a single item on the other. Each player receives a hand of 5 cards, then a card is drawn to determine the single item all players are trying to locate in their hand. The first player to find the required item gets to discard the card with the item.
The winner is the first player to get rid of all of their cards.
Millennium Blades: Promo Pack #3 introduces two new playable characters to the game: Raritti, a master of card fusion, and Shafille, a CCG archaeologist.
Millennium Blades: Promo Pack #4 consists of four new Master-level card sets, each themed around one of the bosses in the Cooperative mode of the Set Rotation expansion.
