boardgame: Hero Realms: Droga, Guild Enforcer Promo Card
description:
Whatever the government officials say, everyone knows that it is the Guild that truly rules the streets of Thandar. If you do business or commit crime within the city without paying the Guild its due, you can expect a visit from Droga or his associates.

This is an exclusive promo for Hero Realms Kickstarter backers, with remaining stock available through conventions and special promotions only.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Description from the publisher:

The 'Connections Card' Game is all about conceptually connecting words found on adjacent cards. It can be described simply as a cross between Scrabble™ and Dominoes.

The game contains 124 cards with 496 English words. Each card has four family-friendly words (one per edge) and a point value between 1 and 5.

There are 17 ways to play the game.

In the basic Classic Mode, players compete to get the highest score by connecting related words. For example, TEACHER can be connected with STUDENT, SCHOOL, RESPECT, LEARNING, JOB as well as a dozen other words.

Hilarious dialog begins when someone attempts a dubious connection and has to “sell” it to the group. This makes it a game about connecting people.

This family and party game is for use at home and school as well as corporate office team building.

The compact box is suitable for travel, camp and cottage.

This undated game uses a tiddly-wink mechanic, but the one disk propels five small plastic rings instead of smaller disks from a red felt mat.

The directions on the box say to prop the lid behind the base as a backboard, and flip the rings onto a target board inset in the box. The board has a central clown face worth 10 on the nose, 5 on the mouth and 2 on the face, along with four small clowns holding starts, worth 3 or 4 points.

The big points come from successfully ringing one of 12 wooden pegs supplied with the game that are pushed through small holes in the boards. Each peg ringed is worth 25 points.

This 1949 game is subtitled "The Football Game Used by Coaches to Train Their Squads. The football field inset in the box is marked to individual yards, and has peg holes along the side to mark scores, quarters and downs. Other parts include a cardboard 10 yard marker, a plastic football and three dice.

Although each play requires a roll of the dice, with the total compared against a list of plays to determine net yards gained or lost, the key to the game lies in two sets of cards. The defensive player each play secretly chooses one of five formation cards (6-2-2-1, 6-3-2, 7-1-2-1, 8-3 or 5-3-2-1).

The offensive player then calls one of eight possible plays and turns over the top card from the 80-card play deck. Cross-referencing the play row with the defensive column provides a modifier to the die roll, as well as possible fumbles and interceptions.

It's a race to the top of the corporate ladder in the fast paced family game! Players must use strategy and their wits to accumulate the most money before they retire. Scheme your way to the Chairmen of the Board and then force your friends to pay your debts. But watch out, they might gang up on you and force you into early retirement! Big money, big risks and backstabbing; it's all in a day's work for big business. WILL YOU SURVIVE?

Based on the poker variant of a similar name, players compete to have the best hand, betting on each round based on their position on the 3D board. Each position limits the players hand size as well as betting minimum, with loss causing the player to incur debt.  When a player reaches the chairman position, they can pass debts they incur onto lower ranked players. Highest ranked player at the end of the game, who has not been forced into retirement, wins.

IntroductionNotakto is a misere 3-in-a-row game for two players. It's played on the squares of a finite number of initially empty 3x3 boards. All pieces are exactly the same and are shared by both players.

				
				
					DefinitionsA board is dead if it contains at least one set of three stones in a straight orthogonal or diagonal line.

A board that is not dead is alive.

				
				
					PlayOn their turn, a player must place a piece on an empty square of a living board.

When all boards are dead, the player who made the last placement loses.

User summary
オトーリバース (OTOOREVERSE) is a card game with a theme of drinking race. In a drinking party in Japan, 'reverse' means "to reverse what you have ingested". It is a mild expression of 'vomit'.

At the beginning of the game, you are assigned a character, who has their own alcohol capacity and beverage preferences. Their favorite beverage's alcohol counts zero, and hated one counts double toward their capacity.

Each turn, you may choose to quaff a beverage (draw a card), and if the total amount of alcohol exceeds your character's capacity, the character 'reverses' and you are out of the round with no victory points. You should stop drinking before your character 'reverses'.

When any character would quaff a beverage (after drawing a card but before resolving it), you may make an 'alhara' (alcohol harassment) to force any character (including yours) to quaff that beverage. You have limited times of 'alhara' attempts.

At the end of round, each non-'reversed' player gains victory points according to beverages their character has quaffed. After several rounds, the highest scorer wins the game.

Publisher's summary
オトーリバースとは、より多く酒をのんだ人が勝者というゲームです。
ただし酒のアルコール度数が肝臓の強さを超えると「リバース」してしまいます。
厄介なのは、吐いてしまった人間は悪酔いして他人に迷惑をかけること。
あなたはこの最悪の宴で生き残ることができるのか…
publishers: Alhara Systems (アルハラシステムズ)

description:
Dodgy Dealers is a collection/deception card game where 2-4 players (up to 12 players with expansions) race to complete their collection and cash in on their contracts. Throughout the game you source your art exclusively on the black market, which is unfortunately full of forgeries and questionable characters. Be the first to reach four genuine contracted artworks to win the game.
​​
Forgeries, Fakes and Fakers! - Work your way through the game by determining which artworks are genuine and which are forgeries. Fake paintings aren't worth anything, but keeping them in your gallery might make opponents think they're authentic!

Multiple paths to victory - Stage heists and steal paintings from opposing players or work through the Black Market to be the first Dodgy Dealer to fulfil their contract.

Completely open trading system - Trades can be struck between any players on a one-for-one basis. Trade face up or face down, be honest or be deceitful, even trade contracts and completely mix up gameplay!

Be secretive - Players must leave their gallery of paintings facedown on the table and may only rearrange them during their own turn.

Be cunning - The pick up and discard systems allow players to develop complex strategies; like removing opponents desired cards out of the game or discarding one of your own contracted paintings temporarily for safety.

Designed with REAL artworks. Dodgy Dealers features paintings of Van Gogh, Claude Monet, Edvard Munch and John Singer Sargent. Eight more artists are available through add-on packs. These include Winslow Homer, James Whistler, Leonardo Da Vinci, Caravaggio, Johannes Vermeer, Rembrandt, William Turner and Eugene Delacroix.

Change the art or invite more to the party - These add-on packs add additional artists of various styles and nationality into the game as well as increasing the game size by two players. Every game comes with enough cards for a four player game but is compatible with up to twelve.

Survive The Horde Party Game is a 2-8 player character-driven game with zombie battles and in-game challenges intended for a 17+ audience.

The game starts by selecting a character card and a set number of brain chips. Players take turns in a clockwise order. To take a turn, a player takes the top card from the zombie deck and reads it out loud. Zombie cards may have an in-game challenge, a Curse, or you may have to attack the zombie with a roll of the dice. When a zombie is defeated, players are rewarded with a survivor card. These survivor cards feature wacky ways to slay zombies, help you survive, and sabotage other players. When a player fails to defeat a zombie or complete a challenge, they must turn one of their brain chips to its undead side. If all brains have been turned to their undead side, a player is now undead and must try to win battles and challenges to get themselves back in the game.

A player wins by being the last player with at least one healthy brain chip.

Red X: Facility 342 is a hidden role playing game where team loyalty is constantly changing.

Players are either part of an alien race that reproduces by taking over the mind and body of a living host, or part of the research team that is attempting to assemble a purification device that will cure the infected players, ultimately saving the entire human race.

Red X: Facility 342 requires multiple playing areas that allow players to go on missions in order to collect parts, but BEWARE, when left alone with an alien you are in danger of being infected yourself. Two players start off already infected. The rest of the players are human researchers, by the second round you don't know who you can trust anymore.

Roles are assigned by passing out DNA Verification Cards at the start of the game. Players choose a Head Researcher and then get sent on a mission with one other player. Mission Rooms are isolated areas located in other parts of the house. Decided by a dice roll researchers may be infected by the alien they were unknowingly paired up with or the researchers could possibly return with a part to the machine needed to kill off the aliens. The aliens win if they can infect the entire research team; the researchers win if they can collect all the parts needed to build the purification device. Either way this exciting hidden role playing game will keep you guessing the entire time.
________________________________________________________________________

"There's a lot of social deduction games out there. What makes yours different? What makes your game special? Why do you enjoy it?" - Aaron

Great question Aaron,
What makes Facility 342 different from what is out there?
I created my game for a couple reasons.

First was because of the number of players current social deduction games can have. We found ourselves with 10-15 players (5-7 couple) quite often. The Resistance cannot be played with that many people. We could play Werewolf but I do not like how you eliminate players.

Second I wanted to add a new element to the social deduction game. Players loyalty can change during game play in Facility 342. What better scenario than body snatchers.

Lastly I wanted a social deduction game that had a role playing feel to it. In my game you have to physically leave the game table to go on a mission with another player. This allows things to happen in private that can affect the whole game. This also allows players to talk in private about their plans and they can use this time to figure out what is going on, or trick players without the interruptions of other players.

boardgame: New York 1901: Goons of New York
description:
Description from the publisher:

New York 1901: Goons of New York is a promotional expansion pack for New York 1901 that adds five new "Boss" cards that give special powers to the players who control each district on the game board.

Once all players in the game have reached the Silver Age, you look to see who controls the most territory of each color on the game board (with pink being excluded in two-player games).

Whoever controls the most of a color takes control of that boss, and at the start of each of your subsequent turns, you can use the "goon power" of one of your bosses to place a goon in a particular location, such as on a street or on a specific Bronze Age skyscraper. You'll score points for this goon based on certain conditions, but if someone ever controls more territory of this color than you, that player takes control of the boss and you have to say goodbye to those goons.

Goons of New York was made in conjunction with the Brooklyn Game Lab, an afterschool program in which children learn to play today's best board games, then discuss the strategies and tactics used to play them. For this project, hundreds of children worked together — in a month-long game-jam — to design and playtest this promotional content for New York 1901, which is currently planned to be made available solely at trade shows and game conventions.
Celebrate R&R Games 20th Anniversary with 120 new famous people for Time's Up!

boardgame: Carson City: The Teacher & Town Hall
description:
The Teacher

When using the Teacher, you must use its yellow side during round 1-3, and the red one during round 4. When playing with the Teacher, you will be the first player to choose a character next turn, no matter when you pass this round. Cash limit: $60.

Red side (only available in the last round): During the final scoring, you get 2 victory points for each School, Prison, or Church you own, and you also get 2 victory points if you don’t own any Saloon(s). Cash limit: $60.

Town Hall

Use this tile instead of a generic red house tile to depict the starting location of Carson City at the start of the game.

---

Note: As these are included in the Big Box of Carson City, this small expansion is meant for owners of the base game.
The Teacher was also a giveaway together with the Lawyer/Editor at Essen but without the Townhall.

boardgame: Marvel Universe Miniature Game: Jean Grey
description: A founding member of the X-Men, a mutant with telepathic and telekinetic powers and the love interest of Cyclops and later his wife. She is both a caring and nurturing person, but also has to deal with being one of the most powerful mutants around and is in a constant struggle to control her powers. An Omega-level mutant, that once she could not control it took on the physical manifestation of the cosmic Phoenix Force or Dark Phoenix.

Description from the publisher:

Sabretooth (born Victor Creed) is a mutant who, has a number of animal-like abilities, such as retractable claws, enhanced senses, and a regenerative healing factor. Sabretooth is a formidable hand-to-hand combatant, having been trained by the Foreigner, the C.I.A., and many others. Sabretooth is also an extraordinary hunter and tracker.

Description from the publisher:
Nightcrawler possesses superhuman agility, the ability to teleport and can climb walls and other obstacles with ease. He also has indigo / blue coloured skin, which allows him to become nearly invisible in the shadows and he has a prehensile tail. A devout Catholic, Nightcrawler's name is Kurt Wagner and he is from a small village called Witzeldorf in Germany and was part of a circus act until he was recruited in to the X-men. Later in the comics it is found out that Nightcrawler’s parents are Azazel and Mystique.

Description from the publisher:

Baby Blues Continues is an expansion for the card game Baby Blues with different parts that can be used separately or in combination. They are:

1) Cards allowing for play with up to six players.

2) A variant for two players: When you play the original game with two players, there are two adjustments compared to the normal game. First, you can't trade, and second, the points on the back of the cards are irrelevant. With the variant, this won't be the case anymore. Players now can trade cards with a closet and the game can end early because the babies are picked up by their parents, so both players have points to count.

3) A new type of card — the action card: On your turn, you can play the same cards as in the base game, but in addition you can play as many action cards as you want. With these cards you can transfer problems to other babies or trade babies with an opponent, making the game a little bit meaner and also more tactical.

4) A new problem — the sick baby: What will happen with a baby if you give him too much pie at a birthday party? He will become nauseous and this problem will only be solved when he vomits. The owner of the baby can choose the direction and the baby to his left or right will start crying immediately. Luckily there is medicine available, but will you be able to give it to the baby in time?

High in the Mountains of Daggerstone, There once existed the most amazing city. A great and wealthy city, Lost. Swallowed up by the very mountains that acted as its strongest defense from invasion. Though its name has been lost to the ages, its legend lives on. Some say, deep within the mountain’s caverns, deep within The City’s ruins, The City’s greatest treasure remains. The Allstone. A gemstone so saturated with magical energy, that whomever possesses it can do the impossible. It’s also REALLY shiny.
Now, The best Petramancers in all the land race to find The City and lay claim to The Allstone.

Players draw Ore Cards in order to claim Tool Cards with matching ore types. Claiming Tool Cards earn you Depth Points. First Player to 10 Depth is the winner

The Trump Pack is a pack of 25 cards that is included in the 'Donald Trump Bug-out Bag'. The pack contains 3 black cards, 22 white cards and one double sided 'Comedy Rations' card.

The publisher describes the Donald Trump Bug-Out Bag as: A pre-packed duffle bag with over a dozen items hand-picked to help you survive the collapse of civilization after Donald Trump is elected President of the United States. Includes the Cards Against Humanity Trump Pack with 25 new jokes about frightening demagogue Donald Trump.

This is a separate edition from the Vote for Trump pack.

In the late 21st century, a coalition of wealthy governments, troubled by the continued destruction of earth's natural habitats, put together a team of elite astronauts to begin the first human settlement on Mars. Surviving Mars: The First Mission, is a game about those first few astronauts and how they managed to create the first settlement on Mars almost a century ago.

In this game, players must work together to construct a predesigned and prefabricated habitat consisting of a biosphere, a sustainable crop system and a number research laboratories. Additionally, players must complete a number of critical life supporting research tasks in order to ready the habitation system and the Martian terrain for the first wave of humans. The game can be played as a single experience, or series of episodes where player continue further the mission, and pick up where the last game left off.

This game is a cooperative (or solo) worker placement, dice rolling and deck building experience, that complement the push-your-luck feel of dice with the ever growing stability of deck building. Player cooperative choose teams of two astronauts (each with their won specialty) to complete missions over a series of episodes. Players take on the role of one of four characters; Biologist, Engineer, Researcher, or Commander. (An expansion is still in development that will allow a 5th character, the Medic.)

Each round players must cooperatively make both tactical and strategic decision with an overwhelming amount of unknowns.  A successful Martian settlement means, players must strategically balance a number of risks: the natural risks of the Martian terrain and extreme Martian temperatures; solar radiation and sandstorms; the immediately fatal risks of damage to their life support systems or habitat; and, or course, starvation. All this while still maintaining the state of mental and physical health necessary to construct the full habitat before the next wave of humans arrives!

Can you survive the first mission to Mars?

description from the publisher:

This is a Snap-style card game most notable for its fantastic graphics. The best are not Old Mother Hubbard herself or her Cupboard, but the five sets featuring her dog, titled: Dog; Laughing Dog; Dancing Dog; (Dog) Riding the Goat; and (sadly) Dead Dog.

The box is undated, but Alex G. Malloy in American Games: Comprehensive Collector's Guide, dates it to 1910 (p. 36, # 1143).

This is a late 19th Century card game in which each of the 17 pairs is composed of a card with a recap and moral from one of Aesop's fables, and another with an illustration of that fable. The odd card is a portrait of Aesop himself, and all the artwork is fantastic.

All the cards are dealt out. The player to the left of the dealer takes one card from the player to his right (the dealer in this case) and then lays down in front of him as many pairs as possible. Each player in turn repeats until all pairs have been laid.

The Aesop card may be played at any time, and has the effect of doubling the number of matches beneath it. Winner is the player with the greatest number of matched pairs at the end of the game.

The box is undated, but Alex G. Malloy, American Games: Comprehensive Collector's Guide, dates it to 1890 (p. 34. #1001).

Description from the publisher:
The Guardians of the Galaxy is a group of cosmic adventurers led by Star-Lord to protect the universe from threats all across space. The team also includes Drax, Gamora, Groot and Rocket Raccoon! The team are good guys, though sometime not that good. Groot is an unknown tree like creature that only talks with Rocket Raccoon through 3 words, "I am Groot".
Two figure pack for Arcadia Quest for heroes Mamba & Rosh
Two figure pack for Arcadia Quest for heroes Viola & Crash
Two figure pack for Arcadia Quest for heroes Anvil & Valma
Two figure pack for Arcadia Quest for heroes Yona & Kuruk
This Expansion brings you 3 brand new Pets to use in your games: Jamon the boar, Cookie the fluffy kitty, and Neville the toad! Each of them comes with their Pet figure and Pet cards for all levels.

This Add-On brings 3 brand new Pets for you to chase down and add to your guilds: Wilhelm the goat, Joey the kangaroo, and Dawn the phoenix! Each of them comes with their Pet figure and cards for all levels.

Description from the publisher:

Richard Rider is a middle-class man from the United States who became a member of the Nova Corps for which he gained superhuman abilities including enhanced strength, flight and resistance to injury. Calling himself "Nova," Rider is an inter-galactic peace-keeper. He is empowered by the Nova Force.

boardgame: Marvel Universe Miniature Game: Black Panther
description:
Description from the publisher:
T'Challa is a brilliant tactician, strategist, scientist, tracker and a master of all forms of unarmed combat whose unique hybrid fighting style incorporates acrobatics and aspects of animal mimicry. He fights to achieve his goals and protect the kingdom of Wakanda.

boardgame: Marvel Universe Miniature Game: The Incredible Hulk
description:
Description from the publisher:

Dr. Bruce Banner's alter ego is the enormous, green skinned Hulk. His strength is limitless, he is practically indestructible and you won't like him when he's angry. He eats nuclear missiles for breakfast and can enter orbit in a single leap making him an invaluable addition to The Avengers. Now "HULK SMAAAASH!"

boardgame: Marvel Universe Miniature Game: The Vision
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Vision is a synthetic android created by Ultron and Dr. Helen Cho, but programmed by Tony Stark and Bruce Banner by uploading the J.A.R.V.I.S. system for his personality. Placed within his forehead is the Mind Stone, one of the Infinity gems and provides him with his power. Vision is a polite, calm, and thoughtful being and unlike Ultron, he values life and humanity. Also Vision is one of only a handful of beings that are worthy enough to wield Mjolnir and with it the power of Thor. Visions capabilities include a Synthetic Body, Invulnerability, Density Manipulation, Superhuman Strength, Intangibility, Levitation, Interfacing with computers, Superhuman Speed, Shapeshifting, Energy Beams and of course the power from the Mind Stone.

Description from the publisher:

Clinton Barton, code name Hawkeye, is a former S.H.I.E.L.D. agent and founding member of the Avengers. Barton has no real superpowers but is the world's greatest marksman armed with a recurve bow. Due to his work with shield, Hawkeye is has a very close friendship with Black Widow.

boardgame: Marvel Universe Miniature Game: Iron Man Mk44 Hulkbuster Armor
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Mark XLIV Armour, better known as the Hulkbuster, is Tony Stark's forty-fourth Iron Man suit, made with the help of Bruce Banner for use in case Banner lost control of his alter-ego, Hulk. It is a modular suit that is operated from within while being linked to the Mark XLIII Armour. This armour is launched from an orbital tracking platform, known as Veronica. This modular suit of armour has many of the traits of the classic Iron Man armour and a few impressive upgrades to take on the Incredible Hulk. These include, Advanced Strength, Advanced Durability, Flying, Repulsor Beams, Prehensile Technology, Grappling Hook, Extended Punches, Chemical Spray and Missiles.

Description from the publisher:

Toughest Card Game Around

A Party Favorite Card Game

MACHO... STRONG... COURAGEOUS...
that's what it takes to turn the next card in this exciting, fun game of cards.

The higher your score, the more MACHO it takes... Do you have it?

Any player starts the game by drawing and laying face up on the table one, or as many cards as he chooses, but he must draw at least one card. He may stop at any time. The points on each card are counted until the player stops, and are added to his score, unless he turns up a hog or a pig card during the draw. Hogs and Pigs can take away a player's points. Other MACHO cards can force a player to draw five cards with possibly devastating consequences. A game ends when a player scores 100 points or more. It's Press-Your-Luck-style gaming as you must choose carefully when deciding to draw the next card!

boardgame: S and W Happy Marriage Game
description:
In the 1930s, canned food company S&W Fine Foods was the sponsor of a popular radio program called "I Want A Divorce", and in 1938 published a promotional board game called "S and W Happy Marriage Game".

The envelope used to ship the game says the show ran on eight radio stations in the Western U.S. and ran on the Columbia Broadcasting System on Sunday evenings at 7:30 Pacific and 8:30 Mountain. The board records the copyright date as 1938.

The game itself is a simple roll-and-move with a colorful folding cardboard game board. The theme is "happy marriage" and the track moves from "Proposal" to "Golden Anniversary at space 50.

The track is littered with squares representing personal faults like Jealousy, Excess, Gossip and Gambling that push players back, as well as virtues like Loyalty and Patience that move them forward. Square 24 is named "I Want a Divorce" after the radio program and sends players back to the start.
publishers: S and W Fine Foods Inc.

description:
Death of the Party is a hidden identity card game with a murder mystery theme. Each player takes on the Persona of a famous detective who has been invited to a party, only to discover their host has been murdered, and one of them is responsible. It is up to the Partygoers to identify and arrest the Killer, or determine if there are multiple Killers, before they get murdered as well.

At the start of the game, there is the hidden identity ritual of having all Killers open their eyes and identify one another. One Killer must decide to make the first murder and then leave a Cause Of Death card and a Clue card, tied to their Persona, on the host's BODY sheet. Regular play begins and everyone goes to the room in which they want to take their turn. Each player, upon entering, takes a Drink card, that determines the order of questioning, and a Drawer card to see if they can find something useful.

When everyone has finished at the table, each player can ask a question of one other, attempting to tie them to the murder, or to uncover the progress of their investigation. Afterwards, the players will disperse to find new players to question. This process of hunting suspects from room to room continues until there is another murder.

At any time during gameplay, one player can attempt to kill another player by giving them a Weapon card (with a Cause Of Death), and a Clue card. But be careful, if the intended victim has a Weapon, they may try to defend themselves. And be sneaky, if the victim sees the attack coming they are allowed a single scream that could be heard by potential witnesses. The wise Killer will sneak up on the other player, and touch the victim on the back. The victim will not get a chance to scream nor defend themselves.

When a murder is discovered an Accusation Phase begins. All the surviving players gather and each is allowed one question to "examine the body." The victim may only reply by showing a card, including the Cause of Death and Clue left behind by the Killer. Based on what everyone has learned over their investigations, an Accusation may be made. All the free and living players vote on whether or not to put the player accused of being a Killer under House Arrest.

Play continues until someone makes a Final Accusation, declaring that they know who all the Killers are. The accuser will name the players (alive, dead, and under arrest) they believe to be Killers. Arguments are made, then a vote is taken. Any accused players who are still free and alive are placed under House Arrest, and the game ends. Identities are revealed, and the players discover who won: the Partygoers or the Killers.

—description from the publisher

Who grabs all peanuts first?

Dexterity game for children:
Who will be the first player collecting 4 peanut chips of different colors with his elephant trunk?

Description from the publisher:

InCube has you trapped! What seemed to be only an innocent game in a strange little box has now revealed itself to be a true nightmare! Stuck in this peculiar prison, you have to move in unison, rolling towards freedom — but be careful as only the one who ends up at the top of the cube will be able to escape from the game!

Description from the designer:

Euro Game about time travelling, inverse-drafting card driven, resource management and victory points.

The game takes place in a fictional future in which there are multiple organizations who started time travelling to steal important goods and hire historical characters to their cause.

There are three turns, each divided into five rounds, and, at the end of a turn, a mutual resolution phase is played.

How a round is played: During rounds, players choose one of the two cards they own in their hands and pick one of the two sides in them. They then cover the other side of the card with an hand and show it at the same time as other players. Cards have an action resolution order, then the chosen card is passed to the left for a new round.

Description from the publisher:

Choose your favorite Millennium token and travel around the board collecting and trading Yu-Gi-Oh! Duel Monsters. To defeat your opponents, you will need to have the strongest monsters lke the Dark Magician, Exodia the Forbidden One, and the Blue-Eyes Ultimate Dragon. Upgrade with custom Game Shops and Duel Arenas to make yourself invincible.

Description from the publisher:

The Balkan Tragedy is a one-two player game simulating the conflict in the balkans during both the first and second balkan war. This is a more traditional hex and counter War-Game and plays in 90-120 mins for each scenario. The first scenario being the first Balkan War, and the Second Balkan War being the other scenario included in the game. This game is meant to setup the background for our further exploration into the conflicts of the time period. Particularly WW1, as well as other post WW1, and inter war period conflicts. The game will feature a paper map, and units counters, and all necessary tracking counters for the game. Nations included in the game are: The Ottoman Empire, Bulgarians, Serbians, Greek, Romanian, and Montenegrin forces. This is your time to see if you can change history in the Balkans, and perhaps influence this critical region at this point in human history. Your the General now!!!

This is a simple but colorful game designed by Howard R. Garis (creator of Uncle Wiggily). The box cover has an illustration of people lining up at a movie theatre box office, which shows the day's feature as a Charlie Chaplin movie. The playing board is laminated to the bottom of the box, and is set up like a theatre, with 11 rows of seats and a centre aisle.

The rules are pasted on the inside of the lid, and describe a spin-and-move effort to weave through the seats all the way to the stage. The winner gets to shake hands with the hero and heroine of the movie, who have appeared that night in person. The 131-space track includes 13 seats with hazards such as "You sat in gum, lose 1 turn" and six seats with bonuses such as "Somebody eats peanuts, go to 48".

This is a set of Kickstarter Exclusive rewards for Myth that was part of the MERCS: Recon Kickstarter. These were stretch goals that were unlocked during the Kickstarter campaign and were part of the "Myth Pledge Level" and "Agent Pledge Level" (and higher pledge levels).

This set includes the following miniatures with associated cards:


    Inquisitor Alger (Agent)
    Hunter (Hero; 2 figures: Male and Female)
    X-3ONE (Mini-Boss)
    Infected (4 figures)


The Agent Hero, X-3ONE Mini-Boss, and Infected may also be used with MERCS: Recon and include cards for both Myth and MERCS: Recon.  The Infected are the same as the Infected in the MERCS: Recon - Blood Contagion expansion (which is a Myth Crossover).

This 1935 title uses dice to determine the results of plays. The board has two folds, with the field in the center section and the list of plays and results, and dials for downs, quarters and yards to go on both side panels, one facing each end of the field.

Kickoff, return, reverse, lateral, line buck, off tackle, end run, pass, punt and spinner plays are resolved with two dice which are read lowest digit first, for 21 possible outcomes. Results of punts, field goals, try for point, free kick after safety, fumbles, blocked kicks and interference penalties are determined by a single die.

There is no defensive play choice. Instead, the defensive player is responsible for looking up the results and keeping tabs of yardage and downs while the offensive player considers his next play. The offensive player must choose his next play within 10 seconds or is hit with a five-yard delay of game penalty.

Test your observation and attention to detail. Look at the picture. You have 30 seconds to memorise as much as possible and then be prepares to answer questions about the picture. Each correct answer earns you 1 point. After three rounds of pictures the player with the most points wins!

A demon ark send by Witch King Morda is nearing the Aventurian coast. It carries a Dark Diamond–a breeding ground for demons and undead. You must stop the Ship of Lost Souls, before it makes landfall and spews out the terrible creatures in its belly.

Ship of Lost Souls is the second expansion for the Aventuria Card Game. This box contains the adventure Ship of Lost Souls in three acts as well as the short adventure Rietholtz’s Treasure. Additionally, it introduces a new hero, Tjalva Garheltdottir, a Thorwalian warrior who uses her physical strength and battle prowess to smite her foes.

•••

Eine Dämonenarche des Zauberkönigs Marda nähert sich der aventurischen Küste. An Bord befindet sich ein Schwarzer Diamant – eine Brutstätte für Dämonen und Untote. Ihr müsst Das Schiff der verlorenen Seelen aufhalten, bevor es das Festland erreicht und die Ungeheuer aus seinem Innern über das Land herfallen.

Das Schiff der verlorenen Seelen ist eine Erweiterung für Aventuria - Das Abenteuerkartenspiel. Neben dem Abenteuer Das Schiff der verlorenen Seelen mit drei Akten, ist das Kurzabenteuer Rietholtz’ Schatz in dieser Box enthalten. Außerdem wird ein neuer Held eingeführt: Tjalva Garheltdottir, eine Thorwalsche Kriegerin, die ihrer überlegenen Körperkraft und ihren mächtigen Waffen vertraut, um ihre Feinde zu zerschmettern.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Farmers’ Market Rally is a set collection game of seasonal local produce and high scores and is for two to four people, aged eight and above. The game is set at your local farmers’ market, where you’ve met up with some friends to search for items on your shopping list and collect anything else in season this week.

For the number of players plus two, two rows of card stacks are dealt to create farmer's stands. Players take turns picking from the dealt stands, with new cards replaced after each turn. Two rounds of stands are available with surprises waiting to be found in the back of the second-row stands after players have made their most of their first-row purchases. The game continues until one of the second-row stands has sold-out, with one turn left for the remaining players.

There are thirteen item card types and 94 total items in the deck, plus ten shopping list cards.

Description from the publisher:

A rising tide lifts all boats, and in New Bedford that means the town is ready to grow.

Rising Tide is an expansion for New Bedford that includes more ships and supplies for a fifth captain, and even more buildings to expand the town. Ships at sea will have tales to tell from the Ship's Log, and while Providence favors the bold, darker Omens are also on the horizon...
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Fruit Case, players attempt to collect sets of fruit to fill their baskets. Points are awarded for complete and partial sets.

The game contains 108 cards with identical backsides. On the front are the following:

    12 each of six different fruits
    24 baskets with two fruits (blue background)
    4 wild pineapples (purple background)
    2 baskets with 3 fruits (purple background)
    2 bombs (purple background)
    1 rotten apple with a worm (purple background)
    3 tree cards (brown background)


The tree cards are laid face up in the middle, with at most one less than the number of players. Each player receives a two-fruit basket to start with and all other cards are shuffled. The starting dealer lays out, face up, a number of cards equal to the number of players. In turn, beginning with the dealer, each player chooses a card and places it in front of them. A player not wishing or unable to take a card instead takes one of the trees (if one is available in the middle). This tree may be returned to the center on a subsequent turn in order to take any fruit from the discard pile (instead of taking a card from the middle).

Each basket starts with two or three fruits already in it. A fruit card can only be added to the basket if that fruit is not yet present. Once a basket has all six fruits, all of its cards are stacked and turned sideways. Any number of baskets can be worked on at one time, but a fruit cannot be taken without a basket to go in.

The pineapple is a wild fruit, but each basket can only have one. If the bomb comes up in a deal, the entire turn is skipped and all cards from the middle are discarded. The rotten apple results in the dealer discarding one entire incomplete basket of theirs, if one exists.

After each round, the dealer role passes to the left. The game ends when there are not enough cards to fill the center. 5 points are awarded for each complete basket, with 1 point for each fruit card added to an incomplete basket.

Rules are also included for longer and shorter variants.

Description from the publisher:

"Players create numeric patterns (sequences and series like 1,2,3,4 or 2,4,6,8 etc.) much like they would create words while playing Scrabble. There is an amazing number of possibilities, a lot of fun and all players compete equally while receiving play money as they show off their creative ability."

Each player starts with $1000 and 7 numeric tiles.  On a player's turn they must create a numeric pattern using at least 3 of their 7 tiles.  Points scored are equal to the sum of the numbers played, multiplied by the number of tiles played.  Additional tiles can be purchased with game money.  Additional money can be earned by creating a pattern where the sum of the numbers is equally divisible by the number of tiles played.  The game ends when no more tiles are remaining.  The player with the most points is the winner.

In Auction House is a classic auction game. Players take on the role of potential bidders, as they bid over lots based on some of highest value items ever sold in real auctions.

Auction House is simple bidding game made of two decks the auction items and the cash cards. Players all start with the same amount of cash and bid in secret on items.

Once revealed the highest bidder wins. Play continues until all players run out of cash cards to bid. At the end of the game the player with the most items is declared the winner.

One player is in a tricky situation and is looking for a solution. The other players have to invent a story around one bizarre fact that will convince the first player to come to their country to solve the problem. If your country is chosen, you receive the situation card. Whoever collects the most cards wins the game.

Who will come up with the craziest story? 
Which countries do those weird facts belong to?

Including more than a hundred surprising facts about the 28 EU countries and thousands of possibilities for fun stories, COME ON OVER! is the perfect entertaining game about Europe.

You and your fellow players are officers trying to save yourselves and some passengers but there is only one last lifeboat remaining, eventually fighting for life. That last lifeboat has a limited number of places...

Players use their action cubes to determine:

     the turn order (it also allows to get courage or money)
     the distance their officer walk. Each one has a list of rooms he must go through before he can leave the sinking boat.
     the money they want to get (it helps to get objects and passengers)
     the courage they want to get (useful in order to move further and guide passengers)
     if they want to get an object (useful when fighting)
     if they want the buoy (to go through a room that has got the water leak)


-*if they want passengers

Then the water leak moves from one room to another. The buoy comes handy there. If an officer is in that same room, he must go back on his rooms list.

Then actions are taken.

Fights are resolved when several officers are in the same room. Passengers and collected objects are used to calculate the force of each opponent's group.

When each officer arrives at the end of his list, he finally moves into the lifeboat. He takes as many places for his passengers as he can, if remaining.

The game ends when the lifeboat is full.
The winner is the player who could save the most passengers.
family: History: The Sinking of the Titanic

description:
Description from the publisher:

Mein Zombie is a flexible and expandable 1:1 rule system for recreating the massive Zombie infection. Designed to be used with miniature scales from 15 up to 28mm, it is based around out innovative Drop-In game system. Players from novice to veteran will find it easy to customize the game to their particular gaming style.

This player-friendly approach to war gaming builds around solid and easily learned basic rules, which present an excellent balance of playability and historical accuracy. Once you quickly learn the core rules, you simply "drop in" additional rule modules as you see fit. Each Drop-In utilizes the same terms and mechanics for entirely seamless integration into a full-featured game system.

Description from the publisher:

The Stronghold is the most powerful ship in the known galaxy.

The Stronghold can simultaneously invade with intense Fleet Strength (5) and Ground Strength (5). When backed up on a variety of unit types, The Stronghold is easier to deploy into the Warzone, so ensure that your commanders are well rounded for battle.

The Stronghold is a promo card for backers of the Core Worlds Digital KS campaign. It was sent to all those who were to receive other physical card rewards from the KS campaign.

boardgame: Statecraft: The Political Card Game – Assets & Agents
description:
Description from the publisher:

Expansion for the Statecraft political card game system that introduces two new card types, Assets and Agents.
publishers: Inside the Box Board Games LLP (ITB)

description:
Tibbits is a variation and combination of several games - but played with cubes. The cubes have A, K & Q on them (like cards) and in different colors. You try to build combinations (like 3 identical As, A-K-Q of the same color, etc.) and collect chips based on what colors and combinations you make.  Ages 8 and up. There is also a colors-only version of game play for younger players.

The game is packaged in a lidded plastic dice cup and comes with 3 Tibbit Cubes, 60 chips, & instructions.

Description from the designer:

Rainbow Roll is a push your luck dice game for 2-4 players.  Players roll a pair of dice with numbered and colored faces.  They must then flip over a tile corresponding to one of the numbers or colors shown.  Each tile has sushi on the backside.  The more sushi showing the more points earned!

Bait and Switch is a poker style card game with cute cuddly sea creatures and deep deep decisions.  A variation on 7 Card Stud, players discard cards from their hand to manipulate which cards they may use from the center. Bait and Switch is extremely easy to learn and teach. Poker players will find the game rich in depth and decision space.  Optional family friendly rules make it a fantastic game for teaching poker concepts.

Description from the designer:

Disasters have befallen Metro City and it's up to the fine folks of the Emergency Dispatch Center to save the day!

Emergency Dispatch is a fast paced co-operative set collection dice game for 2-6 players. Players move colored dice representing various types of emergency equipment around a map of a city. Tiles are placed representing emergency calls that need to be answered. The emergencies are resolved by rolling the dice to get the required combination of symbols. Successfully resolving emergencies earns players bonuses that will help them on future calls. The game is won by solving a minimum number of emergencies over 8 turns without accruing 8 failures.

Description from the designer:

The King has died leaving no heirs.  A great election is to be held to determine the throne.  As an ambitious noble you must use your diplomatic prowess, economic wit, and (if all else fails) military might to secure the votes of enough Shires to garner the crown.

Shires is an epic 4X strategy game for 3-5 players.  The layout of the board, buildings, fields, and armies in each shire vary from game to game.  A blend of map generation, resource management, negotiation, backstabbing, and simple combat make Shires an engaging game with an always climatic conclusion.

Description from the designer:

Empires of the Clouds is a worker placement game for 2-4 players. Players take control of an airship company during the golden age of aviation. Each round players will upgrade their airships, research new technologies, seek out investors, woo clients, and fill their passenger manifests. At the conclusion of each round the airships take flight. Players earn points and cash by completing flights and manipulating passenger manifests.

Description from the publisher:

Steam Park: Play Dirty, a modular expansion for Steam Park, allows you to introduce many different new features one at a time — or all together:


    New stands provide players with additional powers that allow for more diverse strategies to become the richest robot in town.
    Six new characters offer players help in building their amusement park, making every new game of Steam Park different and unique.
    Scary espionage dice allow players to copy the other players' actions, but at a cost!
    New rides that expand the old ones, making them more profitable as long as you can afford the cost up front.
    Components to allow for up to five players to enjoy the experience of creating an amusement park.

Description from the publisher:

In Roboburg, everything is getting fancier! The city's Mayor has decided that finally the time has come for every citizen to get a free upgrade! Thanks to the amazing income the theme parks provide to City Hall, all the Roboburgers received a major luxury treatment for their chassis! After this event, though, a small group of Roboburgers started calling themselves V.I.R. (Very Important Roboburgers), due to their gold and shiny new body!

In Steam Park: Robots, you can exploit this "Robourgeois" to gain even more money and get away with it — but beware since these V.I.R. are picky and don't get along well with all the common Roboburgers, so you will have to make them feel special if you want them in your parks!

The Steam Park: Robots mini-expansion allows you to replace the wooden Roboburgers from the Steam Park base game and the Play Dirty expansion with shiny new plastic miniatures! This mini-expansion contains 56 plastic Robots and 1 embroidered bag compatible with all the other Steam Park™ products.

Description from the publisher:

A hilarious team based party game, inspired by The Whisper Challenge.

The game is played with two teams, max ~16-18 players. One team will draw a total of three "Dub" cards to create a sentence. The words on the cards are completely random and can be arranged in any order (ie "Kanye West" "Spaghetti" and "Pubic Hair", etc.) to create a "Dub". Once the Dub is created, they will tell it to the other team where a player will read it to their teammate to guess, who is wearing headphones playing loud, awful music. 4 points for guessing the Dub in 90 seconds, 8 bonus points for scoring in under 45. The entire Dub must be said without interruptions to score points!

Description from the publisher:

Castle Panic: Engines of War introduces an engineer who uses new resource cards to build catapults, ballistas, barricades, and more in order to better defend the castle from the growing capabilities of the monsters — and you're going to need his help since they're now charging at you with a siege tower, war wagon, and a battering ram. What's more, they've enlisted the help of a shaman, breathtaker, and goblin saboteurs. Prepare your forces!

Castle Panic: Engines of War can be used with the Castle Panic base game on its own or with any combination of other expansions.

Description from the designer:

All of the animals have escaped. From video footage throughout the park, the monkeys seem to be responsible. Whatever the case, it falls to you to restore order. Work with a team of zoo keepers to return the animals to their habitats.

Zoo Keeper is a cooperative card drafting and set collection game for 3-7 zoo keepers. The goal is simple: work through the entire deck, collecting sets of animals so that no zoo keeper exceeds his hand limit (usually six cards).

Play starts with the player who most recently visited a zoo and works clockwise. It will likely be advantageous to shuffle the cards thoroughly before play begins. Each round, or “card drafting phase”, one zoo keeper draws cards equal to the number of zoo keepers in your team.

Each player chooses one of the available cards and passes the cards clockwise. The last card goes to the last zoo keeper. Players place their chosen cards face up on the table in front of them. These cards are considered your “hand”. Zoo keepers look to create sets of two to five cards. Each card features two numbers.  The upper left number is the number needed to complete a set. The upper right number is how many of the card are in the deck. (The number 3/2 means that players must first collect a set of 3 before a set of 2.) Collect sets to free up your hand. Most cards also describe an action that is taken when a set is collected. Zoo keepers may win by collecting a specific set of cards (which varies based on the number of players in the game) or by working through the deck without any zoo keeper exceeding his hand limit (usually 6 cards). If any zoo keeper exceeds his hand limit before the deck runs out, zoo keepers are defeated.

Description from the publisher:

Roll fast and think faster in this addictively simple, simply addictive dice game of quick-thinking categories! From celebrities to science, songs to sports, SCRAMBO has 30 categories for everybody to test their speed, knowledge and nerves! There are over 7,000 category combinations possible!

SCRAMBO is for 2 or more players ages 10 and up, and plays in about 15 minutes.

One player rolls the 5 Category Dice, featuring a total of 30 different popular categories. That player then rolls the Letter Die. The goal of each turn is to match the letter rolled to each of their 5 categories before the time is up!

After the letter is rolled, another player begins rolling the 5 Timer Dice, each of which features 2 faces with a clock icon. Players bank the dice which roll with the clock facing up, and once all 5 show clocks, the turn is over!

A player shouts "SCRAMBO!" once either of these happen:
1. The category player answers all 5 of their Category Dice, or
2. The timer player rolls 5 clocks on the Timer Dice.

Players score 1 point for every category answered.
Timer player scores 1 point if the category player leaves any dice unanswered.
Timer player score 3 points if they end the turn before any categories can be answered.

First player to 20 points is the winner!

Description from the publisher:

Brettspiel Adventskalender 2016 is pretty much what the name says it is: A board game advent calendar for 2016 that contains 24 small expansions (2 of which are categorized as accessories on BGG: King of Tokyo/King of New York: Kookie (promo character) and Quadropolis: Christmas Tiles) for 24 different games, from different publishers. The game rules for each item are included, but they were also posted separately on the publisher's website on each day from December 1st to 24th.

Brettspiel Adventskalender 2016 was sold in two formats: a giant box (as with the Brettspiel Adventskalender 2015 in which each item has its own "window") and a small compact box for the international traveler.
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

boardgame: East Africa (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards: some are for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city: others give you extra facilities to complete your tickets. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

There are border controls which require special passes.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn double points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

There is also a minimap which can be used for a shorter game.

Description from the publisher:

On a damp, overcast Sunday in June 1815, twenty years of continuous warfare the Napoleonic Wars came to a violent and bloody conclusion on a rain-soaked field in Belgium. These wars were truly a world affair, with European powers fighting battles not only on the mainland of Europe but as far as India, Egypt, the Caribbean and America.

The greatest generals of the age finally faced each other on the field of battle; The Duke of Wellington, rooted to a little-known ridge, faced, the Emperor of the French. Napoleon Bonaparte, master of the attack, opposed brigades of British, Germans, Dutch and Belgians; brigades of an infamous army that, despite horrific casualties, clung to the ridge for over nine hours to ensure that the arrival of BlǬcher's Prussian army would put the Duke victory beyond doubt.

The Battle of Waterloo became a true landmark in military history, one that will never fade, and nothing on such a grand scale would be seen again.

This new starter set for Black Powder contains everything needed to start your campaign for victory at Waterloo!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: The Order of Vampire Hunters: From Blood to Dust Expansion
description:
This expansion includes a new species of Vampire, the Lurkers, and a previously unseen Elder that introduce new game mechanisms e.g. Water, Cracks and Blood Lust. Includes 2 new locations, the abandoned subway and the sewers that can be easily integrated into any Hunt.

Box contains:

•    16 Highly detailed plastic miniatures:
•    15 Lurker Vampires in 3 different poses.
•    Drakku, an Elder Vampire.
•    2 Vampire ID Cards.
•    4 Large Double-sided Map Tiles.
•    1 Small Double-sided Map Tile.
•    15 Encounter Cards.
•    4 Vampire Activation Cards.
•    69 Tokens.

No longer satisfied with their Dens in the remote and forgotten corners of the world, the Night brings terror to new urban locations.

Take the fight underground with the From Blood to Dust expansion for The Order of Vampire Hunters. Enter the abandoned subways and venture into the damp underground sewer tunnels in pursuit of a new breed of Vampire, the Lurkers. Agile and tough, these creatures are just a foreshadowing of the nightmarish Elder Drakku.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The purpose of 'Square Game' is to gain control of the most squares by the time the game ends.
In the game players take the pieces and place them in the play space (this is referred to as the board although there is not a physical board). When a player places a piece on the board they perform the action indicated on the piece. When a player places a piece to complete a square they capture the area. 
Players move and play pieces creating or breaking squares until no further moves can be made and the game comes to an end.

Invasion Evasion is a party game that pits friends against each other to prevent or cause a hostile alien takeover.

The invasion has begun! Flying saucers are abducting innocent people off the streets and returning them corrupted and changed. It has become impossible to tell apart harmless civilians from vile alien scum.

Deep inside of an underground bunker is a team of humans trying desperately to stop the attacks. They have developed a cure to the alien parasite to transform humans back to normal, but are unable to mass produce it. As they try to gather the supplies necessary to save the planet, it becomes clear that some among them cannot be trusted… and have more nefarious plans in mind.
Each player receives a secret role and either works with the Humans to mass produce a cure or joins the Aliens
in the construction of a Doomsday Device. The fate of the world hangs in the balance.
 Human Victory: Have one Human player hold all three types of Humanity Cards - Alien Specimen, Chemical Stockroom, and Medical Laboratory

Alien Victory: Have one Alien player hold all three types of Doomsday Cards - Black Hole Bomb, Perpetual Motion Drive, and Quantum Reactor

As each Humanity and Doomsday card is found it becomes more and more important that they are placed in safe hands, but in Invasion Evasion the teams can constantly change. Who do you trust?

Set of 3 cards given with game pre-order from publisher, and some conventions. Includes 2 new characters and a new suspect.

New Suspect card is The Napolean of Crime. He has a Lead value of 21 (!!), but ANY combination of Evidence cards may be played to him. 
"You can play any combination of Evidence cards to this Lead. He is the only Lead Suspect card place during setup. You cannot Pursue a new Lead Suspect."

The new Character cards are labeled EX1 and EX2.

EX1: Dr. John Watson, physician, (0 pipes) "Move the Holmes marker twice when you take the Assist action."

EX2: Mycroft Holmes, Agent, (4 pipes) "When you Assist, do not move the Holmes marker."

"FBI, MI6, Mossad, Interpol: the secret services of the major world powers have long been panicked because the biggest criminals of the world have allied themselves with the aim of creating an international cartel addicted to crime. The night is dark and rainy, another crime was just carried out, and at a cold police station twelve characters were collected seemingly anonymous but perhaps hiding unspeakable criminals qualities."

With Unusual Suspects: Burn After Playing, an expansion for Unusual Suspects you can add 55 new unusual faces and 90 questions to the base game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The game is a classic memory game with a twist. The object of the game is to win FLIP tokens by playing high numbered cards. It is like a game of hot potato or musical chairs. The player left with the best card when they stop moving wins!

Players start with a card and a token.  On a turn, the player draws a second token and plays one of the two.  The tokens rotate the face down cards left or right, or the FLIP card reveals all the cards.  The highest value card wins the flip token.  The first to collect 3 tokens wins.

boardgame: Tanks: Sherman V and Firefly Tank Expansion
description:
The Sherman V and Firefly:
At the core of your Armoured Squadron are the sturdy American Sherman tanks. The versatile 75mm-armed model is supplemented by the Firefly, a British-modified Sherman armed with the powerful 17 pdr gun to counter the Germans’ new heavy Tiger and the Panther tanks. The Sherman Firefly tanks have excellent anti-tank performance with 17 pdr rounds, which slice through Panthers with ease. This combination of firepower and reliability is more than enough to deal with anything the enemy have.

Description from the publisher:

Years have passed since the fall of the magic barrier and the rediscovery of Hyperborea's heart. The six nations are still at war with each other and are always looking for more powerful weapons to win it. Digging in the ancient crypts, they think to have finally found them.

Shortly before their sudden destruction, the mages from the lost civilization poured their souls into magical objects of immense power; the evil souls became Black Soul Cubes, while the good ones became White Soul Cubes. Everything was hidden in sacred arcs, named Relics, that couldn't be controlled by others.

In Hyperborea: Light & Shadow, these powerful Relics provide enormous knowledge and powers and the Soul Cubes are now in the hands of the six factions: Will the destiny of humans be dominated by war, or will wisdom and prosperity prevail between the realms of Hyperborea?

boardgame: Endure the Stars: Cybernetic Onslaught Expansion
description:
These automatons mean nothing to me. Though we may share some aesthetic similarities, there is no soul in those machines. I understand that I may not be seen as human, but I am still a member of this crew and wish to fight for its survival!     The Technician character class has decided to join the fight! With abilities specially designed to counter the Robotic Sentries they are a great addition to the fight for survival!

Pack contents:
Olympian boss deck - 22 cards
3x Dashboards
2x Ability tokens
3x Health tokens
2x Resolve tokens
9x Injury token
9x Radar tokens
10x Artemis miniatures
10x Sip miniatures
1x XL Olympian Sentry miniature
2x Technician miniatures (male and female variants)
2x Colour matched base clips
1x Technician Injury dice

boardgame: Endure the Stars: Redeemers of Purpose Expansion
description:
Adds the Preacher character with miniature, dashboard, followers, and preacher cards.

Contents:
- Health/Injury/Resolve tokens
- 9 Radar tokens
- 1 Preacher dashboard
- 10 Male Fanatic miniatures
- 10 Female Fanatic miniatures
- 1 Preacher miniature
- 22 Preacher cards
- Rules, and new missions

Alchemists: The King's Golem, an expansion to Alchemists, brings you a new logic puzzle with new rewards and consequences. Can you figure out how to animate a golem? And equally important, can you convince the king you are making progress?

The King's Golem actually contains four expansions:

    In Startup Funding, players get a chance to customize the initial resources in their laboratories.
    The Busy Days expansion makes each round different by offering new rewards (and new costs!) on the order spaces.
    The Royal Encyclopedia is an entirely new venue for publishing your research.
    And in The Golem Project, players have the challenge of animating a creature made from magic and clay.


The expansions are presented in order of complexity. Startup Funding and Busy Days will be just fine for beginners. The Royal Encyclopedia is for players accustomed to the logic puzzle of the base game. The Golem Project is for advanced players who want a new deduction challenge.

description:
Description from the publisher:

Super Powered Smash Masters The fast-paced, tongue-in-cheek, superhero-themed, customizable card game you can play with your whole family! It’s crammed full of humor, amazing heroes, bizarro story twists, and dangerous puns.

				
				
					Game Features:
    Recruit a team of heroes to battle other hero teams — for no reason! It’s just like comics!
    Defeat all rivals to win.
    Customize your deck! Choose your best heroes and create a deck that compliments their abilities.
    Can be combined with other SPSM sets.
    Groups of up to 8 people (or more) can play together!
    Fast-Paced Fun! Beat up your friends and still have time for… well… another game of Smash Masters!


				
				
					Cores Set Includes:
    184 fully illustrated cards
    24 Unique Character Cards, each with their own powers and corresponding Super Cards
    36 Story Cards full of crazy, comic-book themed twists and turns.
    20 Team Up Cards
    80 Smash Cards
    Rule Book


				
				
					New Recruits Standalone Expansion Set Includes:
    92 fully illustrated cards
    12 Unique Character Cards, each with their own powers and corresponding Super Cards
    18 Story Cards full of crazy, comic-book themed twists and turns.
    10 Team Up Cards
    40 Smash Cards
    Rule Sheet

Description from the publisher:

España 20: Volume 2 features two complete games covering key battles fought during the Peninsular War (1807-1814).

Rudi Garcia's Talavera 20 recreates the major battle which occurred near the city of Talavera de la Reina on 27 to 28 July 1809. One player commands the French army under Napoleon's brother, Joseph, the self-proclaimed King of Spain, and the other commands the combined allied armies of Great Britain (under Sir Arthur Wellesley) and Spain (under Captain-General Gregorio de la Cuesta). After his attempt to liberate Madrid from the French was repulsed, Cuesta's army fell back to Talavera, which was covered by the British. Starting with a daring night attack, Joseph repeatedly attempted to push the allies out of their superb defensive positions, to no avail. As the French, can you find and exploit a weak spot in the allied lines? As the allies, can you hold your shaky alliance together and fend off the French (all the while keeping a nervous eye out for a second French army that might unexpectedly show up behind your armies)?

Bussaco 20: Second Edition, from designer Lance McMillan, features the battle occurring on 26 September 1810, during the early stages of the French invasion of Portugal. One player commands the French army of Marshal André Masséna, while the other controls Sir Arthur Wellesley, Viscount of Wellington's combined British and reconstituted Portuguese army. Historically, a distracted Masséna launched a series of uncoordinated frontal assaults straight up a steep ridge into the teeth of the British defenses, suffering heavy losses before Wellington elected to disengage and fall back to Lisbon. Can you as Masséna avoid the same mistakes, or as Wellington can you use your superior position to achieve a decisive victory?
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
You've seen them all over YouTube, now they're racing on your table top!

Tube Heroes Racers, the mobile racing game app, has been transformed into a fast-paced card game! Race Tube Heroes DanTDM, Captain Sparkles, Ssundee, Vikkstar123, ExplodingTNT, and Ali-A are all in a fantastic race to the finish line, but watch out for mines, tarantulas, and lost penguins! Play Pit Crew cards to trap your opponents and blast through the track with booster cards!

User summary
行けたら行くよ! (Iketara Ikuyo!) in English would mean "I'll go if I can!". This game is the official board game of Japanese Manga "Majime Kei Kuzu no Nichijou (Everyday of serious type scum)".

"I'll skip school events more smartly than everyone else does!"

In this game, players try to live lazily, eluding tiresome school activities while not destroying your reputation with your classmates. Your reputation is gauged by school hierarchy. You go up ranks in the hierarchy if you accomplish something with your classmates, and go down if you blow off something.

Players have the same deck of cards, each of which is numbered 0 to 2 indicating how much energy they will commit to an activity. Each round, an event card is drawn. Players secretly determine how much energy they commit on that event, placing cards face-down. Then all cards are revealed simultaneously.

If the event is co-operative, players need to commit a certain number of total energy to accomplish. If successful, all involved players go up ranks. If the event is competitive, only players who have committed the highest energy are successful and go up ranks.

But you should not over-commit an event because the victory goes to the player with the most energy saved in their hand. Because of that, you may play a "Blow off" card secretly instead of committing energy. It saves you from wasting your cards, and you may draw two cards instead of regular one card at end of the round. But if two or more players play "Blow off", their ranks go down.

After the round, cards played are discarded, except for "Blow off" cards which return to players' hand. When you go up to a certain rank, you may have a friend who can use a special ability for you.

The game ends after several rounds, and you win the game if you save the most energy in your hand, but you can't claim the victory if you are at the lowest rank in the school hierarchy.

Publisher's summary
「オレは学校をサボる！誰よりも上手く！！」
学校で起こる様々なめんどくさい青春イベントをやり過ごし、
周りに嫌われぬまま、楽して生き抜くことを目指すゲーム。
人間関係わずらわしい！めんどくさい事やりたくない！
周りを欺き、裏では手を抜き、本能のまま生きる事を目指す！
学校行事に参加してスクールカーストを上げ、
ここぞという時にサボって体力を貯めこむ！
漫画『まじめ系クズの日常』公式ゲーム
Maneuver each of the five marbles ("Mazarbles") through the maze to get the highest score.

Striker! is a set of straightforward rules for playing realistic miniature football (or soccer, depending on your side of the pond) matches on the tabletop. Each match plays just like a wargame using miniatures to represent players. Challenges, passes and shots on goal are resolved by rolling dice. Many strategies and tactics that are employed in a real football match also apply to this game: midfield pressing, tiki taka passing, switches of play, and many more. If you can do it on a football field, Striker! allows you to do it on the tabletop. The game is meant for 2 players, but can also be played in groups or even solo, taking about 90 - 120 minutes to play.

Just like the real game there are red cards, yellow cards, and shots on goal. Each phase of play is broken into stages: the attackers move their players, or pass, or dribble etc, and then the defenders get a phase to counter, and cover the attacking runs. Players move specific distances, just as in a standard wargame. The game lasts 40 phases (20 attacking and 20 defending phases for a half of football) with each phase ending after the defenders finalize their moves, or when a pass goes astray and possession is turned over to the defenders through a tackle or wayward pass.

boardgame: The Gottman Couples Retreat Board Game
description:
Join your partner on a virtual island couples retreat, where you’re sure to have fun while building a better relationship! Just one round of play will show you why 86% of couples report significant benefits from using the Gottman approach to create stronger, deeper bonds between you and your sweetie.

Designed for couples who want to deepen their relationship and have fun together. Partners take turns rolling the dice and moving their game pieces with the goal of “returning home” from vacation with more closeness and intimacy.

Strengthen your relationship and deepen your connection by playing together in a tropical paradise! The Gottman Couples Retreat Board Game unites the best of our renowned, research-based relationship tools in a playful, accessible format. Balancing thoughtful conversation topics with fun, intimate action items, this "retreat" makes exploring each other's worlds enjoyable and enlightening. Even more, you'll be making deposits in your "Emotional Bank Account" by building on positive moments with each other during gameplay. Whether you're new partners or have been married for decades, the Gottman Couples Retreat Board Game is ideal for an at-home date night....or use it on anniversaries as a "ritual of connection" in your relationship. Also makes a special birthday gift or wedding present!

The game ends when the ship field is reached.

boardgame: 1941: What If? An Alternative History Wargame of a Second Winter War
description:
1941: What If? An Alternative History Wargame of a Second Winter War is a low-to-intermediate complexity two-player alternative history wargame that’s also easily adaptable to solitaire play. It allows you to investigate the parameters inherent in the situation in Central Eastern Europe, at the end of 1940, had the Bulgarians decided to ally with the Soviets instead of the Germans, which historically they considered doing. Long story short (see the support article elsewhere in this issue), the result would’ve been the Second War’s eastern front erupting half a year prior to its historic start date under both natural and manmade conditions vastly different than pertained at that later date.

Each hexagon on the map represents 45 miles (73 km) from side to opposite side. Each turn represents a half a month of ‘real time.’ Each unit of maneuver represents an army or corps or ad hoc equivalent of those sizes of units.

The Soviet (a.k.a. “Soviet/Allied” or “Allied”) player commands all the Soviet units in the game along with those of Bulgaria, Greece and Britain. The German (a.k.a. “German/Axis” or “Axis”) player commands all the German units in the game, along with those of Turkey, Slovakia, Hungary, Romania and Italy. Yugoslavia starts the game neutral and out of play. Its government is leaning pro-Allied, however, and it will likely join the war on that side during the course of the game. When/if that occurs, the two Yugoslav-Croatian (4th and 7th) armies will either disintegrate, their soldiers dispersing to their homes, or join the Axis while the rest of the Yugoslav armed forces fight in the Allied side.

The orders of battle contain pretty all the units present in these armies at this time within this theater of operations. The Germans are only missing the non-mechanized corps they had garrisoning their western conquests. The Turks are minus the three corps they kept in their Caucasus and Syrian border areas. Similarly, the Soviets have everything they had available historically in the regions of the USSR lying west of the Urals, north of the Caucasus and south of the Arctic. Their reinforcements are based on what they put together during the first three months of the historic war in the east. Their Independent Coastal Army wasn’t organized historically until soon after the start of Operation Barbarossa; however – given the geo-strategic circumstances of this might-have-been war and the fact the resources needed to organize it already existed at this time – it was only reasonable to include the ICA as a starting Soviet unit.

description: A red themed, offense focused expansion to A.D.A.P.T. Includes 5 new bodies, 8 new parts, and 3 status trackers. Also introduces Autotomy, Grab, Inking, Neg Lethality, and Oversize: (Location). Adds +1 player to the base game.

boardgame: A.D.A.P.T.: Green Reptile & Amphibian Expansion Pack
description: A green-themed, defense-heavy expansion to A.D.A.P.T. Includes 5 new bodies, 8 new parts, and 3 status trackers. Also introduces metamorphosis and withdraw. Adds +1 player to the base game.

boardgame: A.D.A.P.T.: Blue Fantasy Creature Expansion Pack
description: A blue-themed, magical expansion to A.D.A.P.T. Includes 5 new bodies, 8 new parts, and 3 status trackers. Also introduces blast, changeling, immune: poison, oversize: (location), and shapeshifting.

User summary
Twin Princes is 双子の王子 (Hutago no Ouji). Originally published in 2015, a new colour edition was made in 2016.

Long ago in a desert country, the Twin Princes decided to race for the right to the throne in a mutually agreeable method, in order not to cause a future struggle because of themselves.

Twin Princes is a card game for two players. There are six territory cards on the table, and players try to control them by placing influence cards on them. Each player also controls a Prince meeple, which move on the territories and affect the placement of cards.

A game consists of six round. Each round, five cards are drawn. One player (divider) split them into two groups, and the other (selector) takes one of these groups. The divider takes the rest. Then they take turns playing a card on a territory.

After sixth round, for each territory, count influence points each player has place on that territory. The player with highest influence controls the territory and scores victory points. The player with the highest total victory points wins the game.

Publisher's summary
テーマ
遠い昔砂漠の国。 双子の王子は 自分たちが原因で争いが起きないよう、お互いが納得できる形で 王位継承権を競ったのです。

遊び方
２人専用のゲームです。
分配者と選択者になります、分配者が５枚のカードを２つに分けて、選択者が選び、手札にします。残った方を分配者が手札にします。
分配者から順番にカードをプレイします。エリアとカードのマジョリティを競います
分配者を交代して６ラウンド行います。
終了時点で勝利点の多いプレイヤーが勝利します。

—description from bodoge-hoobby.net (translated)

A two-player card game in which players compete for the right to succeed to the throne as one of the twin princes.

Distribute cards → Arrange cards in 5 areas, complete 6 rounds and compete for the final score.

And the interesting part of this game is the distribution of cards.
